text: The contact of the senses with their objects, O Arjuna, gives rise to feelings of cold and heat, pleasure and pain. They come and go, never lasting long. Endure them, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But the contacts of the organs with the objects are the producers of cold and heat, happiness, and sorrow. They have a beginning and an end and are transient. Bear them, O descendant of Bharata.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The contact of the senses with the objects, O son of Kunti, which causes heat and cold, pleasure and pain, has a beginning and an end; they are impermanent; endure them bravely, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Kunti! The touches with the matras cause feelings of cold and heat, pleasure and pain; they are of the nature of coming and going and are transient. Bear them, O Descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Those external relations that bring cold and heat, pain and happiness come and go; they are not permanent. Endure them bravely, O Prince!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.15।। कारण कि हे पुरुषोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन! सुख-दुःखमें सम रहनेवाले जिस धीर मनुष्यको ये मात्रास्पर्श (पदार्थ) व्यथित (सुखी-दुःखी) नहीं कर पाते, वह अमर होनेमें समर्थ हो जाता है अर्थात् वह अमर हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.15।। हे पुरुषश्रेष्ठ ! दुख और सुख में समान भाव से रहने वाले जिस धीर पुरुष को ये व्यथित नहीं कर सकते हैं वह अमृतत्व (मोक्ष) का अधिकारी होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For, he whom these do not affect, O chief of men, and to whom pain and pleasure are equal - that steadfast man alone is worthy of immortality.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, who is foremost among men, verily, the person whom these do not torment, the wise man to whom sorrow and happiness are the same—he is fit for immortality.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That firm man, whom surely these afflictions do not, O chief among men, to whom pleasure and pain are the same, is fit for attaining immortality.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O best among persons! That wise person becomes immortal, whom these situations do not trouble, and to whom pleasure and pain are equal.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The hero whose soul is unmoved by circumstances, who accepts pleasure and pain with equanimity, is only fit for immortality.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.16।। (टिप्पणी प0 55) असत् का तो भाव (सत्ता) विद्यमान नहीं है और सत् का अभाव विद्यमान नहीं है, तत्त्वदर्शी महापुरुषोंने इन दोनोंका ही अन्त अर्थात् तत्त्व देखा है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.16।। असत् वस्तु का तो अस्तित्व नहीं है और सत् का कभी अभाव नहीं है। इस प्रकार इन दोनों का ही तत्त्व,  तत्त्वदर्शी ज्ञानी पुरुषों के द्वारा देखा गया है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The unreal never comes into being, the real never ceases to be. The truth about these two is seen by the seers.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: There is no being of the unreal; the real has no non-existence. Yet, the nature of both has been realized by the seers of Truth.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The unreal has no being; there is no non-being of the real; the truth about both has been seen by the knowers of the truth (or the seers of the essence).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Birth does not happen to what is non-existent, and destruction to what is existent; the finality of these two has been seen by the seers of reality.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: That which is not shall never be; that which is shall never cease to be. These truths are self-evident to the wise.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.17।। अविनाशी तो उसको जान, जिससे यह सम्पूर्ण संसार व्याप्त है। इस अविनाशीका विनाश कोई भी नहीं कर सकता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.17।। उस वस्तु को तुम अविनाशी जानों,  जिससे यह सम्पूर्ण जगत् व्याप्त है। इस अव्यय का नाश करने में कोई भी समर्थ नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded. No one can cause the destruction of this immutable.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded. No one can bring about the destruction of this immutable.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know that to be indestructible, by which all this is pervaded. No one can cause the destruction of that, the Imperishable.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: And know That to be indestructible, by Which all this (universe) is pervaded; no one is capable of causing destruction to this unchanging One.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The Spirit, which pervades all that is seen, is imperishable. Nothing can destroy it.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.18।। अविनाशी, अप्रमेय और नित्य रहनेवाले इस शरीरी के ये देह अन्तवाले कहे गये हैं। इसलिये हे अर्जुन! तुम युद्ध करो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.18।। इस नाशरहित अप्रमेय नित्य देही आत्मा के ये सब शरीर नाशवान् कहे गये हैं। इसलिये हे भारत ! तुम युद्ध करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: These bodies of the Jiva (the embodied self) are said to have an end, while the Jiva itself is eternal, indestructible, and incomprehensible. Therefore, fight, O Bharata (Arjuna).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: These destructible bodies are said to belong to the everlasting, indestructible, and indeterminable embodied One. Therefore, O descendant of Bharata, join the battle.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: These bodies of the embodied Self, which are eternal, indestructible, and immeasurable, are said to have an end. Therefore, fight, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: These physical bodies, which have an end and suffer destruction, are declared to belong to the eternal, embodied Soul, which is indestructible and incomprehensible. Therefore, fight, O descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The material bodies that this Eternal, Indestructible, and Immeasurable Spirit inhabits are all finite; therefore, fight, O valiant man!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.19।। जो मनुष्य इस अविनाशी शरीरीको मारनेवाला मानता है और जो मनुष्य इसको मरा मानता है, वे दोनों ही इसको नहीं जानते; क्योंकि यह न मारता है और न मारा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.19।। जो इस आत्मा को मारने वाला समझता है और जो इसको मरा समझता है वे दोनों ही नहीं जानते हैं,  क्योंकि यह आत्मा न मरता है और न मारा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who considers It (the self) to be the slayer, and he who thinks of It as slain—both are ignorant. For, the self neither slays nor is being slain.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who thinks of this One as the killer, and he who thinks of this One as the one being killed, both of them do not know. This One neither kills nor is It killed.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who takes the Self to be the slayer and he who thinks it is slain, neither of them knows. It does not slay, nor is it slain.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever views This as the slayer and whoever believes This to be the slain, both of them do not understand: This does not slay, nor is This slain.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who thinks that the Spirit kills, and he who thinks of it as killed, are both ignorant. The Spirit neither kills nor is it killed.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.20।। यह शरीरी न कभी जन्मता है और न मरता है तथा यह उत्पन्न होकर फिर होनेवाला नहीं है। यह जन्मरहित, नित्य-निरन्तर रहनेवाला, शाश्वत और पुराण (अनादि) है। शरीरके मारे जानेपर भी यह नहीं मारा जाता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.20।। यह आत्मा किसी काल में भी न जन्मता है और न मरता है और न यह एक बार होकर फिर अभावरूप होने वाला है। यह आत्मा अजन्मा, नित्य, शाश्वत और पुरातन है,  शरीर के नाश होने पर भी इसका नाश नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: It is never born; it never dies; having come into existence once, it never ceases to exist. Unborn, eternal, perpetual, and primordial, it is not destroyed when the body is destroyed.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Never is this One born, nor does It ever die; nor, having come into existence, does It cease to be. This One is birthless, eternal, undecaying, and ancient; It is not killed when the body is killed.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: It is not born, nor does it ever die; after having been, it again does not cease to be; unborn, eternal, changeless, and ancient, it is not killed when the body is killed.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This is neither born nor ever dies; nor, having not been at one time, will it come to be again. It is unborn, destructionless, eternal, and ancient, and is not slain even when the body is slain.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It was not born; it will never die, nor, once having been, can it cease to exist. Unborn, eternal, ever-enduring, yet most ancient, the spirit does not die when the body is dead.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.21।। हे पृथानन्दन! जो मनुष्य इस शरीरीको अविनाशी, नित्य, जन्मरहित और अव्यय जानता है, वह कैसे किसको मारे और कैसे किसको मरवाये?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.21।। हे पार्थ ! जो पुरुष इस आत्मा को अविनाशी,  नित्य और अव्ययस्वरूप जानता है,  वह कैसे किसको मरवायेगा और कैसे किसको मारेगा?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who knows this self to be indestructible, unborn, unchanging, and thus eternal—how, O Arjuna, does he cause anyone to be killed, and whom does he kill?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, he who knows this One as indestructible, eternal, birthless, and undecaying—how can that person kill, or cause to be killed, whom?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whoever knows it to be indestructible, eternal, unborn, and inexhaustible, how can that person slay, O Arjuna, or cause to be slain?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever realizes this to be changeless, destructionless, unborn, and immutable, how can that person be slain? How can they slay anyone else? O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who knows the Spirit to be indestructible, immortal, unborn, and always the same, how should he kill or cause to be killed?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.22।। मनुष्य जैसे पुराने कपड़ोंको छोड़कर दूसरे नये कपड़े धारण कर लेता है, ऐसे ही देही पुराने शरीरोंको छोड़कर दूसरे नये शरीरोंमें चला जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.22।। जैसे मनुष्य जीर्ण वस्त्रों को त्यागकर दूसरे नये वस्त्रों को धारण करता है, वैसे ही देही जीवात्मा पुराने शरीरों को त्याग कर दूसरे नए शरीरों को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: As a man casts off worn-out garments and puts on new ones, so does the embodied self cast off its worn-out bodies and enter into new ones.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: As after rejecting worn-out clothes, a man takes up other new ones, likewise, after rejecting worn-out bodies, the embodied one unites with other new ones.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Just as a man casts off worn-out clothes and puts on new ones, so too the embodied Self casts off worn-out bodies and enters others that are new.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Just as a man rejects tattered garments and takes on new ones, so too, the embodied Self, discarding worn-out bodies, rightly obtains new ones.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As a man discards his threadbare robes and puts on new ones, so the Spirit throws off its worn-out bodies and takes on fresh ones.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.23।। शस्त्र इस शरीरीको काट नहीं सकते, अग्नि इसको जला नहीं सकती, जल इसको गीला नहीं कर सकता और वायु इसको सुखा नहीं सकती।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.23।। इस आत्मा को शस्त्र काट नहीं सकते और न अग्नि इसे जला सकती है ; जल इसे गीला नहीं कर सकता और वायु इसे सुखा नहीं सकती।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Weapons cannot cleave It, fire cannot burn It, waters cannot wet It, and wind cannot dry It.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Weapons cannot cut It, fire cannot burn It, water cannot moisten It, and air cannot dry It.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Weapons cannot cut it, fire cannot burn it, water cannot wet it, wind cannot dry it.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Weapons cannot cut This; fire cannot burn This; water cannot make This wet; and the wind cannot make This dry.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Weapons cannot cleave it, fire cannot burn it, water cannot drench it, and wind cannot dry it.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.24।। यह शरीरी काटा नहीं जा सकता, यह जलाया नहीं जा सकता, यह गीला नहीं किया जा सकता और यह सुखाया भी नहीं जा सकता। कारण कि यह नित्य रहनेवाला सबमें परिपूर्ण, अचल, स्थिर स्वभाववाला और अनादि है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.24।। क्योंकि यह आत्मा अच्छेद्य (काटी नहीं जा सकती),  अदाह्य (जलाई नहीं जा सकती),  अक्लेद्य (गीली नहीं हो सकती ) और अशोष्य (सुखाई नहीं जा सकती) है;  यह नित्य,  सर्वगत,  स्थाणु (स्थिर),  अचल और सनातन है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: It cannot be cleaved; It cannot be burned; It cannot be wetted, and It cannot be dried; It is eternal, all-pervasive, stable, immovable, and primordial.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: It cannot be cut, burned, moistened, or dried up; it is eternal, omnipresent, stationary, unmoving, and unchanging.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: This Self cannot be cut, burned, wetted, nor dried up; it is eternal, all-pervasive, stable, immovable, and ancient.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This cannot be cut; this cannot be burnt; this cannot be made wet nor dried; this is eternal, all-pervading, stable, immovable, and everlasting.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is impenetrable; it cannot be drowned, scorched, or dried. It is eternal, all-pervasive, unchanging, immovable, and most ancient.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.25।। यह देही प्रत्यक्ष नहीं दीखता, यह चिन्तनका विषय नहीं है और यह निर्विकार कहा जाता है। अतः इस देहीको ऐसा जानकर शोक नहीं करना चाहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.25।। यह आत्मा अव्यक्त,  अचिन्त्य और अविकारी कहा जाता है;  इसलिए इसको इस प्रकार जानकर तुमको शोक करना उचित नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: This (self) is said to be unmanifest, inconceivable, and unchanging. Therefore, knowing it thus, it does not befit you to grieve.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: It is said that This is unmanifest; This is inconceivable; This is unchangeable. Therefore, having known This thus, you ought not to grieve.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: This Self is said to be unmanifested, unthinkable, and unchangeable. Therefore, knowing this to be so, you should not grieve.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This is declared to be non-evident, imponderable, and unchangeable. Therefore, understanding this as such, you should not lament.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is named the Unmanifest, the Unthinkable, and the immutable. Therefore, knowing the Spirit as such, you have no cause to grieve.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.26।। हे महाबाहो ! अगर तुम इस देहीको नित्य पैदा होनेवाला अथवा नित्य मरनेवाला भी मानो, तो भी तुम्हें इस प्रकार शोक नहीं करना चाहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.26।। और यदि तुम आत्मा को नित्य जन्मने और नित्य मरने वाला मानो तो भी,  हे महाबाहो !  इस प्रकार शोक करना तुम्हारे लिए उचित नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Or, if you consider this self as constantly being born and constantly dying, O mighty-armed one, it still does not become you to feel grief.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: On the other hand, if you think this One is born continually or constantly dies, even then, O mighty-armed one, you should not grieve.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But even if thou thinkest of It as constantly being born and constantly dying, even then, O mighty-armed one, thou shouldst not grieve.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: On the other hand, if you deem this as being born constantly or as dying constantly, even then, O mighty-armed one, you should not lament.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even if thou thinkest of it as constantly being born and constantly dying, O Mighty Man, thou still hast no cause to grieve.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.27।। क्योंकि पैदा हुएकी जरूर मृत्यु होगी और मरे हुएका जरूर जन्म होगा। इस (जन्म-मरण-रूप परिवर्तन के प्रवाह) का परिहार अर्थात् निवारण नहीं हो सकता। अतः इस विषयमें तुम्हें शोक नहीं करना चाहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.27।। जन्मने वाले की मृत्यु निश्चित है और मरने वाले का जन्म निश्चित है;  इसलिए जो अटल है अपरिहार्य - है उसके विषय में तुमको शोक नहीं करना चाहिये।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For, death is certain for the born, and rebirth is certain for the dead; therefore, you should not grieve for what is inevitable.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For the death of anyone born is certain, and the rebirth of the dead is certain. Therefore, you should not grieve over an inevitable fact.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For the born, death is certain, and for the dead, birth is certain; therefore, you should not grieve over the inevitable.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Death is certain indeed for what is born; and birth is certain for the dead. Therefore, you should not lament over something that is unavoidable.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: For death is certain for that which is born, just as birth is certain for that which is dead. Therefore, do not grieve for what is inevitable.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.28।। हे भारत ! सभी प्राणी जन्मसे पहले अप्रकट थे और मरनेके बाद अप्रकट हो जायँगे, केवल बीचमें ही प्रकट दीखते हैं। अतः इसमें शोक करनेकी बात ही क्या है?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.28।। हे भारत ! समस्त प्राणी जन्म से पूर्व और मृत्यु के बाद अव्यक्त अवस्था में रहते हैं और बीच में व्यक्त होते हैं। फिर उसमें चिन्ता या शोक की क्या बात है ?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: O Arjuna, beings have an unknown beginning, a known middle, and an unknown end. What is there to grieve about in all this?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O descendant of Bharata, all beings remain unmanifested in the beginning; they become manifested in the middle. After death, they certainly become unmanifested. What lamentation can there be for them?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Beings are unmanifest in their beginning, manifest in their middle state, O Arjuna, and unmanifest again in their end. What is there to grieve about?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O descendant of Bharata! The beings have an unmanifest beginning, a manifest middle, and an unmanifest end. Therefore, why mourn?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The end and beginning of beings are unknown; we only see the intervening formations. So, what is there to grieve about?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.29।। कोई इस शरीरीको आश्चर्यकी तरह देखता है और वैसे ही अन्य कोई इसका आश्चर्यकी तरह वर्णन करता है तथा अन्य कोई इसको आश्चर्यकी तरह सुनता है; और इसको सुनकर भी कोई नहीं जानता। अर्थात यह शरीरी दुर्विज्ञेय है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.29।। कोई इसे आश्चर्य के समान देखता है;  कोई इसके विषय में आश्चर्य के समान कहता है;  और कोई अन्य पुरुष इसे आश्चर्य के समान सुनता है;  और फिर कोई सुनकर भी नहीं जानता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: One regards This (self) as a wonder; another speaks of It as a wonder; yet another hears of It as a wonder; and even after hearing of It, one does not know It.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Someone visualizes it as a wonder; someone else talks of it as a wonder; someone else hears of it as a wonder; and yet someone else does not even realize it after hearing about it.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: One sees this (the Self) as a wonder; another speaks of it as a wonder; another hears of it as a wonder; yet, having heard, none understands it at all.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This someone observes as a wonder; similarly, another speaks of This as a wonder; another hears This as a wonder; but even after hearing, not even one understands This.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: One hears of the Spirit with surprise, another thinks it marvelous, the third listens without comprehending. Thus, though many are told about it, scarcely is there one who knows it.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.30।। हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन! सबके देहमें यह देही नित्य ही अवध्य है। इसलिये सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके लिये अर्थात् किसी भी प्राणीके लिये तुम्हें शोक नहीं करना चाहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.30।। हे भारत ! यह देही आत्मा सबके शरीर में सदा ही अवध्य है, इसलिए समस्त प्राणियों के लिए तुम्हें शोक करना उचित नहीं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The self in the body, O Arjuna, is eternal and indestructible. This is the case for all selves in all bodies. Therefore, it is not fitting for you to feel grief for any being.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O descendant of Bharata, this embodied Self existing in everyone's body can never be killed; therefore, you ought not to grieve for all these beings.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: This indweller in the body of everyone is ever indestructible, O Arjuna; therefore, you should not grieve for any creature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O descendant of Bharata! This embodied One in the body of everyone is ever incapable of being slain. Therefore, you should not lament over all beings.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Do not be anxious about these armies; the spirit in man is imperishable.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.31।। अपने स्वधर्म (क्षात्रधर्म) को देखकर भी तुम्हें विकम्पित अर्थात् कर्तव्य-कर्मसे विचलित नहीं होना चाहिये; क्योंकि धर्ममय युद्धसे बढ़कर क्षत्रियके लिये दूसरा कोई कल्याणकारक कर्म नहीं है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.31।। और स्वधर्म को भी देखकर तुमको विचलित होना उचित नहीं है,  क्योंकि धर्मयुक्त युद्ध से बढ़कर दूसरा कोई कल्याणकारक कर्त्तव्य क्षत्रिय के लिये नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Further, considering also your own duty, it does not befit you to waver. For, to a Kshatriya, there is no greater good than a righteous war.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even considering your own duty, you should not waver, since there is nothing better for a Ksatriya than a righteous battle.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Further, having regard to your duty, you should not waver, for there is nothing higher for a Kshatriya than a righteous war.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Further, considering your own duty, you should not waver. Indeed, for a Kshatriya there is no duty superior to fighting a righteous war.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: You must look at your duty. Nothing can be more welcome to a soldier than a righteous war. Therefore, wavering in this resolve is unworthy, O Arjuna!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.32।। अपने-आप प्राप्त हुआ युद्ध खुला हुआ स्वर्गका दरवाजा भी है। हे पृथानन्दन ! वे क्षत्रिय बड़े सुखी (भाग्यशाली) हैं, जिनको ऐसा युद्ध प्राप्त होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.32।। और हे पार्थ ! अपने आप प्राप्त हुए और स्वर्ग के लिए खुले हुए द्वाररूप इस प्रकार के युद्ध को भाग्यवान क्षत्रिय लोग ही पाते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Happy are the Ksatriyas, O Arjuna, to whom such a war comes of its own accord; it opens the gates to heaven.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Partha, happy are the Kshatriyas who come across such a battle, which presents itself unsought and is an open gate to heaven.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Happy are the Kshatriyas, O Arjuna! who are called to fight in such a battle that comes of its own accord as an open door to heaven.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Prtha! By good fortune, Ksatriyas, desirous of happiness, get a war of this type, which has come of its own accord and which is an open door to heaven.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Blessed are the soldiers who find their opportunity; this opportunity has opened for thee the gates of heaven.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.33।। अब अगर तू यह धर्ममय युद्ध नहीं करेगा,  तो अपने धर्म और कीर्तिका त्याग करके पापको प्राप्त होगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.33।। और यदि तुम इस धर्मयुद्ध को स्वीकार नहीं करोगे,  तो स्वधर्म और कीर्ति को खोकर पाप को प्राप्त करोगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But if you do not fight this righteous war, you will be turning away from your duty and honor, and will incur sin.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: On the other hand, if you do not fight this righteous battle, then you will incur sin, forsaking your own duty and fame.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But if you will not fight this righteous war, then having abandoned your own duty and reputation, you will incur sin.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: On the other hand, if you do not fight this righteous war, then you will incur sin by shirking your own duty and forfeiting your fame.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Refuse to fight in this righteous cause, and you will be a traitor, losing fame, incurring only sin.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.34।। और सब प्राणी भी तेरी सदा रहनेवाली अपकीर्तिका कथन अर्थात निंदा करेंगे। वह अपकीर्ति सम्मानित मनुष्यके लिये मृत्युसे भी बढ़कर दुःखदायी होती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.34।। और सब लोग तुम्हारी बहुत काल तक रहने वाली अपकीर्ति को भी कहते रहेंगे;  और सम्मानित पुरुष के लिए अपकीर्ति मरण से भी अधिक होती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Further, people will speak ill of you for all time, and for one accustomed to being honored, dishonor is worse than death.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: People also will speak of your unending infamy, and to an honored person, infamy is worse than death.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: People will also recount your everlasting dishonor; and for one who has been honored, dishonor is worse than death.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The creatures will speak of your endless ill-fame; and for one who has been highly esteemed, ill-fame is worse than death.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Men will talk forever of thy disgrace; and to the noble, dishonor is worse than death.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.35।।  महारथीलोग तुझे भयके कारण युद्धसे उपरत (हटा) हुआ मानेंगे। जिनकी धारणामें तू बहुमान्य हो चुका है, उनकी दृष्टिमें तू लघुताको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.35।। और जिनके लिए तुम बहुत माननीय हो उनके लिए अब तुम तुच्छता को प्राप्त होओगे,  वे महारथी लोग तुम्हें भय के कारण युद्ध से निवृत्त हुआ मानेंगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The great warriors will think that you have fled from battle out of fear. Those who held you in high esteem will now speak of you disparagingly.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The great chariot-riders will think of you as having desisted from the fight out of fear, and you will fall into disgrace before them whom you had been estimable to.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The great chariot-warriors will think that you have withdrawn from the battle out of fear, and you will be held in low esteem by those who have held you in high regard.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The mighty charioteers will think of you as having withdrawn from the battle out of fear; having been highly esteemed by these men, you will be viewed lightly.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Great generals will think that you have fled from the battlefield out of cowardice; though once honored, you will seem despicable.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.36।। तेरे शत्रुलोग तेरी सार्मथ्यकी निन्दा करते हुए न कहनेयोग्य बहुत-से वचन भी कहेंगे। उससे बढ़कर और दुःखकी बात क्या होगी?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.36।। तुम्हारे शत्रु तुम्हारे सार्मथ्य की निन्दा करते हुए बहुत से अकथनीय वचनों को कहेंगे,  फिर उससे अधिक दु:ख क्या होगा ?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Your enemies, slandering your prowess, will use words that should never be uttered. What could be more painful than that?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And your enemies will speak many indecent words while denigrating your power. What can be more painful than that?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Your enemies, scoffing at your power, will speak many abusive words—what could be more painful than this?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Your enemies will slander your abilities and speak of you in many sayings that should not be spoken. Is there anything more painful than that?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Your enemies will spread scandal and mock your courage. Is there anything more humiliating?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.37।। अगर युद्धमें तू मारा जायगा तो तुझे स्वर्गकी प्राप्ति होगी और अगर युद्धमें तू जीत जायगा तो पृथ्वीका राज्य भोगेगा। अतः हे कुन्तीनन्दन! तू युद्धके लिये निश्चय करके खड़ा हो जा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.37।। युद्ध में मरकर तुम स्वर्ग प्राप्त करोगे या जीतकर पृथ्वी को भोगोगे;  इसलिय, हे कौन्तेय ! युद्ध का निश्चय कर तुम खड़े हो जाओ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: If slain, you will win heaven; or if victorious, you will enjoy the earth. Therefore, arise, O Arjuna, resolved to fight.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Either by being killed, you will attain heaven, or by winning, you will enjoy the earth. Therefore, O Arjuna, rise up with determination to fight.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Slain, you will obtain heaven; victorious, you will enjoy the earth; therefore, stand up, O son of Kunti, resolved to fight.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: If you are slain, you shall attain heaven; or if you conquer, you shall enjoy the earth. Therefore, O son of Kunti, stand up with resolution made in favor of fighting the battle.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: If you are killed, you will attain Heaven; if you are victorious, you will enjoy the kingdom of earth. Therefore, arise, O Son of Kunti, and fight!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.38।। जय-पराजय, लाभ-हानि और सुख-दुःखको समान करके फिर युद्धमें लग जा। इस प्रकार युद्ध करनेसे तू पापको प्राप्त नहीं होगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.38।। सुख-दु:ख,  लाभ-हानि और जय-पराजय को समान करके युद्ध के लिये तैयार हो जाओ;  इस प्रकार तुमको पाप नहीं होगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Holding pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat as equal, gird yourself up for the battle. Thus, you shall not incur any sin.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Treating happiness and sorrow, gain and loss, and conquest and defeat with equanimity, then engage in battle. Thus, you will not incur sin.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having made pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat equal, engage in battle for the sake of battle; thus, you shall not incur sin.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Viewing pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat alike, you should get yourself ready for the battle. Thus, you will not incur sin.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Look upon pleasure and pain, victory and defeat, with an equal eye. Be prepared for the combat, and you will commit no sin.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.39।। हे पार्थ! यह समबुद्धि तेरे लिए पहले सांख्ययोगमें कही गयी, अब तू इसको कर्मयोगके विषयमें सुन; जिस समबुद्धिसे युक्त हुआ तू कर्मबन्धनका त्याग कर देगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.39।। हे पार्थ ! तुम्हें सांख्य विषयक ज्ञान कहा गया और अब इस (कर्म) योग से सम्बन्धित ज्ञान को सुनो जिस ज्ञान से युक्त होकर तुम कर्मबन्ध का नाश कर सकोगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: This Buddhi concerning the Self (Sankhya) has been imparted to you. Now listen to this with regard to Yoga, by following which you will get rid of the bondage of Karma.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, this wisdom has been imparted to you from the standpoint of Self-realization. But listen to this wisdom from the standpoint of Yoga, endowed with which, you will get rid of the bondage of action.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: This, which has been taught to you, is wisdom concerning Sankhya. Now listen to wisdom concerning Yoga, endowed with which, O Arjuna, you shall cast off the bonds of action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Listen, how this knowledge, imparted to you for your Sankhya, is also for the Yoga; endowed with this knowledge, you shall cast off the bondage of action, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I have told you the philosophy of knowledge. Now, listen, and I will explain the philosophy of action, by means of which, O Arjuna, you will break through the bondage of all action.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.40।। मनुष्यलोकमें इस समबुद्धिरूप धर्मके आरम्भका नाश नहीं होता, इसके अनुष्ठानका उलटा फल भी नहीं होता और इसका थोड़ासा भी अनुष्ठान (जन्म-मरणरूप) महान् भयसे रक्षा कर लेता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.40।। इसमें क्रमनाश और प्रत्यवाय दोष नहीं है। इस धर्म (योग) का अल्प अभ्यास भी महान् भय से रक्षण करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Here, there is no loss of effort, nor any accrual of evil. Even a little of this Dharma (called Karma Yoga) protects one from the great fear.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Here, there is no waste of effort; nor is there any harm. Even a little of this righteousness saves one from great fear.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: In this, there is no loss of effort, nor is there any harm produced, nor any transgression. Even a little of this knowledge protects one from great fear.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Here, there is no loss due to transgression, and there is no contrary downward course (sin); even a little of this righteous thing can save one from great danger.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: On this path, endeavour is never wasted nor repressed. Even a little practice of it protects one from great danger.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.41।। हे कुरुनन्दन! इस समबुद्धिकी प्राप्तिके विषयमें व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि एक ही होती है। अव्यवसायी मनुष्योंकी बुद्धियाँ अनन्त और बहुशाखाओंवाली ही होती हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.41।। हे कुरुनन्दन ! इस (विषय) में निश्चयात्मक बुद्धि एक ही है, अज्ञानी पुरुषों की बुद्धियां (संकल्प) बहुत भेदों वाली और अनन्त होती हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: In this (Karma Yoga), O Arjuna, the resolute mind is single-pointed; the minds of the irresolute are multi-branched and endless.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scion of the Kuru dynasty, in this there is a single, one-pointed conviction. The thoughts of the irresolute ones, however, have many branches indeed and are innumerable.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Here, O joy of the Kurus, there is only one single-pointed determination; many-branched and endless are the thoughts of the indecisive.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O source of joy to the Kurus! The knowledge in the form of determination is one; however, the knowledge (thoughts) of those who lack determination has many branches and has no end.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: By its means, the straying intellect becomes steadied in contemplating one object only; whereas the minds of the irresolute stray into innumerable bypaths.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.42 -- 2.43।। हे पृथानन्दन ! जो कामनाओंमें तन्मय हो रहे हैं, स्वर्गको ही श्रेष्ठ माननेवाले हैं, वेदोंमें कहे हुए सकाम कर्मोंमें प्रीति रखनेवाले हैं, भोगोंके सिवाय और कुछ है ही नहीं - ऐसा कहनेवाले हैं, वे अविवेकी मनुष्य इस प्रकारकी जिस पुष्पित (दिखाऊ शोभायुक्त) वाणीको कहा करते हैं, जो कि जन्मरूपी कर्मफलको देनेवाली है तथा भोग और ऐश्वर्यकी प्राप्तिके लिये बहुतसी क्रियाओंका वर्णन करनेवाली है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.42।। हे पार्थ  अविवेकी पुरुष वेदवाद में रमते हुये जो यह पुष्पिता (दिखावटी शोभा की) वाणी बोलते हैं? इससे (स्वर्ग से) बढ़कर और कुछ नहीं है।।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: - O Partha, the unwise, who rejoice in the letter of the Vedas, say, "There is nothing else." They are full only of worldly desires and they hanker for heaven. They speak flowery words that offer mirth as the fruit of work. They look upon the Vedas as consisting entirely of varied rites for the attainment of pleasure and power. Those who cling so to pleasure and power are attracted by that speech (offering heavenly rewards) and are unable to develop the resolute will of a concentrated mind.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Prtha, those undiscerning people who utter this flowery talk, which promises birth as a result of rites and duties, and is full of various special rites meant for the attainment of enjoyment and affluence, remain engrossed in the utterances of the Vedas and declare that nothing else exists; their minds are full of desires and they have heaven as their goal.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The unwise, taking pleasure in the eulogizing words of the Vedas, utter flowery speech, saying, "There is nothing else," O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: -. O son of Prtha! Those whose very nature is desire, whose goal is heaven, who esteem only the Vedic declaration [of fruits], who declare that there is nothing else, who proclaim this flowery speech about the paths to the lordship of the objects of enjoyment—paths that are full of different actions—and who desire action alone as a fruit of their birth—they are men without insight.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Only the ignorant speak in figurative language; it is they who extol the letter of the scriptures, saying, "There is nothing deeper than this."

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.42 -- 2.43।। हे पृथानन्दन ! जो कामनाओंमें तन्मय हो रहे हैं, स्वर्गको ही श्रेष्ठ माननेवाले हैं, वेदोंमें कहे हुए सकाम कर्मोंमें प्रीति रखनेवाले हैं, भोगोंके सिवाय और कुछ है ही नहीं - ऐसा कहनेवाले हैं, वे अविवेकी मनुष्य इस प्रकारकी जिस पुष्पित (दिखाऊ शोभायुक्त) वाणीको कहा करते हैं, जो कि जन्मरूपी कर्मफलको देनेवाली है तथा भोग और ऐश्वर्यकी प्राप्तिके लिये बहुतसी क्रियाओंका वर्णन करनेवाली है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.43।। कामनाओं से युक्त? स्वर्ग को ही श्रेष्ठ मानने वाले लोग भोग और ऐश्वर्य को प्राप्त कराने वाली अनेक क्रियाओं को बताते हैं जो (वास्तव में) जन्मरूप कर्मफल को देने वाली होती हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: - O Partha, the unwise, who rejoice in the letter of the Vedas, say, "There is nothing else." They are full only of worldly desires and they yearn for heaven. They speak flowery words that offer mirth as the fruit of work. They view the Vedas as consisting entirely of varied rites for the attainment of pleasure and power. Those who cling so to pleasure and power are attracted by that speech (offering heavenly rewards) and are unable to develop the resolute will of a focused mind.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Prtha, those undiscerning people who utter this flowery talk, which promises birth as a result of rites and duties, and is full of various special rites meant for the attainment of enjoyment and affluence, remain engrossed in the utterances of the Vedas and declare that nothing else exists; their minds are full of desires and they have heaven as their goal.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Full of desires, with heaven as their goal, (they speak words that are directed to ends) leading to new births as the result of their works, and prescribe various methods abounding in specific actions, for the attainment of pleasure and power.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: -. O son of Prtha! Those whose very nature is desire, whose goal is heaven, who esteem only the Vedic declaration [of fruits], who declare that there is nothing else, who proclaim this flowery speech about the paths to the lordship of the objects of enjoyment—paths that are full of different actions—and who desire action alone as a fruit of their birth—they are men without insight.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Consulting only their own desires, they construct their own heaven, devising arduous and complex rites to secure their own pleasure and power; and the only result is rebirth.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.44।। उस पुष्पित वाणीसे जिसका अन्तःकरण हर लिया गया है अर्थात् भोगोंकी तरफ खिंच गया है और जो भोग तथा ऐश्वर्यमें अत्यन्त आसक्त हैं, उन मनुष्योंकी परमात्मामें निश्चयात्मिका बुद्धि नहीं होती।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.44।।उससे जिनका चित्त हर लिया गया है ऐसे भोग और एश्र्वर्य‌ मॆ आसक्ति रखने वाले पुरुषों के अन्तकरण मे निश्चयात्मक् बुद्धि नही हॊती अर्थात वे ध्यान का अभ्यास करने योग्य‌ नही होते।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: - O Partha, the unwise, who rejoice in the letter of the Vedas, say, "There is nothing else." They are full only of worldly desires and they hanker for heaven. They speak flowery words that offer mirth as the fruit of work. They look upon the Vedas as consisting entirely of varied rites for the attainment of pleasure and power. Those who cling so to pleasure and power are attracted by that speech (offering heavenly rewards) and are unable to develop the resolute will of a concentrated mind.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One-pointed conviction does not become established in the minds of those who delight in enjoyment and affluence, and whose intellects are carried away by it.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For those who are attached to pleasure and power, whose minds are drawn away by such teachings, their determinate reason is not formed which is steadily bent on meditation and Samadhi (superconscious state).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those who are very attached to the ownership of enjoyable objects and whose minds have been carried away by that (flowery speech) - their knowledge, in the form of determination, is not prescribed for concentration.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: While their minds are absorbed with thoughts of power and personal enjoyment, they cannot focus their discrimination on one point.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.45।। वेद तीनों गुणोंके कार्यका ही वर्णन करनेवाले हैं; हे अर्जुन! तू तीनों गुणोंसे रहित हो जा, निर्द्वन्द्व हो जा, निरन्तर नित्य वस्तु परमात्मा में स्थित हो जा, योगक्षेमकी चाहना भी मत रख और परमात्मपरायण हो जा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.45।। हे अर्जुन  वेदों का विषय तीन गुणों से सम्बन्धित (संसार से) है  तुम त्रिगुणातीत? निर्द्वन्द्व? नित्य सत्त्व (शुद्धता) में स्थित? योगक्षेम से रहित और आत्मवान् बनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Vedas have the three Gunas as their sphere, O Arjuna. You must be free from the three Gunas and be free from the pairs of opposites. Abide in pure Sattva; never care to acquire things and to protect what has been acquired, but be established in the Self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, the Vedas [Meaning only the portion dealing with rites and duties (karma-kanda).] have the three qualities as their object. You become free from worldliness, free from the pairs of duality, ever-poised in the quality of sattva, without (desire for) acquisition and protection, and self-collected.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Vedas deal with the three attributes; be thou above these three attributes. O Arjuna, free yourself from the pairs of opposites and ever remain in the quality of Sattva, freed from acquisition and preservation, and be established in the Self.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Vedas bind through the three strands. Therefore, O Arjuna, be free from the three strands, free from the pairs of opposites; be established in this eternal Being; be free from acquisition and preservation; and possess the Self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The Vedic Scriptures tell of the three constituents of life—the Qualities. Rise above all of them, O Arjuna, above all the pairs of opposing sensations; be steadfast in truth, free from worldly anxieties, and centered in the Self.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.46।। सब तरफसे परिपूर्ण महान् जलाशयके प्राप्त होनेपर छोटे गड्ढों में भरे जल में मनुष्यका जितना प्रयोजन रहता है अर्थात् कुछ भी प्रयोजन नहीं रहता, वेदों और शास्त्रोंको तत्त्वसे जाननेवाले ब्रह्मज्ञानीका सम्पूर्ण वेदोंमें उतना ही प्रयोजन रहता है अर्थात् कुछ भी प्रयोजन नहीं रहता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.46।। सब ओर से परिपूर्ण जलराशि के होने पर मनुष्य का छोटे जलाशय में जितना प्रयोजन रहता है? आत्मज्ञानी ब्राह्मण का सभी वेदों में उतना ही प्रयोजन रहता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: What use does a thirsty person have for a water reservoir when all sides of it are flooded—that is the only use of all the Vedas for a Brahmana who knows.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A Brahmana with realization has that much utility in all the Vedas as a person has in a well when there is a flood all around.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: To the Brahmana who has known the Self, all the Vedas are of as much use as a reservoir of water would be in a place where there is a flood.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: What portion of a reservoir, flooded with water everywhere, is useful for a man in thirst? That much portion alone in all the Vedas is useful for an intelligent student of the Vedas.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As a man can drink water from any side of a full tank, so the skilled theologian can extract from any scripture that which will serve their purpose.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.47।। कर्तव्य-कर्म करनेमें ही तेरा अधिकार है, फलोंमें कभी नहीं। अतः तू कर्मफलका हेतु भी मत बन और तेरी अकर्मण्यतामें भी आसक्ति न हो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.47।। कर्म करने मात्र में तुम्हारा अधिकार है? फल में कभी नहीं। तुम कर्मफल के हेतु वाले मत होना और अकर्म में भी तुम्हारी आसक्ति न हो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: You have the right to work alone, but not to the fruits of it. Do not be driven by the results of your work, nor have attachment to inaction.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Your right is only to act, not to the results. Do not be the cause of the results of action. Do not have any inclination towards inaction.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Your right is only to work, but not to its results; do not let the results of action be your motive, nor let your attachment be to inaction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Let your claim rest on action alone and never on the fruits; you should never be the cause of the fruits of action; let not your attachment be to inaction.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But you have only the right to work, but none to the fruit of it. Let not the fruit of your action be your motive; nor be you enamored of inaction.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.48।। हे धनञ्जय ! तू आसक्तिका त्याग करके सिद्धि-असिद्धिमें सम होकर योगमें स्थित हुआ कर्मोंको कर; क्योंकि समत्व ही योग कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.48।। हे धनंजय  आसक्ति को त्याग कर तथा सिद्धि और असिद्धि में समभाव होकर योग में स्थित हुये तुम कर्म करो। यह समभाव ही योग कहलाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Abandoning attachment and established in Yoga, perform works, viewing success and failure with an even mind. Evenness of mind is said to be Yoga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: By being established in Yoga, O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), undertake actions, casting off attachment and remaining equanimous in success and failure. Equanimity is called Yoga.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Perform action, O Arjuna, being steadfast in Yoga, abandoning attachment and balanced in success and failure; evenness of mind is called Yoga.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Dhananjaya! Establish yourself in the Yoga, perform actions while abandoning attachment, and remain even-minded in success and failure; for, even-mindedness is said to be the Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text:
Revised:
Perform all your actions with your mind concentrated on the Divine, renouncing attachment and looking upon success and failure with an equal eye. Spirituality implies equanimity.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.49।। बुद्धियोग-(समता) की अपेक्षा सकामकर्म दूरसे (अत्यन्त) ही निकृष्ट है। अतः हे धनञ्जय ! तू बुद्धि (समता) का आश्रय ले; क्योंकि फलके हेतु बननेवाले अत्यन्त दीन हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.49।। इस बुद्धियोग की तुलना में(सकाम) कर्म अत्यन्त निकृष्ट हैं? इसलिये हे धनंजय  तुम बद्धि की शरण लो फल की इच्छा करनेवाले कृपण (दीन) हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Action done with attachment, O Arjuna, is far inferior to action done with evenness of mind. Seek refuge in evenness of mind; those who act with a motive for results are miserable.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Dhananjaya, indeed, action is inferior to the yoga of wisdom. Take refuge in wisdom. Those who thirst for rewards are pitiable.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Far lower than the Yoga of wisdom is action, O Arjuna. Seek thou refuge in wisdom; wretched are those whose motive is the fruit.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Dhananjaya! The inferior action stays away at a distance due to the yoga of one's contact with the determining faculty; in the determining faculty, you must seek refuge; wretched are those who are the cause of the fruits of action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Physical action is far inferior to an intellect concentrated on the Divine. Therefore, have recourse to Pure Intelligence. It is only the small-minded who work for reward.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.50।। बुद्धि-(समता) से युक्त मनुष्य यहाँ जीवित अवस्थामें ही पुण्य और पाप दोनोंका त्याग कर देता है। अतः तू योग-(समता-) में लग जा, क्योंकि योग ही कर्मोंमें कुशलता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.50।। समत्वबुद्धि युक्त पुरुष यहां (इस जीवन में) पुण्य और पाप इन दोनों कर्मों को त्याग देता है? इसलिये तुम योग से युक्त हो जाओ। कर्मों में कुशलता योग है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: A person with an evenness of mind discards both good and evil here and now. Therefore, strive for Yoga; for Yoga is skill in action.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Possessed of wisdom, one rejects here both virtue and vice. Therefore, devote yourself to (Karma-) yoga; for yoga is skilfulness in action.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Endowed with wisdom and evenness of mind, one casts off in this life both good and evil deeds; therefore, devote yourself to Yoga; Yoga is skill in action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever is endowed with the faculty of determination casts off both good and bad actions. Therefore, strive for Yoga, which is proficiency in action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When a person attains Pure Reason, they renounce in this world the results of both good and evil. Cling to Right Action. Spirituality is the true art of living.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.51।। समतायुक्त मनीषी साधक कर्मजन्य फलका त्याग करके जन्मरूप बन्धनसे मुक्त होकर निर्विकार पदको प्राप्त हो जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.51।। बुद्धियोग युक्त मनीषी लोग कर्मजन्य फलों को त्यागकर जन्मरूप बन्धन से मुक्त हुये अनामय अर्थात् निर्दोष पद को प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The wise, who possess evenness of mind, relinquish the fruits born of action, and are freed from the bondage of birth, going to the region beyond all ills.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For those devoted to wisdom, they become enlightened men by giving up the fruits of their actions, and thus they reach a state beyond evil, having been freed from the bondage of birth.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The wise, possessing knowledge, having abandoned the fruits of their actions, and being freed from the bonds of birth, go to the place which is beyond all evil.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: By renouncing the fruits of their actions, the intelligent ones, endowed with the power of determination and freed from the bonds of birth, go to a place that is free from illness.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sages, guided by pure intellect, renounce the fruits of their actions; and, thus freed from the shackles of rebirth, they attain the highest bliss.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.52।। जिस समय तेरी बुद्धि मोहरूपी दलदलको तर जायगी, उसी समय तू सुने हुए और सुननेमें आनेवाले भोगोंसे वैराग्यको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.52।। जब तुम्हारी बुद्धि मोहरूप दलदल (कलिल) को तर जायेगी तब तुम उन सब वस्तुओं से निर्वेद (वैराग्य) को प्राप्त हो जाओगे? जो सुनने योग्य और सुनी हुई हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: When your intellect has passed beyond the tangle of delusion, you will yourself feel disgusted with what you have heard and what you will hear.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When your mind goes beyond the turbidity of delusion, then you will acquire dispassion for what has to be heard and what has already been heard.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When your intellect passes beyond the mire of delusion, then you will attain indifference to what has been heard and what has yet to be heard.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When your determining faculty surpasses the impenetrable thicket of delusion, then you will attain an attitude of futility regarding what needs to be heard and what has been heard.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When your reason has crossed the entanglements of illusion, then you will become indifferent to both the philosophies you have heard and those you may yet hear.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.53।। जिस कालमें शास्त्रीय मतभेदोंसे विचलित हुई तेरी बुद्धि निश्चल हो जायगी और परमात्मामें अचल हो जायगी, उस कालमें तू योगको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.53।। जब अनेक प्रकार के विषयों को सुनने से विचलित हुई तुम्हारी बुद्धि आत्मस्वरूप में अचल और स्थिर हो जायेगी तब तुम (परमार्थ) योग को प्राप्त करोगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: When your intellect, having been well enlightened by hearing from Me and firmly placed, stands unshaken in a concentrated mind, then you will attain the vision of the Self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When your mind, which has become bewildered by hearing, becomes unshakable and steadfast in the Self, then you will attain Yoga that arises from discrimination.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When your intellect, which is perplexed by the Vedic texts you have read, stands immovable and steady in the Self, then you will attain Self-realization.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When your determining faculty, which had been previously confused by your hearing of scriptural declarations of fruits, stands stable in concentration, then you shall attain Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When the intellect, bewildered by the multiplicity of holy scriptures, stands unperturbed in blissful contemplation of the infinite, then you have attained spirituality.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.54।। अर्जुन बोले - हे केशव ! परमात्मामें स्थित स्थिर बुद्धिवाले मनुष्यके क्या लक्षण होते हैं? वह स्थिर बुद्धिवाला मनुष्य कैसे बोलता है, कैसे बैठता है और कैसे चलता है?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.54।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- हे केशव  समाधि में स्थित स्थिर बुद्धि वाले पुरुष का क्या लक्षण है स्थिर बुद्धि पुरुष कैसे बोलता है कैसे बैठता है  कैसे चलता है

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "What is the mode of speech of one who is of firm wisdom, established in the control of the mind? What does a man of firm wisdom speak, O Krsna? How does he sit? How does he move?"

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Kesava, what is the description of a man of steady wisdom who is self-absorbed? How does he speak, sit, and move about?"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krishna, what is the description of one who has steady wisdom and is merged in the superconscious state? How does one of steady wisdom speak, how do they sit, and how do they walk?"

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "O Kesava! What is the meaning of sthita-prajna (a man-of-stabilized-intellect) when applied to a man fixed in concentration? What does sthira-dhih (the fixed-minded) imply? Where does the fixed-minded abide? And what is their goal?"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna asked: My Lord! How can we recognize the saint who has attained Pure Intellect, who has reached this state of Bliss, and whose mind is steady? How does he talk, how does he live, and how does he act?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.55।। श्रीभगवान् बोले - हे पृथानन्दन ! जिस कालमें साधक मनोगत सम्पूर्ण कामनाओंका अच्छी तरह त्याग कर देता है और अपने-आपसे अपने-आपमें ही सन्तुष्ट रहता है, उस कालमें वह स्थितप्रज्ञ कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.55।। श्री भगवान् ने कहा -- हे पार्थ? जिस समय पुरुष मन में स्थित सब कामनाओं को त्याग देता है और आत्मा से ही आत्मा में सन्तुष्ट रहता है? उस समय वह स्थितप्रज्ञ कहलाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "When a man renounces all the desires of the mind and is content with himself, then he is said to possess firm wisdom."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed One said, "O Partha, when one fully renounces all the desires that have entered the mind and remains satisfied in the Self alone, then he is called a man of steady wisdom."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "When a man completely casts off, O Arjuna, all the desires of the mind and is satisfied in the Self by the Self, then he is said to be one of steady wisdom."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "O son of Prtha! When a man casts off all desires existing in his mind and remains content in the Self by the self (mind), then he is called a man of stabilized intellect."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: When a person has given up the desires of their heart and is content with the Self alone, they have surely reached the highest state.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.56।। दुःखोंकी प्राप्ति होनेपर जिसके मनमें उद्वेग नहीं होता और सुखोंकी प्राप्ति होनेपर जिसके मनमें स्पृहा नहीं होती तथा जो राग, भय और क्रोधसे सर्वथा रहित हो गया है, वह मननशील मनुष्य स्थिरबुद्धि कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.56।। दुख में जिसका मन उद्विग्न नहीं होता सुख में जिसकी स्पृहा निवृत्त हो गयी है? जिसके मन से राग? भय और क्रोध नष्ट हो गये हैं? वह मुनि स्थितप्रज्ञ कहलाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He whose mind is not perturbed by pain, who has no longing for pleasures, who is free from desire, fear, and anger—he is called a sage of firm wisdom.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That monk is called a man of steady wisdom when his mind is unperturbed even in sorrow, he is free from longing for delights, and has gone beyond attachment, fear, and anger.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He whose mind is not shaken by adversity, who does not long for pleasures, and is free from attachment, fear, and anger, is called a sage of steady wisdom.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He whose mind is undisturbed amidst sorrows, who is free from desire amidst pleasures, and from whom longing, fear, and wrath have departed—he is said to be a firm-minded sage.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sage whose mind is unruffled in suffering, whose desire is not aroused by enjoyment, who is without attachment, anger, or fear—take him to be one who stands at a lofty level.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.57।। सब जगह आसक्तिरहित हुआ जो मनुष्य उस-उस शुभ-अशुभको प्राप्त करके न तो अभिनन्दित होता है और न द्वेष करता है, उसकी बुद्धि प्रतिष्ठित है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.57।। जो सर्वत्र अति स्नेह से रहित हुआ उन शुभ तथा अशुभ वस्तुओं को प्राप्त कर न प्रसन्न होता है और न द्वेष करता है? उसकी प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठित (स्थिर) है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who has no love on any side, who, upon finding good or evil, neither rejoices nor hates—his wisdom is firmly set.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The wisdom of that person remains established who has no attachment to anything anywhere, who neither welcomes nor rejects anything, whatever good or bad, when they come across it.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who is everywhere without attachment, upon encountering anything good or bad, neither rejoices nor hastens; his wisdom is firm.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who has no desire for anything, and who neither rejoices nor hates upon obtaining this or that, good or bad—his intellect is properly stabilized.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who, wherever he goes, is not attached to any person or place by ties of flesh; who accepts good and evil alike, neither welcoming the one nor shrinking from the other—take him to be one who is merged in the Infinite.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.58।। जिस तरह कछुआ अपने अङ्गोंको सब ओरसे समेट लेता है, ऐसे ही जिस कालमें यह कर्मयोगी इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंसे इन्द्रियोंको सब प्रकारसे समेट लेता (हटा लेता) है, तब उसकी बुद्धि प्रतिष्ठित हो जाती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.58।। कछुवा अपने अंगों को जैसे समेट लेता है वैसे ही यह पुरुष जब सब ओर से अपनी इन्द्रियों को इन्द्रियों के विषयों से परावृत्त कर लेता है? तब उसकी बुद्धि स्थिर होती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: When one is able to draw their senses away from the objects of sense on all sides, like a tortoise draws in its limbs, then their wisdom is firmly established.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And when one fully withdraws their senses from the objects of the senses, just as a tortoise withdraws its limbs, then their wisdom remains established.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When, like the tortoise which withdraws all its limbs on all sides, he withdraws his senses from the sense-objects, then his wisdom becomes steady.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When he withdraws all his senses from sense-objects, just as a tortoise withdraws all its limbs, then he is declared to be a man of stabilized intellect.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who can withdraw his senses from the attraction of their objects, as the tortoise draws its limbs within its shell—take it that such a one has attained perfection.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.59।। निराहारी (इन्द्रियोंको विषयोंसे हटानेवाले) मनुष्यके भी विषय तो निवृत्त हो जाते हैं, पर रस निवृत्त नहीं होता। परन्तु इस स्थितप्रज्ञ मनुष्यका तो रस भी परमात्मतत्त्वका अनुभव होनेसे निवृत्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.59।। निराहारी देही पुरुष से विषय तो निवृत्त (दूर) हो जाते हैं? परन्तु (उनके प्रति) राग नहीं  परम तत्व को देखने पर इस (पुरुष) का राग भी निवृत्त हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The objects of the senses, except for the pleasure they bring, turn away from the abstinent dweller in the body. Even the pleasure turns away from him when the Self, which is supreme over the senses, is seen.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The objects recede from an abstinent person, except for the taste for them. Even this taste falls away after realization of the Absolute.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The objects of the senses turn away from the abstinent man, leaving the longing behind; but his longing also turns away upon seeing the Supreme.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Leaving their taste behind, the sense-objects retreat from the embodied one who abstains from food; his taste too disappears when he beholds the Supreme.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The objects of sense turn away from him who is abstinent. Even the taste for them is lost in him who has seen the Truth.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.60।। हे कुन्तीनन्दन! (रसबुद्धि रहनेसे) यत्न करते हुए विद्वान् मनुष्यकी भी प्रमथनशील इन्द्रियाँ उसके मनको बलपूर्वक हर लेती हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.60।। हे कौन्तेय  (संयम का) प्रयत्न करते हुए बुद्धिमान (विपश्चित) पुरुष के भी मन को ये इन्द्रियां बलपूर्वक हर लेती हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The turbulent senses, O Arjuna, can forcibly carry away the mind of even a wise man, though he is ever striving.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For, O son of Kunti, the turbulent senses can violently snatch away the mind of an intelligent person, even while they are striving diligently.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The turbulent senses, O Arjuna, can violently carry away the mind of a wise person, even though they are striving to control them.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For, the turbulent sense-organs can forcibly carry away even the mind of this discerning person, O son of Kunti!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! The mind of one who is attempting to conquer it is forcibly carried away, in spite of their efforts, by their tumultuous senses.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.61।। कर्मयोगी साधक उन सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियोंको वशमें करके मेरे परायण होकर बैठे; क्योंकि जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ वशमें हैं, उसकी बुद्धि प्रतिष्ठित है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.61।। उन सब इन्द्रियों को संयमित कर युक्त और मत्पर होवे। जिस पुरुष की इन्द्रियां वश में होती हैं? उसकी प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठित होती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Having controlled all the senses, let him remain in contemplation, regarding Me as supreme; for his knowledge is firmly established when his senses are under control.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Controlling all of them, one should remain focused on Me as the supreme. For, the wisdom of one whose senses are under control becomes steadfast.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having restrained them all, he should sit steadfast, intent on Me; his wisdom is steady whose senses are under control.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Restraining the same organs by the mind, the master of Yoga would sit, making Me their goal; for the intellect of that person is stabilized whose sense-organs are under control.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Restraining them all, let him steadfastly meditate on Me; for whoever conquers their senses achieves perfection.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.62 -- 2.63।। विषयोंका चिन्तन करनेवाले मनुष्यकी उन विषयोंमें आसक्ति पैदा हो जाती है। आसक्तिसे कामना पैदा होती है। कामनासे क्रोध पैदा होता है। क्रोध होनेपर सम्मोह (मूढ़भाव) हो जाता है। सम्मोहसे स्मृति भ्रष्ट हो जाती है। स्मृति भ्रष्ट होनेपर बुद्धिका नाश हो जाता है। बुद्धिका नाश होनेपर मनुष्यका पतन हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.62।। विषयों का चिन्तन करने वाले पुरुष की उसमें आसक्ति हो जाती है? आसक्ति से इच्छा और इच्छा से क्रोध उत्पन्न होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: To one who is thinking about sense-objects, attachment to them arises; from attachment arises desire, and from desire arises anger.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: - In the case of a person who dwells on objects, there arises attachment to them. From attachment grows hankering, and from hankering springs anger.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When one thinks of objects, attachment to them arises; from attachment, desire is born; from desire, anger arises.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: In a person meditating on sense-objects, attachment to them is born in succession; from attachment springs passion; from passion arises wrath.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When a person dwells on the objects of sense, they create an attraction for them; this attraction develops into desire, and desire breeds anger.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.62 -- 2.63।। विषयोंका चिन्तन करनेवाले मनुष्यकी उन विषयोंमें आसक्ति पैदा हो जाती है। आसक्तिसे कामना पैदा होती है। कामनासे क्रोध पैदा होता है। क्रोध होनेपर सम्मोह (मूढ़भाव) हो जाता है। सम्मोहसे स्मृति भ्रष्ट हो जाती है। स्मृति भ्रष्ट होनेपर बुद्धिका नाश हो जाता है। बुद्धिका नाश होनेपर मनुष्यका पतन हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.63।। क्रोध से उत्पन्न होता है मोह और मोह से स्मृति विभ्रम। स्मृति के भ्रमित होने पर बुद्धि का नाश होता है और बुद्धि के नाश होने से वह मनुष्य नष्ट हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Anger leads to delusion, which causes the loss of memory; the loss of memory leads to the destruction of discrimination, and with the destruction of discrimination, one is lost.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: From anger follows delusion; from delusion, a failure of memory; from the failure of memory, the loss of understanding; and from the loss of understanding, one perishes.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Anger leads to delusion, which causes loss of memory; this, in turn, leads to the destruction of discrimination, resulting in destruction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: From wrath comes delusion; from delusion, the loss of memory; from the loss of memory, the loss of capacity to decide; due to the loss of capacity to decide, he perishes outright.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Anger induces delusion; delusion leads to loss of memory; loss of memory results in the shattering of reason; and the destruction of reason leads to destruction.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.64 -- 2.65।। वशीभूत अन्तःकरणवाला कर्मयोगी साधक रागद्वेषसे रहित अपने वशमें की हुई इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा विषयोंका सेवन करता हुआ अन्तःकरणकी निर्मलता को प्राप्त हो जाता है। निर्मलता प्राप्त होनेपर साधकके सम्पूर्ण दुःखोंका नाश हो जाता है और ऐसे शुद्ध चित्तवाले साधककी बुद्धि निःसन्देह बहुत जल्दी परमात्मामें स्थिर हो जाती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.64।। आत्मसंयमी (विधेयात्मा) पुरुष रागद्वेष से रहित अपने वश में की हुई (आत्मवश्यै) इन्द्रियों द्वारा विषयों को भोगता हुआ प्रसन्नता (प्रस्ेााद) प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But he who goes through the sense-objects with the senses free from love and hate, disciplined and controlled, attains serenity.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But by perceiving objects with the organs that are free from attraction and repulsion, and are under his own control, the self-controlled person attains serenity.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But the self-controlled man, moving among objects with the senses restrained and free from attraction and repulsion, attains peace.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: On the contrary, one who moves about and consumes the sense-objects through their sense-organs, freed from desire and hatred and controlled in the Self—such a one, with a disciplined mind, attains serenity of disposition.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But the self-controlled soul, who moves among sense objects, free from attachment or repulsion, wins eternal peace.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.64 -- 2.65।। वशीभूत अन्तःकरणवाला कर्मयोगी साधक रागद्वेषसे रहित अपने वशमें की हुई इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा विषयोंका सेवन करता हुआ अन्तःकरणकी निर्मलता को प्राप्त हो जाता है। निर्मलता प्राप्त होनेपर साधकके सम्पूर्ण दुःखोंका नाश हो जाता है और ऐसे शुद्ध चित्तवाले साधककी बुद्धि निःसन्देह बहुत जल्दी परमात्मामें स्थिर हो जाती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.65।। प्रसाद के होने पर सम्पूर्ण दुखों का अन्त हो जाता है और प्रसन्नचित्त पुरुष की बुद्धि ही शीघ्र ही स्थिर हो जाती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: In that serenity, all sorrow is lost; for in the case of the person with a serene mind, the Buddhi is quickly established.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When there is serenity, all his sorrows are eradicated, for the wisdom of one with a serene mind soon becomes firmly established.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: In that peace, all pains are destroyed; for the intellect of the tranquil-minded soon becomes steady.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Upon attaining serenity, all miseries are extinguished in succession; the ability to make decisions is quickly stabilized in the mind of one who is serene.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having attained peace, he becomes free from misery; for when the mind gains peace, right discrimination follows.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.66।। जिसके मन-इन्द्रियाँ संयमित नहीं हैं, ऐसे मनुष्यकी व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि नहीं होती और व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि न होनेसे उसमें कर्तव्यपरायणताकी भावना नहीं होती। ऐसी भावना न होनेसे उसको शान्ति नहीं मिलती। फिर शान्तिरहित मनुष्यको सुख कैसे मिल सकता है?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.66।। (संयमरहित) अयुक्त पुरुष को (आत्म) ज्ञान नहीं होता और अयुक्त को भावना और ध्यान की क्षमता नहीं होती भावना रहित पुरुष को शान्ति नहीं मिलती अशान्त पुरुष को सुख कहाँ

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There is no Buddhi for the unintegrated, nor is there contemplation of the self for him, and without contemplation of the self, there is no peace; and for one who lacks peace, where is the happiness?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For the unsteady there is no wisdom, and there is no meditation for the unsteady person. And for an unmeditative person there is no peace. How can there be happiness for one without peace?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There is no knowledge of the Self for the unsteady, and no meditation is possible for the unsteady, and no peace for the unmeditative, and how can there be happiness for one who has no peace?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The ability to make decisions is not for one who is not a master of Yoga; and concentration of the mind is not for one who is not a master of Yoga; and peace is not for one who does not concentrate; whence could happiness come to one who has no peace?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Right discrimination is not for him who cannot concentrate. Without concentration, there cannot be meditation; he who cannot meditate must not expect peace; and without peace, how can one expect happiness?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.67।। अपने-अपने विषयोंमें विचरती हुई इन्द्रियोंमेंसे एक ही इन्द्रिय जिस मनको अपना अनुगामी बना लेती है, वह अकेला मन जलमें नौकाको वायुकी तरह इसकी बुद्धिको हर लेता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.67।। जल में वायु जैसे नाव को हर लेता है वैसे ही विषयों में विरचती हुई इन्द्रियों के बीच में जिस इन्द्रिय का अनुकरण मन करता है? वह एक ही इन्द्रिय इसकी प्रज्ञा को हर लेती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For, when the mind follows the senses, experiencing their objects, his understanding is carried away by them, just as the wind carries away a ship on the waters.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For, the mind which follows in the wake of the wandering senses carries away his wisdom like a boat on the waters.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For the mind, which follows in the wake of the wandering senses, carries away his discrimination, as the wind carries away a boat on the waters.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: That mind, which is directed to follow the wandering sense-organs—that mind carries away his knowledge just as the wind carries away a ship on the waters.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As a ship at sea is tossed by the tempest, so the reason is carried away by the mind when preyed upon by straying senses.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.68।। इसलिये हे महाबाहो ! जिस मनुष्यकी इन्द्रियाँ इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंसे सर्वथा निगृहीत (वशमें की हुई) हैं, उसकी बुद्धि स्थिर है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.68।। इसलिये? हे महाबाहो  जिस पुरुष की इन्द्रियाँ सब प्रकार इन्द्रियों के विषयों के वश में की हुई होती हैं? उसकी बुद्धि स्थिर होती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, he whose senses are restrained from going after their objects on all sides, his wisdom is firmly established.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, this wisdom becomes established when all its senses are withdrawn from their objects.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Therefore, O mighty-armed Arjuna, his knowledge is steady whose senses are completely restrained from sense objects.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, the intellect of that person is stabilized, all of whose sense-organs, starting from the sense-objects, have been well restrained.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Therefore, O Mighty-Armed One, he whose senses are detached from their objects—take it that his reason is purified.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.69।। सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों की जो रात (परमात्मासे विमुखता) है, उसमें संयमी मनुष्य जागता है, और जिसमें सब प्राणी जागते हैं (भोग और संग्रहमें लगे रहते हैं), वह तत्त्वको जाननेवाले मुनिकी दृष्टिमें रात है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.69।। सब प्रणियों के लिए जो रात्रि है? उसमें संयमी पुरुष जागता है और जहाँ सब प्राणी जागते हैं? वह (तत्त्व को) देखने वाले मुनि के लिए रात्रि है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: What is night for all beings, in it the one who is controlled is awake; when all beings are awake, that is the night for the sage who sees.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The self-restrained man stays awake during what is night for all creatures. That which creatures stay awake for, it is night to the discerning sage.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That which is night to all beings, in that the self-controlled man is awake; when all beings are awake, that is night for the sage who sees.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: What is night for every other being, in that a man of self-restraint is awake; wherein every other being is awake, that is night for the sage who sees the truth.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The saint is awake when the world sleeps, and he disregards that for which the world lives.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.70।। जैसे सम्पूर्ण नदियोंका जल चारों ओरसे जलद्वारा परिपूर्ण समुद्रमें आकर मिलता है, पर समुद्र अपनी मर्यादामें अचल प्रतिष्ठित रहता है ऐसे ही सम्पूर्ण भोग-पदार्थ जिस संयमी मनुष्य को विकार उत्पन्न किये बिना ही उसको प्राप्त होते हैं, वही मनुष्य परमशान्तिको प्राप्त होता है, भोगोंकी कामनावाला नहीं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.70।। जैसे सब ओर से परिपूर्ण अचल प्रतिष्ठा वाले समुद्र में (अनेक नदियों के) जल (उसे विचलित किये बिना) समा जाते हैं? वैसे ही जिस पुरुष के प्रति कामनाओं के विषय उसमें (विकार उत्पन्न किये बिना) समा जाते हैं? वह पुरुष शान्ति प्राप्त करता है? न कि भोगों की कामना करने वाला पुरुष।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He into whom all desires enter, just as the waters enter the full and undisturbed sea, attains peace, and not he who longs after objects of desire.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That man attains peace into whom all desires enter, just as waters flow into the sea that remains unchanged even when filled from all sides. Not so one who is desirous of objects.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He attains peace into whom all desires enter, just as waters enter the ocean which, filled from all sides, remains unmoved; but not the man who is full of desires.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Just as waters enter into the ocean which is being filled continuously yet firmly established, so too he into whom all objects of desire enter—he attains peace; not he who longs for the objects of desire.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He attains peace into whom desires flow as rivers into the ocean, which, though brimming with water, remains ever the same; not he whom desires carry away.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.71।। जो मनुष्य सम्पूर्ण कामनाओंका त्याग करके स्पृहारहित, ममतारहित और अहंकाररहित होकर आचरण करता है, वह शान्तिको प्राप्त होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.71।। जो पुरुष सब कामनाओं को त्यागकर स्पृहारहित? ममभाव रहित और निरहंकार हुआ विचरण करता है? वह शान्ति प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The one who, abandoning all desires, abides without longing, possession, and the sense of 'I' and 'mine', wins peace.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That man attains peace who, after rejecting all desires, moves about free from hankering, without the idea of 'me' and 'mine', and devoid of pride.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That person attains peace who, abandoning all desires, moves about without longing, without the sense of ownership, and without egoism.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: That person, who, by abandoning all desires, consumes [objects] without longing, without a sense of ownership, and without egotism, attains peace.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He attains peace who, giving up desire, moves through the world without aspiration, possessing nothing that he can call his own and is free from pride.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।2.72।। हे पृथानन्दन ! यह ब्राह्मी स्थिति है। इसको प्राप्त होकर कभी कोई मोहित नहीं होता। इस स्थितिमें यदि अन्तकालमें भी स्थित हो जाय, तो निर्वाण (शान्त) ब्रह्मकी प्राप्ति हो जाती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।2.72।। हे पार्थ  यह ब्राह्मी स्थिति है। इसे प्राप्त कर पुरुष मोहित नहीं होता। अन्तकाल में भी इस निष्ठा में स्थित होकर ब्रह्मनिर्वाण (ब्रह्म के साथ एकत्व) को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: This is the Brahmic state, O Arjuna. No one attaining to this is deluded. By abiding in this state even at the hour of death, one will attain the Self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, this is the state of being established in Brahman. One does not become deluded after attaining this; one attains identification with Brahman by being established in this state even in the closing years of one's life.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: O son of Pritha, this is the eternal state, the Brahmic seat. Attaining this, one is not deluded. Being established in it, one attains oneness with Brahman even at the end of life.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Prtha! This is the Brahmanic state; having attained this, one never gets deluded again. Even by remaining in this [state] for a while, one attains at the time of death the Brahman, the Transcendent One.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! This is the state of the Self, the Supreme Spirit, to which, if one attains, it shall never be taken away. Even at the time of leaving the body, one will remain firmly enthroned there and become one with the Eternal.
