author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.1 -- 3.2।। अर्जुन बोले -- हे जनार्दन! अगर आप कर्मसे बुद्धि- (ज्ञान-) को श्रेष्ठ मानते हैं, तो फिर हे केशव ! मुझे घोर कर्ममें क्यों लगाते हैं ? आप अपने मिले हुए-से वचनोंसे मेरी बुद्धिको मोहित-सी कर रहे हैं। अतः आप निश्चय करके उस एक बात को कहिये, जिससे मैं कल्याणको प्राप्त हो जाऊँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.1।। हे जनार्दन  यदि आपको यह मान्य है कि कर्म से ज्ञान श्रेष्ठ है तो फिर हे केशव  आप मुझे इस भयंकर कर्म में क्यों प्रवृत्त करते हैं

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, if you deem knowledge to be superior to action, why then do you urge me to engage in this terrible deed?"

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Janardana (Krishna), if it is Your opinion that wisdom is superior to action, why do You urge me to take such horrible action, O Kesava?"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said: If Thou thinkest that knowledge is superior to action, O Krishna, why then, O Kesava, doest Thou ask me to engage in this terrible action?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "O Janardana, if you hold knowledge to be superior to action, then why do you engage me in such terrible action, O Kesava?"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna questioned: My Lord! If wisdom is superior to action, why do you advise me to engage in this terrible fight?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.1 -- 3.2।। अर्जुन बोले -- हे जनार्दन! अगर आप कर्मसे बुद्धि- (ज्ञान-) को श्रेष्ठ मानते हैं, तो फिर हे केशव! मुझे घोर कर्ममें क्यों लगाते हैं ? आप अपने मिले हुए-से वचनोंसे मेरी बुद्धिको मोहित-सी कर रहे हैं। अतः आप निश्चय करके एक बात को कहिये, जिससे मैं कल्याणको प्राप्त हो जाऊँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.2।। आप इस मिश्रित वाक्य से मेरी बुद्धि को मोहितसा करते हैं अत आप उस एक (मार्ग) को निश्चित रूप से कहिये जिससे मैं परम श्रेय को प्राप्त कर सकूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: You confuse my mind with statements that seem to contradict each other; tell me for certain the one way by which I could reach the highest good.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: You bewilder my understanding, as it were, with a seemingly conflicting statement! Tell me for certain which one of these I may use to attain the highest Good.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: With this seemingly perplexing speech, you seem to be confusing my understanding; therefore, tell me one certain way by which I may attain bliss.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: You seem to be confounding my intellect with Your seemingly confusing speech. Therefore, tell me with certainty that one thing by which I may attain the good (emancipation).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Your language confuses me and confounds my reason. Therefore, please tell me the only way by which I can, without doubt, secure my spiritual welfare.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.3।। श्रीभगवान् बोले - हे निष्पाप अर्जुन! इस मनुष्यलोकमें दो प्रकारसे होनेवाली निष्ठा मेरे द्वारा पहले कही गयी है। उनमें ज्ञानियोंकी निष्ठा ज्ञानयोगसे और योगियोंकी निष्ठा कर्मयोगसे होती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.3।। श्री भगवान् ने कहा  हे निष्पाप (अनघ) अर्जुन  इस श्लोक में दो प्रकार की निष्ठा मेरे द्वारा पहले कही गयी है ज्ञानियों की (सांख्यानां) ज्ञानयोग से और योगियों की कर्मयोग से।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "In this world, a two-fold way was of yore laid down by Me, O sinless one: Jnana Yoga for the Sankhyas and Karma Yoga for the Yogins."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, O unblemished one, two kinds of steadfastness in this world were spoken of by Me in days gone by—through the Yoga of Knowledge for the men of realization; through the Yoga of Action for the yogis.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "In this world, there is a twofold path, as I said before, O sinless one: the path of knowledge of the Sankhyas and the path of action of the Yogins."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "The two-fold path in this world—the one with Yoga of knowledge for men of reflection and the other with Yoga of action for men of Yoga—has been declared by Me to be one, O sinless one!"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: In this world, as I have said, there is a twofold path, O sinless one! There is the path of wisdom for those who meditate, and the path of action for those who work.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.4।। मनुष्य न तो कर्मोंका आरम्भ किये बिना निष्कर्मताको प्राप्त होता है और न कर्मोंके त्यागमात्रसे सिद्धिको ही प्राप्त होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.4।। कर्मों के न करने से मनुष्य नैर्ष्कम्य को प्राप्त नहीं होता और न कर्मों के संन्यास से ही वह सिद्धि (पूर्णत्व) प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: No one experiences freedom from action (Naiskarmya) by abstaining from work; and no one ever attains success through mere renunciation of work.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A person does not attain freedom from action by abstaining from action; nor does he attain fulfillment merely through renunciation.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Man does not reach actionlessness by not performing actions; nor does he attain perfection by mere renunciation.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: A person attains actionlessness not just by abstaining from actions, but also by renunciation, he attains success (emancipation).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: No one can attain freedom from action by abstaining from action, nor can they reach perfection by simply not acting.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.5।। कोई भी मनुष्य किसी भी अवस्थामें क्षणमात्र भी कर्म किये बिना नहीं रह सकता; क्योंकि (प्रकृतिके) परवश हुए सब प्राणियोंसे प्रकृतिजन्य गुण कर्म कराते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.5।। कोई भी पुरुष कभी क्षणमात्र भी बिना कर्म किए नहीं रह सकता क्योंकि प्रकृति से उत्पन्न गुणों के द्वारा अवश हुए सब (पुरुषों) से कर्म करवा लिया जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: No one can remain still for even a moment without doing work; for everyone is compelled to act, despite themselves, by the Gunas born of Nature.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For no one ever remains even for a moment without doing work, for all are made to work under compulsion by the gunas born of Nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Verily, no one can remain for even a moment without performing action; for everyone is made to act helplessly, indeed, by the qualities born of Nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For, no one can ever remain, even for a moment, as a non-performer of action; because everyone, not being master of themselves, is forced to perform action by the strands born of the Prakriti (Material cause).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He cannot remain inactive even for a moment, for the qualities of Nature will compel him to act, whether he wills it or not.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.6।। जो कर्मेन्द्रियों- (सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियों-) को हठपूर्वक रोककर मनसे इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंका चिन्तन करता रहता है, वह मूढ़ बुद्धिवाला मनुष्य मिथ्याचारी (मिथ्या आचरण करनेवाला) कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.6।। जो मूढ बुद्धि पुरुष कर्मेन्द्रियों का निग्रह कर इन्द्रियों के भोगों का मन से स्मरण (चिन्तन) करता रहता है वह मिथ्याचारी (दम्भी) कहा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who, controlling the organs of action, lets his mind dwell on the objects of the senses, is a deluded person and a hypocrite.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who, after withdrawing the organs of action, sits mentally recollecting the objects of the senses, is called a hypocrite, for they have a deluded mind.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who, restraining the organs of action, sits thinking of the sense-objects in his mind, he of deluded understanding is called a hypocrite.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Controlling their organs of action, whoever sits with their mind pondering over sense objects—that person is a man of deluded soul and is called a man of deluded action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who remains motionless, refusing to act, yet brooding over sensuous objects, is that deluded soul simply a hypocrite.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.7।। हे अर्जुन! जो मनुष्य मनसे इन्द्रियोंपर नियन्त्रण करके आसक्तिरहित होकर (निष्काम भावसे) समस्त इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा कर्मयोगका आचरण करता है, वही श्रेष्ठ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.7।। परन्तु हे अर्जुन  जो पुरुष मन से इन्द्रियों को वश में करके अनासक्त हुआ कर्मेंन्द्रियों से कर्मयोग का आचरण करता है वह श्रेष्ठ है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But he who, subduing his senses by the mind, O Arjuna, begins to practice Karma Yoga through the organs of action and who is free from attachment, excels.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But, O Arjuna, one who engages in Karma-yoga, controlling the organs of action with the mind and becoming unattached, excels.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But whoever, controlling the senses by the mind, O Arjuna, engages himself in Karma Yoga with the organs of action, without attachment, he excels.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But, controlling the sense-organs with the mind, whoever undertakes the Yoga of action with the action-senses, he, the detached one, is superior, O Arjuna!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But, O Arjuna! All honor to him whose mind controls his senses, for he is thereby beginning to practice Karma-Yoga, the Path of Right Action, keeping himself always unattached.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.8।। तू शास्त्रविधिसे नियत किये हुए कर्तव्य-कर्म कर; क्योंकि कर्म न करनेकी अपेक्षा कर्म करना श्रेष्ठ है तथा कर्म न करनेसे तेरा शरीर-निर्वाह भी सिद्ध नहीं होगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.8।। तुम (अपने) नियत (कर्तव्य) कर्म करो क्योंकि अकर्म से श्रेष्ठ कर्म है। तुम्हारे अकर्म होने से (तुम्हारा) शरीर निर्वाह भी नहीं सिद्ध होगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: You must perform your obligatory action; for action is superior to non-action (Jñāna Yoga). For a person following non-action, not even the sustenance of the body is possible.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: You should perform your obligatory duties, for action is superior to inaction. Moreover, even the maintenance of your body will not be possible without action.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Perform your bounden duty, for action is superior to inaction, and even the maintenance of the body would not be possible for you through inaction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: You must perform the action that has been enjoined upon you. For, action is superior to inaction; and even the maintenance of your body cannot be properly accomplished through inaction.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Do your duty as prescribed, for action for duty's sake is superior to inaction. Even the maintenance of the body would be impossible if one remained inactive.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.9।। यज्ञ (कर्तव्यपालन) के लिये किये जानेवाले कर्मोंसे अन्यत्र (अपने लिये किये जानेवाले) कर्मोंमें लगा हुआ यह मनुष्य-समुदाय कर्मोंसे बँधता है, इसलिये हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! तू आसक्ति-रहित होकर उस यज्ञके लिये ही कर्तव्य-कर्म कर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.9।। यज्ञ के लिये किये हुए कर्म के अतिरिक्त अन्य कर्म में प्रवृत्त हुआ यह पुरुष कर्मों द्वारा बंधता है इसलिए हे कौन्तेय आसक्ति को त्यागकर यज्ञ के निमित्त ही कर्म का सम्यक् आचरण करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: This world is held in the bondage of work only when work is not performed as a sacrifice. O Arjuna, you must perform work for this purpose, free from attachment.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: This man becomes bound by actions other than those intended for God. Without being attached, O son of Kunti, perform actions for Him.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The world is bound by actions other than those performed for the sake of sacrifice; do thou, therefore, O son of Kunti (Arjuna), perform actions for that sake alone, free from attachment.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The world is fettered by action which is different from Yajna-action; therefore, O son of Kunti, free from attachment, perform Yajna-action properly.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: In this world, people are fettered by their actions, unless they are performed as a sacrifice. Therefore, O Arjuna, let your actions be done without attachment, as a sacrifice only.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.10 -- 3.11।। प्रजापति ब्रह्माजीने सृष्टिके आदिकालमें कर्तव्य-कर्मोंके विधानसहित प्रजा-(मनुष्य आदि-) की रचना करके उनसे (प्रधानतया मनुष्योंसे) कहा कि तुमलोग इस कर्तव्यके द्वारा सबकी वृद्धि करो और वह कर्तव्य-कर्म-रूप यज्ञ तुमलोगोंको कर्तव्य-पालनकी आवश्यक सामग्री प्रदान करनेवाला हो। अपने कर्तव्य-कर्मके द्वारा तुमलोग देवताओंको उन्नत करो और वे देवतालोग अपने कर्तव्यके द्वारा तुमलोगोंको उन्नत करें। इस प्रकार एक-दूसरेको उन्नत करते हुए तुमलोग परम कल्याणको प्राप्त हो जाओगे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.10।। प्रजापति (सृष्टिकर्त्ता) ने (सृष्टि के) आदि में यज्ञ सहित प्रजा का निर्माण कर कहा इस यज्ञ द्वारा तुम वृद्धि को प्राप्त हो और यह यज्ञ तुम्हारे लिये इच्छित कामनाओं को पूर्ण करने वाला (इष्टकामधुक्) होवे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: In the beginning, the Lord of all beings created man along with the sacrifice and said, "By this shall you prosper; this shall be the cow of plenty, granting all your desires."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: In days of yore, having created the beings together with the sacrifices, Prajapati said, "By this, you shall multiply. Let this be your yielder of coveted objects of desire."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Creator, having in the beginning created mankind together with sacrifice, said, "By this shall you propagate; let this be the milch cow of your desires—the cow that yields all the desired objects."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having created creatures formerly [at the time of creation], together with the necessary action, the Lord of creatures declared: "By means of this, you shall propagate yourselves; and let this be your wish-fulfilling cow."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: In the beginning, when God created all beings through the sacrifice of Himself, He said to them: "You can procreate through sacrifice, and it shall satisfy all your desires."

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.10 -- 3.11।। प्रजापति ब्रह्माजीने सृष्टिके आदिकालमें कर्तव्य-कर्मोंके विधानसहित प्रजा-(मनुष्य आदि-) की रचना करके (उनसे, प्रधानतया मनुष्योंसे) कहा कि तुमलोग इस कर्तव्यके द्वारा सबकी वृद्धि करो और वह कर्तव्य-कर्म-रूप यज्ञ तुमलोगोंको कर्तव्य-पालनकी आवश्यक सामग्री प्रदान करनेवाला हो। अपने कर्तव्य-कर्मके द्वारा तुमलोग देवताओंको उन्नत करो और वे देवतालोग अपने कर्तव्यके द्वारा तुमलोगोंको उन्नत करें। इस प्रकार एक-दूसरेको उन्नत करते हुए तुमलोग परम कल्याणको प्राप्त हो जाओगे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.11।। तुम लोग इस यज्ञ द्वारा देवताओं की उन्नति करो और वे देवतागण तुम्हारी उन्नति करें। इस प्रकार परस्पर उन्नति करते हुये परम श्रेय को तुम प्राप्त होगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: By this, please the gods, and they will support you. Thus, nourishing each other, you may obtain the highest good.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: 'You nourish the gods with this. Let those gods nourish you in return. By mutually nourishing each other, you shall attain the supreme Good.'

author: Swami Sivananda
text: With this, nourish the gods, and may the gods nourish you; thus, nourishing each other, you shall attain the highest good.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: 'You should gratify the devas with this, and let the devas gratify you; thus, mutually gratifying each other, you will attain the highest good.'

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Worship the powers of Nature, and let them nourish you in return; thus, supporting each other, you shall attain your highest welfare.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.12।। यज्ञसे भावित (पुष्ट) हुए देवता भी तुमलोगोंको (बिना माँगे ही) कर्तव्य-पालनकी आवश्यक सामग्री देते रहेंगे। इस प्रकार उन देवताओंसे प्राप्त हुई सामग्रीको दूसरोंकी सेवामें लगाये बिना जो मनुष्य स्वयं ही उसका उपभोग करता है, वह चोर ही है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.12।। यज्ञ द्वारा पोषित देवतागण तुम्हें इष्ट भोग प्रदान करेंगे। उनके द्वारा दिये हुये भोगों को जो पुरुष उनको दिये बिना ही भोगता है वह निश्चय ही चोर है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The gods, pleased by the sacrifice, will bestow upon you the enjoyments you desire. He who enjoys the bounty of the gods without giving them anything in return is but a thief.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: 'Being nourished by sacrifices, the gods will indeed give you the coveted enjoyments. He is certainly a thief who enjoys what has been given by them without offering it to them.'

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The gods, nourished by the sacrifice, will give you the desired objects. So, he who enjoys the objects given by the gods without offering anything in return is indeed a thief.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The devas, gratified with necessary action, will grant you the things sacrificed. Therefore, whoever enjoys their gifts without offering them to these devas is surely a thief.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: For, fed by sacrifice, nature will give you all the enjoyment you desire. But he who enjoys what she gives without returning is, indeed, a thief.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.13।। यज्ञशेष- (योग-) का अनुभव करनेवाले श्रेष्ठ मनुष्य सम्पूर्ण पापोंसे मुक्त हो जाते हैं। परन्तु जो केवल अपने लिये ही पकाते अर्थात् सब कर्म करते हैं, वे पापीलोग तो पापका ही भक्षण करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.13।। यज्ञ के अवशिष्ट अन्न को खाने वाले श्रेष्ठ पुरुष सब पापों से मुक्त हो जाते हैं किन्तु जो लोग केवल स्वयं के लिये ही पकाते हैं वे तो पापों को ही खाते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Pious people who eat the remnants of sacrifices are freed from all sins, whereas those who are sinful and cook only for their own sake will only incur sin.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: By partaking in the remnants of sacrifices, they become freed from all sins. But those who are unholy and cook for themselves incur sin.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The righteous who eat the remnants of the sacrifice are freed from all sins; but those sinful ones who cook food solely for their own sake indeed consume sin.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The righteous persons, who eat the remnants of the actions to be performed necessarily, are freed from all sins. But those who cook, intending for their own selves, are sinners and consume sin.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sages who enjoy the food that remains after the sacrifice is made are freed from all sins; whereas, the selfish who spread their feast only for themselves feed on sin alone.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.14 -- 3.15।। सम्पूर्ण प्राणी अन्नसे उत्पन्न होते हैं। अन्न  वर्षासे होती है। वर्षा यज्ञसे होती है। यज्ञ कर्मोंसे निष्पन्न होता है। कर्मोंको तू वेदसे उत्पन्न जान और वेदको अक्षरब्रह्मसे प्रकट हुआ जान। इसलिये वह सर्वव्यापी परमात्मा यज्ञ (कर्तव्य-कर्म) में नित्य प्रतिष्ठित है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.14।। समस्त प्राणी अन्न से उत्पन्न होते हैं अन्न की उत्पत्ति पर्जन्य से। पर्जन्य की उत्पत्ति यज्ञ से और यज्ञ कर्मों से उत्पन्न होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: All beings arise from food; food is produced from rain; rain comes from sacrifice; and sacrifice is born of action.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: From food are born the creatures; the origin of food is from rain; rain originates from sacrifice; sacrifice has action as its source.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: From food come forth beings; from rain, food is produced; from sacrifice arises rain, and sacrifice is born of action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: From food arise the things that are born; from the rain-cloud the food arises; from the sacrifice the rain-cloud arises; the sacrifice arises from action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: All creatures are the product of food; food is the product of rain; rain comes from sacrifice, and sacrifice is the noblest form of action.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.14 -- 3.15।। सम्पूर्ण प्राणी अन्नसे उत्पन्न होते हैं। अन्न  वर्षासे होती है। वर्षा यज्ञसे होती है। यज्ञ कर्मोंसे निष्पन्न होता है। कर्मोंको तू वेदसे उत्पन्न जान और वेदको अक्षरब्रह्मसे प्रकट हुआ जान। इसलिये वह सर्वव्यापी परमात्मा यज्ञ (कर्तव्य-कर्म) में नित्य प्रतिष्ठित है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.15।। कर्म की उत्पत्ति ब्रह्माजी से होती है और ब्रह्माजी अक्षर तत्त्व से व्यक्त होते हैं। इसलिये सर्व व्यापी ब्रह्म सदा ही यज्ञ में प्रतिष्ठित है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know that activity springs from Brahman, i.e., the physical body; Brahman arises from the imperishable Self; therefore, the all-pervading Brahman is ever established in sacrifice.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Know that action has the Vedas as its origin; the Vedas have the Immutable as their source. Hence, the all-pervading Vedas are forever based on sacrifice.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know that action comes from Brahma, and Brahma comes from the Imperishable. Therefore, the all-pervasive Brahma ever rests in sacrifice.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Action arises from the Brahman; you should know this; the Brahman arises from that which does not stream forth; therefore, the all-pervading Brahman is permanently based on the sacrifice.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: All actions originate from the Supreme Spirit, which is Imperishable, and in sacrificial actions, the all-pervasive Spirit is consciously present.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.16।। हे पार्थ! जो मनुष्य इस लोकमें इस प्रकार परम्परासे प्रचलित सृष्टिचक्रके अनुसार नहीं चलता, वह इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा भोगोंमें रमण करनेवाला अघायु (पापमय जीवन बितानेवाला) मनुष्य संसारमें व्यर्थ ही जीता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.16।। जो पुरुष यहाँ इस प्रकार प्रवर्तित हुए चक्र का अनुवर्तन नहीं करता हे पार्थ इंन्द्रियों में रमने वाला वह पाप आयु पुरुष व्यर्थ ही जीता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion here, lives in sin, satisfying the senses, O Arjuna; he lives in vain.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, he who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion lives in vain, their life is sinful, and they indulge in the senses.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion, who is of sinful life, rejoicing in the senses, lives in vain, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever does not keep the wheel of life rolling, which has been set in motion in this world, is a man of sinful life, rejoicing in the senses; and he lives in vain, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thus, he who does not help the revolving wheel of sacrifice, but instead leads a sinful life, rejoicing in the gratification of his senses, O Arjuna, is breathing in vain.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.17।।  जो मनुष्य अपने-आपमें ही रमण करनेवाला और अपने-आपमें ही तृप्त तथा अपने-आपमें ही संतुष्ट है, उसके लिये कोई कर्तव्य नहीं है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.17।। परन्तु जो मनुष्य आत्मा में ही रमने वाला आत्मा में ही तृप्त तथा आत्मा में ही सन्तुष्ट हो उसके लिये कोई कर्तव्य नहीं रहता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But the man whose delight is only in the Self, who is satisfied with the Self, who rejoices in the Self, for him nothing remains to be accomplished.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But that man who rejoices only in the Self, is satisfied with the Self, and is content only in the Self—for him there is no duty to perform.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But for that man who rejoices only in the Self, who is satisfied with the Self and is content in the Self alone, indeed there is nothing to do.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But the man who simply rejoices in the Self, is satisfied in the Self, and delights in the Self alone—there exists no action for him to be performed.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: On the other hand, the soul who meditates on the Self is content to serve the Self and rests satisfied in the Self; there is nothing more for them to accomplish.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.18।। उस (कर्मयोगसे सिद्ध हुए) महापुरुषका इस संसारमें न तो कर्म करनेसे कोई प्रयोजन रहता है, और न कर्म न करनेसे ही कोई प्रयोजन रहता है, तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें (किसी भी प्राणीके साथ) इसका किञ्चिन्मात्र भी स्वार्थका सम्बन्ध नहीं रहता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.18।। इस जगत् में उस पुरुष का कृत और अकृत से कोई प्रयोजन नहीं है और न वह किसी वस्तु के लिये भूतमात्र पर आश्रित होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He has no purpose to gain from work done or left undone, nor does he have to rely on any end.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For him, there is no concern here at all with performing action, nor any with non-performance. Moreover, he has no dependence on any object to serve any purpose.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For him, there is no interest whatsoever in what is done or not done; nor does he depend on any being for any purpose.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: No purpose is served for him by what he has done or by what he has not done. For him, there is hardly any dependence on any purpose among all beings.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He has nothing to gain from the performance or non-performance of action; his welfare does not depend on any contribution that an earthly creature can make.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.19।। इसलिये तू निरन्तर आसक्तिरहित होकर कर्तव्य-कर्मका भलीभाँति आचरण कर; क्योंकि आसक्तिरहित होकर कर्म करता हुआ मनुष्य परमात्माको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.19।। इसलिए,  तुम अनासक्त होकर सदैव कर्तव्य कर्म का सम्यक् आचरण करो;  क्योकि,  अनासक्त पुरुष कर्म करता हुआ परमात्मा को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Therefore, do your work without attachment, which ought to be done. For, a person who works without attachment attains the Supreme.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Therefore, remaining unattached, always perform the obligatory duty; for, by performing one's duty without attachment, a person attains the highest.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Therefore, without attachment, always perform the actions that should be done; for by performing actions without attachment, one reaches the Supreme.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Therefore, always remaining unattached, you should perform the action that is to be performed; for, the person performing action without attachment attains the Supreme.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Therefore, do your duty perfectly, without worrying about the results, for he who does his duty without attachment attains the Supreme.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.20।। राजा जनक-जैसे अनेक महापुरुष भी कर्मके द्वारा ही परमसिद्धिको प्राप्त हुए हैं। इसलिये लोकसंग्रहको देखते हुए भी तू (निष्कामभावसे) कर्म करनेके योग्य है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.20।। जनकादि (ज्ञानी जन) भी कर्म द्वारा ही संसिद्धि को प्राप्त हुये लोक संग्रह (लोक रक्षण) को भी देखते हुये;  तुम कर्म करने योग्य हो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Indeed, Janaka and others reached perfection through Karma Yoga alone. Even recognizing its necessity for guiding the world, you must perform action.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For Janaka and others strove to attain liberation through action itself. You should perform your duties, keeping in view the prevention of mankind from going astray.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Janaka and others attained perfection indeed through action alone; even with the intention of protecting the masses, you should perform action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Janaka and others attained emancipation through action alone. Moreover, you should act with the intention of upholding the world.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: King Janaka and others attained perfection through action alone. Even for the sake of enlightening the world, it is your duty to act.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.21।। श्रेष्ठ मनुष्य जो-जो आचरण करता है, दूसरे मनुष्य वैसा-वैसा ही आचरण करते हैं। वह जो कुछ प्रमाण  देता है,  दूसरे मनुष्य उसीके अनुसार आचरण करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.21।। श्रेष्ठ पुरुष जैसा आचरण करता है, अन्य लोग भी वैसा ही अनुकरण करते हैं; वह पुरुष जो कुछ प्रमाण कर देता है, लोग भी उसका अनुसरण करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whatever a great man does, others do too. Whatever standard he sets, the world follows it.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Whatever a superior person does, another person does the same! Whatever they uphold as authority, an ordinary person follows that.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whatever a great man does, others also do; whatever he establishes as the standard, the world follows.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whatever a great man does, others of lesser stature follow suit; whatever standard he sets, the world follows it.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: For whatever a great man does, others imitate; people conform to the standard he has set.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.22।। हे पार्थ! मुझे तीनों लोकोंमें न तो कुछ कर्तव्य है और न कोई प्राप्त करनेयोग्य वस्तु अप्राप्त है, फिर भी मैं कर्तव्यकर्ममें ही लगा रहता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.22।। यद्यपि मुझे त्रैलोक्य में कुछ भी कर्तव्य नहीं हैं तथा किंचित भी प्राप्त होने योग्य (अवाप्तव्यम्) वस्तु अप्राप्त नहीं है, तो भी मैं कर्म में ही बर्तता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For me, Arjuna, there is nothing in all the three worlds that ought to be done, nor is there anything unaccomplished that ought to be accomplished. Yet I continue to work.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: In all the three worlds, O Partha, there is no duty for Me to fulfill; nothing remains unachieved or to be achieved. (Still,) I continue in action.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There is nothing in the three worlds, O Arjuna, that needs to be done by Me, nor is there anything unattained that needs to be attained; yet I engage Myself in action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Prtha! For Me, there is nothing to be done in the three worlds, nor is there anything yet to be attained; and yet I am engaged in action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There is nothing in this universe, O Arjuna, that I am compelled to do, nor anything for me to attain; yet I persistently remain active.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.23 -- 3.24।।  हे पार्थ! अगर मैं किसी समय सावधान होकर कर्तव्यकर्म न करूँ तो बड़ी हानि हो जाय; क्योंकि मनुष्य सब प्रकारसे मेरे ही मार्गका अनुसरण करते हैं। यदि मैं कर्म न करूँ, तो ये सब मनुष्य नष्ट-भ्रष्ट हो जायँ और मैं वर्णसंकरताको करनेवाला होऊँ तथा इस समस्त प्रजाको नष्ट करनेवाला बनूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.23।। यदि मैं सावधान हुआ (अतन्द्रित:) कदाचित कर्म में न लगा रहूँ तो, हे पार्थ ! सब प्रकार से मनुष्य मेरे मार्ग (र्वत्म) का अनुसरण करेंगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: If I did not continue to work unweariedly, O Arjuna, people would follow my path.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For, O Partha, if at any time I do not continue vigilantly in action, people will follow My path in every way.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For, should I not ever engage myself in action, unwearied, people would in every way follow my path, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For, if I were ever not to work unwearied, all men would follow My path, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Were I not to act without ceasing, O prince, people would be inclined to do the same.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.23 -- 3.24।।  हे पार्थ ! अगर मैं किसी समय सावधान होकर कर्तव्य-कर्म न करूँ (तो बड़ी हानि हो जाय; क्योंकि) मनुष्य सब प्रकारसे मेरे ही मार्गका अनुसरण करते हैं। यदि मैं कर्म न करूँ, तो ये सब मनुष्य नष्ट-भ्रष्ट हो जायँ और मैं वर्णसंकरताको करनेवाला  तथा इस समस्त प्रजाको नष्ट करनेवाला बनूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.24।। यदि मैं कर्म न करूँ, तो ये समस्त लोक नष्ट हो जायेंगे; और मैं वर्णसंकर का कर्ता तथा इस प्रजा का हनन करने वाला होऊँगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: If I do not do the work, these people will be lost; and I will be causing chaos in life and thereby ruining all these people.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: These worlds would be ruined if I did not perform action. And I shall become the agent of intermingling (of castes), and shall be destroying these beings.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: These worlds would perish if I did not perform action; I would be the author of confusion of castes and destruction of these beings.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: These worlds would perish if I were not to perform action; and I would be the cause of confusion; I would destroy these people.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: And if I were to refrain from action, the human race would be ruined; I would lead the world to chaos, and destruction would follow.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.25 -- 3.26।। हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन! कर्ममें आसक्त हुए अज्ञानीजन जिस प्रकार कर्म करते हैं, आसक्तिरहित विद्वान भी लोकसंग्रह करना चाहता हुआ उसी प्रकार कर्म करे।  तत्त्वज्ञ महापुरुष कर्मोंमें आसक्तिवाले अज्ञानी मनुष्योंकी बुद्धिमें भ्रम उत्पन्न न करे, प्रत्युत स्वयं समस्त कर्मोंको अच्छी तरहसे करता हुआ उनसे भी वैसे ही करवाये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.25।। हे भारत ! कर्म में आसक्त हुए अज्ञानीजन जैसे कर्म करते हैं वैसे ही विद्वान् पुरुष अनासक्त होकर, लोकसंग्रह (लोक कल्याण) की इच्छा से कर्म करे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Just as the ignorant, attached to their work, act, O Arjuna, so too the learned should act without any attachment, and only for the welfare of the world.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, as the unenlightened people act with attachment to work, so should the enlightened person act, without attachment, desiring to prevent people from going astray.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: As the ignorant act out of attachment to action, O Bharata, so should the wise act without attachment, wishing for the welfare of the world.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Therefore, just as the unwise persons, being attached to action, do, O son of Prtha, so the wise should perform, but being unattached and desiring to hold the world together.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As the ignorant act, out of their fondness for action, so should the wise act without attachment, fixing their eyes, O Arjuna, only on the welfare of the world.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.25 -- 3.26।। हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन! कर्ममें आसक्त हुए अज्ञानीजन जिस प्रकार कर्म करते हैं आसक्तिरहित विद्वान भी लोकसंग्रह करना चाहता हुआ उसी प्रकार कर्म करे। सावधान तत्त्वज्ञ महापुरुष कर्मोंमें आसक्तिवाले अज्ञानी मनुष्योंकी बुद्धिमें भ्रम उत्पन्न न करे, प्रत्युत स्वयं समस्त कर्मोंको अच्छी तरहसे करता हुआ उनसे भी वैसे ही करवाये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.26।। ज्ञानी पुरुष, कर्मों में आसक्त अज्ञानियों की बुद्धि में भ्रम उत्पन्न न करे, स्वयं (भक्ति से) युक्त होकर कर्मों का सम्यक् आचरण कर, उनसे भी वैसा ही कराये।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He should not bewilder the minds of the ignorant who are attached to work; rather, he should himself perform work with devotion and cause others to do the same.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The enlightened man should not disturb the beliefs of the ignorant, who are attached to work. He should remain diligent himself and make them perform all their duties.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Let no wise man unsettle the minds of ignorant people who are attached to action; he should engage them in all actions, himself fulfilling them with devotion.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Let the wise master of Yoga fulfill (or destroy) all actions by performing them all, and let him not create any disturbance in the minds of the ignorant persons attached to action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But a wise man should not disturb the minds of the ignorant, who are attached to action; let him perform his own actions in the right spirit, with concentration on Me, thus inspiring all others to do the same.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.27।। सम्पूर्ण कर्म सब प्रकारसे प्रकृतिके गुणोंद्वारा किये जाते हैं; परन्तु अहंकारसे मोहित अन्तःकरणवाला अज्ञानी मनुष्य 'मैं कर्ता हूँ' -- ऐसा मानता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.27।। सम्पूर्ण कर्म प्रकृति के गुणों द्वारा किये जाते हैं, अहंकार से मोहित हुआ पुरुष,  "मैं कर्ता हूँ"  ऐसा मान लेता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Actions are being performed in every way by the Gunas of Prakrti; he whose nature is deluded by egoism, however, thinks, 'I am the doer.'

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: While actions are being done in every way by the gunas (qualities) of Nature, one who is deluded by egoism thinks, "I am the doer."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: All actions are wrought in all cases by the qualities of Nature alone. He whose mind is deluded by egoism thinks, "I am the doer."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The actions are performed part by part by the strands of Prakriti; yet, the person, having his self (mind) deluded with egoity, imagines, 'I am the sole doer.'

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Action is the product of the qualities inherent in nature. It is only the ignorant man who, misled by personal egotism, says, "I am the doer."

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.28।।  हे महाबाहो! गुण-विभाग और कर्म-विभागको तत्त्वसे जाननेवाला महापुरुष 'सम्पूर्ण गुण ही गुणोंमें बरत रहे हैं' --  ऐसा मानकर उनमें आसक्त नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.28।। परन्तु हे महाबाहो ! गुण और कर्म के विभाग के सत्य (तत्त्व)को जानने वाला ज्ञानी पुरुष यह जानकर कि "गुण गुणों में बर्तते हैं" (कर्म में) आसक्त नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But he who knows the truth about the division of the Gunas and works, O mighty-armed one, through his knowledge that the Gunas 'operate on their own products,' is not attached.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But, O mighty-armed one, the one who is a knower of the facts about the varieties of the gunas and actions does not become attached, thinking thus: 'The organs act on the objects of the organs.'

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But he who knows the Truth, O mighty-armed Arjuna, about the divisions of the qualities and their functions, knowing that the Gunas, as senses, move amidst the Gunas, as the sense-objects, is not attached.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But, O mighty-armed one, the one who knows the true nature of the strands and their respective divisions of work, realizes: 'The strands are in their respective purposes.' And thus, he is not attached.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But he, O Mighty One, who correctly understands the relationship between the Qualities and action, is not attached to the act, for he perceives that it is merely the action and reaction of the Qualities among themselves.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.29।। प्रकृतिजन्य गुणोंसे अत्यन्त मोहित हुए अज्ञानी मनुष्य गुणों और कर्मोंमें आसक्त रहते हैं। उन पूर्णतया न समझनेवाले मन्दबुद्धि अज्ञानियोंको पूर्णतया जाननेवाला ज्ञानी मनुष्य विचलित न करे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.29।। प्रकृति के गुणों से मोहित हुए पुरुष गुण और कर्म में आसक्त होते हैं, उन अपूर्ण ज्ञान वाले (अकृत्स्नविद:) मंदबुद्धि पुरुषों को पूर्ण ज्ञान प्राप्त पुरुष विचलित न करे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those who are deluded by the Gunas of Prakrti are attached to the works of the Gunas. But he who knows the whole truth should not disturb the ignorant who do not know the whole truth.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those who are completely deluded by the gunas of Nature become attached to the activities of the gunas. The one who knows the All should not disturb those of dull intellect, who do not know the All.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Those deluded by the qualities of Nature are attached to the functions of the qualities. The man of perfect knowledge should not unsettle the foolish one who is of imperfect knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Men, completely deluded by the strands of Prakrti, are attached to the actions of those strands. Those who know fully should not confuse them, whereas the dullards who do not know fully.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Those who do not understand the qualities are interested in the act; however, the wise man who knows the truth should not disturb the mind of those who do not.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.30।। तू विवेकवती बुद्धिके द्वारा सम्पूर्ण कर्तव्य-कर्मोंको मेरे अर्पण करके कामना, ममता और संताप-रहित होकर युद्धरूप कर्तव्य-कर्मको कर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.30।। सम्पूर्ण कर्मों का मुझ में संन्यास करके,  आशा और ममता से रहित होकर,  संतापरहित हुए तुम युद्ध करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Surrender all your actions to Me, with a mind focused on the Self, free from desire and selfishness, and fight with the heat of excitement abated.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Free from the fever of the soul, dedicate all actions to Me, with your mind intent on the Self, and become free from expectations and egoism, and engage in battle.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Renouncing all actions in Me, with the mind centered on the Self, free from hope and egoism, and from mental fever, fight thou.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Renouncing all actions in Me, with a mind that concentrates on the Self; being free from the act of resting and from the sense of possession; and consequently being free from mental fever; you should fight.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Therefore, surrender your actions to Me, keep your thoughts focused on the Absolute, free from selfishness and without expecting a reward, with a mind free from excitement, begin to fight.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.31।। जो मनुष्य दोष-दृष्टिसे रहित होकर श्रद्धापूर्वक मेरे इस (पूर्वश्लोकमें वर्णित) मतका सदा अनुसरण करते हैं, वे भी कर्मोंके बन्धनसे मुक्त हो जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.31।। जो मनुष्य दोष बुद्धि से रहित (अनसूयन्त:) और श्रद्धा से युक्त हुए सदा मेरे इस मत (उपदेश) का अनुष्ठानपूर्वक पालन करते हैं, वे कर्मों से (बन्धन से) मुक्त हो जाते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those men who, full of faith, ever practice this teaching of Mine and those who receive it without cavil—even they are released from karma.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those who faithfully follow this teaching of Mine without cavil, they too become freed from their actions.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Those who constantly practice this teaching of Mine with faith and without caviling, they too are freed from actions.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those who constantly follow this doctrine of Mine, with faith and without finding fault, are freed from the results of all actions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Those who always act in accordance with My precepts, firmly in faith and without caviling, they too are freed from the bondage of action.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.32।। परन्तु जो मनुष्य मेरे इस मतमें दोष-दृष्टि करते हुए इसका अनुष्ठान नहीं करते, उन सम्पूर्ण ज्ञानोंमें मोहित और अविवेकी मनुष्योंको नष्ट हुए ही समझो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.32।। परन्तु जो दोष दृष्टि वाले मूढ़ लोग इस मेरे मत का पालन नहीं करते, उन सब ज्ञानों में मोहित चित्तवालों को नष्ट हुये ही तुम समझो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But those who slander it, and those who do not practice this teaching of Mine—know them to be utterly senseless and devoid of all knowledge, and thus lost.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But those who, finding fault where there is none, do not follow My teaching, know them—who are deluded about all knowledge concerning the qualified and the unqualified Brahman and who are devoid of discrimination—to have gone to ruin.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But those who criticize My teaching and do not practice it, deprived of all knowledge and lacking discernment, know them to be doomed to destruction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But those who, finding fault, do not follow this doctrine of Mine - be sure that these men are highly deluded in all branches of knowledge and are lost and brainless.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But they who ridicule My word and do not observe it, are ignorant, devoid of wisdom, and blind. They seek only their own destruction.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.33।। सम्पूर्ण प्राणी प्रकृतिको प्राप्त होते हैं। ज्ञानी महापुरुष भी अपनी प्रकृतिके अनुसार चेष्टा करता है। फिर इसमें किसीका हठ क्या करेगा?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.33।। ज्ञानवान् पुरुष भी अपनी प्रकृति के अनुसार चेष्टा करता है। सभी प्राणी अपनी प्रकृति पर ही जाते हैं, फिर इनमें (किसी का) निग्रह क्या करेगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Even the man of knowledge acts according to his nature; all beings follow their own nature. What will repression accomplish?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even a man of wisdom behaves according to his own nature. They follow their nature. What can restraint do?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Even a wise man acts in accordance with his own nature; beings will follow their nature; what can restraint do?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Even a man of knowledge acts in accordance with his own Prakriti; the elements return to Prakriti; what will restraint avail?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even the wise man acts in accordance with his nature; indeed, all creatures act according to their natures. What is the use of compulsion then?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.34।। इन्द्रिय-इन्द्रियके अर्थमें (प्रत्येक इन्द्रियके प्रत्येक विषयमें) मनुष्यके राग और द्वेष व्यवस्थासे (अनुकूलता और प्रतिकूलताको लेकर) स्थित हैं। मनुष्यको उन दोनोंके वशमें नहीं होना चाहिये; क्योंकि वे दोनों ही इसके (पारमार्थिक मार्गमें विघ्न डालनेवाले) शत्रु हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.34।। इन्द्रियइन्द्रिय (अर्थात् प्रत्येक इन्द्रिय) के विषय के प्रति (मन में) रागद्वेष रहते हैं;  मनुष्य को चाहिये कि वह उन दोनों के वश में न हो;  क्योंकि वे इसके (मनुष्य के) शत्रु हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Each sense has a fixed attachment to and aversion for its corresponding object, but one should not come under their sway, for they are enemies.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Attraction and repulsion are ordained with respect to the objects of all the organs. One should not be swayed by these two, as they are adversaries.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Attachment and aversion for the objects of the senses abide in the senses; let no one come under their sway; for, they are his enemies.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For a man of worldly life, there are clearly fixed likes and dislikes with regard to the objects of each of his sense organs. These are the obstacles for him; the wise would not come under their control.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The love and hate that are aroused by the objects of sense arise from Nature; do not yield to them, as they only obstruct the path.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.35।। अच्छी तरह आचरणमें लाये हुए दूसरेके धर्मसे गुणोंकी कमीवाला अपना धर्म श्रेष्ठ है। अपने धर्ममें तो मरना भी कल्याणकारक है और दूसरेका धर्म भयको देनेवाला है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.35।। सम्यक् प्रकार से अनुष्ठित परधर्म की अपेक्षा गुणरहित स्वधर्म का पालन श्रेयष्कर है;  स्वधर्म में मरण कल्याणकारक है (किन्तु) परधर्म भय को देने वाला है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Better is one's own duty, even if done poorly, than the duty of another done well. It is better to die while performing one's own duty; the duty of another is full of fear.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One's own duty, though defective, is superior to another's duty well-performed. Death is better while engaged in one's own duty; another's duty is full of fear.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Better is one's own duty, though devoid of merit, than the duty of another well discharged. Better is death in one's own duty; the duty of another is fraught with fear.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Better is one's own duty, even if it lacks merit, than the well-performed duty of another; it is better to suffer ruin in one's own duty than to gain success in another's.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is better to do one's own duty, however lacking in merit, than to do that of another, even though efficiently. It is better to die doing one's own duty, for doing the duty of another is fraught with danger.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.36।। अर्जुन बोले - हे वार्ष्णेय ! फिर यह मनुष्य न चाहता हुआ भी जबर्दस्ती लगाये हुएकी तरह किससे प्रेरित होकर पापका आचरण करता है?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.36।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- हे वार्ष्णेय ! फिर यह पुरुष बलपूर्वक बाध्य किये हुये के समान अनिच्छा होते हुये भी किसके द्वारा प्रेरित होकर पाप का आचरण करता है?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "But, O Krsna, what impels one to commit sin, even against their own will, as if constrained by force?"

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "Now then, O scion of the Vrsni dynasty (Krsna), what compels this man to commit sin even against his will, as if he were constrained by force?"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "But what compels man to commit sin, even against his wishes, O Varshneya (Krishna), as if constrained by force?"

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "Then, what induces this person of the world to commit sin, even though they do not desire it, as if they are being overpowered by a force?"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna asked, "My Lord, what is it that drives a person to sin, even against their will and as if by compulsion?"

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.37।। श्रीभगवान् बोले - रजोगुणसे उत्पन्न हुआ  यह काम ही क्रोध  है। यह बहुत खानेवाला और महापापी है। इस विषयमें तू इसको ही वैरी जान।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.37।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -- रजोगुण में उत्पन्न हुई यह 'कामना' है,  यही क्रोध है; यह महाशना (जिसकी भूख बड़ी हो) और महापापी है, इसे ही तुम यहाँ (इस जगत् में) शत्रु जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "It is desire, it is wrath, born of the guna of rajas; it is a great devourer, an impeller of sin. Know this to be the enemy here."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "This desire, this anger, born of the quality of rajas, is a great devourer, a great source of sin. Know this to be the enemy here."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "It is desire and it is anger, both of the quality of Rajas, all-devouring and all-sinful; know this as the foe here in this world."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "This desire, this wrath, born of the Rajas-strand, is a swallower of festivity and a mighty bestower of sins. Know this to be the enemy here."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna: It is desire, it is aversion, born of passion. Desire consumes and corrupts all things. It is the greatest enemy of man.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.38।। जैसे धुएँसे अग्नि और मैलसे दर्पण ढक जाता है तथा जैसे जेरसे गर्भ ढका रहता है, ऐसे ही उस कामके द्वारा यह ज्ञान ( विवेक) ढका हुआ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.38।। जैसे धुयें से अग्नि और धूलि से दर्पण ढक जाता है तथा जैसे भ्रूण गर्भाशय से ढका रहता है, वैसे उस (काम) के द्वारा यह (ज्ञान) आवृत होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: As a fire is enveloped by smoke, as a mirror is covered by dust, and as an embryo is encased in the amniotic sac, so is this world enveloped by desire.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: As fire is enveloped by smoke, as a mirror is obscured by dirt, and as a fetus remains enclosed in the womb, so is this shrouded by that.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: As fire is enveloped by smoke, as a mirror is covered by dust, and as an embryo is surrounded by the amniotic sac, so is this enveloped by that.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: As fire is concealed by smoke, a mirror by dirt, and an embryo by its membrane-cover, so He is concealed by this foe.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As fire is shrouded by smoke, a mirror is covered by dust, and a child is enveloped by the womb, so is the universe enveloped in desire.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.39।। और हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! इस अग्निके समान कभी तृप्त न होनेवाले और विवेकियोंके नित्य वैरी इस कामके द्वारा मनुष्यका विवेक ढका हुआ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.39।। हे कौन्तेय ! अग्नि के समान जिसको तृप्त करना कठिन है ऐसे कामरूप,  ज्ञानी के इस नित्य शत्रु द्वारा ज्ञान आवृत है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The knowledge of the intelligent self is enveloped by this constant enemy, O Arjuna, which is of the nature of desire and is difficult to gratify and insatiable.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Kunti, knowledge is covered by this constant enemy of the wise, in the form of desire, which is an insatiable fire.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: O Arjuna, wisdom is enveloped by this constant enemy of the wise, in the form of desire, which is insatiable like fire.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Kunti! The knowledge of the wise is concealed by this eternal foe, which appears desirable yet is insatiable like fire.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is the wise man's constant enemy; it tarnishes the face of wisdom, being as insatiable as a flame of fire.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.40।। इन्द्रियाँ, मन और बुद्धि इस कामके वास-स्थान कहे गये हैं। यह काम इन- (इन्द्रियाँ, मन और बुद्धि-) के द्वारा ज्ञानको ढककर देहाभिमानी मनुष्यको मोहित करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.40।। इन्द्रियाँ,  मन और बुद्धि इसके निवास स्थान कहे जाते हैं;  यह काम इनके द्वारा ही ज्ञान को आच्छादित करके देही पुरुष को मोहित करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The senses, the mind, and the intellect are said to be its instruments; by these, it overpowers the embodied self, enveloping its knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The organs, mind, and intellect are said to be its abode. This one diversely deludes the embodied being, veiling Knowledge with the help of these.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The senses, the mind, and the intellect are said to be its seat; through these, it deludes the embodied one, veiling their wisdom.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: It is said to be based on the sense-organs, the mind, and the intellect. With these, it deludes the embodied by concealing knowledge.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It works through the senses, the mind, and the reason; and with their help, it destroys wisdom and confounds the soul.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.41।। इसलिये हे भरतवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! तू सबसे पहले इन्द्रियोंको वशमें करके इस ज्ञान और विज्ञानका नाश करनेवाले महान् पापी कामको अवश्य ही बलपूर्वक मार डाल।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.41।। इसलिये, हे अर्जुन ! तुम पहले इन्द्रियों को वश में करके, ज्ञान और विज्ञान के नाशक इस कामरूप पापी को नष्ट करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Therefore, O Arjuna, control your senses from the outset and slay this sinful thing that destroys both knowledge and discernment.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Therefore, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, after first controlling the organs, completely renounce this one which is sinful and a destroyer of learning and wisdom.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Therefore, O best of the Bharatas, control your senses first and then kill this sinful thing, which destroys knowledge and realization.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Therefore, O best among the Bharatas, you must avoid this sinful one, destroying knowledge and action, by controlling completely the sense-organs in the beginning itself.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Therefore, O Arjuna, first control your senses and then slay desire, for it is full of sin and is the destroyer of knowledge and wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.42 -- 3.43।। इन्द्रियोंको (स्थूलशरीरसे) पर (श्रेष्ठ, सबल, प्रकाशक, व्यापक तथा सूक्ष्म) कहते हैं। इन्द्रियोंसे पर मन है, मनसे भी पर बुद्धि है औऱ जो बुद्धिसे भी पर है, वह (काम) है। इस तरह बुद्धिसे पर - (काम-) को जानकर अपने द्वारा अपने-आपको वशमें करके हे महाबाहो ! तू इस कामरूप दुर्जय शत्रुको मार डाल।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.42।। (शरीर से) परे (श्रेष्ठ) इन्द्रियाँ कही जाती हैं;  इन्द्रियों से परे मन है और मन से परे बुद्धि है, और जो बुद्धि से भी परे है, वह है आत्मा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: They say that the senses are high, the mind is higher than the senses, the intellect is higher than the mind, but what is greater than the intellect is that (desire).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: They say that the organs are superior to the gross body; the mind is superior to the organs; and the intellect is superior to the mind. However, He is superior to the intellect.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: They say that the senses are superior to the body; the mind is superior to the senses; the intellect is superior to the mind; and He (the Self) is superior even to the intellect.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: It is said that the sense-organs are different from their objects; the mind is different from the sense-organs; the intellect is different from the mind; and That (Self) is different from the intellect.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is said that the senses are powerful; however, beyond the senses is the mind, beyond the mind is the intellect, and greater than the intellect is He.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।3.42 -- 3.43।। इन्द्रियोंको (स्थूलशरीरसे) पर (श्रेष्ठ, सबल, प्रकाशक, व्यापक तथा सूक्ष्म) कहते हैं। इन्द्रियोंसे पर मन है, मनसे भी पर बुद्धि है औऱ जो बुद्धिसे भी पर है वह (काम) है। इस तरह बुद्धिसे पर - (काम-) को जानकर अपने द्वारा अपने-आपको वशमें करके हे महाबाहो ! तू इस कामरूप दुर्जय शत्रुको मार डाल।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।3.43।। इस प्रकार बुद्धि से परे (शुद्ध) आत्मा को जानकर आत्मा (बुद्धि) के द्वारा आत्मा (मन) को वश में करके, हे महाबाहो ! तुम इस दुर्जेय (दुरासदम्) कामरूप शत्रु को मारो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus, knowing that which is higher than the intellect and fixing the mind with the help of the intellect in Karma Yoga, O Arjuna, slay this enemy which takes the form of desire, and which is difficult to overcome.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: [The Ast, introduces this verse with, 'Tatah kim, what follows from that?'-Tr.] Understanding the Self thus as superior to the intellect, and completely establishing (the Self) through spiritual absorption with the (help of) the mind, O mighty-armed one, the enemy in the form of desire, which is difficult to subdue, vanishes.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Thus, knowing Him who is superior to the intellect and restraining the self by the Self, slay thou, O mighty-armed Arjuna, the enemy in the form of desire, hard to conquer.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, being conscious that it is different from the intellect, and steadying the self with the self, kill the foe that is of the form of desire and that is hard to approach.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thus, O Mighty-Armed One, knowing Him to be beyond the intellect, and with His help, subdue your personal ego and kill your enemy, Desire, even though it is extremely difficult.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.1।। श्रीभगवान् बोले - मैंने इस अविनाशी योगको सूर्यसे कहा था। फिर सूर्यने (अपने पुत्र) वैवस्वत मनुसे कहा और मनुने (अपने पुत्र) राजा इक्ष्वाकुसे कहा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.1।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा ---  मैंने इस अविनाशी योग को विवस्वान् (सूर्य देवता) से कहा (सिखाया);  विवस्वान् ने मनु से कहा;  मनु ने इक्ष्वाकु से कहा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "I taught this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan; Vivasvan taught it to Manu; Manu declared it to Iksvaku."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "I imparted this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan, who then taught it to Manu, who in turn transmitted it to Iksavaku.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "I taught this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan; he then told it to Manu; Manu proclaimed it to Ikshvaku.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavad said, "This changeless Yoga I had properly taught thus to Vivasvat; Vivasvat correctly told it to Manu; and Manu declared it to Iksvaku."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna said: I taught this imperishable philosophy to Vivaswān, the founder of the Sun dynasty; Vivaswān gave it to Manu, the lawgiver, and Manu to King Ikshvāku.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.2।। हे परंतप ! इस तरह परम्परासे प्राप्त इस कर्मयोग को राजर्षियोंने जाना। परन्तु बहुत समय बीत जानेके कारण वह योग इस मनुष्यलोकमें लुप्तप्राय हो गया।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.2।। इस प्रकार परम्परा से प्राप्त हुये इस योग को राजर्षियों ने जाना, (परन्तु) हे परन्तप ! वह योग बहुत काल (के अन्तराल) से यहाँ (इस लोक में) नष्टप्राय हो गया।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus handed down in succession, the royal sages knew this (Karma Yoga). But with a long lapse of time, O Arjuna, that Yoga was lost to the world.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The king-sages knew this yoga, which was received in regular succession. That yoga, O destroyer of foes, is now lost due to a long lapse of time.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: This, handed down in regular succession by the royal sages, was known. This Yoga, however, has been lost here over time, O Parantapa (burner of the foes).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, the regal sages knew this, which was received in regular succession. Over time, however, this Yoga has been lost, O scorcher of enemies!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The Divine Kings knew it, for it was their tradition; then, after a long time, it was at last forgotten.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.3।। तू मेरा भक्त और प्रिय सखा है, इसलिये वही यह पुरातन योग आज मैंने तुझसे कहा है; क्योंकि यह बड़ा उत्तम रहस्य है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.3।। वह ही यह पुरातन योग आज मैंने तुम्हें कहा (सिखाया) क्योंकि तुम मेरे भक्त और मित्र हो। यह उत्तम रहस्य है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: It is the same ancient Yoga that I am now teaching you, for you are My devotee and My friend. For, this is a supreme mystery.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That ancient Yoga itself, which is this, has been taught to you by Me today, considering you to be My devotee and friend, for this Yoga is a profound secret.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That same ancient yoga has been today taught to you by me, for you are my devotee and my friend; it is the supreme secret.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The same ancient Yoga has now been taught to you by Me, on the grounds that you are both My devotee and friend. This is the highest secret.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is the same ancient path that I have now revealed to you, for you are my devotee and my friend. It is the supreme secret.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.4।। अर्जुन बोले - आपका जन्म तो अभीका है और सूर्यका जन्म बहुत पुराना है; अतः आपने ही सृष्टिके आदिमें सूर्यसे यह योग कहा था - यह बात मैं कैसे समझूँ?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.4।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- आपका जन्म अपर अर्थात् पश्चात का है और विवस्वान् का जन्म (आपके) पूर्व का है, इसलिये यह मैं कैसे जानूँ कि (सृष्टि के) आदि में आपने (इस योग को) कहा था?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "Your birth was later, and Vivasvan's birth was earlier. How then can I understand that you taught it in the beginning?"

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "Your birth was later, whereas Vivasvan's birth was earlier. How am I to understand that You instructed him in the beginning?"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "Later was Thy birth, and prior to it was the birth of Vivasvan (the Sun); how am I to understand that Thou hast taught this Yoga from the beginning?"

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "Your birth is later, while the birth of Vivasvat is earlier; how am I then to understand that You had properly taught him this in the beginning?"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna asked: My Lord! Vyasawana was born before You; how then can You have revealed it to him?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.5।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हे परन्तप अर्जुन ! मेरे और तेरे बहुत-से जन्म हो चुके हैं। उन सबको मैं जानता हूँ, पर तू नहीं जानता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.5।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -- हे अर्जुन ! मेरे और तुम्हारे बहुत से जन्म हो चुके हैं, (परन्तु) हे परन्तप ! उन सबको मैं जानता हूँ और तुम नहीं जानते।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "Many of My births have passed, O Arjuna, as have yours. I know them all, but you do not know them."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "O Arjuna, many lives of Mine have passed, and so have yours. I know them all, but you do not know, O scorcher of enemies!"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "Many births of Mine have passed, as well as of thine, O Arjuna; I know them all, but thou knowest not, O Parantapa (scorcher of foes)."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "O Arjuna, many births of Mine as well as yours have passed. All of them I know, but you do not, O scorcher of foes!"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: I have been born again and again, from time to time; thou too, O Arjuna! My births are known to me, but yours are unknown to you.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.6।। मैं अजन्मा और अविनाशी-स्वरूप होते हुए भी तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका ईश्वर होते हुए भी अपनी प्रकृतिको अधीन करके अपनी योगमायासे प्रकट होता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.6।। यद्यपि मैं अजन्मा और अविनाशी स्वरूप हूँ और भूतमात्र का ईश्वर हूँ (तथापि) अपनी प्रकृति को अपने अधीन रखकर (अधिष्ठाय) मैं अपनी माया से जन्म लेता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Though I am birthless and of immutable nature, and the Lord of all beings, yet by employing My own Nature (Prakrti), I am born out of My own free will.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Though I am birthless and undecaying by nature, and the Lord of beings, yet by subjugating My Prakriti, I take birth through My own Maya.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Though I am unborn and of imperishable nature, and though I am the Lord of all beings, yet, governing my own nature, I am born by my own Maya.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Though I am unborn and changeless Self, though I am the Lord of all beings, yet, presiding over My own nature, I take birth by My own trick of illusion.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There is no beginning to Me. Though I am imperishable and the Lord of all that exists, I manifest Myself by My own will and power.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.7।। हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन! जब-जब धर्मकी हानि और अधर्मकी वृद्धि होती है, तब-तब ही मैं अपने-आपको साकाररूपसे प्रकट करता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.7।। हे भारत ! जब-जब धर्म की हानि और अधर्म की वृद्धि होती है,  तब-तब मैं स्वयं को प्रकट करता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whenever there is a decline of Dharma, O Arjuna, and an uprising of Adharma, then I incarnate myself.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, whenever there is a decline in virtue and an increase in vice, then I manifest Myself.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whenever there is a decline of righteousness and an increase of unrighteousness, O Arjuna, then I manifest Myself.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For, whenever there is a decay of righteousness and the rise of unrighteousness, then, O descendant of Bharata, I send forth that which the Self is unimportant.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Whenever spirituality decays and materialism is rampant, then, O Arjuna, I reincarnate myself.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.8।। साधुओं-(भक्तों-) की रक्षा करनेके लिये, पापकर्म करनेवालोंका विनाश करनेके लिये और धर्मकी भलीभाँति स्थापना करनेके लिये मैं युग-युगमें प्रकट हुआ करता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.8।। साधु पुरुषों के रक्षण,  दुष्कृत्य करने वालों के नाश,  तथा धर्म संस्थापना के लिये,  मैं प्रत्येक युग में प्रगट होता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For the protection of the good and the destruction of the wicked, for the establishment of Dharma, I am born from age to age.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For the protection of the pious, the destruction of the evil-doers, and the establishment of virtue, I manifest myself in every age.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For the protection of the good, for the destruction of the wicked, and for the establishment of righteousness, I am born in every age.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For the protection of the good people, and for the destruction of evildoers, and for the purpose of firmly establishing righteousness, I take birth in every age.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: To protect the righteous, to destroy the wicked, and to establish the kingdom of God, I am reborn from age to age.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.9।। हे अर्जुन ! मेरे जन्म और कर्म दिव्य हैं। इस प्रकार (मेरे जन्म और कर्मको) जो मनुष्य तत्त्वसे जान लेता अर्थात् दृढ़तापूर्वक मान लेता है, वह शरीरका त्याग करके पुनर्जन्मको प्राप्त नहीं होता, प्रत्युत मुझे प्राप्त होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.9।। हे अर्जुन ! मेरा जन्म और कर्म दिव्य है,  इस प्रकार जो पुरुष तत्त्वत:  जानता है, वह शरीर को त्यागकर फिर जन्म को नहीं प्राप्त होता;  वह मुझे ही प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who thus knows in truth My divine birth and actions does not get rebirth after leaving the body; he will come to Me, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who thus knows truly the divine birth and actions of Mine does not get rebirth after casting off the body. He attains Me, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who thus knows, in their true light, My divine birth and actions, having abandoned the body, is not born again; he comes to Me, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever knows correctly the divine birth and action of Mine, he, upon abandoning the body, does not go to rebirth, but goes to Me, O Arjuna!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who realizes the divine truth concerning My birth and life does not take birth again; and when he leaves his body, he becomes one with Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.10।। राग, भय और क्रोधसे सर्वथा रहित, मेरेमें ही तल्लीन, मेरे ही आश्रित तथा ज्ञानरूप तपसे पवित्र हुए बहुत-से भक्त मेरे भाव- (स्वरूप-) को प्राप्त हो चुके हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.10।। राग भय और क्रोध से रहित मनमय मेरे शरण हुए बहुत से पुरुष ज्ञान रुप तप से पवित्र‌ हुए मेरे स्वरुप को प्राप्त हुए हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Freed from desire, fear, and wrath, absorbed in Me, depending upon Me, purified by the austerity of knowledge, many have attained My state.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Many who were devoid of attachment, fear, and anger, who were absorbed in Me, who had taken refuge in Me, and were purified by the austerity of knowledge, have attained My state.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Freed from attachment, fear, and anger, absorbed in Me, taking refuge in Me, purified by the fire of knowledge, many have attained My Being.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Many persons, who are free from passion, fear, and anger; are full of Me; take refuge in Me; and have become pure through the austerity of wisdom—they have come to My being.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Many have merged their existences in Mine, being freed from desire, fear, and anger, always filled with Me and purified by the illuminating flame of self-abnegation.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.11।। हे पृथानन्दन ! जो भक्त जिस प्रकार मेरी शरण लेते हैं, मैं उन्हें उसी प्रकार आश्रय देता हूँ; क्योंकि सभी मनुष्य सब प्रकारसे मेरे मार्गका अनुकरण करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.11।। जो मुझे जैसे भजते हैं,  मैं उन पर वैसे ही अनुग्रह करता हूँ;  हे पार्थ सभी मनुष्य सब प्रकार से, मेरे ही मार्ग का अनुवर्तन करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whoever resorts to Me in any manner, I favor them in the same manner; men experience Me alone in different ways, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: According to the manner in which they approach Me, I favor them in that same manner. O son of Partha, humans follow My path in every way.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: In whatever way men approach Me, even so do I reward them; My path do men tread in all ways, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The way in which people resort to Me, in the same way I favor them. O son of Prtha, all kinds of people follow My path.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: However men try to worship Me, I welcome them. Whatever path they take, it leads to Me in the end.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.12।। कर्मोंकी सिद्धि (फल) चाहनेवाले मनुष्य देवताओंकी उपासना किया करते हैं; क्योंकि इस मनुष्यलोकमें कर्मोंसे उत्पन्न होनेवाली सिद्धि जल्दी मिल जाती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.12।। (सामान्य मनुष्य) यहाँ (इस लोक में) कर्मों के फल को चाहते हुये देवताओं को पूजते हैं;  क्योंकि मनुष्य लोक में कर्मों के फल शीघ्र ही प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those who desire the fruits of their ritualistic acts, sacrifice to the gods here; for, success quickly accrues in the world of men.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Longing for the fruition of their actions (of their rites and duties), they worship the gods here. For, in the human world, success from action comes quickly.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Those who long for success in action in this world sacrifice to the gods; for success is quickly attained by men through action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those who desire success in their actions perform sacrifices, intending them for the deities. For, the success born of ritualistic actions is quick in the world of men.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Those who seek success worship the Powers, and their actions in this world bear immediate fruit.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.13 -- 4.14।। मेरे द्वारा गुणों और कर्मोंके विभागपूर्वक चारों वर्णोंकी रचना की गयी है। उस-(सृष्टि-रचना आदि-) का कर्ता होनेपर भी मुझ अव्यय रमेश्वरको तू अकर्ता जान। कारण कि कर्मोंके फलमें मेरी स्पृहा नहीं है, इसलिये मुझे कर्म लिप्त नहीं करते। इस प्रकार जो मुझे तत्त्वसे जान लेता है, वह भी कर्मोंसे नहीं बँधता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.13।। गुण और कर्मों के विभाग से चातुर्वण्य मेरे द्वारा रचा गया है। यद्यपि मैं उसका कर्ता हूँ, तथापि तुम मुझे अकर्ता और अविनाशी जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The system of four stations was created by Me, according to the distinction of Gunas and Karma. Though I am their creator, know Me to be non-agent and immutable.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The four castes have been created by Me through a classification of the gunas and duties. Even though I am the agent of that (act of classification), still know Me to be non-agentive and changeless.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The fourfold caste has been created by Me according to the differentiation of Guna and Karma; though I am the author of it, know Me as non-doer and immutable.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I have created the four-fold caste structure according to the division of their respective qualities and actions. Though I am the creator of this, know Me as the changeless, non-creator.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The four divisions of society—the wise, the soldier, the merchant, and the laborer—were created by Me, according to the natural distribution of qualities and instincts. I am the author of them, though I Myself do not act and am changeless.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.13 -- 4.14।। मेरे द्वारा गुणों और कर्मोंके विभागपूर्वक चारों वर्णोंकी रचना की गयी है। उस-(सृष्टि-रचना आदि-) का कर्ता होनेपर भी मुझ अव्यय परमेश्वरको तू अकर्ता जान। कारण कि कर्मोंके फलमें मेरी स्पृहा नहीं है, इसलिये मुझे कर्म लिप्त नहीं करते। इस प्रकार जो मुझे तत्त्वसे जान लेता है, वह भी कर्मोंसे नहीं बँधता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.14।। कर्म मुझे लिप्त नहीं करते;  न मुझे कर्मफल में स्पृहा है। इस प्रकार मुझे जो जानता है, वह भी कर्मों से नहीं बन्धता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Works cannot contaminate Me. There is no desire for the fruits of actions in Me. He who understands this is not bound by actions.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Actions do not taint Me; for I have no hankering for the results of actions. One who knows Me thus, does not become bound by their actions.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Actions do not taint Me, nor do I have a desire for the fruit of actions. He who knows Me thus is not bound by actions.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Actions do not stain me, nor do I have a desire for the fruits of them either. Whoever understands me in this way is not bound by their actions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: My actions do not bind Me, nor do I desire anything that they can bring. He who thus realizes Me is not enslaved by action.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.15।। पूर्वकालके मुमुक्षुओंने भी इस प्रकार जानकर कर्म किये हैं, इसलिये तू भी पूर्वजोंके द्वारा सदासे किये जानेवाले कर्मोंको ही (उन्हींकी तरह) कर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.15।। पूर्व के मुमुक्ष पुरुषों द्वारा भी इस प्रकार जानकर ही कर्म किया गया है;  इसलिये तुम भी पूर्वजों द्वारा सदा से किये हुए कर्मों को ही करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Knowing thus, even ancient seekers of liberation did their work. Therefore, do your work only as the ancients did in olden times.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Thus having known, even the ancient seekers of liberation performed their duties. Therefore, you should undertake action as was performed earlier by the ancients.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having known this, the ancient seekers of freedom also performed action; therefore, do thou also perform action, as the ancients did in days of yore.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Realizing in this fashion, ancient seekers of salvation also undertook action. Hence, you too should, by all means, perform the action that was performed by the ancients.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: In the light of wisdom, our ancestors who sought deliverance performed their acts. Act thou also as our fathers of old did.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.16।। कर्म क्या है और अकर्म क्या है -- इस प्रकार इस विषयमें विद्वान् भी मोहित हो जाते हैं। अतः वह कर्म-तत्त्व मैं तुम्हें भलीभाँति कहूँगा, जिसको जानकर तू अशुभ- (संसार-बन्धन-) से मुक्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.16।। कर्म क्या है और अकर्म क्या है? इस विषय में बुद्धिमान पुरुष भी भ्रमित हो जाते हैं। इसलिये मैं तुम्हें कर्म कहूँगा,  (अर्थात् कर्म और अकर्म का स्वरूप समझाऊँगा) जिसको जानकर तुम अशुभ (संसार बन्धन) से मुक्त हो जाओगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: What is action? What is non-action? Even the wise are puzzled in respect of these. I shall declare to you that kind of action, knowing which you will be freed from evil.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even the intelligent are confounded as to what is action and what is inaction. I shall tell you of that action, knowing which you will become free from evil.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: What is action? What is inaction? Even the wise are confused about this. Therefore, I shall teach you the nature of action and inaction, by knowing which you will be liberated from the evil of Samsara, the wheel of birth and death.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Even the wise are perplexed about what is action and what is non-action; I shall properly teach you the action, by knowing which you will be freed from evil.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: What is action and what is inaction? This is a question that has bewildered the wise. But I will declare to you the philosophy of action, and knowing it, you will be free from evil.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.17।। कर्मका तत्त्व भी जानना चाहिये और अकर्मका तत्त्व भी जानना चाहिये तथा विकर्मका तत्त्व भी जानना चाहिये; क्योंकि कर्मकी गति गहन है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.17।। कर्म का (स्वरूप) जानना चाहिये और विकर्म का (स्वरूप) भी जानना चाहिये ; (बोद्धव्यम्) तथा अकर्म का भी (स्वरूप) जानना चाहिये (क्योंकि) कर्म की गति गहन है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For, there is what ought to be known in action; likewise, there is what ought to be known in multi-form action; and there is what ought to be understood in non-action. Thus, mysterious is the way of action.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For there is something to be known even about action, and something to be known about prohibited action; and something needs to be known about inaction. The true nature of action is inscrutable.

author: Swami Sivananda
