text: For verily, the true nature of action enjoined by the scriptures should be known, as well as that of forbidden or unlawful action, and of inaction; the nature of action is hard to understand.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Something has to be understood about good action; something has to be understood about wrong action; and something has to be understood about non-action. It is difficult to comprehend the way of action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is necessary to consider what is right action, what is wrong action, and what is inaction, for the law of action is mysterious.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.18।। जो मनुष्य कर्ममें अकर्म देखता है और जो अकर्ममें कर्म देखता है, वह मनुष्योंमें बुद्धिमान् है,  योगी है और सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंको करनेवाला  है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.18।। जो पुरुष कर्म में अकर्म और अकर्म में कर्म देखता है,  वह मनुष्यों में बुद्धिमान है,  वह योगी सम्पूर्ण कर्मों को करने वाला है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who sees inaction in action and also action in inaction is wise among men. He is fit for release and has accomplished all actions.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who finds inaction in action, and action in inaction, is the wise one [possessed of the knowledge of Brahman] among men; they are engaged in yoga and are performers of all actions!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who sees inaction in action and action in inaction, he is wise among men; he is a yogi and performer of all actions.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who finds non-action in action, and action in non-action, is an intelligent one among men and is said to be a performer or destroyer of all actions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who can see inaction in action, and action in inaction, is the wisest among men. He is a saint, even though he still acts.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.19।। जिसके सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंके आरम्भ संकल्प और कामनासे रहित हैं तथा जिसके सम्पूर्ण कर्म ज्ञानरूपी अग्निसे जल गये हैं, उसको ज्ञानिजन भी पण्डित (बुद्धिमान्) कहते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.19।। जिसके समस्त कार्य कामना और संकल्प से रहित हैं,  ऐसे उस ज्ञानरूप अग्नि के द्वारा भस्म हुये कर्मों वाले पुरुष को ज्ञानीजन पण्डित कहते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He whose every undertaking is free from desire and delusive identification (of the body with the self), whose actions are burned up in the fire of knowledge—the wise describe him as a sage.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The wise call him learned whose actions are all devoid of desires and their thoughts, and whose actions have been burned away by the fire of wisdom.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He whose undertakings are all devoid of desires and selfish purposes, and whose actions have been burned by the fire of knowledge, the wise call him a sage.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He whose every exertion is devoid of intention for desirable objects, and whose actions are burnt up by the fire of wisdom—the wise call such a person a man of learning.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The wise call him a sage, for whatever he undertakes is free from the motive of desire, and his deeds are purified by the fire of wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.20।। जो कर्म और फलकी आसक्तिका त्याग करके आश्रयसे रहित और सदा तृप्त है, वह कर्मोंमें अच्छी तरह लगा हुआ भी वास्तवमें कुछ भी नहीं करता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.20।। जो पुरुष,  कर्मफलासक्ति को त्यागकर,  नित्यतृप्त और सब आश्रयों से रहित है वह कर्म में प्रवृत्त होते हुए भी (वास्तव में) कुछ भी नहीं करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Having renounced attachment to the fruits of their actions, ever contented with the eternal self, and dependent on none, one does not act at all, even while being engaged in action.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Having given up attachment to the results of action, he who is ever-content, dependent on nothing, does not really do anything even though he is engaged in action.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having abandoned attachment to the fruits of the action, ever content, depending on nothing, he does not do anything even while being engaged in activity.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: By abandoning attachment to the fruits of actions, remaining ever content and depending on nothing, that person, even though they are engaged in action, does not perform anything at all.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having surrendered all claim to the results of his actions, he is always contented and independent; in reality, he does nothing, even though he appears to be acting.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.21।। जिसका शरीर और अन्तःकरण अच्छी तरहसे वशमें किया हुआ है, जिसने सब प्रकारके संग्रहका परित्याग कर दिया है, ऐसा आशारहित कर्मयोगी केवल शरीर-सम्बन्धी कर्म करता हुआ भी पापको प्राप्त नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.21।। जो आशा रहित है तथा जिसने चित्त और आत्मा (शरीर) को संयमित किया है,  जिसने सब परिग्रहों का त्याग किया है,  ऐसा पुरुष शारीरिक कर्म करते हुए भी पाप को नहीं प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Free from desire, his intellect and mind controlled, having given up all possessions, and doing bodily work only, he is not subject to evil.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who is without solicitation, who has the mind and senses under control, and is totally without possessions, incurs no sin by performing actions merely for the maintenance of the body.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Without hope, controlling the mind and the self, having abandoned all covetousness, and performing only bodily actions, one incurs no sin.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having rid of cravings, controlling the mind and body, abandoning all sense of possession, and performing actions with the body alone, one does not incur any sin.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Expecting nothing, his mind and personality controlled, without greed, doing only bodily actions; though he acts, he remains untainted.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.22।। जो (कर्मयोगी) फल की इच्छा के बिना, अपने-आप जो कुछ मिल जाय, उसमें सन्तुष्ट रहता है और जो ईर्ष्यासे रहित, द्वन्द्वोंसे अतीत तथा सिद्धि और असिद्धिमें सम है, वह कर्म करते हुए भी उससे नहीं बँधता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.22।। यदृच्छया (अपने आप) जो कुछ प्राप्त हो उसमें ही सन्तुष्ट रहने वाला,  द्वन्द्वों से अतीत तथा मत्सर से रहित,  सिद्धि व असिद्धि में समभाव वाला पुरुष कर्म करके भी नहीं बन्धता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Content with what chance may bring, rising above the pairs of opposites, free from ill-will, even-minded in success and failure, he acts, yet is not bound.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Remaining satisfied with what comes unasked for, having transcended the dualities, being free from spite, and balanced under success and failure, he is not bound even by performing actions.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Content with what comes to him without effort, free from the pairs of opposites and envy, even-minded in success and failure, he acts yet is not bound.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Remaining content with the gain brought by chance, transcending the dualities (pairs of opposites), entertaining no jealousy, and remaining equal in success and in failure, he does not get bound, even when he acts.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Content with what comes to him without any effort of his own, rising above the pairs of opposites, free from envy, his mind balanced in both success and failure; though he acts, the consequences do not bind him.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.23।। जिसकी आसक्ति सर्वथा मिट गयी है, जो मुक्त हो गया है, जिसकी बुद्धि स्वरूपके ज्ञानमें स्थित है, ऐसे केवल यज्ञके लिये कर्म करनेवाले मनुष्यके सम्पूर्ण कर्म विलीन हो जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.23।। जो आसक्तिरहित और मुक्त है,  जिसका चित्त ज्ञान में स्थित है,  यज्ञ के लिये आचरण करने वाले ऐसे पुरुष के समस्त कर्म लीन हो जाते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: One whose attachments have been relinquished, who is liberated, whose mind is established in knowledge, and who works only for sacrifices—their Karma is entirely dissolved.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The liberated person, who has rid themselves of attachment and whose mind is fixed in knowledge, has their actions undertaken for a sacrifice completely destroyed.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: To one who is devoid of attachment, who is liberated, whose mind is established in knowledge, and who works for the sake of sacrifice (for the sake of God), the whole action is dissolved.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The action is completely dissolved in the case of the person who undertakes it for the sake of sacrifice; who is free from attachment and has been liberated; and whose mind is fixed in wisdom.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who is without attachment, free, his mind centered in wisdom, and his actions done as a sacrifice, leave no trace behind.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.24।। जिस यज्ञमें अर्पण भी ब्रह्म है, हवी भी ब्रह्म है और ब्रह्मरूप कर्ताके द्वारा ब्रह्मरूप अग्निमें आहुति देनारूप क्रिया भी ब्रह्म है, (ऐसे यज्ञको करनेवाले) जिस मनुष्यकी ब्रह्ममें ही कर्म-समाधि हो गयी है, उसके द्वारा प्राप्त करनेयोग्य फल भी ब्रह्म ही है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.24।। अर्पण (अर्थात् अर्पण करने का साधन श्रुवा) ब्रह्म है और हवि (शाकल्य अथवा हवन करने योग्य द्रव्य) भी ब्रह्म है;  ब्रह्मरूप अग्नि में ब्रह्मरूप कर्ता के द्वारा जो हवन किया गया है,  वह भी ब्रह्म ही है। इस प्रकार ब्रह्मरूप कर्म में समाधिस्थ पुरुष का गन्तव्य भी ब्रह्म ही है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Brahman is the instrument with which to offer; Brahman is the oblation. By Brahman is the oblation offered into the fire of Brahman; Brahman alone is to be attained by one who meditates on Him in their works.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The ladle is Brahman, the oblations are Brahman, and the offering is poured by Brahman into the fire of Brahman. He who has concentration on Brahman as the objective can only reach Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Brahman is the oblation; Brahman is the melted butter (ghee); by Brahman is the oblation poured into the fire of Brahman; Brahman indeed shall be attained by one who always sees Brahman in action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Brahman-oblation that is to be offered to the Brahman is poured into the Brahman-fire by the Brahman; it is nothing but the Brahman that is to be attained by one whose deep contemplation is the Brahman-action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: For him, the sacrifice is the Spirit; the Spirit and the offering are one; it is the Spirit that is sacrificed in its own fire, and the man, even in action, is united with God, since his mind never ceases to be fixed on Him while performing the act.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.25।। अन्य योगीलोग भगवदर्पणरूप यज्ञका ही अनुष्ठान करते हैं और दूसरे योगीलोग ब्रह्मरूप अग्निमें विचाररूप यज्ञके द्वारा ही जीवात्मारूप यज्ञका हवन करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.25।। कोई योगीजन देवताओं के पूजनरूप यज्ञ को ही करते हैं ; और दूसरे (ज्ञानीजन) ब्रह्मरूप अग्नि में यज्ञ के द्वारा यज्ञ को हवन करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Some yogis resort only to the sacrifice relating to gods; others offer sacrifice into the fire of Brahman solely through sacrifice.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Other yogis undertake sacrifice to gods alone, while others offer the Self as a sacrifice by themselves, in the fire of Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Some yogis perform sacrifice to the gods alone; while others, who have realized the Self, offer the Self as sacrifice in the fire of Brahman alone.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Certain other men of Yoga are completely devoted to yajna, connected with the devas, and offer that yajna, simply as a yajna, into the insatiable fire of the Brahman.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Some sages sacrifice to the powers, while others offer themselves on the altar of the eternal.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.26।। अन्य योगीलोग श्रोत्रादि समस्त इन्द्रियोंका संयमरूप अग्नियोंमें हवन किया करते हैं और दूसरे योगीलोग शब्दादि विषयोंका इन्द्रियरूप अग्नियोंमें हवन किया करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.26।। अन्य (योगीजन) श्रोत्रादिक सब इन्द्रियों को संयमरूप अग्नि में हवन करते हैं,  और अन्य (लोग) शब्दादिक विषयों को इन्द्रियरूप अग्नि में हवन करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Others offer as oblations hearing and other senses into the fires of restraint. Some others offer as oblations the objects of the senses, such as sound and the rest, into the fires of their senses.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Others offer their organs, such as their ears, etc., in the fires of self-control. Others offer the objects, such as sound, etc., in the fires of their organs.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Some again offer the organ of hearing and other senses as a sacrifice in the fire of restraint; others offer sound and other objects of the senses as a sacrifice in the fire of the senses.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Others offer their sense-organs such as the sense of hearing and the rest into the fires of restraint; others offer objects such as sound and the rest into the fires of their sense-organs.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Some sacrifice their physical senses in the fire of self-control; others offer up their contact with external objects in the sacrificial fire of their senses.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.27।। अन्य योगीलोग सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियोंकी क्रियाओंको और प्राणोंकी क्रियाओंको ज्ञानसे प्रकाशित आत्मसंयमयोगरूप अग्निमें हवन किया करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.27।। दूसरे (योगीजन) सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियों के तथा प्राणों के कर्मों को ज्ञान से प्रकाशित आत्मसंयमयोगरूप अग्नि में हवन करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Some again offer as an oblation the functions of the senses and the activity of the vital breaths into the fire of the Yoga of restraint of the mind, kindled by knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Others offer all the activities of the organs and the activities of the vital force into the fire of the yoga of self-control, lit by Knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Others again sacrifice all the functions of the senses and those of the breath (vital energy, or Prana) in the fire of the Yoga of self-restraint, kindled by knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Some others offer all the actions of their sense-organs and the actions of their life-breath into the fire of the Yoga of self-control, ignited by wisdom.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Others again sacrifice their activities and vitality in the spiritual fire of self-abnegation, kindled by wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.28।। दूसरे कितने ही तीक्ष्ण व्रत करनेवाले प्रयत्नशील साधक द्रव्य-सम्बन्धी यज्ञ करनेवाले हैं, और कितने ही तपोयज्ञ करनेवाले हैं, और दूसरे कितने ही योगयज्ञ करनेवाले हैं, तथा कितने ही स्वाध्यायरूप ज्ञानयज्ञ करनेवाले हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.28।। कुछ (साधक) द्रव्ययज्ञ, तपयज्ञ और योगयज्ञ करने वाले होते हैं;  और दूसरे कठिन व्रत करने वाले स्वाध्याय और ज्ञानयज्ञ करने वाले योगीजन होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Self-controlled and firm of resolve, others perform the sacrifice of material objects or austerities or yoga; while others offer their scriptural study and knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Similarly, others are performers of sacrifices through wealth, through austerity, through yoga, and through study and knowledge; others are ascetics with severe vows.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Others again offer wealth, austerity, and Yoga as sacrifice, while ascetics of self-restraint and rigid vows offer the study of scriptures and knowledge as sacrifice.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: These are the performers of sacrifices with material objects, the performers of sacrifices with penance, and the performers of sacrifices with Yoga. Likewise, there are other ascetics with rigid vows whose sacrifices are the svadhyaya-knowledge.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Others offer as their sacrifice wealth, austerities, and meditation. Monks, wedded to their vows, renounce their scriptural learning and even their spiritual powers.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.29 -- 4.30।। दूसरे कितने ही प्राणायामके परायण हुए योगीलोग अपानमें प्राणका पूरक करके, प्राण और अपानकी गति रोककर फिर प्राणमें अपानका हवन करते हैं; तथा अन्य कितने ही नियमित आहार करनेवाले प्राणोंका प्राणोंमें हवन किया करते हैं। ये सभी साधक यज्ञोंद्वारा पापोंका नाश करनेवाले और यज्ञोंको जाननेवाले हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.29।। अन्य (योगीजन) अपानवायु में प्राणवायु को हवन करते हैं,  तथा प्राण में अपान की आहुति देते हैं,  प्राण और अपान की गति को रोककर,  वे प्राणायाम के ही समलक्ष्य समझने वाले होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Others, with restricted diets, are devoted to the control of breath. Some sacrifice the inward breath in the outward breath; similarly, others sacrifice the outward breath in the inward breath. Some others, stopping the flow of both the inward and outward breaths, sacrifice the inward and outward breaths.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Constantly practicing control of the vital forces by stopping the movements of the outgoing and incoming breaths, some offer as a sacrifice the outgoing breath into the incoming breath; while still others, the incoming breath into the outgoing breath.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Others offer as sacrifice the outgoing breath into the incoming, and the incoming into the outgoing, restraining the flow of the outgoing and the incoming breaths, solely absorbed in the restraint of the breath.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Some sages offer the prana into the apana; likewise, others offer the apana into the prana. Having controlled both the courses of the prana and apana, the same sages, with their desires fulfilled by the above activities, and with their food restricted, offer the pranas into pranas. All these persons know what sacrifices are and have their sins destroyed by them.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There are some who practice controlling the vital energy and governing the subtle forces of prana and apana, thereby sacrificing their prana to apana, or their apana to prana.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.29 -- 4.30।। दूसरे कितने ही प्राणायामके परायण हुए योगीलोग अपानमें प्राणका पूरक करके, प्राण और अपानकी गति रोककर फिर प्राणमें अपानका हवन करते हैं; तथा अन्य कितने ही नियमित आहार करनेवाले प्राणोंका प्राणोंमें हवन किया करते हैं। ये सभी साधक यज्ञोंद्वारा पापोंका नाश करनेवाले और यज्ञोंको जाननेवाले हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.30।। दूसरे नियमित आहार करने वाले (साधक जन) प्राणों को प्राणों में हवन करते हैं। ये सभी यज्ञ को जानने वाले हैं, जिनके पाप यज्ञ के द्वारा नष्ट हो चुके हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: All these know the meaning of sacrifices, and through sacrifices, their sins are eradicated. Those who subsist on the ambrosial food, the remnants of sacrifices, go to eternal Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Others, having their food regulated, offer the vital forces in the vital forces. All of them are knowers of the sacrifice and have their sins destroyed by the sacrifice.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Others who regulate their diet offer life-breaths in each life-breath. All these are knowers of sacrifice, whose sins are destroyed through sacrifice.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: - . [Some sages] offer the prana into the apana; likewise, others offer the apana into the prana. Having controlled both the courses of the prana and apana, the same sages, with their desires fulfilled by the above activities, and with their food restricted, offer the pranas into pranas. All these persons know what sacrifices are and have their sins destroyed by them.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Others, controlling their diet, sacrifice their worldly life to the spiritual fire. All understand the principle of sacrifice, and by it their sins are washed away.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.31।। हे कुरुवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! यज्ञसे बचे हुए अमृतका अनुभव करनेवाले सनातन परब्रह्म परमात्माको प्राप्त होते हैं। यज्ञ न करनेवाले मनुष्यके लिये यह मनुष्यलोक भी सुखदायक नहीं है, फिर परलोक कैसे सुखदायक होगा?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.31।। हे कुरुश्रेष्ठ ! यज्ञ के अवशिष्ट अमृत को भोगने वाले पुरुष सनातन ब्रह्म को प्राप्त होते हैं। यज्ञ रहित पुरुष को यह लोक भी नहीं मिलता,  फिर परलोक कैसे मिलेगा?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: This world is not for one who makes no sacrifice; how then for the other, O Arjuna?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those who partake of the nectar left over after a sacrifice reach the eternal Brahman. This world ceases to exist for one who does not perform sacrifices; what, then, to speak of the other world, O best among the Kurus (Arjuna)!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Those who eat the remnants of the sacrifice, which are like nectar, go to the eternal Brahman. This world is not for the one who does not perform sacrifice; how then can they have the other, O Arjuna?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The eaters of the sacrifice-ordained nectar attain the eternal Brahman. This world is not for a non-sacrificer; how can there be the other? O best of the Kurus!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Tasting the nectar of immortality, as the reward of sacrifice, they reach the Eternal. This world is not for those who refuse to sacrifice; nor is the other world.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.32।। इस प्रकार और भी बहुत तरहके यज्ञ वेदकी वाणीमें विस्तारसे कहे गये हैं। उन सब यज्ञोंको तू कर्मजन्य जान। इस प्रकार जानकर यज्ञ करनेसे तू (कर्मबन्धनसे) मुक्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.32।। ऐसे अनेक प्रकार के यज्ञों का ब्रह्मा के मुख अर्थात् वेदों में प्रसार है अर्थात् वर्णित हैं। उन सब को कर्मों से उत्पन्न हुए जानो;  इस प्रकार जानकर तुम मुक्त हो जाओगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus, many forms of sacrifices have been spread out as means of reaching Brahman (individual self in its own nature). Know that all of these are born of actions. Knowing thus, you will be free.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Thus, various kinds of sacrifices are spread at the mouth of the Vedas. Know them all to be born of action. Knowing this, you will become liberated.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Thus, manifold sacrifices are spread out before Brahman at the face of Brahman. Know them all to be born of action, and thus knowing, you shall be liberated.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, sacrifices of many varieties have been elaborated upon by the mouth of the Brahman. Know them all as having sprung from actions. By knowing thus, you shall be liberated.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: In this way, other sacrifices may also be undertaken for the sake of the Spirit. Know that they all depend on action. Knowing this, you will be free.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.33।। हे परन्तप अर्जुन ! द्रव्यमय यज्ञसे ज्ञानयज्ञ श्रेष्ठ है। सम्पूर्ण कर्म और पदार्थ ज्ञान-(तत्त्वज्ञान-) में समाप्त हो जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.33।। हे परन्तप ! द्रव्यों से सम्पन्न होने वाले यज्ञ की अपेक्षा ज्ञानयज्ञ श्रेष्ठ है। हे पार्थ ! सम्पूर्ण अखिल कर्म ज्ञान में समाप्त होते हैं,  अर्थात् ज्ञान उनकी पराकाष्ठा है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The sacrifice of knowledge is superior to material sacrifice. O Arjuna, all actions and everything else culminate in knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O destroyer of enemies, knowledge considered as a sacrifice is greater than sacrifices requiring materials. O son of Prtha, all actions in their totality culminate in knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Superior is wisdom-sacrifice to the sacrifice with objects, O Parantapa (scorcher of the foes). All actions in their entirety, O Arjuna, culminate in knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The sacrifice of knowledge is superior to the sacrifice of material things. O son of Prtha, destroyer of enemies! All actions, leaving nothing behind, come to an end in knowledge.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sacrifice of wisdom is superior to any material sacrifice, for, O Arjuna, the pinnacle of action is always realization.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.34।। उस- (तत्त्वज्ञान-) को (तत्त्वदर्शी ज्ञानी महापुरुषोंके पास जाकर) समझ। उनको साष्टाङ्ग दण्डवत् प्रणाम करनेसे, उनकी सेवा करनेसे और सरलतापूर्वक प्रश्न करनेसे वे तत्त्वदर्शी ज्ञानी महापुरुष तुझे उस तत्त्वज्ञानका उपदेश देंगे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.34।। उस (ज्ञान) को (गुरु के समीप जाकर) साष्टांग प्रणिपात,  प्रश्न तथा सेवा करके जानो;  ये तत्त्वदर्शी ज्ञानी पुरुष तुम्हें ज्ञान का उपदेश करेंगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know this through prostration, questioning, and service. The wise who have realized the truth will instruct you in knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Know that the wise ones who have realized the Truth will impart the Knowledge to you through prostration, inquiry, and service.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know that the wise who have realized the truth will instruct thee in that knowledge through long prostration, supplication, and service.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Learn this from those endowed with knowledge, through prostration, inquiry, and service offered to them. Those who are endowed with knowledge and are capable of showing the truth will give you the truth nearby.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: This you shall learn by prostrating yourself at the master's feet, questioning them, and serving them. The wise who have realized the truth will teach you wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.35।। जिस- (तत्त्वज्ञान-) का अनुभव करनेके बाद तू फिर इस प्रकार मोहको नहीं प्राप्त होगा, और हे अर्जुन ! जिस- (तत्त्वज्ञान-) से तू सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंको निःशेषभावसे पहले अपनेमें और उसके बाद मुझ सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्मामें देखेगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.35।। जिसको जानकर तुम पुन इस प्रकार मोह को नहीं प्राप्त होगे,  और हे पाण्डव ! जिसके द्वारा तुम भूतमात्र को अपने आत्मस्वरूप में तथा मुझमें भी देखोगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Knowing this, O Arjuna, you will not fall again into delusion in this way—by that knowledge, you will see all beings without exception in yourself and then in Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Knowing this, O Pandava, you will not be deluded again in this way, and through it, you will see all beings, without exception, in the Self and also in Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Knowing that thou shalt not, O Arjuna, again be deluded like this; and by that thou shalt see all beings in thyself and also in me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: By knowing this, you will not be deluded again, O son of Pandu; and by this means, you will see all beings without exception in yourself, that is, in Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having known That, you shall never again be confounded; and, O Arjuna, by the power of that wisdom, you shall see all these people as if they were your own Self, and therefore as Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.36।। अगर तू सब पापियोंसे भी अधिक पापी है, तो भी तू ज्ञानरूपी नौकाके द्वारा निःसन्देह सम्पूर्ण पापसमुद्रसे अच्छी तरह तर जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.36।। यदि तुम सब पापियों से भी अधिक पाप करने वाले हो,  तो भी ज्ञानरूपी नौका द्वारा,  निश्चय ही सम्पूर्ण पापों का तुम संतरण कर जाओगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Even if you are the most sinful of all sinners, you can cross over all sins by the boat of knowledge alone.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even if you are the worst sinner among all sinners, you can still cross over all wickedness with the raft of Knowledge alone.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Even if thou art the most sinful of all sinners, yet thou shalt surely cross over all sins by the raft of knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Even if you are the highest sinner among all sinners, you can cross over the ocean of all sins by the boat of knowledge.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even if you are the greatest of sinners, you will cross over all sin by the ferryboat of wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.37।। हे अर्जुन ! जैसे प्रज्वलित अग्नि ईंधनोंको सर्वथा भस्म कर देती है, ऐसे ही ज्ञानरूपी अग्नि सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंको सर्वथा भस्म कर देती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.37।। जैसे प्रज्जवलित अग्नि ईन्धन को भस्मसात् कर देती है,  वैसे ही,  हे अर्जुन ! ज्ञानरूपी अग्नि सम्पूर्ण कर्मों को भस्मसात् कर देती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Just as a burning fire turns fuel to ashes, O Arjuna, so does the fire of knowledge turn all karmas to ashes.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, just as a blazing fire reduces wood to ashes, similarly the fire of Knowledge reduces all actions to ashes.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: As the blazing fire reduces fuel to ashes, O Arjuna, so does the fire of knowledge reduce all actions to ash.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Just as a fire that is well-inflamed reduces fuel to ashes, so too does the fire of knowledge reduce all actions to ashes.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As the kindled fire consumes the fuel, so, O Arjuna, the embers of action are burnt to ashes in the flame of wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.38।। इस मनुष्यलोकमें ज्ञानके समान पवित्र करनेवाला निःसन्देह दूसरा कोई साधन नहीं है। जिसका योग भली-भाँति सिद्ध हो गया है, वह (कर्मयोगी) उस तत्त्वज्ञानको अवश्य ही स्वयं अपने-आपमें पा लेता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.38।। इस लोक में ज्ञान के समान पवित्र करने वाला,  निसंदेह,  कुछ भी नहीं है। योग में संसिद्ध पुरुष स्वयं ही उसे (उचित) काल में आत्मा में प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For there is no purifier equal to knowledge; he who is perfected in Karma Yoga finds this knowledge of his own accord in himself in due time.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Indeed, there is nothing purifying here comparable to Knowledge. One who has become perfected over time through yoga, realizes That in their own heart.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Verily, there is no purifier in this world like knowledge. He who is perfected in Yoga finds it within the Self in due time.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: In this world, there exists no purifier comparable to knowledge. One who becomes perfect in Yoga finds this, of their own accord, in their Self in due course.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There is nothing in the world so purifying as wisdom; and he who is a perfect saint finds that at last within himself.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.39।। जो जितेन्द्रिय तथा साधन-परायण है, ऐसा श्रद्धावान् मनुष्य ज्ञानको प्राप्त होता है और ज्ञानको प्राप्त होकर वह तत्काल परम शान्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.39।। श्रद्धावान्,  तत्पर और जितेन्द्रिय पुरुष ज्ञान प्राप्त करता है। ज्ञान को प्राप्त करके शीघ्र ही वह परम शान्ति को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who has faith, is intent on it, and has mastered his senses, attains knowledge. Having attained knowledge, he quickly goes to supreme peace.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The one who has faith, is diligent, and has control over their organs, attains Knowledge. Achieving Knowledge, one soon attains supreme Peace.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The one who is full of faith, devoted to it, and has subdued their senses obtains this knowledge; and upon obtaining the knowledge, they attain the supreme peace immediately.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who has faith gains knowledge, if he is solely intent upon it and has his sense-organs well-controlled. Having gained the knowledge, he soon attains the Supreme Peace.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who is full of faith attains wisdom, and he who can control his senses, having attained that wisdom, will soon attain Supreme Peace.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.40।। विवेकहीन और श्रद्धारहित संशयात्मा मनुष्यका पतन हो जाता है। ऐसे संशयात्मा मनुष्यके लिये न यह लोक  है न परलोक है और न सुख ही है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.40।। अज्ञानी तथा श्रद्धारहित और संशययुक्त पुरुष नष्ट हो जाता है,  (उनमें भी) संशयी पुरुष के लिये न यह लोक है,  न परलोक और न सुख।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The ignorant, the faithless, and the doubting one perish; for the doubting one, there is neither this world nor the one beyond, nor happiness.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who is ignorant and faithless, and has a doubting mind, perishes. Neither this world nor the next, nor happiness, exists for one who has a doubting mind.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The ignorant, the faithless, and the doubting self go to destruction; there is neither this world nor the other, nor happiness for the doubting one.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But he who is ignorant and has no faith, perishes, with his mind full of doubts. Neither this world nor the other, nor happiness is for a person who is by nature full of doubts.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But the ignorant man, and he who has no faith, and the skeptic are lost. Neither in this world nor elsewhere is there any happiness in store for him who always doubts.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.41।। हे धनञ्जय ! योग- (समता-) के द्वारा जिसका सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंसे सम्बन्ध-विच्छेद हो गया है और ज्ञानके द्वारा जिसके सम्पूर्ण संशयोंका नाश हो गया है, ऐसे स्वरूप-परायण मनुष्यको कर्म नहीं बाँधते।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.41।। जिसने योगद्वारा कर्मों का संन्यास किया है,  ज्ञानद्वारा जिसके संशय नष्ट हो गये हैं,  ऐसे आत्मवान् पुरुष को,  हे धनंजय ! कर्म नहीं बांधते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Actions do not bind him, O Arjuna, who has renounced them through Karma Yoga and whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, and who thus possesses a steady mind.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), actions do not bind one who has renounced actions through yoga, whose doubts have been fully dispelled by Knowledge, and who is not inattentive.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who has renounced actions through Yoga, whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, and who is self-possessed—such a one is not bound by actions, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Dhananjaya! Actions do not bind him who has renounced all actions through Yoga; who has cut off his doubts by the sword of knowledge; and who is a master of his own self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But the one who has renounced their actions for meditation, who has cleaved their doubts in two by the sword of wisdom, and who remains always enthroned in their Self, is not bound by their actions.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।4.42।। इसलिये हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन ! हृदयमें स्थित इस अज्ञानसे उत्पन्न अपने संशयका ज्ञानरूप तलवारसे छेदन करके योग -(समता-) में स्थित हो जा, (और युद्धके लिये) खड़ा हो जा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।4.42।। इसलिये अपने हृदय में स्थित अज्ञान से उत्पन्न आत्मविषयक संशय को ज्ञान खड्ग से काटकर,  हे भारत ! योग का आश्रय लेकर खड़े हो जाओ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Therefore, sever, with the sword of knowledge, this doubt present in your heart resulting from ignorance concerning the Self. Practice this Yoga, O Arjuna, and arise.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Therefore, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, take recourse to yoga and rise up, cutting asunder with the sword of knowledge this doubt in your heart, arising from ignorance.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Therefore, with the sword of knowledge (of the Self), cut asunder the doubt of the self, born of ignorance, residing in your heart, and take refuge in Yoga. Arise, O Arjuna!

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Therefore, thus cutting off, by means of the sword of knowledge, the doubt that has sprung from ignorance and exists in your heart, practice the Yoga! Stand up, O descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Therefore, cleave asunder with the sword of wisdom the doubts of the heart, which your own ignorance has engendered, and follow the path of wisdom and arise!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.1।। अर्जुन बोले -- हे कृष्ण ! आप कर्मोंका स्वरूपसे त्याग करनेकी और फिर कर्मयोगकी प्रशंसा करते हैं। अतः इन दोनों साधनोंमें जो एक निश्चितरूपसे कल्याणकारक हो, उसको मेरे लिये कहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.1।। अर्जुन ने कहा हे --  कृष्ण ! आप कर्मों के संन्यास की और फिर योग (कर्म के आचरण) की प्रशंसा करते हैं। इन दोनों में एक जो निश्चय पूर्वक श्रेयस्कर है, उसको मेरे लिए कहिये।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, you praise the renunciation of actions, and then you also praise Karma Yoga. Please tell me with certainty which of these is the superior one, leading to the ultimate good.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, you praise renunciation of actions and, at the same time, Karma-yoga. Please tell me for certain which one is better between these two."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krishna, you praise renunciation of actions and also yoga. Please tell me conclusively which is better of the two."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, you commend both renunciation of action and the Yoga of action; which one of these two is superior?" Please tell me that for certain.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna said: My Lord! At one moment, You praise renunciation of action; at another, You praise right action. Tell me truly, I pray, which of these is more conducive to my highest welfare?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.2।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- संन्यास (सांख्ययोग) और कर्मयोग दोनों ही कल्याण करनेवाले हैं। परन्तु उन दोनोंमें भी कर्मसंन्यास- (सांख्ययोग-) से कर्मयोग श्रेष्ठ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.2।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा --  कर्मसंन्यास और कर्मयोग ये दोनों ही परम कल्याणकारक हैं;  परन्तु उन दोनों में कर्मसंन्यास से कर्मयोग श्रेष्ठ है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "Renunciation of actions and Karma Yoga both lead to the highest excellence; however, of the two, Karma Yoga surpasses renunciation of actions."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "Both renunciation of actions and Karma-yoga lead to liberation; however, Karma-yoga excels over renunciation of actions."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "Renunciation and the Yoga of action both lead to the highest bliss; but of the two, the Yoga of action is superior to the renunciation of action."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "Both renunciation and the Yoga of action effect salvation; however, of these two, the Yoga of action is superior to renunciation of action."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: Both renunciation of action and the path of right action lead to the highest; however, right action is the better of the two.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.3।। हे महाबाहो ! जो मनुष्य न किसीसे द्वेष करता है और न किसीकी आकाङ्क्षा करता है; वह (कर्मयोगी) सदा संन्यासी समझनेयोग्य है; क्योंकि द्वन्द्वोंसे रहित मनुष्य सुखपूर्वक संसार-बन्धनसे मुक्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.3।। जो पुरुष न किसी से द्वेष करता है और न किसी की आकांक्षा,  वह सदा संन्यासी ही समझने योग्य है;  क्योंकि,  हे महाबाहो ! द्वन्द्वों से रहित पुरुष सहज ही बन्धन मुक्त हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who neither hates nor desires, and is beyond the pairs of opposites, is to be understood as an ever-renouncer. Therefore, he is easily set free from bondage, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who neither hates nor craves should be known as a man of constant renunciation.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He should be known as a perpetual Sannyasi who neither hates nor desires; for, free from the pairs of opposites, O mighty-armed Arjuna, he is easily freed from bondage.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: That person may be considered a man of permanent renunciation, who neither hates nor desires. For, O mighty-armed one! He who is free from the pairs of opposites is easily released from bondage of action.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He is a true ascetic who never desires nor dislikes, who is unaffected by the opposites and is easily freed from bondage.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.4।। बेसमझ लोग सांख्ययोग और कर्मयोगको अलग-अलग फलवाले कहते हैं, न कि पण्डितजन; क्योंकि इन दोनोंमेंसे एक साधनमें भी अच्छी तरहसे स्थित मनुष्य दोनोंके फलरूप परमात्माको प्राप्त कर लेता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.4।। बालक अर्थात् बालबुद्धि के लोग सांख्य (संन्यास) और योग को परस्पर भिन्न समझते हैं;  किसी एक में भी सम्यक् प्रकार से स्थित हुआ पुरुष दोनों के फल को प्राप्त कर लेता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Children, not the learned, speak of Sankhya and Yoga as distinct; however, he who is firmly set in one, attains the fruit of both.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The fools, not the learned ones, speak of Sankhya and (Karma-) yoga as being different. Anyone who properly resorts to either one of them gets the results of both.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Children, not the wise, speak of knowledge and the Yoga of action, or the performance of action, as though they are distinct and different; he who is truly established in one, obtains the fruits of both.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The childish, and not the wise, proclaim the paths of knowledge and Yoga to be different. He who has properly resorted to even one of these two, gets the fruit of both.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Only the unenlightened speak of wisdom and right action as separate; the wise do not. If anyone knows one, they enjoy the fruit of both.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.5।। सांख्ययोगियोंके द्वारा जो तत्त्व प्राप्त किया जाता है, कर्मयोगियोंके द्वारा भी वही प्राप्त किया जाता है। अतः जो मनुष्य सांख्ययोग और कर्मयोगको (फलरूपमें) एक देखता है, वही ठीक देखता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.5।। जो स्थान ज्ञानियों द्वारा प्राप्त किया जाता है,  उसी स्थान पर कर्मयोगी भी पहुँचते हैं। इसलिए जो पुरुष सांख्य और योग को (फलरूप से) एक ही देखता है,  वही (वास्तव में) देखता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That state which is reached by the Sankhyans, the same is also attained by the Yogins; that is, the same state is achieved by those who are Karma Yogins. He alone is wise who perceives that the Sankhya and the Yoga are one and the same, due to their having the same result.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The state that is reached by the Sankhyas, and that is also reached by the yogis, is seen by one who sees Sankhya and yoga as one.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That place which is reached by the Sankhyas or the Jnanis is also reached by the Yogis (Karma Yogis). He who sees knowledge and the performance of action (Karma Yoga) as one, sees truly.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those who are knowledgeable reach the same state as those who practice Yoga; so whoever sees the knowledge-path and the Yoga as one, sees correctly.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The level which is attained through wisdom is also reached through right action. He who perceives that the two are one, knows the truth.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.6।।  परन्तु हे महाबाहो ! कर्मयोगके बिना संन्यास सिद्ध होना कठिन है। मननशील कर्मयोगी शीघ्र ही ब्रह्मको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.6।। परन्तु,  हे महाबाहो ! योग के बिना संन्यास प्राप्त होना कठिन है;  योगयुक्त मननशील पुरुष परमात्मा को शीघ्र ही प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But, O mighty-armed one, renunciation is hard to attain without practicing Yoga. The sage who contemplates and follows Yoga soon reaches the Brahman (the self or Atman).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But, O mighty-armed one, renunciation is hard to attain without (Karma-) yoga. The meditative man equipped with yoga attains Brahman without delay.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But, O mighty-armed Arjuna, renunciation is hard to attain without Yoga; the sage who is in harmony with Yoga quickly goes to Brahman.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O mighty-armed Arjuna! Renunciation is indeed hard to attain, except through Yoga; the sage who is the master of Yoga quickly attains the Brahman.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Without concentration, O Mighty One, renunciation is difficult. But the sage who is always meditating on the Divine, shall soon attain the Absolute.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.7।। जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ अपने वशमें हैं, जिसका अन्तःकरण निर्मल है, जिसका शरीर अपने वशमें है और सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंकी आत्मा ही जिसकी आत्मा है, ऐसा कर्मयोगी कर्म करते हुए भी लिप्त नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.7।। जो पुरुष योगयुक्त, विशुद्ध अन्तकरण वाला, शरीर को वश में किये हुए, जितेन्द्रिय तथा भूतमात्र में स्थित आत्मा के साथ एकत्व अनुभव किये हुए है वह कर्म करते हुए भी उनसे लिप्त नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who follows the Yoga and is pure in mind, who has subdued his self and has conquered his senses, and whose self has become the self of all beings, even while he is acting, remains untainted.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Endowed with yoga, pure in mind, controlled in body, a conqueror of the organs, the Self of the selves of all beings—he does not become tainted even while performing actions. When this person resorts to nitya and naimittika rites and duties as a means to the achievement of full illumination, and thus becomes fully enlightened, then, even when he acts through the apparent functions of the mind, organs, etc., he does not become affected.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who is devoted to the path of action, whose mind is pure, who has conquered the self, who has subdued his senses, and who realizes his Self as the Self in all beings, though acting, is not tainted.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: A master of yoga, whose self (mind and intellect) is very pure, the sense-organs are controlled, and the soul is realized to be the soul of all beings—he is not stained, even though he is a performer of actions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who is spiritual, pure, has overcome his senses and his personal self, and has realized his highest Self as the Self of all—even though he acts, he is not bound by his actions.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.8 -- 5.9।। तत्त्वको जाननेवाला सांख्ययोगी देखता, सुनता, छूता, सूँघता, खाता, चलता, ग्रहण करता, बोलता, मल-मूत्र का त्याग करता, सोता, श्वास लेता तथा आँखें खोलता और मूँदता भी सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियाँ इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंमें बरत रही हैं' -- ऐसा समझकर 'मैं (स्वयं) कुछ भी नहीं करता हूँ' -- ऐसा माने।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.8।। तत्त्ववित् युक्त पुरुष यह सोचेगा (अर्थात् जानता है) कि  "मैं किंचित् मात्र कर्म नहीं करता हूँ"  देखता हुआ, सुनता हुआ, स्पर्श करता हुआ, सूंघता हुआ, खाता हुआ, चलता हुआ, सोता हुआ, श्वास लेता हुआ,।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The one who knows the truth and is devoted to yoga should think, "I am not doing anything," even though they are seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, and breathing.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: - Remaining absorbed in the Self, the knower of Reality should think, 'I certainly do not do anything', even while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing, speaking, releasing, holding, opening, and closing the eyes—remembering that the organs function in relation to the objects of the organs.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I do nothing at all," thus would the harmonized knower of Truth think, seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, going, sleeping, and breathing.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: A master of Yoga, knowing the reality, would think, 'I do not perform any action at all.' For, he who, while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, going, sleeping, and breathing;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Though the saint sees, hears, touches, smells, eats, moves, sleeps, and breathes, yet he knows the truth and knows that it is not he who acts.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.8 -- 5.9।। तत्त्वको जाननेवाला सांख्ययोगी देखता, सुनता, छूता, सूँघता, खाता, चलता, ग्रहण करता, बोलता, मल-मूत्र का त्याग करता, सोता हुआ, श्वास लेता तथा आँखें खोलता और मूँदता हुआ भी 'सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियाँ इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंमें बरत रही हैं' -- ऐसा समझकर 'मैं (स्वयं) कुछ भी नहीं करता हूँ' -- ऐसा माने।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.9।। बोलता हुआ,  त्यागता हुआ,  ग्रहण करता हुआ  तथा आँखों को खोलता और बन्द करता हुआ (वह) निश्चयात्मक रूप से जानता है कि सब इन्द्रियाँ अपने-अपने विषयों में विचरण कर रही हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Speaking, discharging, grasping, opening, and closing his eyes, etc., he should always bear in mind that the senses operate among sense-objects.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: - Remaining absorbed in the Self, the knower of Reality should think, 'I certainly do not do anything', even while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing, speaking, releasing, holding, opening, and closing the eyes—remembering that the organs function in relation to the objects of the organs.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Speaking, letting go, seizing, opening, and closing the eyes, one should be convinced that the senses move among the sense-objects.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Taking, rejecting, receiving, opening, and closing the eyes, bear in mind that the sense-organs are on their respective objects; and

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Though he talks, gives, receives, opens his eyes, and shuts them, he still knows that his senses are merely disporting themselves among the objects of perception.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.10।। जो (भक्तियोगी) सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंको भगवान् में अर्पण करके और आसक्तिका त्याग करके कर्म करता है, वह जलसे कमलके पत्तेकी तरह पापसे लिप्त नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.10।। जो पुरुष सब कर्म ब्रह्म में अर्पण करके और आसक्ति को त्यागकर करता है,  वह पुरुष कमल के पत्ते के सदृश पाप से लिप्त नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who acts without attachment, reposing all actions on Brahman (Prakṛti), is untouched by evil, just as a lotus leaf is untouched by water.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who acts by dedicating their actions to Brahman and by renouncing attachment, they do not become polluted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not by water.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who does actions, offering them to Brahman and abandoning attachment, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not tainted by water.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Who performs actions by offering them to the Brahman and giving up attachment—they are not stained by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not stained by water.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who dedicates his actions to the Spirit, without any personal attachment to them, is no more tainted by sin than the water lily is wetted by water.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.11।। कर्मयोगी आसक्तिका त्याग करके केवल (ममतारहित) इन्द्रियाँ-शरीर-मन-बुद्धि के द्वारा केवल अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धिके लिये ही कर्म करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.11।। योगीजन, शरीर, मन, बुद्धि और इन्द्रियों द्वारा आसक्ति को त्याग कर आत्मशुद्धि (चित्तशुद्धि) के लिए कर्म करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Yogins, renouncing attachment, do actions with merely the body, mind, intellect, and senses, for the purification of the self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: By giving up attachment, the yogis undertake work solely through the body, mind, intellect, and even the organs, for their own purification.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Yogis, having abandoned attachment, perform actions only through the body, mind, intellect, and even the senses, for the purification of the self.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having given up attachment, the men of Yoga perform action just with the body, with the mind, with intellect, and also with their sense-organs, for attaining the Self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sage performs his action dispassionately, using his body, mind, intellect, and even his senses, always as a means for purification.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.12।। कर्मयोगी कर्मफलका त्याग करके नैष्ठिकी शान्तिको प्राप्त होता है। परन्तु सकाम मनुष्य कामनाके कारण फलमें आसक्त होकर बँध जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.12।। युक्त पुरुष कर्मफल का त्याग करके परम शान्ति को प्राप्त होता है;  और अयुक्त पुरुष फल में आसक्त हुआ कामना के द्वारा बँधता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: A yogi, renouncing the fruits of their actions, attains lasting peace. But the unsteady person who is attached to the fruits of their actions, being driven by desire, is bound.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Giving up the results of work by becoming resolute in faith, one attains peace arising from steadfastness. One who is lacking in resolute faith, being attached to the results under the impulse of desire, becomes bound.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The one who is united (the well-poised or harmonized) having abandoned the fruit of action attains eternal peace; whereas the one who is not united (the unsteady or unbalanced), impelled by desire and attached to the fruit, is bound.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having abandoned attachment to the fruit of actions, the master of Yoga attains the highest Peace. But, one who is not a master of Yoga and is attached to the fruit of action, is bound by their actions born of desire.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having abandoned the fruit of action, he wins eternal peace. Others, unacquainted with spirituality, led by desire and clinging to the benefit they think will follow their actions, become entangled in them.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.13।। जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ और मन वशमें हैं, ऐसा देहधारी पुरुष नौ द्वारोंवाले शरीररूपी पुरमें सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंका विवेकपूर्वक मनसे त्याग करके निःसन्देह न करता हुआ और न करवाता हुआ सुखपूर्वक (अपने स्वरूपमें) स्थित रहता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.13।। सब कर्मों का मन से संन्यास करके संयमी पुरुष नवद्वार वाली शरीर रूप नगरी में सुख से रहता हुआ न कर्म करता है और न करवाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The embodied self, mentally resigning all actions as belonging to the city of nine gates (i.e., the body) and becoming self-controlled, dwells happily, neither acting himself nor causing the body to act.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The embodied man of self-control, having given up all actions mentally, continues happily in the city of nine gates, without doing or causing anyone else to do anything.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Mentally renouncing all actions and being self-controlled, the embodied one happily rests in the nine-gated city, neither acting nor causing others (body and senses) to act.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having renounced all actions by mind, a man of self-control dwells happily in his body, a nine-windowed mansion, neither performing nor causing others to perform any actions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Mentally renouncing all actions, the self-controlled soul enjoys bliss in this body, the city of the nine gates, neither doing anything themselves nor causing anything to be done.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.14।। परमेश्वर मनुष्योंके न कर्तापनकी, न कर्मोंकी और न कर्मफलके साथ संयोगकी रचना करते हैं; किन्तु स्वभाव ही बरत रहा है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.14।। लोकमात्र के लिए प्रभु (ईश्वर) न कर्तृत्व, न कर्म और न कर्मफल के संयोग को रचता है। परन्तु प्रकृति (सब कुछ) करती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord of the body (the Self, i.e., the Jiva) does not create agency, nor actions, nor union with the fruits of actions in relation to the world of selves; only the inherent tendencies function.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Self does not create agency or any objects of desire for anyone, nor does it associate with the results of actions; rather, it is Nature that acts.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Neither does the Lord create agency nor actions for the world, nor union with the fruits of actions; rather, it is Nature that acts.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Lord (Self) acquires neither the state of being a creator of the world, nor the actions, nor the connection with the fruits of their actions. But it is the inherent nature [in It] that exerts.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The Lord of this universe has not ordained activity, nor any incentive thereto, nor any relation between an act and its consequences; all this is the work of Nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.15।। सर्वव्यापी परमात्मा न किसीके पापकर्मको और न शुभकर्मको ही ग्रहण करता है; किन्तु अज्ञानसे ज्ञान ढका हुआ है, उसीसे सब जीव मोहित हो रहे हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.15।। विभु परमात्मा न किसी के पापकर्म को और न पुण्यकर्म को ही ग्रहण करता है;  (किन्तु) अज्ञान से ज्ञान ढका हुआ है,  इससे सब जीव मोहित होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The all-pervasive One takes away neither the sin nor the merit of anyone. Knowledge is enveloped by ignorance, and creatures are thereby deluded.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Omnipresent neither accepts anyone's sin nor even virtue; knowledge remains covered by ignorance, thus the creatures become deluded.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Lord takes neither the demerit nor the merit of any; knowledge is enveloped by ignorance, and beings are deluded.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Omnimanifest Soul takes neither sin nor merit born of any action upon itself. But, perfect knowledge is clouded by Illusion, and thus the creatures are deluded.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The Lord does not accept responsibility for any person's sin or merit; they are deluded, for wisdom is submerged in ignorance within them.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.16।। परन्तु जिन्होंने अपने जिस ज्ञान-(विवक-) के द्वारा उस अज्ञानका नाश कर दिया है, उनका वह ज्ञान सूर्यकी तरह परमतत्त्व परमात्माको प्रकाशित कर देता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.16।। परन्तु जिनका वह अज्ञान आत्मज्ञान से नष्ट हो जाता है,  उनके लिए वह ज्ञान,  सूर्य के सदृश,  परमात्मा को प्रकाशित करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But for those in whom this ignorance is destroyed by the knowledge of the Self, that knowledge, in their case, is supreme and shines brightly like the sun.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But in the case of those whose ignorance is destroyed by knowledge (of the Self), their knowledge, like the sun, reveals that supreme Reality.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But to those whose ignorance is destroyed by knowledge of the Self, like the sun, knowledge reveals the Supreme Brahman.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: In the case of those whose illusion has been destroyed by Self-knowledge, that knowledge illuminates itself, like the sun.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Surely, wisdom is like the sun, revealing the supreme truth to those whose ignorance has been dispelled by the wisdom of the Self.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.17।। जिनकी बुद्धि तदाकार हो रही है, जिनका मन तदाकार हो रहा है, जिनकी स्थिति परमात्मतत्वमें है, ऐसे परमात्मपरायण साधक ज्ञानके द्वारा पापरहित होकर अपुनरावृत्ति (परमगति) को प्राप्त होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.17।। जिनकी बुद्धि उस (परमात्मा) में स्थित है,  जिनका मन तद्रूप हुआ है,  उसमें ही जिनकी निष्ठा है,  वह (ब्रह्म) ही जिनका परम लक्ष्य है,  ज्ञान के द्वारा पापरहित पुरुष अपुनरावृत्ति को प्राप्त होते हैं,  अर्थात् उनका पुनर्जन्म नहीं होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those whose intellects pursue it, whose minds think about it, who undergo discipline for it, and who hold it as their highest object, have their impurities cleansed by knowledge and go from whence there is no return.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those whose intellect is absorbed in That, whose Self is That, who are steadfast in That, and who have That as their supreme Goal—they attain the state of non-returning, their impurities having been removed by Knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Their intellect absorbed in That, their self being That, established in That, with That as their supreme goal, they go whence there is no return, their sins dispelled by knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those who have their intellect and self (mind) devoted to This; who have established themselves in This and have This [alone] as their supreme goal; and who have washed off their sins through [perfect] knowledge—they reach a state from which there is no more return.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Meditating on the divine, having faith in the divine, concentrating on the divine, and losing themselves in the divine, their sins dissolve in wisdom, and they go from whence there is no return.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.18।। ज्ञानी महापुरुष विद्या-विनययुक्त ब्राह्मणमें और चाण्डालमें तथा गाय, हाथी एवं कुत्तेमें भी समरूप परमात्माको देखनेवाले होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.18।। (ऐसे वे) ज्ञानीजन विद्या और विनय से सम्पन्न ब्राह्मण,  तथा गाय,  हाथी,  श्वान और चाण्डाल में भी सम तत्त्व को देखते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The sages look with an equal eye on one endowed with learning and humility, a Brahmana, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and a dog-eater.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The learned ones look with equanimity on a Brahmana endowed with learning and humility, a cow, an elephant, and even a dog, as well as one who eats dog meat.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Sages look with an equal eye on a Brahmana endowed with learning and humility, on a cow, an elephant, a dog, and even an outcaste.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The wise, by nature, look equally upon a Brahmana, rich in learning and humility, a cow, an elephant, and even a dog and a dog-cooker (an outcaste).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Sages look upon all equally, whether they be a minister of learning and humility, an infidel, a cow, an elephant, or a dog.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.19।। जिनका अन्तःकरण समतामें स्थित है, उन्होंने इस जीवित-अवस्थामें ही सम्पूर्ण संसारको जीत लिया है; ब्रह्म निर्दोष और सम है, इसलिये वे ब्रह्ममें ही स्थित हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.19।। जिनका मन समत्वभाव में स्थित है,  उनके द्वारा यहीं पर यह सर्ग जीत लिया जाता है; क्योंकि ब्रह्म निर्दोष और सम है इसलिये वे ब्रह्म में ही स्थित हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Here itself, those whose minds rest in equanimity overcome samsara. For the Brahman (individual self), when uncontaminated by Prakriti, is the same everywhere; therefore, they abide in Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Here itself is mirth attained by those whose minds are established in sameness. Since Brahman is the same in all and free from defects, they are established in Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Even here in this world, those whose minds rest in reality overcome birth; Brahman is indeed spotless and real; therefore they are established in Brahman.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The one who knows Brahman, who is disillusioned, established in Brahman, and has a firm intellect, would neither rejoice upon meeting a friend nor get agitated upon meeting a foe.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even in this world, those whose minds remain always balanced, fixed on the Supreme, conquer their earthly life; for the Supreme has neither blemish nor bias.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.20।। जो प्रियको प्राप्त होकर हर्षित न हो और अप्रियको प्राप्त होकर उद्विग्न न हो, वह स्थिर बुद्धिवाला, मूढ़तारहित तथा ब्रह्मको जाननेवाला मनुष्य ब्रह्ममें स्थित है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.20।। जो स्थिरबुद्धि,  संमोहरहित ब्रह्मवित् पुरुष ब्रह्म में स्थित है,  वह प्रिय वस्तु को प्राप्त होकर हर्षित नहीं होता और अप्रिय को पाकर उद्विग्न नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who knows Brahman (individual self) and abides in Brahman, whose mind is steadfastly focused on the self and undeluded by body consciousness—he neither rejoices at gaining what is pleasant nor grieves on obtaining what is unpleasant.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A knower of Brahman, who is established in Brahman, should have their intellect steady and should not be deluded. They should not get delighted by getting what is desirable, nor become dejected by getting what is undesirable.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Resting in Brahman, with a steady intellect and undeluded, the knower of Brahman neither rejoices upon obtaining what is pleasant nor grieves upon obtaining what is unpleasant.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who, with his mind not attached to external contacts, finds happiness in the Self—that person, with his mind engaged in Yoga, easily pervades the Brahman, suffering no loss.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who knows and lives in the Absolute remains unmoved and unperturbed; he is not elated by pleasure nor depressed by pain.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.21।। बाह्यस्पर्शमें आसक्तिरहित अन्तःकरणवाला साधक आत्मामें जो सुख है, उसको प्राप्त होता है। फिर वह ब्रह्ममें अभिन्नभावसे स्थित मनुष्य अक्षय सुखका अनुभव करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.21।। बाह्य विषयों में आसक्तिरहित अन्त:करण वाला पुरुष आत्मा में ही सुख प्राप्त करता है;  ब्रह्म के ध्यान में समाहित चित्त वाला पुरुष अक्षय सुख प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He whose mind is detached from external contact and finds happiness in the Self—he has his mind engaged in the contemplation of Brahman and enjoys undecaying bliss.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: With his heart unattached to external objects, he gets the bliss that is in the Self. With his heart absorbed in meditation on Brahman, he acquires undying Bliss.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: With the self unattached to external contacts, he finds happiness in the Self; with the self engaged in the meditation of Brahman, he attains endless happiness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The enjoyments that arise from contact [with objects] are nothing but sources of misery, having both a beginning and an end. Therefore, an intelligent person does not take delight in them, O son of Kunti!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He finds happiness in his own Self and enjoys eternal bliss, whose heart does not yearn for the contacts of the earth and whose Self is one with the Everlasting.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.22।। क्योंकि हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! जो इन्द्रियों और विषयोंके संयोगसे पैदा होनेवाले भोग (सुख) हैं, वे आदि-अन्तवाले और दुःखके ही कारण हैं। अतः विवेकशील मनुष्य उनमें रमण नहीं करता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.22।। हे कौन्तेय (इन्द्रिय तथा विषयों के) संयोग से उत्पन्न होने वाले जो भोग हैं वे दु:ख के ही हेतु हैं, क्योंकि वे आदि-अन्त वाले हैं। बुद्धिमान् पुरुष उनमें नहीं रमता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For those pleasures that are born of contact are wombs of pain. They have a beginning and an end, O Arjuna; the wise do not rejoice in them.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Since enjoyments that result from contact with objects are indeed the sources of sorrow, having a beginning and an end, O son of Kunti, the wise one does not delight in them.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The enjoyments that arise from contact are only sources of pain, for they have a beginning and an end, O Arjuna; the wise do not rejoice in them.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whosoever, right here, before abandoning the body, is capable of bearing the force sprung from desire and wrath—he is considered to be a man of Yoga and a happy man.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The joys that spring from external associations bring pain; they have their beginnings and their endings. The wise person does not rejoice in them.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.23।। इस मनुष्य-शरीरमें जो कोई (मनुष्य) शरीर छूटनेसे पहले ही काम-क्रोधसे उत्पन्न होनेवाले वेगको सहन करनेमें समर्थ होता है, वह नर योगी है और वही सुखी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.23।। जो मनुष्य इसी लोक में शरीर त्यागने के पूर्व ही काम और क्रोध से उत्पन्न हुए वेग को सहन करने में समर्थ है,  वह योगी (युक्त) और सुखी मनुष्य है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who is able, even here, before being released from the body, to bear the impulse generated by desire and anger, he is a Yogin (competent for self-realisation); he is the happy man.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who can withstand here itself, before departing from the body, the impulse arising from desire and anger, that person is a yogi; they are happy.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who is able, while still here in this world, to withstand the impulse born out of desire and anger before the liberation from the body, he is a Yogi, and he is a happy man.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He, whose pleasure, delight, and light are all within—O son of Prtha!—he attains the supreme Yoga, becoming the Brahman himself.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who, before leaving his body, learns to overcome the promptings of desire and anger, is a saint and is happy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.24।। जो मनुष्य केवल परमात्मामें सुखवाला है और केवल परमात्मामें रमण करनेवाला है तथा जो केवल परमात्मामें ज्ञानवाला है, वह ब्रह्ममें अपनी स्थितिका अनुभव करनेवाला सांख्ययोगी निर्वाण ब्रह्मको प्राप्त होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.24।। जो पुरुष अन्तरात्मा में ही सुख वाला,  आत्मा में ही आराम वाला तथा आत्मा में ही ज्ञान वाला है,  वह योगी ब्रह्मरूप बनकर ब्रह्मनिर्वाण अर्थात् परम मोक्ष को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He whose joy is within, whose pleasure is within, and whose light is within—he is a yogin, who, having become Brahman, attains the bliss of Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who is content within, whose pleasure is within, and whose light is only within, that yogi, having become Brahman, attains absorption into Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who is happy within, who rejoices within, and who is illuminated within, that Yogi attains absolute freedom, or Moksha, becoming Brahman himself.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The seers, whose doubts have decayed; by whom dualities have been cut off; whose minds are controlled; and who delight in the welfare of all; they attain the Brahman, the Transcendent One.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who is happy within himself and has found its peace, and in whom the inner light shines, that sage attains eternal bliss and becomes one with the spirit.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.25।। जिनका शरीर मन-बुद्धि-इन्द्रियोंसहित वशमें है, जो सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हितमें रत हैं, जिनके सम्पूर्ण संशय मिट गये हैं, जिनके सम्पूर्ण कल्मष (दोष) नष्ट हो गये हैं, वे विवेकी साधक निर्वाण ब्रह्मको प्राप्त होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.25।। वे ऋषिगण मोक्ष को प्राप्त होते हैं - जिनके पाप नष्ट हो गये हैं, जो छिन्नसंशय, संयमी और भूतमात्र के हित में रमने वाले हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The sages, who are free from the pairs of opposites, whose minds are well subdued, and who are devoted to the welfare of all beings, become cleansed of all impurities and attain the bliss of Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The seers whose sins have been attenuated, who are free from doubt, whose senses are under control, who are engaged in doing good to all beings, attain absorption in Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The sages obtain absolute freedom or Moksha when their sins have been destroyed, their dualities have been torn asunder, they are self-controlled, and they are intent on the welfare of all beings.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: At all times, there is the transcendent Brahman for the ascetics who have severed their connection with desire and anger, who have controlled their minds and have realized their Self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Sages whose sins have been washed away, whose sense of separateness has dissipated, who have subdued themselves, and seek only the welfare of all, come to the Eternal Spirit.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.26।। काम-क्रोधसे सर्वथा रहित,  जीते हुए मनवाले और स्वरूपका साक्षात्कार किये हुए सांख्ययोगियोंके लिये दोनों ओरसे--शरीरके रहते हुए अथवा शरीर छूटनेके बाद) निर्वाण ब्रह्म परिपूर्ण है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.26।। काम और क्रोध से रहित,  संयतचित्त वाले तथा आत्मा को जानने वाले यतियों के लिए सब ओर मोक्ष (या ब्रह्मानन्द) विद्यमान रहता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: To those who are free from desire and wrath, who are accustomed to exerting themselves, whose thoughts are controlled, and who have conquered it—the beatitude of Brahman is close at hand.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: To those monks who have control over their internal organs, who are free from desire and anger, who have known the Self, there is absorption into Brahman either way.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Absolute freedom exists on all sides for those self-controlled ascetics who are free from desire and anger, who have controlled their thoughts, and who have realized the Self.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Warding off external contacts; keeping the sense of sight between the two wandering ones; counterbalancing both the forward and backward moving forces that travel within what is crooked;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Saints who know themselves, who control their minds, and feel neither desire nor anger, find eternal bliss everywhere.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.27 -- 5.28।। बाह्य पदार्थोंको बाहर ही छोड़कर और नेत्रोंकी दृष्टिको भौंहोंके बीचमें स्थित करके तथा नासिकामें विचरनेवाले प्राण और अपान वायुको सम करके जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ, मन और बुद्धि अपने वशमें हैं, जो मोक्ष-परायण है तथा जो इच्छा, भय और क्रोधसे सर्वथा रहित है, वह मुनि सदा मुक्त ही है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.27।। बाह्य विषयों को बाहर ही रखकर नेत्रों की दृष्टि को भृकुटि के बीच में स्थित करके तथा नासिका में विचरने वाले प्राण और अपानवायु को सम करके,।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Shutting off outward contacts, fixing the gaze between the eyebrows, realizing inward and outward breaths moving in the nostrils;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: - Keeping external objects outside, fixing the eyes at the juncture of the eye-brows, and controlling the outgoing and incoming breaths that move through the nostrils, the contemplative who has mastery over his organs, mind, and intellect should be fully intent on Liberation and free from desire, fear, and anger. He who is ever thus is verily free.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Shutting out all external contacts and fixing the gaze between the eyebrows, realizing the outgoing and incoming breaths moving within the nostrils.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The sage, who has controlled his sense-organs, mind, and intellect; whose chief aim is emancipation; and from whom desire, fear, and wrath have departed—he remains ever free.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Excluding external objects, with his gaze fixed between the eyebrows, and the inward and outward breathings passing equally through his nostrils;

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.27 -- 5.28।। बाह्य पदार्थोंको बाहर ही छोड़कर और नेत्रोंकी दृष्टिको भौंहोंके बीचमें स्थित करके तथा नासिकामें विचरनेवाले प्राण और अपान वायुको सम करके जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ, मन और बुद्धि अपने वशमें हैं, जो मोक्ष-परायण है तथा जो इच्छा, भय और क्रोधसे सर्वथा रहित है, वह मुनि सदा मुक्त ही है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.28।। जिस पुरुष की इन्द्रियाँ,  मन और बुद्धि संयत हैं, ऐसा मोक्ष परायण मुनि इच्छा, भय और क्रोध से रहित है, वह सदा मुक्त ही है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The sage who has controlled his senses, mind, and intellect, who is intent on release as his final goal, freed forever from desire, fear, and wrath, is indeed liberated forever.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: - Keeping external objects outside, fixing the eyes at the juncture of the eye-brows, and controlling the outgoing and incoming breaths that move through the nostrils, the contemplative who has mastery over his organs, mind, and intellect should be fully intent on Liberation and free from desire, fear, and anger. He who is ever thus is verily free.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: With the senses, mind, and intellect ever controlled, having liberation as their supreme goal, free from desire, fear, and anger, the sage is truly liberated forever.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having known Me as the Enjoyer of the fruits of sacrifices and austerities, as the great Lord of all the worlds, and as the Friend of all beings, he attains peace.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Governing sense, mind, and intellect, intent on liberation, free from desire, fear, and anger, the sage is eternally free.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।5.29।। भक्त मुझे सब यज्ञों और तपोंका भोक्ता, सम्पूर्ण लोकोंका महान् ईश्वर तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका सुहृद् (स्वार्थरहित दयालु और प्रेमी) जानकर शान्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।5.29।। (साधक भक्त) मुझे यज्ञ और तपों का भोक्ता और सम्पूर्ण लोकों का महान् ईश्वर तथा भूतमात्र का सुहृद् (मित्र) जानकर शान्ति को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Knowing Me as the enjoyer of all sacrifices and austerities, as the Supreme Lord of all worlds, as the Friend of every being, he attains peace.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One attains peace by knowing Me, who am the great Lord of all the worlds, the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, and the friend of all creatures.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who knows Me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great Lord of all the worlds, and the friend of all beings, attains peace.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: There is no such translation for this shloka.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Knowing Me as the One who gladly receives all offerings of austerity and sacrifice, as the Mighty Ruler of all the Worlds and the Friend of all beings, he passes to Eternal Peace.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.1।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- कर्मफलका आश्रय न लेकर जो कर्तव्यकर्म करता है, वही संन्यासी तथा योगी है; और केवल अग्निका त्याग करनेवाला संन्यासी नहीं होता तथा केवल क्रियाओंका त्याग करनेवाला योगी नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.1।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -- जो पुरुष कर्मफल पर आश्रित न होकर कर्तव्य कर्म करता है, वह संन्यासी और योगी है, न कि वह जिसने केवल अग्नि का और क्रियायों का त्याग किया है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "He who performs works that ought to be done without seeking their fruits—he is a sannyasin and yogin, and not he who maintains no sacred fires and performs no actions."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "He who performs an action which is his duty, without depending on the result of the action, is a monk and a yogi; not so in he who does not keep afire and is actionless."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said: He who performs his bounden duty without depending on the fruits of his actions—he is a sannyasi and a yogi, not he who is without fire and without action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavad said, "He who performs his bounden action without depending on its fruit is the man of renunciation and the man of Yoga, not he who simply remains without his fires and actions."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna said: He who acts out of duty, without considering the consequences, is truly spiritual and a true ascetic; not he who merely observes rituals or shuns all action.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.2।। हे अर्जुन ! लोग जिसको संन्यास कहते हैं, उसीको तुम योग समझो; क्योंकि संकल्पोंका त्याग किये बिना मनुष्य कोई-सा भी योगी नहीं हो सकता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.2।। हे पाण्डव ! जिसको (शास्त्रवित्) संन्यास कहते हैं, उसी को तुम योग समझो; क्योंकि संकल्पों को न त्यागने वाला कोई भी पुरुष योगी नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That which is called Sannyasa (Jnana Yoga), know that to be Yoga (Karma Yoga), O Arjuna. For no one, among Karma Yogins, whose delusive identification of the body with the self has not been abandoned, becomes a true Karma Yogin.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That which they call monasticism, know that to be Yoga, O Pandava, for nobody who has not given up expectations can be a yogi.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Do you, O Arjuna, know that Yoga is what they call renunciation; no one indeed becomes a Yogi who has not renounced their thoughts.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
