text: What the learned call renunciation, O son of Pandu, know that to be the same as the Yoga. For, without renouncing intention for fruit, one does not become a man of Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna, renunciation is indeed what is known as right action. No one can become spiritually enlightened who has not renounced all desires.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.3।। जो योग-(समता-) में आरूढ़ होना चाहता है, ऐसे मननशील योगीके लिये कर्तव्य-कर्म करना कारण कहा गया है और उसी योगारूढ़ मनुष्यका शम (शान्ति) परमात्मप्राप्तिमें कारण कहा गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.3।। योग में आरूढ़ होने की इच्छा वाले मुनि के लिए कर्म करना ही हेतु (साधन) कहा है और योगारूढ़ हो जाने पर उसी पुरुष के लिए शम को (शांति, संकल्पसंन्यास) साधन कहा गया है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Action is said to be the means for the sage who seeks to climb the heights of Yoga; but when he has climbed the heights of Yoga, tranquility is said to be the means.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For the sage who wishes to ascend to (Dhyana-) yoga, action is said to be the means. For that person, once he has ascended to (Dhyana-)yoga, inaction alone is said to be the means.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For a sage who wishes to attain to Yoga, action is said to be the means; for the same sage who has attained Yoga, inaction is said to be the means.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For a sage, who is desirous of mounting upon the Yoga, action is said to be the cause; for the same sage, when he has mounted upon the Yoga, stillness is said to be the cause.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: For the sage who seeks the heights of spiritual meditation, practice is the only method, and when they have attained it, they must maintain themselves there through continual self-control.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.4।। जिस समय न इन्द्रियोंके भोगोंमें तथा न कर्मोंमें ही आसक्त होता है, उस समय वह सम्पूर्ण संकल्पोंका त्यागी मनुष्य योगारूढ़ कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.4।। जब (साधक) न इन्द्रियों के विषयों में और न कर्मों में आसक्त होता है तब सर्व संकल्पों के संन्यासी को योगारूढ़ कहा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For, when one loses attachment to the things of the senses and to actions, then they have abandoned all desires and are said to have climbed the heights of Yoga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Verily, when a man who has given up thought about everything does not get attached to sense-objects or actions, he is then said to be established in Yoga.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When a person is not attached to the sense-objects or to actions, having renounced all thoughts, then they are said to have attained Yoga.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When a person indulges neither in what is desired by the senses nor in the actions for it, then, being a man who has renounced all intentions, he is said to have mounted on the path of Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When a man renounces even the thought of initiating action, and is not interested in sense objects or any results that may flow from his acts, then he truly understands spirituality.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.5।। अपने द्वारा अपना उद्धार करे, अपना पतन न करे; क्योंकि आप ही अपना मित्र है और आप ही अपना शत्रु है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.5।। मनुष्य को अपने द्वारा अपना उद्धार करना चाहिये और अपना अध: पतन नहीं करना चाहिये; क्योंकि आत्मा ही आत्मा का मित्र है और आत्मा (मनुष्य स्वयं) ही आत्मा का (अपना) शत्रु है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: One should raise oneself by one's own mind and not allow oneself to sink; for the mind alone is the friend of the self, and the mind alone is the foe of the self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One should save oneself by oneself; one should not degrade oneself. For oneself is indeed one's own friend; oneself is indeed one's own enemy.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: One should raise oneself by one's own self alone; let not one lower oneself; for the self alone is one's own friend, and the self alone is one's own enemy.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Let a person lift themselves by themselves and let them not depress themselves. For, the self alone is the friend of the self and the self alone is the foe of the self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Let him seek liberation with the help of his Highest Self, and never disgrace his own Self. For that Self is his only friend; yet it can also be his enemy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.6।। जिसने अपने-आपसे अपने-आपको जीत लिया है, उसके लिये आप ही अपना बन्धु है और जिसने अपने-आपको नहीं जीता है, ऐसे अनात्माका आत्मा ही शत्रुतामें शत्रुकी तरह बर्ताव करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.6।। जिसने आत्मा (इंद्रियों,आदि) को आत्मा के द्वारा जीत लिया है, उस पुरुष का आत्मा उसका मित्र होता है, परन्तु अजितेन्द्रिय के लिए आत्मा शत्रु के समान स्थित होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The mind is the friend of him by whom it has been conquered. But for him whose mind is not conquered, the mind, like an enemy, remains hostile.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who has conquered his own self by the self, his self is his own friend. But for one who has not conquered his own self, his self itself acts like an enemy.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Self is the friend of the self of him by whom the Self has been conquered; but to the unconquered self, this Self stands in the position of an enemy, like an external foe.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Self is the friend of that Self by which the Self has been verily subdued; but in the case of a person with an unsubdued Self, the Self alone would abide in enmity like an enemy.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: To one who has conquered their lower nature with its help, the Self is a friend; but to one who has not done so, it is an enemy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.7।। जिसने अपने-आपपर अपनी विजय कर ली है, उस शीत-उष्ण (अनुकूलता-प्रतिकूलता) सुख-दुःख तथा मान-अपमानमें निर्विकार मनुष्यको परमात्मा नित्यप्राप्त हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.7।। शीत-उष्ण, सुख-दु:ख तथा मान-अपमान में जो प्रशान्त रहता है, ऐसे जितात्मा पुरुष के लिये परमात्मा सम्यक् प्रकार से स्थित है, अर्थात्, आत्मरूप से विद्यमान है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of him whose mind is conquered and who is serene, the great self is well-secured in heat and cold, in pleasure and pain, and in honor and dishonor.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The supreme Self of one who has control over the aggregate of his body and organs, and who is tranquil, becomes manifest. He should be poised in the midst of cold and heat, happiness and sorrow, as well as honor and dishonor.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Supreme Self of him who is self-controlled and peaceful remains balanced in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, as well as in honor and dishonor.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The thinking of the person, with a subdued mind and thus with complete calmness, remains in equilibrium in the case of others and himself, in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, as well as in honor and dishonor.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The Self of one who is self-controlled and has attained peace is equally unmoved by heat or cold, pleasure or pain, honor or dishonor.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.8।। जिसका अन्तःकरण ज्ञान-विज्ञानसे तृप्त है, जो कूटकी तरह निर्विकार है, जितेन्द्रिय है और मिट्टीके ढेले, पत्थर तथा स्वर्णमें समबुद्धिवाला है -- ऐसा योगी युक्त (योगारूढ़) कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.8।। जो योगी ज्ञान और विज्ञान से तृप्त है, जो विकार रहित (कूटस्थ) और जितेन्द्रिय है, जिसको मिट्टी, पाषाण और कंचन समान है, वह (परमात्मा से) युक्त कहलाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The yogin whose mind is content with knowledge of the self and also with knowledge of the difference between the self and Prakrti, who is established in the self, whose senses are subdued, and to whom earth, stone, and gold all seem alike, is called integrated.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One whose mind is satisfied with knowledge and realization, who is unmoved, who has his organs under control, is said to be Self-absorbed. The yogi treats all alike, a lump of earth, a stone, and gold.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Yogi who is satisfied with the knowledge and wisdom of the Self, who has conquered the senses, and to whom a clod of earth, a piece of stone, and gold are all the same, is said to have attained Nirvikalpa Samadhi.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He whose self is satisfied with knowledge and with what consists of varied thoughts; who remains peak-like and has completely subdued his senses; and to whom a clod, a stone, and a piece of gold are the same—that man of Yoga is called a master of Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who desires nothing but wisdom and spiritual insight, who has conquered his senses, and who looks upon a lump of earth, a stone, and fine gold with the same eye, is a real saint.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.9।। सुहृद्, मित्र, वैरी, उदासीन, मध्यस्थ, द्वेष्य और सम्बन्धियोंमें तथा साधु-आचरण करनेवालोंमें और पाप-आचरण करनेवालोंमें भी समबुद्धिवाला मनुष्य श्रेष्ठ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.9।। जो पुरुष सुहृद्, मित्र, शत्रु, उदासीन, मध्यस्थ, द्वेषी और बान्धवों में तथा धर्मात्माओं में और पापियों में भी समान भाव वाला है, वह श्रेष्ठ है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who regards with an equal eye well-wishers, friends, foes, the indifferent, neutrals, the hateful, the relations, and even the good and the sinful—he excels.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He excels who has sameness of view with regard to a benefactor, a friend, an enemy, a neutral, an arbiter, the hateful, a relative, good people, and even sinners.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who is of the same mind towards the good-hearted, friends, enemies, the indifferent, the neutral, the hateful, the relatives, the righteous, and the unrighteous, excels.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He whose mind is equal in the case of friend, companion, enemy, the indifferent one, the one who remains in the middle, the foe, the relative, the righteous, and also the sinful—he excels [all].

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He looks impartially on all—lovers, friends, or foes; indifferent, hostile, alien, or relative; virtuous or sinful.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.10।। भोगबुद्धिसे संग्रह न करनेवाला, इच्छारहित और अन्तःकरण तथा शरीरको वशमें रखनेवाला योगी अकेला एकान्तमें स्थित होकर मनको निरन्तर परमात्मामें लगाये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.10।। शरीर और मन को संयमित किया हुआ योगी एकान्त स्थान पर अकेला रहता हुआ आशा और परिग्रह से मुक्त होकर निरन्तर मन को आत्मा में स्थिर करे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The yogin should constantly fix his mind on yoga, remaining in a solitary place all alone, controlling his thoughts and mind, free from desire and a sense of possession.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A yogi should constantly concentrate his mind by staying in a solitary place, alone, with mind and body controlled, free from expectations, and free from acquisition.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Let the yogi constantly strive to keep the mind steady, remaining in solitude, alone, with the body and mind controlled, and free from hope and greed.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Let the man of Yoga always yoke the self (mind) by remaining alone in a lonely place, controlling his mind and body, without desire and without a sense of possession.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Let the student of spirituality try unceasingly to concentrate his mind; let him live in seclusion, absolutely alone, with his mind and personality controlled, free from desire and without possessions.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.11।। शुद्ध भूमिपर, जिसपर क्रमशः कुश, मृगछाला और वस्त्र बिछे हैं, जो न अत्यन्त ऊँचा है और न अत्यन्त नीचा, ऐसे अपने आसनको स्थिरस्थापन करके।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.11।। शुद्ध (स्वच्छ) भूमि में कुश, मृगशाला और उस पर वस्त्र रखा हो ऐसे अपने आसन को न अति ऊँचा और न अति नीचा स्थिर स्थापित करके....৷৷.।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Having established for himself, in a clean spot, a firm seat that is neither too high nor too low, and covering it with cloth, deer-skin, and Kusa grass in reverse order—

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Having firmly established in a clean place his seat, neither too high nor too low, composed of cloth, skin, and kusa-grass placed successively one below the other;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: In a clean spot, having established a firm seat of his own, neither too high nor too low, made of cloth, skin, and kusha grass layered one over the other.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Setting up in a clean place his own suitable, firm seat predominantly made of cloth, skin, and kusa-grass, neither too high nor too low for him;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having chosen a holy place, let him sit in a firm posture on a seat that is neither too high nor too low, and cover it with a grass mat, a deer skin, and a cloth.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.12।। उस आसनपर बैठकर चित्त और इन्द्रियोंकी क्रियाओंको वशमें रखते हुए मनको एकाग्र करके अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धिके लिये योगका अभ्यास करे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.12।। वहाँ (आसन में बैठकर) मन को एकाग्र करके, चित्त और इन्द्रियों की क्रियाओं को वश में किये हुये आत्मशुद्धि के लिए योग का अभ्यास करे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Sitting on the seat, with a concentrated mind, controlling the mind and senses, one should practice Yoga for the purification of the self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Sitting on that seat, he should concentrate his mind for the purification of the internal organ, making the mind one-pointed and keeping the actions of the mind and senses under control.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There, having made the mind one-pointed, with the actions of the mind and senses controlled, let him, seated on the seat, practice Yoga for the purification of the self.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Sitting there on the seat and making the mind single-pointed, let him, with the activities of his mind and senses subdued, practice Yoga for self-purification.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Seated thus, with his mind concentrated, his functions controlled, and his senses governed, let him practice meditation for the purification of his lower nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.13।। काया, शिर और ग्रीवाको सीधे अचल धारण करके तथा दिशाओंको न देखकर केवल अपनी नासिकाके अग्रभागको देखते हुए स्थिर होकर बैठे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.13।। काया, सिर और ग्रीवा को समान और अचल धारण किये हुए स्थिर होकर अपनी नासिका के अग्र भाग को देखकर अन्य दिशाओं को न देखता हुआ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Holding the trunk, head, and neck erect, motionless and steady, gazing at the tip of the nose and not looking in any direction;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Holding the body, head, and neck erect and still, being steady, looking at the tip of his own nose and not looking around.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Let him firmly hold his body, head, and neck erect and still, gazing at the tip of his nose without looking around.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Holding the body, head, and neck erect and motionless; remaining firm; looking properly at one's own nose-tip; and not looking in different directions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Let him hold his body, head, and neck erect, motionless and steady; let him look fixedly at the tip of his nose, turning neither to the right nor to the left.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.14।। जिसका अन्तःकरण शान्त है, जो भय-रहित है और जो ब्रह्मचारिव्रतमें स्थित है, ऐसा सावधान योगी मनका संयम करके मेरेमें चित्त लगाता हुआ मेरे परायण होकर बैठे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.14।। (साधक को) प्रशान्त अन्त:करण, निर्भय और ब्रह्मचर्य ब्रत में स्थित होकर, मन को संयमित करके चित्त को मुझमें लगाकर मुझे ही परम लक्ष्य समझकर बैठना चाहिए।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Serene and fearless, firm in their vow of celibacy, controlling their minds and fixing their thoughts on Me, they should sit in Yoga, intent on Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He should remain seated with a placid mind, free from fear, firm in the vow of celibacy, and with the mind fixed on Me by controlling it through concentration, having Me as the supreme goal.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Serene-minded, fearless, firm in the vow of a Brahmachari, having controlled their mind, thinking of Me and balanced in mind, let them sit, having Me as their supreme goal.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Being calm-minded, fearless, and firmly devoted to celibacy; controlling the mind completely; let the master of Yoga remain, fixing his mind on Me and having Me as his supreme goal.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: With peace in his heart and no fear, observing the vow of celibacy, with his mind controlled and fixed on Me, let the student lose himself in contemplation of Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.15।। नियत  मनवाला योगी मनको इस तरहसे सदा परमात्मामें लगाता हुआ मेरेमें सम्यक् स्थितिवाली जो निर्वाणपरमा शान्ति है, उसको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.15।। इस प्रकार सदा मन को स्थिर करने का प्रयास करता हुआ संयमित मन का योगी मुझमें स्थित परम निर्वाण (मोक्ष) स्वरूप शांति को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Applying his mind in this way, the Yogin of controlled mind, ever attains the peace which is the summit of beatitude and which abides in Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Concentrating the mind thus, forever, the yogi of a controlled mind achieves the peace that culminates in liberation and abides in Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Thus, always keeping the mind balanced, the yogi, with the mind controlled, attains the peace abiding in Me, culminating in liberation.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Yoking his mind incessantly in this manner, My devotee, with their mind not attached to anything else, realizes peace which culminates in nirvana and ends in Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thus, keeping his mind always in communion with Me and subduing his thoughts, he shall attain that Peace which is Mine and will lead him to liberation at last.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.16।। हे अर्जुन ! यह योग न तो अधिक खानेवालेका और न बिलकुल न खानेवालेका तथा न अधिक सोनेवालेका और न बिलकुल न सोनेवालेका ही सिद्ध होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.16।। परन्तु, हे अर्जुन ! यह योग उस पुरुष के लिए सम्भव नहीं होता, जो अधिक खाने वाला है या बिल्कुल न खाने वाला है तथा जो अधिक सोने वाला है या सदा जागने वाला है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Yoga is not for him who overeats, nor for him who fasts excessively; not for him, O Arjuna, who sleeps too much, nor for him who stays awake too long.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But, O Arjuna, Yoga is not for one who eats excessively, nor for one who does not eat at all; neither for one who habitually sleeps too long, nor for one who stays awake.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Verily, Yoga is not possible for him who eats too much, nor for him who does not eat at all, nor for him who sleeps too much, nor for him who is always awake, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Yoga is neither for him who eats too much, nor for him who totally abstains from eating, nor for him who is prone to sleeping too much, nor for him who keeps awake too much.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Meditation is not for him who eats too much, nor for him who eats not at all; not for him who is overly addicted to sleep, nor for him who is always awake.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.17।। दुःखोंका नाश करनेवाला योग तो यथायोग्य आहार और विहार करनेवालेका, कर्मोंमें यथायोग्य चेष्टा करनेवालेका तथा यथायोग्य सोने और जागनेवालेका ही सिद्ध होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.17।। उस पुरुष के लिए योग दु:खनाशक होता है, जो युक्त आहार और विहार करने वाला है, यथायोग्य चेष्टा करने वाला है और परिमित शयन और जागरण करने वाला है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Yoga becomes the destroyer of sorrows for those who are temperate in food and recreation, temperate in their actions, and temperate in their sleep and wakefulness.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Yoga becomes a destroyer of sorrow for one whose eating and movements are regulated, whose effort in works is moderate, and whose sleep and wakefulness are temperate.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Yoga becomes the destroyer of pain for him who is moderate in eating and recreation (such as walking, etc.), who exercises moderation in action, and who is moderate in sleep and wakefulness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Yoga becomes a misery-killer for him whose effort for food is appropriate, exertion in activities is proper, and whose sleep and waking are proportionate.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But for him who regulates his food and recreation, who is balanced in action, in sleep, and in waking, it shall dispel all unhappiness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.18।। वशमें किया हुआ चित्त जिस कालमें अपने स्वरूपमें ही स्थित हो जाता है और स्वयं सम्पूर्ण पदार्थोंसे निःस्पृह हो जाता है, उस कालमें वह योगी है - ऐसा कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.18।। वश में किया हुआ चित्त जिस कालमें अपने स्वरुपमें ही स्थित हो जाता है और स्वयं सम्पूर्ण पदार्थों नि: स्पृह हो जाता है, उस कालमें वह योगी कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: When the subdued mind rests solely on the Self, then, free from all yearning for objects of desire, one is said to be fit for Yoga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A person who has become free from hankering for all desirable objects is said to be Self-absorbed when the controlled mind rests solely in the Self.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When the perfectly controlled mind rests in the Self alone, free from longing for any of the objects of desire, then it is said, 'He is united'.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When his mind, well-controlled, is established in nothing but the Self, and he is free from craving for any desired object—at that time he is called a master of Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When the mind is completely controlled, centered in the Self, and free from all earthly desires, then the man is truly spiritual.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.19।। जैसे स्पन्दनरहित वायुके स्थानमें स्थित दीपककी लौ चेष्टारहित हो जाती है, योगका अभ्यास करते हुए वश में किए हुए चित्तवाले योगीके चित्तकी वैसी ही उपमा कही गयी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.19।। जैसे स्पन्दनरहित वायुके स्थानमें स्थित दीपककी लौ चेष्टारहित हो जाती है, योगका अभ्यास करते हुए यतचित्तवाले योगीके चित्तकी वैसी ही उपमा कही गयी है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: A lamp does not flicker in a windless place"—that is the simile used for the tranquil mind of a yogi who practices yoga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: As a lamp kept in a windless place does not flicker, so is the simile for the yogi whose mind is under control and who is engaged in concentration on the Self.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: As a lamp placed in a windless spot does not flicker, so is the Yogi of a controlled mind, who practices Yoga in the Self, compared.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Just as a lamp in a windless place does not shake, this simile is recalled in the case of the man of Yoga, with a subdued mind, practicing Yoga in the Self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The wise man who has conquered his mind and is absorbed in the Self is like a lamp that does not flicker, as it stands sheltered from every wind.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.20।। योगका सेवन करनेसे जिस अवस्थामें निरुद्ध चित्त उपराम हो जाता है तथा जिस अवस्थामें स्वयं अपने-आप से अपने-आपको देखता हुआ अपने-आपमें ही सन्तुष्ट हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.20।। योगका सेवन करनेसे जिस अवस्थामें निरुध्द चित्त उपराम हो जाता है तथा जिस अवस्थामें स्वयं अपने-आपमें अपने-आपको देखता हुआ अपने-आपमें सन्तुष्ट हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Where the mind, controlled through the practice of Yoga, rests, and where one sees the Self by the Self and is delighted by the Self alone.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: At the time when the mind, restrained through the practice of Yoga, is withdrawn, and just when one sees the Self by the self and remains contented in the Self alone;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When the mind, restrained by the practice of yoga, attains quietude, and when one sees the Self by the Self, they are satisfied in their own Self.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Where the mind, restrained through yoga practice, remains still; and where, observing, the yogi finds satisfaction in the Self alone, by perceiving only the Self within.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There, where the whole of nature is seen in the light of the Self, where the person abides within their Self and is satisfied there, their functions restrained by their union with the Divine, the mind finds rest.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.21।। जो सुख आत्यन्तिक, अतीन्द्रिय और बुद्धिग्राह्य है, उस सुखका जिस अवस्थामें अनुभव करता है और जिस सुखमें स्थित हुआ यह ध्यानयोगी फिर कभी तत्त्वसे विचलित नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.21।। जो सुख आत्यन्तिक, अतीन्द्रिय और बुध्दिग्राह्म है, उस सुखका जिस अवस्थामें अनुभव करता है और जिस सुखमें स्थित हुआ यह ध्यानयोगी फिर कभी तत्वसे विचलित नहीं होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Where one knows that infinite happiness which can be grasped by the intellect but is beyond the reach of the senses, and is established in that condition, one does not swerve from it.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When one experiences that absolute Bliss, which can be intuited by the intellect and which is beyond the senses, and being thus established, this person surely does not swerve from Reality.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When he (the Yogi) feels that infinite bliss which can be grasped by the pure intellect and which transcends the senses, and is established therein, never moving away from the reality.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Where he realizes the limitless Bliss, which is to be grasped by intellect and is beyond the senses; remaining where he does not depart from the Reality.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When he enjoys the bliss that surpasses the senses and can only be grasped by the pure intellect, when he comes to rest within his highest self, he will never again stray from reality.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.22।। जिस लाभकी प्राप्ति होनेपर उससे अधिक कोई दूसरा लाभ उसके माननेमें भी नहीं आता और जिसमें स्थित होनेपर वह बड़े भारी दुःखसे भी विचलित नहीं किया जा सकता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.22।। जिस लाभकी प्राप्ति होनेपर उससे अधिक कोई दूसरा लाभ उसके माननेमें भी नहीं आता और जिसमें स्थित होनेपर वह बड़े भारी दु:ख से भी विचलित नहीं होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Having gained it, one holds that there is no greater gain; being established in it, one is not moved even by the heaviest sorrow.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Obtaining which one does not consider any other acquisition to be superior, and being established in which one is not perturbed even by great sorrow;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having obtained it, he thinks there is no other gain superior to it; established in it, he is not moved even by heavy sorrow.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: And having attained it, he does not think of any other gain as superior to that; being established in it, he is not shaken much by misery, however powerful it may be.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Finding That, he will realize that there is no possession so precious. And once established there, no calamity can disturb him.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.23।। जिसमें दुःखोंके संयोगका ही वियोग है, उसीको 'योग' नामसे जानना चाहिये। (वह योग जिस ध्यानयोगका लक्ष्य है,) उस ध्यानयोगका अभ्यास न उकताये हुए चित्तसे निश्चयपूर्वक करना चाहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.23।। दु:ख के संयोग से वियोग है, उसीको 'योग' नामसे जानना चाहिये । (वह योग जिस ध्यानयोग लक्ष्य है,) उस ध्यानयोका अभ्यास न उकताये हुए चित्तसे   निश्चयपूर्वक करना चाहिये।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know this deliverance from association with misery to be Yoga. This Yoga must be practiced with determination and with a mind free from despondency.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One should know that severance of contact with sorrow to be what is called Yoga. That Yoga should be practiced with perseverance and with an undepressed heart.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Let this be known by the name of Yoga, the severance from union with pain. This Yoga should be practiced with determination and with an undespairing mind.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: That he would realize to be the cause for his cessation of contact with misery and to be the one made known by Yoga. With determination, that is to be yoked in Yoga by a person of undepressed mind (or of the depressed mind).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: This inner severance from the affliction of misery is spirituality. It should be practiced with determination and with a heart that refuses to be depressed.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.24।। संकल्पसे उत्पन्न होनेवाली सम्पूर्ण कामनाओंका सर्वथा त्याग करके और मनसे ही इन्द्रिय-समूहको सभी ओरसे हटाकर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.24।। संकल्प से उत्पन्न समस्त कामनाओं को नि:शेष रूप से परित्याग कर मन के द्वारा इन्द्रिय समुदाय को सब ओर से सम्यक् प्रकार वश में करके।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Renouncing entirely all desires born of volition and restraining the mind from all the senses on all sides,

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: By totally eschewing all desires that arise from thoughts and restraining all the organs with the mind from every side;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Abandoning unreservedly all desires born of Sankalpa (thought and imagination) and completely restraining the whole group of senses by the mind from all sides.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: In order to renounce completely all desires that are born of intention, let a person restrain the group of sense-organs from all sides by mind alone.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Renouncing every desire that can be imagined, controlling the senses at every point through the power of the mind;

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.25।। धैर्ययुक्त बुद्धिके द्वारा संसारसे धीरे-धीरे उपराम हो जाय और परमात्मस्वरूपमें मन-(बुद्धि-) को सम्यक् प्रकारसे स्थापन करके फिर कुछ भी चिन्तन न करे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.25।। शनै: शनै: धैर्ययुक्त बुद्धि के द्वारा (योगी) उपरामता (शांति) को प्राप्त होवे;  मन को आत्मा में स्थित करके फिर अन्य कुछ भी चिन्तन न करे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Little by little, one should withdraw oneself from objects other than the self, with the help of the intellect held by firm resolution; and then one should think of nothing else, having fixed the mind upon the Self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One should gradually withdraw with the intellect, endowed with steadiness. Making the mind fixed in the Self, one should not think of anything at all.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Little by little, let him attain steadiness of the intellect by holding it firmly; having made the mind establish itself in the Self, let him not think of anything else.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Very slowly remain still, keeping the mind well established in the Self by means of the intellect held in steadiness; and let him not think of anything (object).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Little by little, with the help of his reason controlled by fortitude, let him attain peace; and, fixing his mind on the Self, let him not think of anything else.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.26।। यह अस्थिर और चञ्चल मन जहाँ-जहाँ विचरण करता है, वहाँ-वहाँसे हटाकर इसको एक परमात्मामें ही  लगाये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.26।। यह चंचल और अस्थिर मन जिन कारणों से (विषयों में) विचरण करता है, उनसे संयमित करके उसे आत्मा के ही वश में लावे अर्थात् आत्मा में स्थिर करे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Wherever the fickle and unsteady mind wanders, one should subdue it and then bring it back under the control of the self alone.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The yogi should bring this mind under the subjugation of the Self itself, restraining it from all those causes that cause the restless, unsteady mind to wander away.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: From whatever cause the restless and unsteady mind wanders away, let him restrain it from that and bring it under the control of the Self alone.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: By whatever things the shaky and unsteady mind strays, let him restrain it from those things and bring it back under the control of the Self alone.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When the volatile and wavering mind wanders, let him restrain it and bring it back to its allegiance to the Self.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.27।। जिसके सब पाप नष्ट हो गये हैं, जिसका रजोगुण तथा मन सर्वथा शान्त(निर्मल) हो गया है, ऐसे इस ब्रह्मस्वरूप योगीको निश्चित ही उत्तम (सात्त्विक) सुख प्राप्त होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.27।। जिसका मन प्रशान्त है, जो पापरहित (अकल्मषम्) है और जिसका रजोगुण (विक्षेप) शांत हुआ है, ऐसे ब्रह्मरूप हुए इस योगी को उत्तम सुख प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For supreme happiness comes to the Yogin whose mind is at peace, who is free from evil, from whom Rajas has departed, and who has become the Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Supreme Bliss comes to this yogi alone whose mind has become perfectly tranquil, whose quality of rajas has been eliminated, who has become identified with Brahman, and is untainted.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Supreme Bliss indeed comes to this Yogi whose mind is made peaceful, whose passion is quelled, who has become Brahman, and who is free from sin.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Indeed, the Supreme Bliss comes to this highly tranquil-minded man of Yoga, whose passions remain stilled, who has become one with the Brahman, and who is free from sins.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Supreme bliss is the lot of the sage whose mind attains peace, whose passions subside, who is without sin, and who becomes one with the Absolute.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.28।। इस प्रकार अपने-आपको सदा परमात्मामें लगाता हुआ पापरहित योगी सुखपूर्वक ब्रह्मप्राप्तिरूप अत्यन्त सुखको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.28।। इस प्रकार मन को सदा आत्मा में स्थिर करने का योग करने वाला पापरहित योगी सुखपूर्वक ब्रह्मसंस्पर्श का परम सुख प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus, devoting himself to the Yoga of the Self, freed from impurities, the yogin easily attains the supreme bliss of contact with the Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: By constantly concentrating his mind in this way, the taintless yogi easily attains the absolute bliss of contact with Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The yogi, always engaging the mind thus (in the practice of yoga), is freed from sins and easily enjoys the infinite bliss of contact with Brahman (the Eternal).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, yoking the self always, the man of Yoga, with a subdued mind, easily attains complete union, that is, the Brahman.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thus, free from sin, abiding always in the Eternal, the saint enjoys, without effort, the bliss that flows from the realization of the Infinite.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.29।। सब जगह अपने स्वरूपको देखनेवाला और ध्यानयोगसे युक्त अन्तःकरणवाला योगा अपने स्वरूपको सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें स्थित देखता है और सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंको अपने स्वरूपमें देखता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.29।। योगयुक्त अन्त:करण वाला और सर्वत्र समदर्शी योगी आत्मा को सब भूतों में और भूतमात्र को आत्मा में देखता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He whose mind is fixed in Yoga sees reality everywhere; he sees his Self as abiding in all beings and all beings in his Self.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who has their mind Self-absorbed through Yoga, and who has the vision of sameness everywhere, sees this Self existing in everything, and everything in their Self.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: With the mind harmonized by Yoga, he sees the Self abiding in all beings and all beings in the Self; he sees the same everywhere.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who has yoked himself in Yoga and observes everything truly perceives the Self to be abiding in all beings and all beings to be abiding in the Self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who experiences the unity of life sees his own Self in all beings, and all beings in his own Self, and looks upon everything with an impartial eye.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.30।। जो सबमें मुझे देखता है और सबको मुझमें देखता है, उसके लिये मैं अदृश्य नहीं होता और वह मेरे लिये अदृश्य नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.30।। जो पुरुष मुझे सर्वत्र देखता है और सबको मुझमें देखता है, उसके लिए मैं नष्ट नहीं होता (अर्थात् उसके लिए मैं दूर नहीं होता) और वह मुझसे वियुक्त नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: To him who sees Me in every self and every self in Me—I am not lost to him, nor is he lost to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who sees Me in everything and sees all things in Me—I do not lose sight of him, and he also is not lost to My vision.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, never becomes separated from Me, nor do I from him.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who observes Me in all and observes all in Me—for him, I am not lost, and he too is not lost for me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who sees Me in everything and everything in Me, I shall never forsake him, nor shall he lose Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.31।। मेरेमें एकीभावसे स्थित हुआ जो योगी सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें स्थित मेरा भजन करता है, वह सब कुछ बर्ताव करता हुआ भी मेरेमें ही बर्ताव कर रहा है अर्थात् वह सर्वथा मेरेमें ही स्थित है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.31।। जो पुरुष एकत्वभाव मंे स्थित हुआ सम्पूर्ण भूतों में स्थित मुझे भजता है, वह योगी सब प्रकार से वर्तता हुआ (रहता हुआ) मुझमें स्थित रहता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The yogi who, fixed in oneness, worships Me dwelling in all beings—he abides in Me, no matter how he may live.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That yogi, who, being established in unity, adores Me as existing in all things, exists in Me—in whatever condition he may be.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who, being established in unity, worships Me, who dwells in all beings, that yogi abides in Me, whatever their mode of living may be.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who is firmly established in the oneness of Me, experiences Me immanent in all beings—that man of Yoga is never stained, no matter what stage he may be in.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sage who realizes the unity of life and who worships Me in all beings, lives in Me, no matter what their lot may be.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.32।। हे अर्जुन ! जो (ध्यानयुक्त ज्ञानी महापुरुष) अपने शरीरकी उपमासे सब जगह अपनेको समान देखता है और सुख अथवा दुःखको भी समान देखता है, वह परम योगी माना गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.32।। हे अर्जुन ! जो पुरुष अपने समान सर्वत्र सम देखता है, चाहे वह सुख हो या दु:ख, वह परम योगी माना गया है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who, due to the similarity of selves everywhere, sees pleasure or pain as the same everywhere—that yogi, O Arjuna, is considered the closest.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, that yogi is considered the best who judges what is happiness and sorrow in all beings by the same standard as they would apply to themselves.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who, through the likeness of the Self, O Arjuna, sees reality everywhere, be it pleasure or pain, is regarded as the highest Yogi.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever finds pleasure or pain equally in all, as if it were in themselves—that person is considered to be a great man of Yoga, O Arjuna!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! He is the perfect saint who, having been taught by the likeness within himself, sees the same Self everywhere, regardless of whether the outer form be pleasurable or painful.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.33।। अर्जुन बोले -- हे मधुसूदन ! आपने समतापूर्वक जो यह योग कहा है, मनकी चञ्चलताके कारण मैं इस योगकी स्थिर स्थिति नहीं देखता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.33।। अर्जुन ने कहा --  हे मधुसूदन ! जो यह साम्य योग आपने कहा, मैं मन के चंचल होने से इसकी चिरस्थायी स्थिति को नहीं देखता हूं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, this Yoga of reality, which You have declared, I do not see how it can be steady due to the fickleness of the mind."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Madhusudana (Krsna), this Yoga that You have spoken of as sameness, I do not see its steady continuance due to the restlessness of the mind."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krishna, I do not see how this Yoga of equanimity, which you have taught me, can be maintained steadily, due to the restlessness of the mind."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "O slayer of Mandhu, this Yoga of equanimity which You have spoken of, I do not find a proper foundation for it due to the unsteadiness of the mind."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna said: I do not see how I can attain this state of equanimity which You have revealed, owing to the restlessness of my mind.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.34।। क्योंकि हे कृष्ण ! मन बड़ा ही चञ्चल, प्रमथनशील, दृढ़ (जिद्दी) और बलवान् है। उसका निग्रह करना मैं वायुकी तरह अत्यन्त कठिन मानता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.34।। क्योंकि हे कृष्ण ! यह मन चंचल और प्रमथन स्वभाव का तथा बलवान् और दृढ़ है; उसका निग्रह करना मैं वायु के समान अति दुष्कर मानता हूँ ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For the mind is fickle, O Krsna, impetuous, powerful, and stubborn; I think that restraining it is as difficult as restraining the wind.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For, O Kṛṣṇa, the mind is unsteady, turbulent, strong, and obstinate; I consider its control to be as difficult as that of the wind.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The mind is indeed restless, turbulent, strong, and unyielding, O Krishna; I consider it as difficult to control as controlling the wind.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Krsna! The mind is indeed unsteady, destructive, strong, and obstinate; I believe it is very difficult to control it, just as it is to control the wind.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: My Lord! Verily, the mind is fickle and turbulent, obstinate, and strong; indeed, it is extremely difficult to control, like the wind.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.35।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हे महाबाहो ! यह मन बड़ा चञ्चल है और इसका निग्रह करना भी बड़ा कठिन है -- यह तुम्हारा कहना बिलकुल ठीक है। परन्तु हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! अभ्यास और वैराग्यके द्वारा इसका निग्रह किया जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.35।। श्रीभगवान् कहते हैं --  हे महबाहो ! नि:सन्देह मन चंचल और कठिनता से वश में होने वाला है; परन्तु, हे कुन्तीपुत्र ! उसे अभ्यास और वैराग्य के द्वारा वश में किया जा सकता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "The mind is indeed hard to subdue and fickle, O mighty-armed one, but, O son of Kunti, it can be brought under control through practice and the exercise of dispassion."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, O mighty-armed one, undoubtedly the mind is untamable and restless. But, O son of Kunti, it can be brought under control through practice and detachment.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed Arjuna, the mind is difficult to control and restless; but with practice and dispassion, it can be restrained."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, O mighty-armed one! No doubt, the mind is unsteady and hard to control. But it can be controlled through practice and an attitude of desirelessness, O son of Kunti!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: Doubtless, O Mighty One, the mind is fickle and exceedingly difficult to restrain; however, O Son of Kunti, with practice and renunciation, it can be done.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.36।। जिसका मन पूरा वशमें नहीं है, उसके द्वारा योग प्राप्त होना कठिन है। परन्तु उपायपूर्वक यत्न करनेवाले वश्यात्माको योग प्राप्त हो सकता है, ऐसा मेरा मत है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.36।। असंयत मन के पुरुष द्वारा योग प्राप्त होना कठिन है, परन्तु स्वाधीन मन वाले प्रयत्नशील पुरुष द्वारा उपाय से योग प्राप्त होना संभव है, यह मेरा मत है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: In my opinion, Yoga is hard to attain by a person of an unrestrained mind; however, it can be attained through the right means by one who strives for it and has a subdued mind.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: My conviction is that Yoga is difficult to attain for one with an uncontrolled mind. But it is possible to attain through the aforementioned means for one who strives and has a controlled mind.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I think Yoga is hard to be attained by one with an uncontrolled self, but the self-controlled and striving one can attain it by the appropriate means.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: My belief is that attaining Yoga is difficult for a person with an uncontrolled mind; but it is possible to attain by proper means with a subdued self.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is not possible to attain Self-Realization unless one knows how to control oneself; however, for one who, striving by the proper means, learns such control, it is possible.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.37।। अर्जुन बोले - हे कृष्ण ! जिसकी साधनमें श्रद्धा है, पर जिसका प्रयत्न शिथिल है, वह अन्तसमयमें अगर योगसे विचलितमना हो जाय, तो वह योगसिद्धिको प्राप्त न करके किस गतिको चला जाता है?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.37।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- हे कृष्ण ! जिसका मन योग से चलायमान हो गया है, ऐसा अपूर्ण प्रयत्न वाला (अयति) श्रद्धायुक्त पुरुष योग की सिद्धि को न प्राप्त होकर किस गति को प्राप्त होता है?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "If a person who is possessed of faith but has put in only inadequate effort finds his mind wandering away from Yoga, and then fails to attain perfection—what way does he go, O Krishna?"

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, what goal does one attain who, despite having faith, fails to achieve perfection in Yoga due to lack of diligence, and whose mind becomes deflected from Yoga?"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "He who is unable to control himself, even though he has faith, and whose mind wanders away from Yoga, what end does he meet, having failed to attain perfection in Yoga, O Krishna?"

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "A person who has faith and is desirous of reaching the path of the good, but whose mind has been severed from Yoga—to which goal does he go, having failed to attain success in Yoga? O Krsna!"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna asked: What of him who fails to control himself, whose mind falls from spiritual contemplation, yet retains his faith and does not attain perfection?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.38।। हे महाबाहो ! संसारके आश्रयसे रहित और परमात्मप्राप्तिके मार्गमें मोहित अर्थात् विचलित -- इस तरह दोनों ओरसे भ्रष्ट हुआ साधक क्या छिन्न-भिन्न बादलकी तरह नष्ट तो नहीं हो जाता ?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.38।। हे महबाहो ! क्या वह ब्रह्म के मार्ग में मोहित तथा आश्रयरहित पुरुष छिन्न-भिन्न मेघ के समान दोनों ओर से भ्रष्ट हुआ नष्ट तो नहीं हो जाता है?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Without any support, confused in the path leading to the Brahman, and thus having fallen from both, does he not perish, O mighty-armed one, like a rent cloud?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Mighty-armed one, having fallen from both [paths], without support, deluded on the path to Brahman, does he not get ruined like a scattered cloud?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Fallen from both, does he not perish like a rent cloud, supportless, O mighty-armed one, deluded on the path of Brahman?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Does he, fallen from both, get lost like a broken cloud? Or, O mighty-armed one! Having no support, does he meet total destruction?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having failed in both, my Lord, is he without hope, like a riven cloud with no support, lost on the spiritual path?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.39।। हे कृष्ण! मेरे इस सन्देहका सर्वथा छेदन करनेके लिये आप ही योग्य हैं; क्योंकि इस संशयका छेदन करनेवाला आपके सिवाय दूसरा कोई हो नहीं सकता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.39।। हे कृष्ण ! मेरे इस संशय को नि:शेष रूप से छेदन (निराकरण) करने के लिए आप ही योग्य है; क्योंकि आपके अतिरिक्त अन्य कोई इस संशय का छेदन करन वाला (छेत्ता) मिलना संभव नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: O Krsna, you should altogether remove this doubt of mine, for there is no other remover of this doubt than you.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Krsna, you should totally eradicate this doubt of mine, for none other than yourself can be the dispeller of this doubt!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: O Krishna, please completely dispel this doubt of mine, for it is not possible for anyone but You to do so.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Please dispel this doubt of mine completely. But for You, O Krsna, no one else is capable of eradicating this doubt.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: My Lord! You are worthy to solve this doubt once and for all; there is no one else competent to do so, save Yourself.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.40।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हे पृथानन्दन ! उसका न तो इस लोकमें और न परलोकमें ही विनाश होता है; क्योंकि हे प्यारे ! कल्याणकारी काम करनेवाला कोई भी मनुष्य दुर्गतिको प्राप्त नहीं जाता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.40।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -- हे पार्थ ! उस पुरुष का, न तो इस लोक में और न ही परलोक में ही नाश होता है; हे तात ! कोई भी शुभ कर्म करने वाला दुर्गति को नहीं प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "Neither here in this world nor there in the next is there destruction for him. For no one who does good ever comes to an evil end."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "O Partha, there is certainly no ruin for him here or hereafter, for no one engaged in good meets with a deplorable end, my son!"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "O Arjuna, neither in this world nor in the next will there be destruction for him; none, indeed, who does good, O my son, ever comes to grief."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "O dear Partha! Neither in this world nor in the other is there destruction for him. Certainly, no one who performs an auspicious act ever comes to a grievous state."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: My beloved child! There is no destruction for him, either in this world or the next. No evil fate awaits him who treads the path of righteousness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.41।। वह योगभ्रष्ट पुण्यकर्म करनेवालोंके लोकोंको प्राप्त होकर और वहाँ बहुत वर्षोंतक रहकर फिर यहाँ शुद्ध  श्रीमानोंके घरमें जन्म लेता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.41।। योगभ्रष्ट पुरुष पुण्यवानों के लोकों को प्राप्त होकर वहाँ दीर्घकाल तक वास करके शुद्ध आचरण वाले श्रीमन्त (धनवान) पुरुषों के घर में जन्म लेता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who has fallen away from Yoga is born again in the house of the pure and prosperous, having attained to the worlds of doers of good deeds and dwelled there for many long years.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Attaining the worlds of the righteous and residing there for eternal years, the man who has fallen from Yoga is born in the house of the pious and prosperous.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having attained to the worlds of the righteous and having dwelt there for everlasting years, he who fell from Yoga is born in a house of the pure and wealthy.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having attained the worlds of those who perform pious acts, and having resided there for years of eternity, the one who has fallen from Yoga is born again in the house of the pure and wealthy.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having reached the worlds where the righteous dwell, and having remained there for many years, he who has strayed from the path of spirituality will be born again into the family of the pure, benevolent, and prosperous.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.42।। अथवा (वैराग्यवान्) योगभ्रष्ट ज्ञानवान् योगियोंके कुलमें ही जन्म लेता है। इस प्रकारका जो यह जन्म है, यह संसारमें बहुत ही दुर्लभ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.42।। अथवा, (साधक) ज्ञानवान् योगियों के ही कुल में जन्म लेता है, परन्तु इस प्रकार का जन्म इस लोक में नि:संदेह अति दुर्लभ है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Or he is born into a family of wise yogis. But such a birth in this world is rare.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Or, he is born in the family of wise yogis. Such a birth is indeed more difficult to come by in the world.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Or he is born in a family of even the wisest of yogis; verily, such a birth is very difficult to obtain in this world.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Or, he is born nowhere else but in the family of the intelligent men of Yoga; for, this birth is more difficult to obtain in the world.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Or, he may be born in the family of wise sages, though such a birth is indeed very difficult to obtain.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.43।। हे कुरुनन्दन ! वहाँपर उसको पूर्वजन्मकृत साधन-सम्पत्ति अनायास ही प्राप्त हो जाती है। फिर उससे वह साधनकी सिद्धिके विषयमें पुनः विशेषतासे यत्न करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.43।। हे कुरुनन्दन ! वह पुरुष वहाँ पूर्व देह में प्राप्त किये गये ज्ञान से सम्पन्न होकर योगसंसिद्धि के लिए उससे भी अधिक प्रयत्न करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There, O Arjuna, he regains the disposition of mind that he had in his former body, and from there he strives even more for success in Yoga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: There he becomes endowed with the wisdom acquired in the previous body, and he strives even more than before for perfection, O scion of the Kuru dynasty.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Then he comes into contact with the knowledge acquired in his former body and strives even more for perfection, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: There, in that life, he regains the link of mentality transmitted from his former body. Consequently, once again he strives for full success, O rejoicer of the Kurus!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Then, the experience acquired in his former life will revive, and with it, he will more eagerly strive for perfection than before.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.44।। वह (श्रीमानोंके घरमें जन्म लेनेवाला योगभ्रष्ट मनुष्य) भोगोंके परवश होता हुआ भी पूर्वजन्ममें किये हुए अभ्यास-(साधन-) के कारण ही परमात्माकी तरफ खिंच जाता है; क्योंकि योग-(समता-) का जिज्ञासु भी वेदोंमें कहे हुए सकाम कर्मोंका अतिक्रमण कर जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.44।। उसी पूर्वाभ्यास के कारण वह अवश हुआ योग की ओर आकर्षित होता है। योग का जो केवल जिज्ञासु है वह शब्दब्रह्म का अतिक्रमण करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: By the power of his earlier practice, he is carried forward even against his will. Even though he is an enquirer about Yoga, he transcends the Sabda-Brahman, i.e., Prakṛti or matter.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For, by that very past practice, he is carried forward even in spite of himself! Even a seeker of Yoga transcends the results of the performance of Vedic rituals!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: By that same former practice, he is borne on in spite of himself. Even he who merely wishes to know Yoga goes beyond the Brahmanic word.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Not being a master of himself, he is only dragged by that former practice (its mental impression); only a seeker of the knowledge of Yoga passes beyond what strengthens the [sacred] text.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Unconsciously, he will return to the practices of his old life; thus, one who strives to attain spiritual consciousness is certainly superior to one who merely talks about it.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.45।। परन्तु जो योगी प्रयत्नपूर्वक यत्न करता है और जिसके पाप नष्ट हो गये हैं तथा जो अनेक जन्मोंसे सिद्ध हुआ है, वह योगी फिर परमगतिको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.45।। परन्तु प्रयत्नपूर्वक अभ्यास करने वाला योगी सम्पूर्ण पापों से शुद्ध होकर अनेक जन्मों से (शनै: शनै:) सिद्ध होता हुआ, तब परम गति को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But the yogi, striving earnestly, cleansed of all his stains and perfected through many births, reaches the supreme state.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: However, the yogi, applying himself assiduously, becoming purified from sin and attaining perfection through many births, they achieve the highest Goal.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But the Yogi who strives assiduously, purified of sins and perfected gradually over many births, reaches the highest goal.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: After that, the assiduous man of Yoga, having his sins completely cleansed and being perfected through many births, reaches the Supreme Goal.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Then, after many lives of earnest striving and absolution of sins, the student of spirituality attains perfection and reaches the Supreme.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.46।। (सकामभाववाले) तपस्वियोंसे भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है, ज्ञानियोंसे भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है और कर्मियोंसे भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है -- ऐसा मेरा मत है। अतः हे अर्जुन ! तू योगी हो जा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.46।। क्योंकि योगी तपस्वियों से श्रेष्ठ है और (केवल शास्त्र के) ज्ञान वालों से भी श्रेष्ठ माना गया है तथा कर्म करने वालों से भी योगी श्रेष्ठ है, इसलिए हे अर्जुन तुम योगी बनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Greater than the ascetics, greater than those who possess knowledge, greater than the ritualists is the Yogin. Therefore, O Arjuna, become a Yogin.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A yogi is higher than those of austerity; they are considered higher even than those of knowledge. The yogi is also higher than those of action. Therefore, O Arjuna, become a yogi.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The yogi is thought to be superior to the ascetics, even superior to those who have knowledge obtained through the study of scriptures; he is also superior to men of action; therefore, be thou a yogi, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The person of Yoga is superior to the persons of austerities and is considered superior even to the persons of knowledge; and the person of Yoga is superior to the persons of action. Therefore, O Arjuna! you should become a person of Yoga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The wise person is superior to the ascetic, the scholar, and the man of action; therefore, be thou a wise person, O Arjuna!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।6.47।। सम्पूर्ण योगियोंमें भी जो श्रद्धावान् भक्त मुझमें तल्लीन हुए मनसे मेरा भजन करता है, वह मेरे मतमें सर्वश्रेष्ठ योगी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।6.47।। समस्त योगियों में जो भी श्रद्धावान् योगी मुझ में युक्त हुये अन्तरात्मा से (अर्थात् एकत्व भाव से मुझे भजता है, वह मुझे युक्ततम (सर्वश्रेष्ठ) मान्य है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who, with faith, worships Me, and whose innermost self is fixed in Me, I consider him to be the greatest of the Yogins.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even among all the yogis, he who adores Me with his mind fixed on Me and with faith is considered by Me to be the best of the yogis.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And among all the Yogis, he who, full of faith and with his inner self merged in Me, worships Me is deemed by Me to be the most devoted.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who has faith and serves Me with his inner self devoted to Me, is considered by Me to be the best of all yogis.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I look upon him as the best of mystics, who, full of faith, worships Me and abides in Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.1।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हे पृथानन्दन ! मुझमें आसक्त मनवाला, मेरे आश्रित होकर योगका अभ्यास करता हुआ तू मेरे समग्ररूपको निःसन्देह जैसा जानेगा, उसको सुन।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.1।। हे पार्थ ! मुझमें असक्त हुए मन वाले तथा मदाश्रित होकर योग का अभ्यास करते हुए जिस प्रकार तुम मुझे समग्ररूप से, बिना किसी संशय के, जानोगे वह सुनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "With your mind focused on Me, taking Me as your support, and practicing yoga—how can you, without doubt, know Me fully? Hear, O Arjuna."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "O Partha, hear how, with your mind fixed on Me, practicing the Yoga of Meditation and taking refuge in Me, you will certainly and fully know Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "O Arjuna, hear how you shall, without doubt, know Me fully, with your mind intent on Me, practicing Yoga and taking refuge in Me."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "O son of Prtha, hear from Me how, with your mind attached to Me, practicing yoga, and taking refuge in Me, you shall understand Me fully, without any doubt."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna said: Listen, O Arjuna! I will tell you how you can know Me in my full perfection. Practice meditation with your mind devoted to Me and take Me as your refuge.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.2।। तेरे लिये मैं विज्ञानसहित ज्ञान सम्पूर्णतासे कहूँगा, जिसको जाननेके बाद फिर यहाँ कुछ भी जानना बाकी नहीं रहेगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.2।। मैं तुम्हारे लिए विज्ञान सहित इस ज्ञान को अशेष रूप से कहूँगा जिसको जानकर यहाँ (जगत् में) फिर और कुछ जानने योग्य (ज्ञातव्य) शेष नहीं रह जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I will declare to you in full this knowledge of God, along with the knowledge that makes it distinguished, knowing which nothing else remains to be known.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: I shall tell you in detail of this Knowledge, which is combined with realization, after experiencing which there remains nothing else here to be known.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I will declare to you in full this knowledge combined with realization, after knowing which nothing else remains to be known here.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I shall teach you this knowledge in full, together with action; for a person who has known this, there remains nothing else to be known in this world.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I will reveal this knowledge to you and how it can be realized; once accomplished, there is nothing else worth having in this life.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.3।। हजारों मनुष्योंमें कोई एक वास्तविक सिद्धिके लिये यत्न करता है और उन यत्न करनेवाले सिद्धोंमें कोई एक ही मुझे तत्त्वसे जानता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.3।। सहस्रों मनुष्यों में कोई ही मनुष्य पूर्णत्व की सिद्धि के लिए प्रयत्न करता है और उन प्रयत्नशील साधकों में भी कोई ही पुरुष मुझे तत्त्व से जानता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Among thousands of men, some strive for perfection; even among those who strive for perfection, only some know Me; and among those who know Me, only some truly know Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among thousands of men, a rare one endeavors for perfection. Even among the perfected ones who are diligent, one perchance knows Me in truth.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among thousands of men, one may perchance strive for perfection; even among those successful strivers, only one may perchance know Me in essence.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Among thousands of men, perhaps one makes an effort for the determined knowledge. Among those, even though they make an effort, perhaps one realizes Me correctly with the determined knowledge.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Among thousands of men, scarcely one strives for perfection, and even among those who gain occult powers, perhaps only one knows me in truth.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.4 -- 7.5।।  पृथ्वी, जल, तेज, वायु, आकाश -- ये पञ्चमहाभूत और मन, बुद्धि तथा अहंकार -- यह आठ प्रकारके भेदोंवाली मेरी 'अपरा' प्रकृति है। हे महाबाहो ! इस अपरा प्रकृतिसे भिन्न जीवरूप बनी हुई मेरी 'परा' प्रकृतिको जान, जिसके द्वारा यह जगत् धारण किया जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.4।। पृथ्वी, जल, अग्नि, वायु और आकाश तथा मन, बुद्धि और अहंकार - यह आठ प्रकार से विभक्त हुई मेरी प्रकृति है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, Manas, Buddhi, and the ego-sense—thus My Prakriti is divided into eight parts.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: This Prakrti of Mine is divided eightfold thus: earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect, and egoism.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect, and egoism—thus is My Nature divided eightfold.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: My nature is divided into eight parts: Earth, Water, Fire, Wind, Ether, Mind, Intellect, and Ego.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect, and personality—this is the eightfold division of My manifested nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.4 -- 7.5।।  पृथ्वी, जल, तेज, वायु, आकाश -- ये पञ्चमहाभूत और मन, बुद्धि तथा अहंकार -- यह आठ प्रकारके भेदोंवाली मेरी अपरा प्रकृति है। हे महाबाहो ! इस 'अपरा' प्रकृतिसे भिन्न मेरी जीवरूपा बनी हुुई मेरी 'परा' प्रकृतिको जान, जिसके द्वारा यह जगत् धारण किया जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.5।। हे महाबाहो ! यह अपरा प्रकृति है। इससे भिन्न मेरी जीवरूपी पराप्रकृति को जानो, जिससे यह जगत् धारण किया जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: This is My lower Prakṛti. But, O mighty-armed one, know that My higher nature is another. It is the life-principle (Jīva-bhūta), by which this universe is sustained.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O mighty-armed one, this is the inferior Prakrti. Know the other Prakrti of Mine, which is however higher than this, which has taken the form of individual souls, and by which this world is upheld.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: O mighty-armed Arjuna, this is the inferior Prakriti; know it as distinct from My higher Prakriti, the very life-element, by which this world is upheld.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This is My lower nature. My superior nature, which has become the individual Soul and by which this world is maintained, is not different from this. O mighty-armed Arjuna, you must know this.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: This is My inferior nature; however, O valiant one, know that distinct from this is My superior nature, which is the very life that sustains the universe.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.6।। अपरा और परा -- इन दोनों प्रकृतियोंके संयोगसे ही सम्पूर्ण प्राणी उत्पन्न होते हैं, ऐसा तुम समझो। मैं सम्पूर्ण जगत् का प्रभव तथा प्रलय हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.6।। यह जानो कि समम्पूर्ण भूत इन दोनों प्रकृतियों से उत्पत्ति वाले हैं। (अत:) मैं सम्पूर्ण जगत् का उत्पत्ति तथा प्रलय स्थान हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know that all beings have these two as the source of their birth. Therefore, I am the origin and the dissolver of the whole universe.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Understand thus that all things—sentient and insentient—have these as their source. I am the origin as well as the end of the entire universe.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know that these two are the womb of all beings; thus, I am the source and dissolution of the whole universe.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: All beings are born from this womb; hence, keep them close. I am the origin and dissolution of the entire world.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is the womb of all beings; for I am He by Whom the worlds were created and shall be dissolved.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.7।। हे धनञ्जय ! मेरे बढ़कर (इस जगत् का) दूसरा कोई किञ्चिन्मात्र भी कारण नहीं है। जैसे सूतकी मणियाँ सूतके धागेमें पिरोयी हुई होती हैं, ऐसे ही यह सम्पूर्ण जगत् मेरेमें ही ओत-प्रोत है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.7।। हे धनंजय ! मुझसे श्रेष्ठ (परे) अन्य किचिन्मात्र वस्तु नहीं है। यह सम्पूर्ण जगत् सूत्र में मणियों के सदृश मुझमें पिरोया हुआ है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There is nothing higher than Me, O Arjuna. All this is strung on Me, like rows of gems on a thread.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Dhananjaya, there is nothing else higher than Myself. All this is strung on Me like pearls on a string.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There is nothing higher than Me, O Arjuna. All this is strung on Me, like clusters of gems on a string.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: There is nothing beyond Me, O Dhananjaya; all this is strung on Me, just as pearls are strung on a string.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! There is nothing higher than Me; all is strung on Me like rows of pearls on a thread.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.8।। हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! जलोंमें रस मैं हूँ, चन्द्रमा और सूर्यमें प्रभा (प्रकाश) मैं हूँ, सम्पूर्ण वेदोंमें प्रणव (ओंकार) मैं हूँ, आकाशमें शब्द और मनुष्योंमें पुरुषार्थ मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.8।। हे कौन्तेय ! जल में मैं रस हूँ, चन्द्रमा और सूर्य में प्रकाश हूँ, सब वेदों में प्रणव (ँ़कार) हूँ तथा आकाश में शब्द और पुरुषों में पुरुषत्व हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the taste in the waters, O Arjuna! I am the light in the sun and the moon; the sacred syllable Om in all the Vedas; sound in the ether; and manhood in men, I am.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Kunti, I am the taste of water, I am the effulgence of the moon and the sun; the letter 'Om' in all the Vedas, the sound in space, and manhood in men.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the flavor in water, O Arjuna; I am the light in the moon and the sun; I am the syllable Om in all the Vedas, sound in the ether and virility in men.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Kunti! I am the taste in waters, the light in the moon and sun, the best hymn (OM) in the entire Vedas, the sound that exists in the ether, and the manly vigor in men.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! I am the fluidity in water, the light in the sun and moon, the mystic syllable Om in the Vedic scriptures, the sound in ether, and the virility in man.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.9।। पृथ्वीमें पवित्र गन्ध मैं हूँ, और अग्निमें तेज मैं हूँ, तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें जीवनीशक्ति मैं हूँ और तपस्वियोंमें तपस्या मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.9।। पृथ्वी में पवित्र गन्ध हूँ और अग्नि में तेज हूँ; सम्पूर्ण भूतों में जीवन हूँ और तपस्वियों में मैं तप हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the pure scent in the earth; I am the radiance in the fire; I am the life-force in all beings, and austerity in ascetics.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: I am also the sweet fragrance in the earth; I am the brilliance in the fire, and the life in all beings; and I am the austerity of the ascetics.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the sweet fragrance in the earth and the brilliance in the fire, the life in all beings, and I am the austerity of ascetics.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the pure scent in the earth; I am also the radiance in the sun; I am the life in all beings and the austerity in the ascetics.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the fragrance of the earth, the brilliance of fire, the life force in all beings, and the austerity of ascetics.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.10।। हे पृथानन्दन ! सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका अनादि बीज मुझे जान। बुद्धिमानोंमें बुद्धि और तेजस्वियोंमें तेज मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.10।। हे पार्थ ! सम्पूर्ण भूतों का सनातन बीज (कारण) मुझे ही जानो; मैं बुद्धिमानों की बुद्धि और तेजस्वियों का तेज हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know Me, O Arjuna, to be the primeval seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the discerning and the brilliance of the brilliant.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, know Me to be the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intellect of the intelligent, and I am the courage of the courageous.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know Me, O Arjuna, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intelligence of the intelligent, and the splendour of the splendid objects.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Prtha! Know Me as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intellect of the intellectuals and the brilliance of the brilliant.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Know, O Arjuna, that I am the eternal seed of being; I am the intelligence of the intelligent, the splendour of the resplendent.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.11।। हे भरतवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! बलवालोंमें काम और रागसे रहित बल मैं हूँ। मनुषयोंमें धर्मसे अविरुद्ध (धर्मयुक्त) काम मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.11।। हे भरत श्रेष्ठ ! मैं बलवानों का कामना तथा आसक्ति से रहित बल हूँ और सब भूतों में धर्म के अविरुद्ध अर्थात् अनुकूल काम हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: In the strong, I am strength, devoid of desire and passion. In all beings, I am the desire which is not contrary to Dharma, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And of the strong, I am the strength that is devoid of passion and attachment. Among creatures, I am desire that is not contrary to righteousness, O scion of the Bharata dynasty.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Of the strong, I am the strength devoid of desire and attachment, and in all beings, I am the desire in accordance with Dharma, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the strong, I am the strength that is free from desire and attachment. O best of the Bharatas, in all beings, I am the desire that is not contrary to dharma.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the strength of the strong, those who are free from attachment and desire; and, O Arjuna, I am the desire for righteousness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.12।। (और तो क्या कहूँ)  जितने भी सात्त्विक, राजस और तामस भाव हैं, वे सब मुझ से ही होते हैं -- ऐसा समझो। पर मैं उनमें और वे मेरेमें नहीं हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.12।। जो भी सात्त्विक (शुद्ध), राजसिक (क्रियाशील) और तामसिक (जड़) भाव हैं, उन सबको तुम मेरे से उत्पन्न हुए जानो; तथापि मैं उनमें नहीं हूँ, वे मुझमें हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know that all those states of Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas are from Me alone; however, I am not in them; rather, they are in Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those things that are made of the quality of sattva, and those things that are made of the quality of rajas and tamas, know them to have sprung from Me alone. However, I am not in them; they are within Me!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whatever beings (and objects) that are pure, active, and inert, know that they proceed from Me. They are in Me, yet I am not in them.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whatever beings are there—whether they are of the Sattva, Rajas, or Tamas strands—be sure that they are from Me; I am not in them, but they are within Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Whatever be the nature of their life, whether it be pure, passionate, or ignorant, they are all derived from Me. They are in Me, but I am not in them.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.13।। किन्तु - इन तीनों गुणरूप भावोंसे मोहित यह सब जगत् इन गुणोंसे अतीत अविनाशी मुझे नहीं जानता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.13।। त्रिगुणों से उत्पन्न इन भावों (विकारों) से सम्पूर्ण जगत् (लोग) मोहित हुआ इन (गुणों) से परे अव्यय स्वरूप मुझे नहीं जानता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The entire universe is deluded by these three states originating from the Gunas (of Prakrti), and fails to recognize Me, who am beyond them and immutable.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: All this world, deluded as it is by these three qualities made of the gunas, does not know Me, who am transcendental to these and undecaying.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Deluded by these Natures, composed of the three qualities of Nature, all this world does not know Me as distinct from them and immutable.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Being deluded by these three strands of nature, this entire world does not recognize Me, who am eternal and transcend these strands.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The inhabitants of the world, misled by the natures engendered by the Qualities, do not know that I am higher than them all and that I do not change.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.14।। क्योंकि मेरी यह गुणमयी दैवी माया बड़ी दुरत्यय है अर्थात् इससे पार पाना बड़ा कठिन है। जो केवल मेरे ही शरण होते हैं, वे इस मायाको तर जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.14।। यह दैवी त्रिगुणमयी मेरी माया बड़ी दुस्तर है। परन्तु जो मेरी शरण में आते हैं, वे इस माया को पार कर जाते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text:
(a) For this divine Maya or Mine, consisting of the three Gunas (assumed for purposes of sport), is hard to overcome.
৷৷
(b) ৷৷ But those who take refuge in Me alone shall pass beyond the Maya.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Since this divine Maya of Mine, which is constituted by the gunas, is difficult to cross over, those who take refuge in Me alone can transcend this Maya.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Verily, this divine illusion of Mine, composed of the three qualities, is difficult to cross over; those who take refuge in Me alone, can cross over this illusion.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This is My divine play, composed of the strands of illusion, and it is hard to cross over. Those who take refuge in Me alone, they cross over the illusion.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Verily, this divine illusion of phenomenon manifesting itself in the qualities is difficult to surmount. Only they who devote themselves exclusively to Me can accomplish it.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.15।।   मायाके द्वारा अपहृत ज्ञानवाले, आसुर भावका आश्रय लेनेवाले और मनुष्योंमें महान् नीच तथा पाप-कर्म करनेवाले मूढ़ मनुष्य मेरे शरण नहीं होते।९

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.15।। दुष्कृत्य करने वाले, मूढ, नराधम पुरुष मुझे नहीं भजते हैं; माया के द्वारा जिनका ज्ञान हर लिया गया है, वे आसुरी भाव को धारण किये रहते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The evil-doers, the foolish, the lowest of men, those persons deprived of knowledge by delusion (Maya) and those who are dominated by a demoniac nature - they do not seek refuge in Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The foolish evildoers, who are the most depraved among men, who are deprived of their wisdom by Maya, and who resort to demonic ways, do not take refuge in Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The evil-doers and the deluded, who are the lowest of men, do not seek Me; those whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow the ways of demons.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The deluded evil-doers, the vilest of men, who are robbed of knowledge by the trick of illusion and have taken refuge in the demonic nature—they do not resort to Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The sinner, the ignorant, the vile, deprived of spiritual perception by the glamour of illusion, and he who leads a godless life—none of them shall find me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।7.16।। हे भरतवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुनव ! पवित्र कर्म करनेवाले अर्थार्थी, आर्त, जिज्ञासु और ज्ञानी अर्थात् प्रेमी -- ये चार प्रकारके मनुष्य मेरा भजन करते हैं अर्थात् मेरे शरण होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।7.16।। हे भरत श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! उत्तम कर्म करने वाले (सुकृतिन:) आर्त, जिज्ञासु, अर्थार्थी और ज्ञानी ऐसे चार प्रकार के लोग मुझे भजते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Four types of men of good deeds worship Me, O Arjuna: the distressed, the seekers of knowledge, the seekers of wealth, and the men of knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, foremost of the Bharata dynasty, four classes of people of virtuous deeds adore Me: the afflicted, the seekers of Knowledge, the seekers of wealth, and the man of Knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Four kinds of virtuous men worship Me, O Arjuna, and they are the distressed, the seekers of knowledge, the seekers of wealth, and the wise, O Lord of the Bharatas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Men of good action who always worship Me are of four types: the afflicted, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the man of wisdom, O best among the Bharatas!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
