text: ।।9.16 -- 9.18।। क्रतु मैं हूँ, यज्ञ मैं हूँ, स्वधा मैं हूँ, औषध मैं हूँ, मन्त्र मैं हूँ, घृत मैं हूँ, अग्नि मैं हूँ और हवनरूप क्रिया भी मैं हूँ। जाननेयोग्य पवित्र, ओंकार, ऋग्वेद, सामवेद और यजुर्वेद भी मैं ही हूँ। इस सम्पूर्ण जगत् का पिता, धाता, माता, पितामह, गति, भर्ता, प्रभु, साक्षी, निवास, आश्रय, सुहृद्, उत्पत्ति, प्रलय, स्थान, निधान तथा अविनाशी बीज भी मैं ही हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.17।। मैं ही इस जगत् का पिता, माता, धाता (धारण करने वाला) और पितामह हूँमैं वेद्य (जानने योग्य) वस्तु हूँ, पवित्र, ओंकार, ऋग्वेद, सामवेद और यजुर्वेद भी मैं ही हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the father, mother, creator, and grandfather of the universe; I am the purifier. I am the syllable Om, as well as Rk, Saman, and Yajus.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Of this world, I am the father, mother, ordainer, and grandfather; I am the knowable, the sanctifier, the syllable Om, as well as the Rk, Sama, and Yajus.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the father of this world, the mother, the dispenser of the fruits of actions, and the grandfather; the one thing to be known, the purifier, the sacred monosyllable (Om), and also the Rik, Sama, and Yajur Vedas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the father, the mother, the sustainer, and the paternal grandparent of this world; I am the sacred object of knowledge, the syllable Om, the Rk, the Saman, and the Yajus as well.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the Father of the universe, its Mother, Nourisher, and Grandfather; I am the Knowable and the Pure; I am Om, and I am the Sacred Scriptures.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.16 -- 9.18।।  क्रतु मैं हूँ, यज्ञ मैं हूँ, स्वधा मैं हूँ, औषध मैं हूँ, मन्त्र मैं हूँ, घृत मैं हूँ, अग्नि मैं हूँ और हवनरूप क्रिया भी मैं हूँ। जाननेयोग्य पवित्र, ओंकार, ऋग्वेद, सामवेद और यजुर्वेद भी मैं ही हूँ। इस सम्पूर्ण जगत्का पिता, धाता, माता, पितामह, गति, भर्ता, प्रभु, साक्षी, निवास, आश्रय, सुहृद्, उत्पत्ति, प्रलय, स्थान, निधान तथा अविनाशी बीज भी मैं ही हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.18।। गति (लक्ष्य), भरण-पोषण करने वाला, प्रभु (स्वामी), साक्षी, निवास, शरणस्थान तथा मित्र और उत्पत्ति, प्रलयरूप तथा स्थान (आधार), निधान और अव्यय कारण भी मैं हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the goal, the supporter, the Lord, the witness, the abode, the refuge, and the friend. I am the origin, the dissolution, the basis for preservation, and the imperishable seed.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: I am the fruit of actions, the nourisher, the Lord, witness, abode, refuge, friend, origin, end, foundation, store, and the imperishable seed.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the goal, the supporter, the Lord, the witness, the abode, the shelter, the friend, the origin, the dissolution, the foundation, the treasure-house, and the imperishable seed.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the method, the nourisher, the lord, the witness, the abode, the refuge, the good-hearted friend, the origin, the dissolution, the sustenance, the repository, and the imperishable seed of the world.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the goal, the sustainer, the Lord, the witness, the home, the shelter, the lover, and the origin; I am life and death; I am the fountain and the imperishable seed.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.19।। हे अर्जुन ! (संसारके हितके लिये) मैं ही सूर्यरूपसे तपता हूँ, जलको ग्रहण करता हूँ और फिर उस जलको वर्षारूपसे बरसा देता हूँ। (और तो क्या कहूँ) अमृत और मृत्यु तथा सत् और असत् भी मैं ही हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.19।। हे अर्जुन ! मैं ही (सूर्य रूप में) तपता हूँ; मैं वर्षा का निग्रह और उत्सर्जन करता हूँ। मैं ही अमृत और मृत्यु एवं सत् और असत् हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I give heat, I withhold and send forth the rain; I am immortality and also death, O Arjuna. I am the being and the non-being.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, I give heat, I withhold and pour down rain; I am verily the nectar, and also death, existence, and non-existence.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: As the sun, I give heat; I withhold and send forth the rain; I am immortality and also death, existence and non-existence, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I give heat; I withhold and also send forth rains; I am immortality and also death, the real and the unreal, O Arjuna!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the heat of the sun, I release and withhold the rain. I am death and immortality; I am being and non-being.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.20।। वेदत्रयीमें कहे हुए सकाम अनुष्ठानको करनेवाले और सोमरसको पीनेवाले जो पापरहित मनुष्य यज्ञोंके द्वारा इन्द्ररूपसे मेरा पूजन करके स्वर्ग-प्राप्तिकी प्रार्थना करते हैं, वे पुण्यके फलस्वरूप इन्द्रलोकको प्राप्त करके वहाँ स्वर्गमें देवताओंके दिव्य भोगोंको भोगते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.20।। तीनों वेदों के ज्ञाता (वेदोक्त सकाम कर्म करने वाले), सोमपान करने वाले एवं पापों से पवित्र हुए पुरुष मुझे यज्ञों के द्वारा पूजकर स्वर्ग प्राप्ति चाहते हैं; वे पुरुष अपने पुण्यों के फलरूप इन्द्रलोक को प्राप्त कर स्वर्ग में दिव्य देवताओं के भोग भोगते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those who are versed in the three Vedas, having been purified from sin by drinking the Soma juice, pray for passage to heaven and worship Me through sacrifices. Reaching the holy realm of the chief of the gods, they enjoy celestial pleasures in heaven.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those who are versed in the Vedas, who drink Soma and are purified of sin, pray for the heavenly goal by worshipping Me through sacrifices. Having reached the place (world) of the king of gods, which is the result of righteousness, they enjoy the divine pleasure of the gods in heaven.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The knowers of the three Vedas, the drinkers of Soma, purified of all sins, worshipping Me through sacrifices, pray for the way to heaven; they reach the holy world of the Lord of the gods and enjoy the divine pleasures of the gods in heaven.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The masters of the three Vedas, the soma-drinkers, purified of their sins, aspire for the heavenly goal by offering sacrifices to Me. They attain the meritorious world of the Lord of Gods and taste in the heavens the heavenly pleasures of the gods.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Those who are versed in the scriptures, who drink the mystic Soma-juice and are purified from sin, and who, while worshipping Me with sacrifices, pray that I will lead them to heaven; they reach the holy world where the Controller of the Powers of Nature resides, and they enjoy the feasts of Paradise.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.21।। वे उस विशाल स्वर्गलोकके भोगोंको भोगकर पुण्य क्षीण होनेपर मृत्युलोकमें आ जाते हैं। इस प्रकार तीनों वेदोंमें कहे हुए सकाम धर्मका आश्रय लिये हुए भोगोंकी कामना करनेवाले मनुष्य आवागमनको प्राप्त होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.21।। वे उस विशाल स्वर्गलोक को भोगकर, पुण्यक्षीण होने पर, मृत्युलोक को प्राप्त होते हैं। इस प्रकार तीनों वेदों में कहे गये कर्म के शरण हुए और भोगों की कामना वाले पुरुष आवागमन (गतागत) को प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Having enjoyed the spacious world of heaven, they return to the world of mortals, their merit having been exhausted. Thus, those who follow the Vedic rituals and are drawn by desires come and go.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: After having enjoyed that vast heavenly world, they enter into the human world upon the exhaustion of their merit. Thus, those who follow the rites and duties prescribed in the three Vedas, and are desirous of pleasures, attain the state of coming and going.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: They, having enjoyed the vast heaven, enter the world of mortals when their merit is exhausted; thus abiding by the injunctions of the three (Vedas) and desiring objects of desires, they attain to the state of coming and going.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having enjoyed that vast world of heaven, they, when their merit is exhausted, enter the world of mortals. Thus, those who long for pleasure and continuously take refuge in the code of conduct prescribed by the Three Vedas, attain the state of going and coming.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Yet, although they enjoy the spacious glories of Paradise, when their merit is exhausted, they are born again into this world of mortals. They have followed the letter of the scriptures, yet, because they have sought to fulfill their own desires, they must depart and return again and again.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.22।। जो अनन्य भक्त मेरा चिन्तन करते हुए मेरी उपासना करते हैं, मेरेमें निरन्तर लगे हुए उन भक्तोंका योगक्षेम (अप्राप्तकी प्राप्ति और प्राप्तकी रक्षा) मैं वहन करता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.22।। अनन्य भाव से मेरा चिन्तन करते हुए जो भक्तजन मेरी ही उपासना करते हैं, उन नित्ययुक्त पुरुषों का योगक्षेम मैं वहन करता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those who, excluding all else, think of Me and worship Me, aspiring after eternal union with Me, I look after their prosperity and welfare (Yoga and Ksema).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those persons who, becoming non-different from Me and meditating, worship Me everywhere, for them, who are ever attached to Me, I arrange for securing what they lack and preserving what they have.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For those men who worship Me alone, thinking of no one else, for those ever-united, I secure what they have not already possessed and preserve what they already possess.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those men who, having nothing else as their goal, worship Me everywhere and are constantly thinking of Me alone; to them, who are fully and constantly attached to Me, I bear acquisition and the security of acquisition.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But if a person meditates on Me and Me alone, worships Me always and everywhere, I will take upon Myself the fulfillment of their aspiration, and I will safeguard whatever they attain.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.23।। हे कुन्तीनन्दन! जो भी भक्त (मनुष्य) श्रद्धापूर्वक अन्य देवताओंका पूजन करते हैं, वे भी करते हैं मेरा ही पूजन, पर करते है अविधिपूर्वक

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.23।। हे कौन्तेय ! श्रद्धा से युक्त जो भक्त अन्य देवताओं को पूजते हैं, वे भी मुझे ही अविधिपूर्वक पूजते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Even those who are devoted to other divinities with faith in their hearts, worship Me alone, O Arjuna, though not in accordance with the Sastras.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even those who, being devoted to other deities and endowed with faith, worship them, they also, O son of Kunti, worship Me alone, though following the wrong method.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Even those devotees who, endowed with faith, worship other gods, worship Me alone, O Arjuna, but by the wrong method.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Kunti! Even those who are devotees of other gods and worship them with faith, worship Me alone, but following non-injunctive practices.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even those who worship the lesser powers, if they do so with faith, they are thereby worshipping Me, though not in the correct manner.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.24।। क्योंकि मैं ही सम्पूर्ण यज्ञोंका भोक्ता और स्वामी भी हूँ;  परन्तु वे मेरेको तत्त्वसे नहीं जानते, इसीसे उनका पतन होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.24।। क्योंकि सब यज्ञों का भोक्ता और स्वामी मैं ही हूँ, परन्तु वे मुझे तत्त्वत: नहीं जानते हैं, इसलिए वे गिरते हैं, अर्थात् संसार को प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For, I am the only enjoyer and the only Lord of all sacrifices. They do not recognize Me in My true nature; hence, they fall.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Indeed, I am the enjoyer and the Lord of all sacrifices, but they do not know Me truly. Therefore, they fall.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For I alone am the enjoyer and Lord of all sacrifices; but they do not know Me in reality, and thus they return to this mortal world.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: For I am the enjoyer and the Lord of all sacrifices, yet they do not recognize Me correctly and thus they turn away.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the willing recipient of sacrifice, and I am its true Lord. But they do not know me truly, so they fall back.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.25।। (सकामभावसे) देवताओंका पूजन करनेवाले (शरीर छोड़नेपर) देवताओंको प्राप्त होते हैं। पितरोंका पूजन करनेवाले पितरोंको प्राप्त होते हैं। भूत-प्रेतोंका पूजन करनेवाले भूत-प्रेतोंको प्राप्त होते हैं। परन्तु मेरा पूजन करनेवाले मुझे ही प्राप्त होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.25।। देवताओं के पूजक देवताओं को प्राप्त होते हैं, पितरपूजक पितरों को जाते हैं, भूतों का यजन करने वाले भूतों को प्राप्त होते हैं और मुझे पूजने वाले भक्त मुझे ही प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Devotees of gods go to the gods; manes-worshippers go to the manes; worshippers of Bhutas go to the Bhutas; and those who worship Me come to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Those who worship the gods reach the gods; the worshippers of the manes go to the manes; the devotees of the Beings reach the Beings; and those who worship Me come to Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The worshippers of the gods go to them; the ancestor-worshippers go to the manes; the worshippers of the deities who preside over the elements go to them; but My devotees come to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The devotees of the gods attain the gods; the devotees of the ancestors attain the ancestors; those who perform sacrifices for the spirits attain the spirits; and those who perform sacrifices for Me attain Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The votaries of the lesser powers go to them; the devotees of spirits go to them; they who worship the powers of darkness, to such powers shall they go; and so too, those who worship me shall come to me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.26।।जो भक्त पत्र, पुष्प, फल, जल आदि (यथासाध्य प्राप्त वस्तु) को भक्तिपूर्वक मेरे अर्पण करता है, उस मेरेमें तल्लीन हुए अन्तःकरणवाले भक्तके द्वारा भक्तिपूर्वक दिये हुए उपहार-(भेंट-) को मैं खा लेता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.26।। जो कोई भी भक्त मेरे लिए पत्र, पुष्प, फल, जल आदि भक्ति से अर्पण करता है, उस शुद्ध मन के भक्त का वह भक्तिपूर्वक अर्पण किया हुआ (पत्र पुष्पादि) मैं भोगता हूँ अर्थात् स्वीकार करता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whoever offers Me with true devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or some water, I accept this offering made with devotion by them who are pure of heart.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Whoever offers Me with devotion—a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water—I accept that gift of the pure-hearted man which has been presented devotionally.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whoever offers Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or a little water, that, so offered devotedly by the pure-minded, I accept.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever offers Me a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or even a little water with devotion, I accept that offering of devotion from one who has a well-controlled mind.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Whatever a person offers to Me, be it a leaf, flower, fruit, or water, I accept it, for it is offered with devotion and a pure mind.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.27।। हे कुन्तीपुत्र ! तू जो कुछ करता है, जो कुछ खाता है, जो कुछ यज्ञ करता है, जो कुछ दान देता है और जो कुछ तप करता है, वह सब मेरे अर्पण कर दे।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.27।। हे कौन्तेय ! तुम जो कुछ कर्म करते हो, जो कुछ खाते हो, जो कुछ हवन करते हो, जो कुछ दान देते हो और जो कुछ तप करते हो, वह सब तुम मुझे अर्पण करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer, whatever you give away, whatever austerity you practice, O Arjuna, do that as an offering to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Kunti, whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer as a sacrifice, whatever you give, and whatever austerities you undertake—all of that you offer to Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice, whatever you give, whatever austerity you practice, O Arjuna, do it as an offering to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever oblation you offer, whatever gift you make, and whatever austerity you perform, O son of Kunti, do that as an offering to Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you sacrifice and give, whatever austerities you practice, do all as an offering to Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.28।। इस प्रकार मेरे अर्पण करनेसे जिनसे कर्मबन्धन होता है, ऐसे शुभ (विहित) और अशुभ (निषिद्ध) सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंके फलोंसे तू मुक्त हो जायगा। ऐसे अपनेसहित सब कुछ मेरे अर्पण करनेवाला और सबसे मुक्त हुआ तू मेरेको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.28।। इस प्रकार तुम शुभाशुभ फलस्वरूप कर्मबन्धनों से मुक्त हो जाओगे; और संन्यासयोग से युक्तचित्त हुए तुम विमुक्त होकर मुझे ही प्राप्त हो जाओगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus equipped in mind with the Yoga of renunciation, you will free yourself from the bonds of Karma, productive of both auspicious and inauspicious fruits. Thus liberated, you will come to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Thus, you will become free from bondage in the form of actions which are productive of good and bad results. Having your mind imbued with the yoga of renunciation and becoming free, you will attain Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Thus, you shall be freed from the bonds of actions yielding good and evil fruits; with the mind steadfast in the Yoga of renunciation, and liberated, you shall come to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, you shall be freed from the good and evil results which are the action-bonds. Having your innate nature immersed in the Yoga of renunciation and, thus, being fully liberated, you shall attain Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: So, your action will have no result, either good or bad; but, through the spirit of renunciation, you will come to Me and be free.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.29।। मैं सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें समान हूँ। उन प्राणियोंमें न तो कोई मेरा द्वेषी है और न कोई प्रिय है। परन्तु जो भक्तिपूर्वक मेरा भजन करते हैं, वे मेरेमें हैं और मैं  उनमें हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.29।। मैं समस्त भूतों में सम हूँ; न कोई मुझे अप्रिय है और न प्रिय; परन्तु जो मुझे भक्तिपूर्वक भजते हैं, वे मुझमें और मैं भी उनमें हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the same to all creation; there is none hateful or dear to me. But those who worship me with devotion abide in me, and I in them.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: I am impartial towards all beings; to Me there is none detestable or none dear. But those who worship Me with devotion, they exist in Me, and I too exist in them.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the same to all beings; there is none hateful or dear to Me; but those who worship Me with devotion are in Me, and I am also in them.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the same in all beings; none is hateful to Me and none is dear; but whoever worships Me with devotion, they are in Me and I am in them.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the same to all beings; I do not favour any, nor do I hate any. However, those who worship Me devotedly, they live in Me and I in them.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.30।। अगर कोई दुराचारी-से-दुराचारी भी अनन्यभावसे मेरा भजन करता है, तो उसको साधु ही मानना चाहिये। कारण कि उसने निश्चय बहुत अच्छी तरह कर लिया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.30।। यदि कोई अतिशय दुराचारी भी अनन्यभाव से मेरा भक्त होकर मुझे भजता है, वह साधु ही मानने योग्य है, क्योंकि वह यथार्थ निश्चय वाला है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: If even the most sinful man worships Me with undivided devotion, he should be regarded as holy, for he has rightly resolved.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Even if a man of very bad conduct worships Me with single-minded devotion, he is to be considered truly good; for he has rightly resolved.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Even if the most sinful worships Me, with devotion to no one else, he should indeed be regarded as righteous, for he has rightly resolved.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Even if an incorrigible evil-doer worships Me, not resorting to any other [goal], he should be deemed to be righteous; for, he has properly undertaken his task.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even the most sinful, if he worships Me with his whole heart, shall be considered righteous, for he is treading the right path.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.31।। वह तत्काल (उसी क्षण) धर्मात्मा हो जाता है और निरन्तर रहनेवाली शान्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है। हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! तुम प्रतिज्ञा करो कि मेरे भक्तका विनाश (पतन) नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.31।। हे कौन्तेय, वह शीघ्र ही धर्मात्मा बन जाता है और शाश्वत शान्ति को प्राप्त होता है। तुम निश्चयपूर्वक सत्य जानो कि मेरा भक्त कभी नष्ट नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Quickly, he becomes righteous and obtains everlasting peace. Affirm on My behalf, O Arjuna, that My devotee never perishes.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He soon becomes possessed of a virtuous mind; he attains everlasting peace. Do you proclaim boldly, O son of Kunti, that My devotee will not be ruined.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Soon he becomes righteous and attains eternal peace; O Arjuna, proclaim thou for certain that My devotee never perishes.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Quickly he becomes righteous-minded and attains permanent peace. O son of Kunti! I swear that my devotee never gets lost.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He will soon attain spirituality and eternal peace. O Arjuna! Believe me, my devotee is never lost.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.32।। हे पृथानन्दन ! जो भी पापयोनिवाले हों तथा जो भी स्त्रियाँ, वैश्य और शूद्र हों, वे भी सर्वथा मेरे शरण होकर निःसन्देह परमगतिको प्राप्त हो जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.32।। हे पार्थ ! स्त्री, वैश्य और शूद्र ये जो कोई पापयोनि वाले हों, वे भी मुझ पर आश्रित (मेरे शरण) होकर परम गति को प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: By taking refuge in Me, even those of evil birth, women, Vaisyas, and Sudras, attain the supreme state.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For, O son of Prtha, even those who are born of sin-women, Vaisyas, as well as Sudras—[S.'s construction of this portion is: women, Vaisyas as well as Sudras, and even others who are born of sin (i.e., those who are born low and are of vile deeds, viz Mlecchas, Pukkasas and others). M.S. also takes papa-yonayah (born of sin) as a separate phrase, and classifies women and others only as those darred from Vedic study, etc.-Tr.]—even they reach the highest Goal by taking shelter under Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For, taking refuge in Me, they who, O Arjuna, may be of a sinful birth—women, Vaisyas, and Sudras—attain the Supreme Goal.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Prtha, even those who are of sinful birth, women, men of working class, and the members of the fourth caste—even they, having taken refuge in Me, attain the highest goal.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Even the children of sinful parents, those miscalled the weaker sex, merchants, and laborers, if they make Me their refuge, they shall attain the highest.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।9.33।। जो पवित्र आचरणवाले ब्राह्मण और ऋषिस्वरूप क्षत्रिय भगवान्  के भक्त हों, वे परमगतिको प्राप्त हो जायँ, इसमें तो कहना ही क्या है। इसलिये इस अनित्य और सुखरहित शरीरको प्राप्त करके तू मेरा भजन कर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.33।। फिर क्या कहना है कि पुण्यशील ब्राह्मण और राजर्षि भक्तजन (परम गति को प्राप्त होते हैं); (इसलिए) इस अनित्य और सुखरहित लोक को प्राप्त होकर (अब) तुम भक्तिपूर्वक मेरी ही पूजा करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: How much more then, for the pure Brahmanas and royal sages who are My devotees! Having obtained this transient and joyless world, they should worship Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: What of the holy Brahmanas and devoted, wise sages? Having come to this fleeting, miserable world, worship Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: How much more easily, then, do Brahmins and devoted royal saints attain the goal? Having come to this impermanent and unhappy world, do thou worship Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Certainly, it should be so in the case of the pious men of the priestly class and devoted royal seers. Having come to this transient and joyless world, you should be devoted to Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: What need is there, then, to mention the holy ministers of God, the devotees, and the saintly rulers? Therefore, do you, born in this changing and miserable world, also worship Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text:
।।9.34।। तू मेरा भक्त हो जा, मेरेमें मनवाला हो जा, मेरा पूजन करनेवाला हो जा और मेरेको नमस्कार कर। इस प्रकार मेरे साथ अपने-आपको लगाकर, मेरे परायण हुआ तू मेरेको ही प्राप्त होगा।

,

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।9.34।। (तुम) मुझमें स्थिर मन वाले बनो; मेरे भक्त और मेरे पूजन करने वाले बनो; मुझे नमस्कार करो; इस प्रकार मत्परायण (अर्थात् मैं ही जिसका परम लक्ष्य हूँ ऐसे) होकर आत्मा को मुझसे युक्त करके तुम मुझे ही प्राप्त होओगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Focus your mind on Me, be My devotee, worship Me. Bow down to Me. Engaging your mind in this manner and regarding Me as the supreme goal, you will come to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Having your mind fixed on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, and bow down to Me. By concentrating your mind and accepting Me as the supreme goal, you shall surely attain Me, who am thus the Self.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; sacrifice to Me; bow down to Me; having thus united your whole self to Me, taking Me as the supreme goal, you will come to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; offer sacrifice to Me; pay homage to Me. Thus, having your self fixed and Me as your supreme goal, you will certainly attain Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Fix your mind on Me, devote yourself to Me, sacrifice for Me, surrender to Me, make Me the object of your aspirations, and you shall assuredly become one with Me, Who am your own Self.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.1।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हे महाबाहो अर्जुन ! मेरे परम वचनको तुम फिर भी सुनो, जिसे मैं तुम्हारे हितकी कामनासे कहूँगा; क्योंकि तुम मेरेमें अत्यन्त प्रेम रखते हो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.1।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -- हे महाबाहो ! पुन: तुम मेरे परम वचनों का श्रवण करो, जो मैं तुझ अतिशय प्रेम रखने वाले के लिये हित की इच्छा से कहूँगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "Further, O Arjuna, listen to My Supreme word. Desiring your good, I shall speak to you who loves Me."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, O mighty-armed one, listen once more to My supreme utterance, which I, wishing for your welfare, shall speak to you who take delight in it.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, Again, O mighty-armed Arjuna, listen to my supreme word which I will declare to you, who are beloved, for your welfare.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "O mighty-armed Arjuna! Listen to My best message once more, which I shall declare to you with good intention, for you are dear to Me."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna said: Now, O Prince! Listen to My supreme advice, which I give you for the sake of your welfare, for you are My beloved.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.2।। मेरे प्रकट होनेको न देवता जानते हैं और न महर्षि; क्योंकि मैं सब प्रकारसे देवताओं और महर्षियोंका आदि हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.2।। मेरी उत्पत्ति (प्रभव) को न देवतागण जानते हैं और न महर्षिजन; क्योंकि मैं सब प्रकार से देवताओं और महर्षियों का भी आदिकारण हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Neither the host of gods nor the great sages know My power; for I am indeed the source of the gods and the great sages.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Neither the gods nor the great sages know My majesty, for I am the source of them all in all respects.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Neither the hosts of the gods nor the great sages know My origin; for I am the source of all the gods and the great sages in every way.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know My origin, for I am the first in every respect among the gods and great sages.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Neither the professors of divinity nor the great ascetics know My origin, for I am the source from which they all originate.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.3।। जो मनुष्य मुझे अजन्मा, अनादि और सम्पूर्ण लोकोंका महान् ईश्वर जानता है अर्थात् दृढ़तासे मानता है, वह मनुष्योंमें असम्मूढ़ (जानकार) है और वह सम्पूर्ण पापोंसे मुक्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.3।। जो मुझे अजन्मा, अनादि और लोकों के महान् ईश्वर के रूप में जानता है, र्मत्य मनुष्यों में ऐसा संमोहरहित (ज्ञानी) पुरुष सब पापों से मुक्त हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who knows Me as unborn, without a beginning, and the great Lord of the worlds—he among mortals is undeluded and is released from all sin.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who knows Me—the birthless, beginningless, and great Lord of the worlds—he, the undeluded one among mortals, becomes freed from all sins.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who knows Me as unborn and beginningless, as the great Lord of the worlds, he among mortals is undeluded and is liberated from all sins.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever knows Me as the unborn and beginningless Absolute Lord of the universe, that person, not deluded among mortals, is freed from all sins.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who knows Me as the unborn, without beginning, the Lord of the universe, he, stripped of his delusions, becomes free from all conceivable sins.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.4 -- 10.5।। बुद्धि, ज्ञान, असम्मोह, क्षमा, सत्य, दम, शम, सुख, दुःख, भव, अभाव, भय, अभय, अहिंसा, समता, तुष्टि, तप, दान, यश और अपयश -- प्राणियोंके ये अनेक प्रकारके और अलग-अलग (बीस) भाव मेरेसे ही होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.4।। बुद्धि, ज्ञान, मोह का अभाव, क्षमा, सत्य, दम (इन्द्रिय संयम), शम (मन: संयम), सुख, दु:ख, जन्म और मृत्यु, भय और अभय।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Intelligence, knowledge, non-delusion, forbearance, truth, restraint, self-control, pleasure, pain, exaltation, and depression, fear, and fearlessness;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Intelligence, wisdom, non-delusion, forgiveness, truth, control of the external organs, control of the internal organs, happiness, sorrow, birth, death, and fearlessness;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Intellect, wisdom, non-delusion, forgiveness, truth, self-restraint, calmness, happiness, pain, existence or birth, non-existence or death, fear, and also fearlessness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Intellect, knowledge, steadiness, patience, truth, control over sense-organs, tranquility of mind, pleasure, pain, birth, death, fear, and courage.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Intelligence, wisdom, non-delusion, forgiveness, truth, self-control, calmness, pleasure, pain, birth, death, fear, and fearlessness;

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.4 -- 10.5।। बुद्धि, ज्ञान, असम्मोह, क्षमा, सत्य, दम, शम, सुख, दुःख, भव, अभाव, भय, अभय, अहिंसा, समता, तुष्टि, तप, दान, यश और अपयश -- प्राणियोंके ये अनेक प्रकारके और अलग-अलग (बीस) भाव मेरेसे ही होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.5।। अहिंसा, समता, सन्तोष, तप, दान. यश और अपयश ऐसे ये प्राणियों के नानाविध भाव मुझ से ही प्रकट होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Non-violence, reality, cheerfulness, austerity, beneficence, fame, and infamy—these different qualities of beings arise from Me alone.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Non-injury, equanimity, satisfaction, austerity, charity, fame, and infamy—these different dispositions of beings spring from Me alone.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Non-injury, equanimity, contentment, austerity, beneficence, fame, and ill-fame—these different qualities of beings arise from Me alone.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: [Also], non-injury, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, repute, and ill-repute—all these diverse dispositions of beings emanate from none other than Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Harmlessness, equanimity, contentment, austerity, beneficence, fame, and failure—all these characteristics of beings spring from Me alone.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.6।। सात महर्षि और उनसे भी पूर्वमें होनेवाले चार सनकादि तथा चौदह मनु -- ये सब-के-सब मेरे मनसे पैदा हुए हैं और मेरेमें भाव (श्रद्धाभक्ति) रखनेवाले हैं, जिनकी संसारमें यह सम्पूर्ण प्रजा है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.6।। सात महर्षिजन, पूर्वकाल के चार (सनकादि) तथा (चौदह) मनु ये मेरे प्रभाव वाले मेरे संकल्प से उत्पन्न हुए हैं, जिनकी संसार (लोक) में यह प्रजा है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The seven great seers of yore, as well as the four Manus, all possessing My mental disposition, were born from My mind. All these creatures of the world are descended from them.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The seven great sages, as well as the four Manus of ancient days, from whom all these creatures in the world have descended, had their thoughts fixed on Me, and they were born from My mind.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The seven great sages, the ancient four, and the Manus, possessing powers like Mine (due to their minds being fixed on Me), were born from My mind; from them, these creatures have been born in this world.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The ancient Seven Great Sages and also the Four Manus, from whom these creatures in this world are offspring—they have been born from My mental dispositions.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The seven Great Seers, the progenitors of mankind, the Ancient Four, and the Lawgivers were born of My will and come forth directly from Me. The race of mankind has sprung from them. (Mareechi, Atri, Angira, Pulah, Kratu, Pulastya, Vahishta; the Masters: Sanak, Sanandan, Sanatan, Sanatkumar.)

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.7।। जो मनुष्य मेरी इस विभूतिको और योगको तत्त्वसे जानता है अर्थात् दृढ़तापूर्वक मानता है, वह अविचल भक्तियोगसे युक्त हो जाता है; इसमें कुछ भी संशय नहीं है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.7।। जो पुरुष इस मेरी विभूति और योग को तत्त्व से जानता है, वह पुरुष अविकम्प योग (अर्थात् निश्चल ध्यान योग) से युक्त हो जाता है, इसमें कुछ भी संशय नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who truly knows this supreme manifestation and splendor of My auspicious attributes, becomes united with the unshakeable Yoga of Bhakti—there is no doubt of this.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who knows truly this majesty and yoga of Mine, he becomes imbued with unwavering yoga. There is no doubt about it.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who truly knows these manifold manifestations of My Being and this Yoga-power of Mine, becomes established in unshakable Yoga; there is no doubt about it.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who knows correctly this extensively manifesting power and My Yogic power—he is endowed with unwavering Yoga; there is no doubt about it.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who rightly understands My manifested glory and My creative power attains perfect peace without a doubt.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.8।। मैं संसारमात्रका प्रभव (मूलकारण) हूँ, और मुझसे ही सारा संसार प्रवृत्त हो रहा है अर्थात् चेष्टा कर रहा है -- ऐसा मेरेको मानकर मेरेमें ही श्रद्धा-प्रेम रखते हुए बुद्धिमान् भक्त मेरा ही भजन करते हैं -- सब प्रकारसे मेरे ही शरण होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.8।। मैं ही सबका प्रभव स्थान हूँ; मुझसे ही सब (जगत्) विकास को प्राप्त होता है, इस प्रकार जानकर बुधजन भक्ति भाव से युक्त होकर मुझे ही भजते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the origin of all; from me everything proceeds. Thinking thus, the wise worship me with all devotion (Bhava).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: I am the origin of all; everything moves on due to Me. Realizing this, the wise ones, filled with fervor, adore Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the source of all; from me everything evolves; Understanding this, the wise, endowed with meditation, worship me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: 'He is the source of all, and from Him all comes forth' - Thus viewing, the wise revere Me with devotion.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the source of all; from me everything flows. Therefore, the wise worship me with an unchanging devotion.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.9।।। मेरेमें चित्तवाले, मेरेमें प्राणोंको अर्पण करनेवाले भक्तजन आपसमें मेरे गुण, प्रभाव आदिको जानते हुए और उनका कथन करते हुए ही नित्य-निरन्तर सन्तुष्ट रहते हैं और मेरेमें प्रेम करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.9।। मुझमें ही चित्त को स्थिर करने वाले और मुझमें ही प्राणों (इन्द्रियों) को अर्पित करने वाले भक्तजन, सदैव परस्पर मेरा बोध कराते हुए, मेरे ही विषय में कथन करते हुए सन्तुष्ट होते हैं और रमते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: With their minds focused on Me, their pranas centered in Me, inspiring one another and always speaking of Me, they live in contentment and bliss at all times.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: With their minds fixed on Me, dedicating their lives to Me, enlightening one another, and always speaking of Me, they derive satisfaction and rejoice.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: With their minds and lives wholly absorbed in Me, they enlighten each other and ever speak of Me, being satisfied and delighted.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having their minds fixed on Me, having their lives gone into Me, enlightening one another, and constantly talking about Me, they are pleased and delighted.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: With minds concentrated on Me, with lives absorbed in Me, and enlightening one another, they ever feel content and happy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.10।। उन नित्य-निरन्तर मेरेमें लगे हुए और प्रेमपूर्वक मेरा भजन करनेवाले भक्तोंको मैं वह बुद्धियोग देता हूँ, जिससे उनको मेरी प्राप्ति हो जाती है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.10।। उन (मुझ से) नित्य युक्त हुए और प्रेमपूर्वक मेरा भजन करने वाले भक्तों को, मैं वह 'बुद्धियोग' देता हूँ जिससे वे मुझे प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: To those who are ceaselessly united with Me and who worship Me with immense love, I lovingly grant that mental disposition (Buddhi-yoga) which leads them to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: To those who are ever devoted and worship Me with love, I grant the possession of wisdom that leads them to Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: To those who are ever steadfast, worshipping me with love, I give the yoga of discrimination, by which they come to me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: To these persons, who thus mingle with Me and revere Me with love, I grant the knowledge-Yoga by which they can reach Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: To those who are always devout and who worship Me with love, I give the power of discrimination that leads them to Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.11।। उन भक्तोंपर कृपा करनेके लिये ही उनके स्वरूप (होनेपन) में रहनेवाला मैं उनके अज्ञानजन्य अन्धकारको देदीप्यमान ज्ञानरूप दीपकके द्वारा सर्वथा नष्ट कर देता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.11।। उनके ऊपर अनुग्रह करने के लिए मैं उनके अन्त:करण में स्थित होकर, अज्ञानजनित अन्धकार को प्रकाशमय ज्ञान के दीपक द्वारा नष्ट करता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Out of compassion for them alone, I, abiding in their mental activity as its object, dispel the darkness born of ignorance with the brilliant lamp of knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Out of compassion for them alone, I, residing in their hearts, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Out of mere compassion for them, I, dwelling within their selves, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Out of compassion only towards these men, I, who remain as their very Self, destroy with the shining light of wisdom, their darkness born of ignorance.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: By My grace, I live in their hearts, and I dispel the darkness of ignorance with the shining light of wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.12 -- 10.12।। अर्जुन बोले -- परम ब्रह्म, परम धाम और महान् पवित्र आप ही हैं। आप शाश्वत, दिव्य पुरुष, आदिदेव, अजन्मा और विभु (व्यापक) हैं -- ऐसा सब-के-सब ऋषि, देवर्षि नारद, असित, देवल तथा व्यास कहते हैं और स्वयं आप भी मेरे प्रति कहते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.12।। अर्जुन ने कहा आप -परम ब्रह्म, परम धाम और परम पवित्र हंै; सनातन दिव्य पुरुष, देवों के भी आदि देव, जन्म रहित और सर्वव्यापी हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Light, and the Supreme Sanctifier. All the seers proclaim You as the eternal, divine Person, the Primal Lord, the unborn, and all-pervading. So too do the divine sages Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa proclaim. You Yourself also proclaim this."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "You are the supreme Brahman, the supreme Light, the supreme Sanctifier. All the sages, as well as the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa [Although Narada and the other sages are already mentioned by the words 'all the sages', they are still named separately due to their eminence. Asita is the father of Devala.] call You the eternal divine Person, the Primal God, the Birthless, the Omnipresent; and You Yourself indeed tell me this."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "You are the Supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the supreme purifier, eternal, divine Person, the primeval God, unborn, and omnipresent."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode, and the Supreme Purifier. All the seers, as well as the divine seer Narada, Asita Devala, and Vyasa, describe You as the Eternal Divine Soul, the Unborn, and the All-Manifesting First-God. You too have said this to me."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna asked: Thou art the Supreme Spirit, the Eternal Home, the Holiest of Holies, the Eternal Divine Self, the Primal God, the Unborn, and Omnipresent.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.12 -- 10.13।। अर्जुन बोले -- परम ब्रह्म, परम धाम और महान् पवित्र आप ही हैं। आप शाश्वत, दिव्य पुरुष, आदिदेव, अजन्मा और विभु (व्यापक) हैं -- ऐसा सब-के-सब ऋषि, देवर्षि नारद, असित, देवल तथा व्यास कहते हैं और स्वयं आप भी मेरे प्रति कहते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.13।। ऐसा आपको समस्त ऋषिजन कहते हैं;  वैसे ही देवर्षि नारद, असित, देवल ऋषि तथा व्यास और स्वयं आप भी मेरे प्रति कहते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Light, and the Supreme Sanctifier. All the seers proclaim You as the eternal, divine Person, the Primal Lord, the unborn, and all-pervading. So too do the divine sages Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa proclaim. You Yourself also proclaim this."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "You are the supreme Brahman, the supreme Light, the supreme Sanctifier. All the sages, as well as the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa [Although Narada and the other sages are already mentioned by the words 'all the sages', they are still named separately due to their eminence. Asita is the father of Devala.] call You the eternal divine Person, the Primal God, the Birthless, the Omnipresent; and You Yourself indeed tell me this."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: All the sages have thus declared Thee, as also the divine sage Narada; so also Asita, Devala, and Vyasa; and now Thou Thyself dost say so to me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode, and the Supreme Purifier. All the seers, as well as the divine seer Narada, Asita Devala, and Vyasa, describe You as the Eternal Divine Soul, the Unborn, and the All-Manifesting First-God. You too have said this to me."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: So have said the seers, the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa; and Thou Thyself also sayest it.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.14।। हे केशव ! मेरेसे आप जो कुछ कह रहे हैं, यह सब मैं सत्य मानता हूँ। हे भगवन् ! आपके प्रकट होनेको न तो देवता जानते हैं और न दानव ही जानते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.14।। हे केशव ! जो कुछ भी आप मेरे प्रति कहते हैं, इस सबको मैं सत्य मानता हूँ। हे भगवन्, आपके (वास्तविक) स्वरूप को न देवता जानते हैं और न दानव।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I deem as true all that you have said to me, O Krishna. Truly, O Lord, neither the gods nor the demons know your manifestation.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Kesava, I accept as true all that You have told me. Certainly, O Lord, neither the gods nor the demons can comprehend Your glory.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I believe all that You have said to me to be true, O Krishna; indeed, O blessed Lord! Neither the gods nor the demons know Your manifestation (origin).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: What You tell me, I take all to be true, O Kesava! For, O Bhagavat, neither the gods nor the great sages know Your manifestation.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I believe in what You have said, my Lord! For neither the godly nor the godless comprehend Your manifestation.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.15।। हे भूतभावन ! हे भूतेश ! हे देवदेव ! हे जगत्पते ! हे पुरुषोत्तम ! आप स्वयं ही अपने-आपसे अपने-आपको जानते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.15।। हे पुरुषोत्तम ! हे भूतभावन ! हे भूतेश ! हे देवों के देव ! हे जगत् के स्वामी ! आप स्वयं ही अपने आप को जानते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: O Supreme Person, O Creator of beings, O Lord of beings, O God of gods, O Ruler of the universe, You Yourself know Yourself alone.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Supreme Person, Creator of beings, Lord of beings, God of gods, Lord of the worlds, You alone know Yourself by Yourself.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Verily, Thou Thyself knowest Thyself by Thyself, O Supreme Person, O source and Lord of all beings, O God of gods, O ruler of the world!

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Only yourself know yourself by yourself, O Supreme Purusha, Creator of all beings, Lord of beings, God of gods, Lord of the universe!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: You alone know Yourself, by the power of Your Self; You are the Supreme Spirit, the Source and Master of all being, the Lord of Lords, the Ruler of the Universe.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.16।।  जिन विभूतियोंसे आप इन सम्पूर्ण लोकोंको व्याप्त करके स्थित हैं, उन सभी अपनी दिव्य विभूतियोंका सम्पूर्णतासे वर्णन करनेमें आप ही समर्थ हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.16।। आप ही उन अपनी दिव्य विभूतियों को अशेषत: कहने के लिए योग्य हैं, जिन विभूतियों के द्वारा इन समस्त लोकों को आप व्याप्त करके स्थित हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: You should tell Me without reserve Your divine manifestations where You abide, pervading all these worlds.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Be pleased to speak in full of Your own divine manifestations, through which You exist pervading these worlds.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: You should indeed tell, without reserve, of your divine glories by which you exist, pervading all these worlds. (No one else can do so.)

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: You alone are capable of fully declaring the auspicious manifesting powers of Yours, through which manifesting power You remain pervading these worlds.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Please tell me about all of Your glorious manifestations, through which You pervade the world.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.17।। हे योगिन् ! हरदम साङ्गोपाङ्ग चिन्तन करता हुआ मैं आपको कैसे जानूँ ? और हे भगवन् ! किन-किन भावोंमें आप मेरे द्वारा चिन्तन किये जा सकते हैं अर्थात् किन-किन भावोंमें मैं आपका चिन्तन करूँ ?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.17।। हे योगेश्वर ! मैं किस प्रकार निरन्तर चिन्तन करता हुआ आपको जानूँ, और हे भगवन् ! आप किनकिन भावों में मेरे द्वारा चिन्तन करने योग्य हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: How can I, Your devotee, know You by constantly meditating on You? And in what ways, O Lord, should I meditate on You?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Yogi, how shall I know You by remaining ever-engaged in meditation? And through what objects, O Lord, should I meditate on You?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: How shall I, ever meditating, know you, O Yogin? In what aspects or things, O blessed Lord, should I think of you?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Mighty Yogin! How should I know you, meditating on you? In what various forms, O Bhagavat, should I contemplate you?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Master! How can I, through constant meditation, come to know You? My Lord! What are Your various manifestations through which I can meditate on You?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.18।। हे जनार्दन ! आप अपने योग (सामर्थ्य) को और विभूतियोंको विस्तारसे फिर कहिये; क्योंकि आपके अमृतमय वचन सुनते-सुनते मेरी तृप्ति नहीं हो रही है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.18।। हे जनार्दन ! अपनी योग शक्ति और विभूति को पुन: विस्तारपूर्वक कहिए, क्योंकि आपके अमृतमय वचनों को सुनते हुए मुझे तृप्ति नहीं होती।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Speak to me again in full, O Krishna, about Your attributes and glories. For I am not sated by hearing Your ambrosial words.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Janardana, please narrate to me again in detail Your own yoga and divine manifestations. For, while hearing Your nectar-like words, I am not satiated.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Tell me again in detail, O Krishna, of your yogic power and glory; for I am not satiated with what I have heard of your life-giving and nectar-like speech.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: In detail, please expound once again Your own Yogic power and the manifesting power. O Janardana! I don't feel contented in hearing Your nectar-like exposition.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Tell me again, I pray, of Thy power and Thy glory in full; for I feel that I am never satisfied when I listen to Thy immortal words.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.19।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हाँ, ठीक है। मैं अपनी दिव्य विभूतियोंको तेरे लिये प्रधानतासे (संक्षेपसे) कहूँगा; क्योंकि हे कुरुश्रेष्ठ ! मेरी विभूतियोंके विस्तारका अन्त नहीं है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.19।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -हन्त अब मैं तुम्हें अपनी दिव्य विभूतियों को प्रधानता से कहूँगा। हे कुरुश्रेष्ठ मेरे विस्तार का अन्त नहीं है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "Indeed, I shall tell you, O Arjuna, My auspicious manifestations—those that are most prominent among them. There is no end to their extent."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, O best of the Kurus, now, according to their importance, I shall describe to you My own glories, which are indeed divine. There is no end to My manifestations.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "Very well! Now I will declare to you My divine glories in their prominence, O Arjuna; there is no end to their detailed description."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "Yes, O best among the Kurus! I shall expound to you only the chief, auspicious manifesting powers of Mine, for there would be no end to My details."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: So be it, My beloved friend! I will unfold to you some of the chief aspects of My glory. There is no end to its full extent.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.20।। हे नींदको जीतनेवाले अर्जुन ! सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके आदि, मध्य तथा अन्तमें भी मैं ही हूँ और प्राणियोंके अन्तःकरणमें आत्मरूपसे भी मैं ही स्थित हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.20।। हे गुडाकेश (निद्राजित्) ! मैं समस्त भूतों के हृदय में स्थित सबकी आत्मा हूँ तथा सम्पूर्ण भूतों का आदि, मध्य और अन्त भी मैं ही हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am the Self, O Arjuna, dwelling in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Gudakesa, I am the Self residing in the hearts of all beings, and I am the beginning, middle, and end of all beings.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the Self, O Gudakesa, seated in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O conqueror of sleep! I am the Soul residing in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the very end of all beings.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! I am the Self, seated in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the life, and the end of them all.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.21।।  मैं अदितिके पुत्रोंमें विष्णु (वामन) और प्रकाशमान वस्तुओंमें किरणोंवाला सूर्य हूँ। मैं मरुतोंका तेज और नक्षत्रोंका अधिपति चन्द्रमा हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.21।। मैं (बारह) आदित्यों में विष्णु और ज्योतियों में अंशुमान् सूर्य हूँ; मैं (उनचास) मरुतों (वायु देवताओं) में मरीचि हूँ और नक्षत्रों में शशी (चन्द्रमा) हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of the Adityas, I am Vishnu; of luminous bodies, I am the radiant sun. Of the Maruts, I am Marici, and among the constellations, I am the moon.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among the Adityas [viz Dhata, Mitra, Aryama, Rudra, Varuna, Surya, Bhaga, Vivasvan, Pusa, Savita, Tvasta, and Visnu.-Tr.], I am Visnu; among the luminaries, the radiant sun; among the (forty-nine) Maruts [The seven groups of Maruts are Avaha, Pravaha, Vivaha, Paravaha, Udvaha, Samvaha, and Parivaha.-Tr.], I am Marici; among the stars, I am the moon.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the twelve Adityas, I am Vishnu; among luminaries, the radiant sun; among the seven or forty-nine Maruts, I am Marichi; among stars, I am the moon.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the sons of Aditi, I am Vishnu; of the luminaries, the radiant Sun; of the Maruts, I am Marici; of the stars, I am the Moon.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Of all the creative powers, I am the Creator; of luminaries, the Sun; of winds, the Whirlwind; and of planets, the Moon.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.22।। मैं वेदोंमें सामवेद हूँ, देवताओंमें इन्द्र हूँ, इन्द्रियोंमें मन हूँ और प्राणियोंकी चेतना हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.22।। मैं वेदों में सामवेद हूँ, देवों में वासव (इन्द्र) हूँ; मैं इन्द्रियों में मन और भूतप्राणियों में चेतना (ज्ञानशक्ति) हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of the Vedas, I am the Samaveda; of gods, I am Indra; of sense-organs, I am the mind; and of living beings, I am consciousness.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among the Vedas, I am the Sama-Veda; among the gods, I am Indra. Among the organs, I am the mind, and I am the intelligence in creatures.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the Vedas, I am the Sama-Veda; among the gods, I am Vasava; among the senses, I am the mind; and among living beings, I am intelligence.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the Vedas, I am the Samaveda; of the gods, I am Vasava (Indra); of the sense-organs, I am the mind; of the beings, I am the consciousness.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Of the Vedas, I am the hymns; I am the electric force in the powers of nature; of the senses, I am the mind; and I am the intelligence in all that lives.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.23।। रुद्रोंमें शंकर और यक्ष-राक्षसोंमें कुबेर मैं हूँ।वसुओंमें पावक (अग्नि) और शिखरवाले पर्वतोंमें सुमेरु मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.23।। मैं (ग्यारह) रुद्रों में शंकर हूँ और यक्ष तथा राक्षसों में धनपति कुबेर (वित्तेश) हूँ; (आठ) वसुओं में अग्नि हूँ तथा शिखर वाले पर्वतों में मेरु हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of the Rudras, I am Sankara. Of the Yaksas and Raksasas, I am the Lord of Wealth (Kubera). Of the Vasus, I am Agni; of the mountains, I am Meru.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among the Rudras, I am Aja, Ekapada, Ahirbudhnya, Pinaki, Aparajita, Tryam-baka, Mahesvara, Vrsakapi, Sambhu, Harana, and Isvara; among the Yaksas and goblins, I am Kubera; among the Vasus, I am Fire; and among the mountains, I am Meru.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And among the Rudras, I am Sankara; among the Yakshas and Rakshasas, the Lord of Wealth (Kubera); among the Vasus, I am Pavaka (Fire); and among the seven mountains, I am Meru.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: And of the Rudras, I am Sankara; of the Yaksas and the Raksas, I am the Lord of Wealth (Kubera); of the Vasus, I am the Fire-God; of the mountains, I am Meru.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Among forces of vitality, I am the life; I am Mammon to the heathens and the godless; I am the energy in fire, earth, wind, sky, heaven, sun, moon, and planets; and among mountains, I am Mount Meru.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.24।। हे पार्थ ! पुरोहितोंमें मुख्य बृहस्पतिको मेरा स्वरूप समझो। सेनापतियोंमें स्कन्द और जलाशयोंमें समुद्र मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.24।। हे पार्थ ! पुरोहितों में मुझे बृहस्पति जानो; मैं सेनापतियों में स्कन्द और जलाशयों में समुद्र हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Among family priests, O Arjuna, know Me to be the chief Brhaspati; of generals, I am Skanda; of reservoirs of water, I am the ocean.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Prtha, know me to be Brhaspati, the foremost among the priests of kings. Among commanders of armies, I am Skanda; among large expanses of water, I am the sea.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And, among the household priests of kings, O Arjuna, know Me to be the chief, Brihaspati; among the army generals, I am Skanda; among lakes, I am the ocean.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the royal priests, I am the chief, viz., Brhaspati (the priest of gods), O son of Prtha; you should know that. Of the army generals, I am Skanda [the War-god]; of the water reservoirs, I am the ocean.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Among the priests, know, O Arjuna, that I am the priest Brihaspati; of generals, I am Skanda, the Commander-in-Chief, and of waters, I am the Ocean.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.25।। महर्षियोंमें भृगु और वाणियों-(शब्दों-) में एक अक्षर अर्थात् प्रणव मैं हूँ। सम्पूर्ण यज्ञोंमें जपयज्ञ और स्थिर रहनेवालोंमें हिमालय मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.25।। मैं महर्षियों में भृगु और वाणी (शब्दों) में एकाक्षर ओंकार हूँ। मैं यज्ञों में जपयज्ञ और स्थावरों (अचलों) में हिमालय हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of the great seers, I am Bhrgu; of words, I am the single-lettered word Om; of sacrifices, I am the sacrifice of Japa; of immovable things, I am the Himalayas.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among the great sages, I am Bhrgu; of words, I am the single syllable (Om). Among rituals, I am the ritual of Japa of the immovables, the Himalayas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the great sages, I am Bhrigu; among words, I am the one syllable (Om); among sacrifices, I am the sacrifice of silent repetition; among the immovable things, I am the Himalayas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the great seers, I am Bhrgu; of the words, I am the single-syllable (Om); of the sacrifices [performed with external objects], I am the sacrifice of muttered prayer; of the immovables, I am the Himalayan range.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Of the great seers, I am Bhrigu; of words, I am Om; of offerings, I am the silent prayer; among things immovable, I am the Himalayas.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.26।। सम्पूर्ण वृक्षोंमें पीपल, देवर्षियोंमें नारद, गन्धर्वोंमें चित्ररथ और सिद्धोंमें कपिल मुनि मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.26।। मैं समस्त वृक्षों में अश्वत्थ (पीपल) हूँ और देवर्षियों में नारद हूँ; मैं गन्धर्वों में चित्ररथ और सिद्ध पुरुषों में कपिल मुनि हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of trees I am the Asvattha; of celestial seers, I am Narada; of the Gandharvas, I am Citraratha; of the perfected, I am Kapila.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among all trees, I am the Ashvattha tree, and among divine sages, Narada. Among the Gandharvas, I am Citraratha; among the perfected ones, I am the sage Kapila.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among all the trees, I am the Peepul; among the divine sages, I am Narada; among the Gandharvas, I am Chitraratha; among the perfected, I am the sage Kapila.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of all trees, I am the Pipal tree; of the divine seers, Narada; of the Gandharvas, Citraratha; and of the perfected ones, the sage Kapila.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Of trees, I am the sacred fig tree; of divine seers, Narada; of heavenly singers, I am Chitraratha, their leader; and of sages, I am Kapila.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.27।। घोड़ोंमें अमृतके साथ समुद्रसे प्रकट होनेवाले उच्चैःश्रवा नामक घोड़ेको, श्रेष्ठ हाथियोंमें ऐरावत नामक हाथीको और मनुष्योंमें राजाको मेरी विभूति मानो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.27।। अश्वों में अमृत से उत्पन्न हुए उच्चैश्रवा नामक अश्व, हाथियों में ऐरावत और मनुष्यों में राजा मुझे ही जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of horses, know Me to be Uccaihsravas, the nectar-born. Of lordly elephants, I am Airavata, and of men, I am the monarch.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among horses, know Me to be Uccaihsravas, born from nectar; Airavata among the lordly elephants; and among men, the King of men. [Uccaihsravas and Airavata are respectively the divine horse and elephant of Indra.]

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know Me as Ucchaisravas, born of nectar, among horses; Airavata among lordly elephants; and the king among men.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the horses, you should know Me to be the nectar-born Uccaihsravas (Indra's horse); of the best elephants, the Airavata (Indra's elephant); and of the men, their king.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Know that among horses I am Pegasus, heaven-born; among the lordly elephants I am the white one, and I am the ruler among men.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.28।। आयुधोंमें वज्र और धेनुओंमें कामधेनु मैं हूँ। सन्तान-उत्पत्तिका हेतु कामदेव मैं हूँ और सर्पोंमें वासुकि मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.28।। मैं शस्त्रों में वज्र और धेनुओं (गायों) में कामधेनु हूँ, प्रजा उत्पत्ति का हेतु कन्दर्प (कामदेव) मैं हूँ और सर्पों में वासुकि हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of weapons, I am the Vajra (thunderbolt); of cows, I am Kamadhuk; I am Kandarpa, the cause of progeny; of serpents, I am Vasuki.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows I am Kamadhenu. I am Kandarpa, the Progenitor, and among serpents I am Vasuki.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among weapons, I am the thunderbolt; among cows, I am the wish-fulfilling cow called Kamadhenu; I am the progenitor, the god of love; among serpents, I am Vasuki.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the weapons, I am the Vajra of Indra; of the cows, I am the Wish-fulfilling Cow of the heavens; of the progenitors, I am Kandarpa (the god of love); of the serpents, I am Vasuki.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the thunderbolt among weapons; I am the Cow of Plenty among cows; I am Passion in those who procreate, and I am the Cobra among serpents.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.29।। नागोंमें अनन्त (शेषनाग) और जल-जन्तुओंका अधिपति वरुण मैं हूँ। पितरोंमें अर्यमा और शासन करनेवालोंमें यमराज मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.29।। मैं नागों में अनन्त (शेषनाग) हूँ और जल देवताओं में वरुण हूँ; मैं पितरों में अर्यमा हँ और नियमन करने वालों में यम हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of snakes, I am Ananta; of aquatic beings, I am Varuna; of manes, I am Aryama; of subduers, I am Yama.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among snakes, I am Ananta; among gods of the waters, Varuna; among the manes, Aryama; and among the maintainers of law and order, I am Yama (King of death).

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am Ananta among the Nagas; I am Varuna among water-deities; Aryaman among the Manes; I am Yama among the governors.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the snakes, I am Ananta; of the water-beings, I am Varuna; of the manes, I am Aryaman; of the controllers, I am Yama (the Death-god).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the king-python among snakes, I am the aqueous principle among those that live in water, I am the father of fathers, and among rulers I am death.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.30।। दैत्योंमें प्रह्लाद और गणना करनेवालोंमें काल मैं हूँ । पशुओंमें सिंह और पक्षियोंमें गरुड मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.30।। मैं दैत्यों में प्रह्लाद और गणना करने वालों में काल हूँ, मैं 'पशुओं' में सिंह (मृगेन्द्र) और पक्षियों में गरुड़ हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of Daityas, I am Prahlada; of reckoners, I am Death; of beasts, I am the lion; and of birds, I am Garuda, the son of Vinata.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among demons, I am Prahlada, and among reckoners of time, I am Time. Among animals, I am the lion, and among birds, I am Garuda.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And I am Prahlada among the demons, I am Time among reckoners, I am the lion among beasts, and Vainateya (Garuda) among birds.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: No translation is available for this sloka.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: And I am the devotee Prahlada among the heathens; of Time I am the Eternal Present; I am the Lion among beasts and the Eagle among birds.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.31।। पवित्र करनेवालोंमें वायु और शास्त्रधारियोंमें राम मैं हूँ। जल-जन्तुओंमें मगर मैं हूँ। बहनेवाले स्त्रोतोंमें गङ्गाजी मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.31।। मैं पवित्र करने वालों में वायु हूँ और शस्त्रधारियों में राम हूँ; तथा मत्स्यों (जलचरों) में मैं मगरमच्छ और नदियों में मैं गंगा हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of moving things, I am the wind; of those who bear weapons, I am Rama; of fishers, I am Makara; and of rivers, I am the Ganga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Of the purifiers, I am the air; among the wielders of weapons, I am Rama. Among fishes, I am the shark; among rivers, I am Ganga.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the purifiers, I am the wind; among the warriors, I am Rama; among the fishes, I am the shark; among the streams, I am the Ganga.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the progeny of Diti, I am Prahlada; of the measuring ones, I am the shark; of rivers, I am the daughter of Jahnu—the Ganga.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the wind among purifiers, the king Rama among warriors; I am the crocodile among fishes, and I am the Ganges among rivers.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.32।। हे अर्जुन ! सम्पूर्ण सर्गोंके आदि, मध्य तथा अन्तमें मैं ही हूँ। विद्याओंमें अध्यात्मविद्या और परस्पर शास्त्रार्थ करनेवालोंका(तत्त्व-निर्णयके लिये किया जानेवाला) वाद मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.32।। हे अर्जुन ! सृष्टियों का आदि, अन्त और मध्य भी मैं ही हूँ, मैं विद्याओं में अध्यात्मविद्या और विवाद करने वालों में (अर्थात् विवाद के प्रकारों में) मैं वाद हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of creatures, I am the beginning, the middle, and the end, O Arjuna. Of sciences, I am the science of the Self (of the individual and Universal Self). Of those who argue, I am the fair reasoning.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, of creations I am the beginning, the middle, and the end; I am the knowledge of the Self among knowledge; of those who debate, I am vada.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among creations I am the beginning, the middle, and the end, O Arjuna; among the sciences, I am the science of the Self; and I am the logic among controversialists.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the creations, I am the beginning, the end, and the middle, O Arjuna! Of the sciences, I am the science of the Self; of arguers, I am the argument.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of creation; among sciences, I am the science of spirituality; I am the discussion among disputants.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.33।। अक्षरोंमें अकार और समासोंमें द्वन्द्व समास मैं हूँ। अक्षयकाल अर्थात् कालका भी महाकाल तथा सब ओर मुखवाला धाता भी मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.33।। मैं अक्षरों (वर्णमाला) में अकार और समासों में द्वन्द्व (नामक समास) हूँ; मैं अक्षय काल और विश्वतोमुख (विराट् स्वरूप) धाता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of letters, I am the alphabet 'A'; of compound words, I am the dvandva (copulative); I am myself imperishable time; I am the creator, facing all sides.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Of the letters, I am the letter A, and of the group of compound words, I am the compound called Dvandva. I am Myself the infinite time; I am the Dispenser with faces everywhere.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the letters of the alphabet, I am the letter 'A' and the dual among compounds. I am verily the inexhaustible and everlasting time; I am the dispenser of the fruits of actions, having faces in all directions.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the syllables, I am A; of the compounds, the Dvandva; none but Me is immortal Time; I am the dispenser of fruits of actions, facing on all sides.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Of letters, I am A; I am the copulative in compound words; I am inexhaustible Time; and I am the all-pervading Preserver.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.34।। सबका हरण करनेवाली मृत्यु और उत्पन्न होनेवालोंका उभ्दव मैं हूँ तथा स्त्री-जातिमें कीर्ति, श्री, वाक्, स्मृति, मेधा, धृति और क्षमा मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.34।। मैं सर्वभक्षक मृत्यु और भविष्य में होने वालों की उत्पत्ति का कारण हूँ; स्त्रियों में कीर्ति, श्री, वाक (वाणी), स्मृति, मेधा, धृति और क्षमा हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am also death, which snatches away all. I am the origin of all that will be born. Among women, I am fame, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, endurance, and forgiveness.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And I am Death, the destroyer of all; and the bestower of prosperity to those destined to be prosperous. Of the feminine [Narinam may mean 'of the feminine qualities'. According to Sridhara Swami and S., the words fame etc. signify the goddesses of the respective qualities. According to M.S. these seven goddesses are the wives of the god Dharma.-Tr.] (I am) fame, beauty, speech, memory, intelligence, fortitude, and forbearance.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And I am the all-devouring Death, and the source of prosperity for those who are to be prosperous; among the feminine qualities, I am fame, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, firmness, and forgiveness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the Death that carries away all and also the Birth of all that are to be born; of the wives of men, I am Fame, Fortune, Speech, Memory, Wisdom, Constancy, and Patience.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am all-devouring Death; I am the Origin of all that will happen; I am Fame, Fortune, Speech, Memory, Intellect, Constancy, and Forgiveness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.35।। गायी जानेवाली श्रुतियोंमें बृहत्साम और वैदिक छन्दोंमें गायत्री छन्द मैं हूँ। बारह महीनोंमें मार्गशीर्ष और छः ऋतुओंमें वसन्त मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.35।। सामों (गेय मन्त्रों) में मैं बृहत्साम और छन्दों में गायत्री छन्द हूँ; मैं मासों में मार्गशीर्ष (दिसम्बरजनवरी के भाग) और ऋतुओं में वसन्त हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of Saman hymns, I am the Brhatsaman; of meters, I am the Gayatri; of months, I am Margasira (Nov-Dec); and of seasons, I am the season of flowers.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: I am also the Brhat-sama of the Sama-mantras; of the meters, Gayatri. Of the months, I am Marga-sirsa, and of the seasons, spring.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the hymns, I am the Brihatsaman; among meters, I am Gayatri; among months, I am Margasirsha; among seasons, I am the flowery season.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Likewise, of the modes of singing, I am the Brhatsaman; of the metres, I am the Gayatri; of the months, I am Margasirsa; of the seasons, I am the season abounding with flowers.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Of hymns, I am Brihatsama; of meters, I am Garatri; among months, I am Margasheersha (December); and I am Spring among seasons.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.36।। छल करनेवालोंमें जूआ और तेजस्वियोंमें तेज मैं हूँ। जीतनेवालोंकी विजय, निश्चय करनेवालोंका निश्चय और सात्त्विक मनुष्योंका सात्त्विक भाव मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.36।। मैं छल करने वालों में द्यूत हूँ और तेजस्वियों में तेज हूँ, मैं विजय हूँ; मैं व्यवसाय (उद्यमशीलता) हूँ और सात्विक पुरुषों का सात्विक भाव हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of the fraudulent, I am gambling; I am the brilliance of the brilliant; I am victory, I am effort; I am the magnanimity of the magnanimous.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Of the fraudulent, I am the gambling; I am the irresistible end of the mighty. I am excellence, I am effort, I am the sattva quality of those possessed of sattva.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: I am the gambling of the deceitful; I am the splendor of the splendid; I am victory; I am the resolve of the resolute; I am the goodness of the good.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the destroyer of the fraudulent; I am the brilliance of the brilliant; I am the victory; I am the determination; I am the strength of the strong.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the gambler's cheat and the splendor of the splendid; I am victory; I am effort; and I am the purity of the pure.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.37।। वृष्णिवंशियोंमें वासुदेव और पाण्डवोंमें धनञ्जय मैं हूँ। मुनियोंमें वेदव्यास और कवियोंमें कवि शुक्राचार्य भी मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.37।। मैं वृष्णियों में वासुदेव हूँ और पाण्डवों में धनंजय, मैं मुनियों में व्यास और कवियों में उशना कवि हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of the Vrsnis, I am Vasudeva; of the Pandavas, I am Arjuna; of sages, I am Vyasa; and of seers, I am Usana (Sukra).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Of the Vrsnis, I am Vasudeva; of the Pandavas, Dhananjaya (Arjuna). Of the wise, I am Vyasa; of the omniscient, the omniscient Usanas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Among the Vrishnis, I am Vaasudeva; among the Pandavas, I am Arjuna; among the sages, I am Vyasa; among the poets, I am Usanas, the poet.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of the Vrsnis, I am the son of Vasudeva; of the sons of Pandu, I am Dhananjaya (Arjuna); of the sages, I am Vyasa; and of the seers, I am Usanas.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am Shri Krishna among the Vishnu-clan, Arjuna among the Pandavas, Vyasa among the saints, and Shukracharya among the sages.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.38।। दमन करनेवालोंमें दण्डनीति और विजय चाहनेवालोंमें नीति मैं हूँ। गोपनीय भावोंमें मौन और ज्ञानवानोंमें ज्ञान मैं हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.38।। मैं दमन करने वालों का दण्ड हूँ और विजयेच्छुओं की नीति हूँ; मैं गुह्यों में मौन हूँ और ज्ञानवानों का ज्ञान हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of those who punish, I am the principle of punishment. Of those who seek victory, I am policy. Of secrets, I am also silence. And of the wise, I am wisdom.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Of the punishers, I am the rod; I am the righteous policy of those who desire to conquer. And of things secret, I am verily silence; I am the knowledge of the wise.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Of those who punish, I am the scepter; among those who seek victory, I am statesmanship; and among secrets, I am silence; I am knowledge among knowers.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am the punishment of the punishers; I am the political wisdom of those seeking victory; I am also the silence of the secret ones; I am the knowledge of the knowers.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the scepter of rulers, the strategy of conquerors, the silence of mystery, and the wisdom of the wise.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.39।। हे अर्जुन! सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका जो बीज है, वह बीज मैं ही हूँ; क्योंकि मेरे बिना कोई भी चर-अचर प्राणी नहीं है अर्थात् चर-अचर सब कुछ मैं ही हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.39।। हे अर्जुन ! जो समस्त भूतों की उत्पत्ति का बीज (कारण) है, वह भी में ही हूँ, क्योंकि ऐसा कोई चर और अचर भूत नहीं है, जो मुझसे रहित है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: I am also that which is the seed of all beings, O Arjuna. Nothing that moves or does not move exists without Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Moreover, O Arjuna, I am the seed of all beings. There is nothing, moving or non-moving, that can exist without Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And whatever is the seed of all beings, that too am I, O Arjuna; there is no being, be it moving or unmoving, that can exist without Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Further, O Arjuna, I am the seed of all beings; there is no being, whether moving or non-moving, that can exist without Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am the seed of all beings, O Arjuna! No creature, moving or unmoving, can live without Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.40।। हे परंतप अर्जुन ! मेरी दिव्य विभूतियोंका अन्त नहीं है। मैंने तुम्हारे सामने अपनी विभूतियोंका जो विस्तार कहा है, यह तो केवल संक्षेपसे कहा है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.40।। हे परन्तप ! मेरी दिव्य विभूतियों का अन्त नहीं है; अपनी विभूतियों का यह विस्तार मैंने एक देश से अर्थात् संक्षेप में कहा है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There is no limit to My divine manifestations. Here, I have briefly described the extent of such manifestations.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O destroyer of enemies, there is no limit to My divine manifestations. This description of My manifestations, however, has been stated by Me as an illustration.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There is no end to My divine glories, O Arjuna, but this is a brief statement by Me of the particulars of My divine glory.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O scorcher of foes! There is no end to My extraordinary manifesting power. The above details of My manifesting power have been declared by Me only as examples.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! The aspects of My divine life are endless; I have mentioned only a few as an example.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.41।। जो-जो ऐश्वर्ययुक्त, शोभायुक्त और बलयुक्त प्राणी तथा वस्तु है, उस-उसको तुम मेरे ही तेज-(योग-) के अंशसे उत्पन्न हुई समझो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.41।। जो कोई भी विभूतियुक्त, कान्तियुक्त अथवा शक्तियुक्त वस्तु (या प्राणी) है, उसको तुम मेरे तेज के अंश से ही उत्पन्न हुई जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whatever being is possessed of power, or splendour, or energy, know that as coming from a fragment of My power.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Whatever object [all living beings] is verily endowed with majesty, possessed of prosperity, or is energetic, you know for certain each of them as having a part of My power as its source.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Whatever being there is glorious, prosperous, or powerful, know that to be a manifestation of a part of My splendor.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whatever being exists with manifesting power, beauty, and vigor, be sure that it is born only from a bit of My illumination.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Whatever is glorious, excellent, beautiful, and mighty, be assured that it comes from a fragment of My splendor.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।10.42।। अथवा हे अर्जुन ! तुम्हें इस प्रकार बहुत-सी बातें जाननेकी क्या आवश्यकता है?  मैं अपने किसी एक अंशसे  सम्पूर्ण जगत् को व्याप्त करके स्थित हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।10.42।। अथवा हे अर्जुन ! बहुत जानने से तुम्हारा क्या प्रयोजन है? मैं इस सम्पूर्ण जगत् को अपने एक अंश मात्र से धारण करके स्थित हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: But of what use is all this extensive knowledge to you, O Arjuna? I sustain this whole universe with only a fragment of My power.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Or, on the other hand, what is the need for you to know this extensively, O Arjuna? I remain sustaining this whole creation in a special way with a part of Myself.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But, of what avail is the knowledge of all these details to you, O Arjuna? I exist, supporting this whole world with one part of Myself.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
