text: ।।14.5।। हे महाबाहो ! सत्त्व, रज और तम ये प्रकृति से उत्पन्न तीनों गुण देही आत्मा को देह के साथ बांध देते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Gunas of Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas arise from Prakrti and bind the immutable Self in the body, O Arjuna.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O mighty-armed one, the qualities, viz. sattva, rajas, and tamas, born of Nature, are the immutable embodiments belonging to the body.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: These qualities, O Arjuna, born of Nature, bind fast in the body of the embodied, the indestructible: purity, passion, and inertia.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The strands, namely the sattva, rajas, and tamas, born from the prime cause (the said mother), bind the changeless embodied soul to the body, O mighty-armed one!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Purity, passion, and ignorance are the qualities that the law of nature brings forth. They fetter the free spirit in all beings.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.6।।हे पापरहित अर्जुन ! उन गुणोंमें सत्त्वगुण निर्मल (स्वच्छ) होनेके कारण प्रकाशक और निर्विकार है। वह सुख और ज्ञानकी आसक्तिसे (देहीको) बाँधता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.6।। हे निष्पाप अर्जुन ! इन (तीनों) में, सत्त्वगुण निर्मल होने से प्रकाशक और अनामय (निरुपद्रव, निर्विकार या निरोग) है; (वह जीव को) सुख की आसक्ति से और ज्ञान की आसक्ति से बांध देता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Of these, sattva, being without impurity, is luminous and free from morbidity. It binds, O Arjuna, through attachment to pleasure and knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Among them, sattva, being pure and nirmala (pure-transparent, i.e., capable of resisting any form of ignorance, and hence an illuminator, i.e. a revealer of Consciousness), is harmless. O sinless one, it binds through attachment to happiness and attachment to knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Of these, sattva, which is luminous and healthy due to its stainlessness, binds one by attachment to happiness and knowledge, O sinless one.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Among them, the Sattva, being free from impurity, is illuminating and health-giving; and it binds the embodied one with attachment to happiness and also with attachment to knowledge, O sinless one!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Sinless One! Of these, purity, being luminous, strong, and invulnerable, binds one with its yearning for happiness and illumination.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.7।।हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! तृष्णा और आसक्तिको पैदा करनेवाले रजोगुणको तुम रागस्वरूप समझो। वह कर्मोंकी आसक्तिसे शरीरधारीको बाँधता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.7।। हे कौन्तेय ! रजोगुण को रागस्वरूप जानो, जिससे तृष्णा और आसक्ति उत्पन्न होती है। वह देही आत्मा को कर्मों की आसक्ति से बांधता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know, O Arjuna, that Rajas is of the nature of passion springing from thirst and attachment. It binds the embodied self with attachment to action.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Know rajas to be of the nature of passion, born of hankering and attachment. O son of Kunti, it binds the embodied one through attachment to action.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Know, O Arjuna, that Rajas is of the nature of passion, the source of thirst and attachment; it binds fast the embodied one by attachment to action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: You should know that Rajas is of the nature of desire and is a source of craving-attachment; and it binds the embodied one by attachment to action, O son of Kunti!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Passion, engendered by thirst for pleasure and attachment, binds the soul through its fondness for activities.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.8।।हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन ! सम्पूर्ण देहधारियोंको मोहित करनेवाले तमोगुणको तुम अज्ञानसे उत्पन्न होनेवाला समझो। वह प्रमाद, आलस्य और निद्राके द्वारा देहधारियोंको बाँधता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.8।। और हे भारत ! तमोगुण को अज्ञान से उत्पन्न जानो; जो समस्त देहधारियों (जीवों) को मोहित करने वाला है। वह प्रमाद, आलस्य और निद्रा के द्वारा जीव को बांधता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know that Tamas is born of false knowledge and deludes all embodied beings. It binds, O Arjuna, with negligence, indolence, and sleep.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: On the other hand, know tamas, which deludes all embodied beings, to be born of ignorance. O scion of the Bharata dynasty, it binds through inadvertence, laziness, and sleep.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But know thou Tamas to be born of ignorance, deluding all embodied beings; it binds fast, O Arjuna, through heedlessness, indolence, and sleep.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But, you should also know that Tamas is born of ignorance and deludes all the Embodied; it binds them with negligence, laziness, and sleep, O descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But Ignorance, the product of darkness, stupefies the senses of all embodied beings, binding them with chains of folly, indolence, and lethargy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.9।।हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन ! सत्त्वगुण सुखमें और रजोगुण कर्ममें लगाकर मनुष्यपर विजय करता है तथा तमोगुण ज्ञानको ढककर एवं प्रमादमें भी लगाकर मनुष्यपर विजंय करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.9।। हे भारत ! सत्त्वगुण सुख में आसक्त कर देता है और रजोगुण कर्म में, किन्तु तमोगुण ज्ञान को आवृत्त करके जीव को प्रमाद से युक्त कर देता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Sattva generates attachment to pleasure, Rajas to action, O Arjuna. But Tamas, obscuring knowledge, generates attachment to negligence.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, sattva attaches one to happiness, rajas to action, while tamas, obscuring knowledge, leads to inadvertence as well.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Sattva attaches to happiness, Rajas to action, O Arjuna, while Tamas, verily shrouding knowledge, attaches to heedlessness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O descendant of Bharata! The Sattva fully dominates the Embodied in the field of happiness; the Rajas in action; but the Tamas also totally dominates in the field of negligence, by veiling knowledge.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Purity brings happiness, passion brings commotion, and ignorance, which obscures wisdom, leads to a life of failure.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.10।।हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन ! रजोगुण और तमोगुणको दबाकर सत्त्वगुण सत्त्वगुण, और तमोगुणको दबाकर रजोगुण, वैसे ही सत्त्वगुण और रजोगुणको दबाकर तमोगुण बढ़ता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.10।। हे भारत ! कभी रज और तम को अभिभूत (दबा) करके सत्त्वगुण की वृद्धि होती है, कभी रज और सत्त्व को दबाकर तमोगुण की वृद्धि होती है, तो कभी तम और सत्त्व को अभिभूत कर रजोगुण की वृद्धि होती है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Prevailing over Rajas and Tamas, Sattva predominates, O Arjuna. Prevailing over Tamas and Sattva, Rajas predominates. Prevailing over Rajas and Sattva, Tamas predominates.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, sattva increases by subduing rajas and tamas; rajas increases by overpowering sattva and tamas; and tamas increases by dominating over sattva and rajas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Now, O Arjuna, Sattva prevails, having overpowered Rajas and Tamas; then Rajas, having overpowered Sattva and Tamas; and then Tamas, having overpowered Sattva and Rajas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O descendant of Bharata! The Sattva increases by overpowering the Rajas and the Tamas; the Rajas increases by overpowering the Sattva and the Tamas; and the Tamas increases likewise by overpowering the Sattva and the Rajas.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Prince! Purity prevails when passion and ignorance are overcome; passion, when purity and ignorance are overcome; and ignorance when it is overcome by purity and passion.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.11।।जब इस मनुष्यशरीरमें सब द्वारों-(इन्द्रियों और अन्तःकरण-) में प्रकाश (स्वच्छता) और ज्ञान (विवेक) प्रकट हो जाता है, तब जानना चाहिये कि सत्त्वगुण बढ़ा हुआ है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.11।। जब इस देह के द्वारों अर्थात् समस्त इन्द्रियों में ज्ञानरूप प्रकाश उत्पन्न होता है, तब सत्त्वगुण को प्रवृद्ध हुआ जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: When knowledge, like light, illuminates from all gateways (i.e., the senses), then one should know that Sattva prevails.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When the illumination of knowledge radiates through all the doors of the senses in this body, then one should know that sattva has greatly increased.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When the wisdom-light shines through every gate of this body, then it may be known that Sattva is predominant.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When the knowledge-light arises in all the gates of this body, then one should also know that the Sattva has predominantly increased.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When the light of knowledge gleams forth from all the gates of the body, then be sure that purity prevails.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.12।।हे भरतवंशमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! रजोगुणके बढ़नेपर लोभ, प्रवृत्ति, कर्मोंका आरम्भ, अशान्ति और स्पृहा -- ये वृत्तियाँ पैदा होती हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.12।। हे भरत-श्रेष्ठ ! रजोगुण के प्रवृद्ध होने पर लोभ, प्रवृत्ति (सामान्य चेष्टा) कर्मों का आरम्भ, शम का अभाव तथा स्पृहा, ये सब उत्पन्न होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Greed, activity, undertaking of work, restlessness, and longing—these arise, O Arjuna, when Rajas predominates.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O best of the Bharata dynasty, when rajas becomes predominant, these come into being: avarice, restlessness, undertaking of actions, agitation, and craving.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Greed, activity, the undertaking of actions, restlessness, and longing—these arise when Rajas is predominant, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Greed, exertion, undertaking of actions, restlessness, and craving—these are born when Rajas predominates, O chief of the Bharatas!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O best of Indians! Avarice, the impulse to act, and the beginning of action itself are all due to the dominance of passion.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.13।।हे कुरुनन्दन ! तमोगुणके बढ़नेपर अप्रकाश, अप्रवृत्ति, प्रमाद और मोह -- ये वृत्तियाँ भी पैदा होती हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.13।। हे कुरुनन्दन ! तमोगुण के प्रवृद्ध होने पर अप्रकाश, अप्रवृत्ति, प्रमाद और मोह ये सब उत्पन्न होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Non-illumination, inactivity, negligence, and even delusion—these arise, O Arjuna, when Tamas prevails.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty, when tamas predominates, these come into being without exception: non-discrimination, inactivity, inadvertence, and delusion.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Darkness, inertia, carelessness, and delusion—these arise when Tamas is predominant, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Absence of mental illumination, lack of exertion, negligence, and mere delusion—these are born when Tamas predominates, O darling of the Kurus!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Darkness, stagnation, folly, and infatuation are the results of the dominance of ignorance, O joy of the Kuru clan!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.14।।जिस समय सत्त्वगुण बढ़ा हो, उस समय यदि देहधारी मनुष्य मर जाता है, तो वह,उत्तमवेत्ताओंके निर्मल लोकोंमें जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.14।। जब यह जीव (देहभृत्) सत्त्वगुण की प्रवृद्धि में मृत्यु को प्राप्त होता है, तब उत्तम कर्म करने वालों के निर्मल अर्थात् स्वर्गादि लोकों को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: If the embodied self dissolves when Sattva prevails, then it proceeds to the pure worlds of those who know the highest.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When an embodied one undergoes death while sattva is exclusively predominant, then he attains the untainted worlds of those who know the highest (entities).

author: Swami Sivananda
text: If the embodied one meets death when Sattva is predominant, then they attain the spotless worlds of the knowers of the Highest.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But if the body-bearer dies when sattva is on the increase, then he attains to the spotless worlds of those who know the highest.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When purity prevails, the soul, upon leaving the body, passes on to the pure regions where those who know the Highest reside.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.15।।रजोगुणके बढ़नेपर मरनेवाला प्राणी मनुष्ययोनिमें जन्म लेता है तथा तमोगुणके बढ़नेपर मरनेवाला मूढ़योनियोंमें जन्म लेता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.15।। रजोगुण के प्रवृद्ध काल में मृत्यु को प्राप्त होकर कर्मासक्ति वाले (मनुष्य) लोक में वह जन्म लेता है तथा तमोगुण के प्रवृद्धकाल में (मरण होने पर) मूढ़योनि में जन्म लेता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text:
(a) When Rajas is prevalent, one meets with dissolution and is born among those attached to work.

(b) Similarly, when Tamas prevails, one who has met with dissolution is born in the womb of beings lacking intelligence.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When one dies while rajas predominates, they are born among people attached to activity. Similarly, when one dies while tamas predominates, they take birth among the stupid species.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Meeting death in Rajas, he is born among those who are attached to action; and dying in Tamas, he is born in the womb of the thoughtless.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: By meeting death when Rajas is on the increase, he is born among those who are attached to action; likewise, by meeting death when Tamas is on the increase, he is born in the wombs of the deluded.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When passion prevails, the soul is reborn among those who love activity; when ignorance rules, it enters the wombs of the ignorant.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.16।।(विवेकी पुरुषोंने) शुभ-कर्मका तो सात्त्विक निर्मल फल कहा है, राजस कर्मका फल दुःख कहा है और तामस कर्मका फल अज्ञान (मूढ़ता) कहा है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.16।। शुभ कर्म का फल सात्विक और निर्मल कहा गया है; रजोगुण का फल दु;ख और तमोगुण का फल अज्ञान है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: It is said that the fruit of a good deed is pure and of the nature of Sattva; the fruit of Rajas is pain; and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: They say that the result of good work is pure and is born of Sattva. But the result of Rajas is sorrow; the result of Tamas is ignorance.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: They say that the fruit of good action is Sattvic and pure; indeed, the fruit of Rajas is pain, and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: It is said that the fruit of good action is spotless and of the Sattva nature; whereas the fruit of Rajas is pain, and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: They say the fruit of a meritorious action is spotless and pure; the outcome of passion is misery, and of ignorance, darkness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.17।।सत्त्वगुणसे ज्ञान और रजोगुणसे लोभ आदि ही उत्पन्न होते हैं; तमोगुणसे प्रमाद, मोह एवं अज्ञान भी उत्पन्न होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.17।। सत्त्वगुण से ज्ञान उत्पन्न होता है। रजोगुण से लोभ तथा तमोगुण से प्रमाद, मोह और अज्ञान उत्पन्न होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: From Sattva arises knowledge, from Rajas arises greed, from Tamas arises negligence and delusion, and from it arises ignorance.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: From sattva is born knowledge, and from rajas, avarice indeed. From tamas are born inadvertence, delusion, and ignorance, to be sure.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: From Sattva arises knowledge, and greed from Rajas; heedlessness and delusion arise from Tamas, and also ignorance.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: From Sattva arises wisdom; from Rajas, only greed; and from Tamas arise negligence, delusion, and ignorance.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Purity engenders wisdom, passion avarice, and ignorance folly, infatuation, and darkness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.18।।सत्त्वगुणमें स्थित मनुष्य ऊर्ध्वलोकोंमें जाते हैं, रजोगुणमें स्थित मनुष्य मृत्युलोकमें जन्म लेते हैं और निन्दनीय तमोगुणकी वृत्तिमें स्थित मनुष्य अधोगतिमें जाते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.18।। सत्त्वगुण में स्थित पुरुष उच्च (लोकों को) जाते हैं; राजस पुरुष मध्य (मनुष्य लोक) में रहते हैं और तमोगुण की अत्यन्त हीन प्रवृत्तियों में स्थित तामस लोग अधोगति को प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Those who rest in Sattva ascend upwards; those who abide in Rajas stay in the middle; and those, abiding in the tendencies of Tamas descend downwards.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: People who conform to sattva go higher up; those who conform to rajas stay in the middle; those who conform to tamas, who conform to the actions of the lowest quality, go down.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Those seated in Sattva ascend; those of Rajasic nature dwell in the middle; and those of Tamasic nature, abiding in the function of the lowest Guna, descend.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those established in Sattva ascend; those given to Rajas remain in the middle; those given to Tamas, established in the tendencies of bad qualities, descend.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When purity is in the ascendant, the man evolves; when passion is in the ascendant, he neither evolves nor degenerates; when ignorance is in the ascendant, he is lost.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.19।।जब विवेकी (विचारकुशल) मनुष्य तीनों गुणोंके सिवाय अन्य किसीको कर्ता नहीं देखता और अपनेको गुणोंसे पर अनुभव करता है, तब वह मेरे स्वरूपको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.19।। जब द्रष्टा (साधक) पुरुष तीनों गुणों के अतिरिक्त किसी अन्य को कर्ता नहीं देखता, अर्थात् नहीं समझता है और तीनों गुणों से परे मेरे तत्व को जानता है, तब वह मेरे स्वरूप को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: When the seer beholds no agent of action other than the Gunas, and knows what is beyond the Gunas, he attains to My state.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When the witness sees none other than the qualities as the agent, and knows that which is superior to the qualities, he attains My nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When the seer beholds no agent other than the Gunas and knows that which is higher than them, he attains to My Being.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: When the Perceiver (the Self) finds no agent other than the Strands, and realizes That which is beyond the Strands, then he attains My state.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As soon as one understands that it is only the qualities that act and nothing else, and perceives that which is beyond, they attain My divine nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.20।।देहधारी (विवेकी मनुष्य) देहको उत्पन्न करनेवाले इन तीनों गुणोंका अतिक्रमण करके जन्म, मृत्यु और वृद्धावस्थारूप दुःखोंसे रहित हुआ अमरताका अनुभव करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.20।। यह देही पुरुष शरीर की उत्पत्ति के कारणरूप तीनों गुणों से अतीत होकर जन्म, मृत्यु, जरा और दु:खों से विमुक्त हुआ अमृतत्व को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The embodied Self, crossing beyond these three Gunas that arise in the body, and being freed from birth, death, aging, and pain, attains immortality.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Having transcended these three qualities, which are the origin of the body, the embodied one becomes free from birth, death, old age, and sorrows, and experiences Immortality.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The embodied one, having crossed beyond these three Gunas from which the body is evolved, is freed from birth, death, decay, and pain, and attains immortality.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Transcending these three strands, from which the body [etc.] is born, the embodied (the soul) is freed from birth, death, old age, and sorrow, and attains immortality.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When the soul transcends the Qualities, which are the real cause of physical existence, then, freed from birth and death, from old age and misery, he drinks the nectar of immortality.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.21।।अर्जुन बोले -- हे प्रभो ! इन तीनों गुणोंसे अतीत हुआ मनुष्य किन लक्षणोंसे युक्त होता है? उसके आचरण कैसे होते हैं? और इन तीनों गुणोंका अतिक्रमण कैसे किया जा सकता है?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.21।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- हे प्रभो ! इन तीनो गुणों से अतीत हुआ पुरुष किन लक्षणों से युक्त होता है ? वह किस प्रकार के आचरण वाला होता है ? और, वह किस उपाय से इन तीनों गुणों से अतीत होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "What are the marks of a person who has transcended the three Gunas? What is their behavior? And how do they transcend the three Gunas?"

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Lord, by what signs is one known who has gone beyond these three qualities? What is their behavior, and how do they transcend these three qualities?"

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "What are the marks of one who has transcended the three qualities, O Lord? What is their conduct, and how do they go beyond these three qualities?"

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "O Master! With what characteristic marks does he who has transcended these three strands exist? What is his behavior? How does he pass beyond these three strands?"

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna asked, "My Lord, how can one recognize someone who has transcended the qualities? How do they act and live beyond them?"

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.22।।श्रीभगवान् बोले -- हे पाण्डव ! प्रकाश, प्रवृत्ति तथा मोह -- ये सभी अच्छी तरहसे प्रवृत्त हो जायँ तो भी गुणातीत मनुष्य इनसे द्वेष नहीं करता, और ये सभी निवृत्त हो जायँ तो इनकी इच्छा नहीं करता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.22।। श्रीभगवान् ने कहा -- हे पाण्डव ! (ज्ञानी पुरुष) प्रकाश, प्रवृत्ति और मोह के प्रवृत्त होने पर भी उनका द्वेष नहीं करता तथा निवृत्त होने पर उनकी आकांक्षा नहीं करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "He does not hate illumination, nor activity, nor even delusion, O Arjuna, while these prevail, nor does He long for them when they cease."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "O son of Pandu, he neither dislikes illumination (knowledge), activity, nor delusion when they appear, nor does he long for them when they disappear."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "When light, activity, and delusion are present, he does not hate them, nor does he long for them when they are absent.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, "O son of Pandu! He neither abhors nor craves for illumination, exertion, and delusion, as and when they arise or cease to be."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna replied: O Prince! He who does not shun the quality that is present and does not long for that which is absent;

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.23।।जो उदासीनकी तरह स्थित है और जो गुणोंके द्वारा विचलित नहीं किया जा सकता तथा गुण ही (गुणोंमें) बरत रहे हैं -- इस भावसे जो अपने स्वरूपमें ही स्थित रहता है और स्वयं कोई भी चेष्टा नहीं करता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.23।। जो उदासीन के समान आसीन होकर गुणों के द्वारा विचलित नहीं किया जा सकता और "गुण ही व्यवहार करते हैं" ऐसा जानकर स्थित रहता है और उस स्थिति से विचलित नहीं होता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who sits like one unconcerned, undisturbed by the Gunas; who knows, 'It is the Gunas that are in motion,' and so remains unshaken;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who, sitting like one indifferent, is not distracted by the three qualities; he who, thinking that the qualities alone act, remains firm and does not move surely;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who, seated like one unconcerned, is not moved by the dualities, and who, knowing that the dualities are active, is self-centered and does not move.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who, sitting like an unconcerned person, is not perturbed by the strands; who is ignorant of the existence of the strands; or who remains simply aware that the strands alone exist; who is not shaken;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who maintains an attitude of indifference, who is not disturbed by the qualities, who realizes that it is only they who act, and remains calm;

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.24।।जो धीर मनुष्य सुख-दुःखमें सम तथा अपने स्वरूपमें स्थित रहता है; जो मिट्टीके ढेले, पत्थर और सोनेमें सम रहता है जो प्रिय-अप्रियमें तथा अपनी निन्दा-स्तुतिमें सम रहता है; जो मान-अपमानमें तथा मित्र-शत्रुके पक्षमें सम रहता है जो सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंके आरम्भका त्यागी है, वह मनुष्य गुणातीत कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.24।। जो स्वस्थ (स्वरूप में स्थित), सुख-दु:ख में समान रहता है तथा मिट्टी, पत्थर और स्वर्ण में समदृष्टि रखता है; ऐसा वीर पुरुष प्रिय और अप्रिय को तथा निन्दा और आत्मस्तुति को तुल्य समझता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who is alike in pleasure and pain, who dwells in his Self, who looks upon a clod, a stone, and a piece of gold as of equal value, who remains the same towards things dear and hateful, and who is intelligent, who regards both blame and praise of himself as equal.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He to whom sorrow and happiness are alike, who is established in his own Self, to whom a lump of earth, iron, and gold are the same, to whom the agreeable and the disagreeable are the same, who is wise, to whom censure and his own praise are the same;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Who is the same in pleasure and pain, who dwells in the Self, to whom a clod of earth, a stone, and gold are all alike, who is the same to the dear and the unfriendly, who is firm, and to whom censure and praise are one and the same.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: To whom pain, pleasure, and sleep are all the same; to whom a cold, a stone, and a lump of gold are all the same; to whom both the pleasant and unpleasant things are equal; who is firm-minded; to whom blame and personal commendation are equal.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Who accepts pain and pleasure as they come, is centered in their Self, to whom a piece of clay, stone, or gold are all the same, who neither likes nor dislikes, who is steadfast, indifferent alike to praise or censure;

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.25।।जो धीर मनुष्य सुख-दुःखमें सम तथा अपने स्वरूपमें स्थित रहता है; जो मिट्टीके ढेले, पत्थर और सोनेमें सम रहता है जो प्रिय-अप्रियमें तथा अपनी निन्दा-स्तुतिमें सम रहता है जो मान-अपमानमें तथा मित्र-शत्रुके पक्षमें सम रहता है; जो सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंके आरम्भका त्यागी है, वह मनुष्य गुणातीत कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.25।। जो मान और अपमान में सम है; शत्रु और मित्र के पक्ष में भी सम है, ऐसा सर्वारम्भ परित्यागी पुरुष गुणातीत कहा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who is the same in honor and dishonor, and the same to friend and foe, and who has abandoned all enterprises—he is said to have risen above the Gunas.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who is the same under honor and dishonor, who is equitably disposed towards both friends and foes, who has renounced all undertakings—he is said to have gone beyond dualities.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Who is the same in honor and dishonor, the same to friend and foe, abandoning all undertakings, he is said to have transcended the dualities.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Who remains equal to honor and dishonor, and equal to the sides of both the friend and the foe; and who has given up all fruits of his actions—he is said to have transcended the bonds.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Who looks equally upon honor and dishonor, loves friends and foes alike, and abandons all initiative, such is he who transcends the qualities.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.26।।जो मनुष्य अव्यभिचारी भक्तियोगके द्वारा मेरा सेवन करता है, वह इन गुणोंका अतिक्रमण करके ब्रह्मप्राप्तिका पात्र हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.26।। जो पुरुष अव्यभिचारी भक्तियोग के द्वारा मेरी सेवा अर्थात् उपासना करता है, वह इन तीनों गुणों के अतीत होकर ब्रह्म बनने के लिये योग्य हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: And he who, with unwavering devotion through Yoga, serves Me, he, transcending the Gunas, becomes fit for the state of Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And he who serves Me through the unswerving Yoga of Devotion, he, having gone beyond these qualities, qualifies for becoming Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And he who serves Me with unwavering devotion, he, crossing beyond the dualities, is fit for becoming Brahman.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever serves me alone with an unwavering devotion to yoga, they will transcend these strands and become Brahman.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: And he who serves Me alone, with unwavering devotion, shall overcome the Qualities and become one with the Eternal.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।14.27।।क्योंकि ब्रह्म, अविनाशी अमृत, शाश्वत धर्म और ऐकान्तिक सुखका आश्रय मैं ही हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।14.27।। क्योंकि मैं अमृत, अव्यय, ब्रह्म, शाश्वत धर्म और ऐकान्तिक अर्थात् पारमार्थिक सुख की प्रतिष्ठा हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: For I am the ground of Brahman, the immortal, immutable, and eternal Dharma, and perfect bliss.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: For I am the Abode of Brahman—the indestructible, immutable, eternal, Dharma, and absolute Bliss.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: For I am the abode of Brahman, the immortal, immutable, and everlasting Dharma, and absolute bliss.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: 'I' am the support for the immortal and changeless Brahman and for its eternal attribute, the unalloyed happiness.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: For I am the home of the spirit, the continual source of immortality, eternal righteousness, and infinite joy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.1।। श्रीभगवान् बोले -- ऊपरकी ओर मूलवाले तथा नीचेकी ओर शाखावाले जिस संसाररूप अश्वत्थवृक्षको अव्यय कहते हैं और वेद जिसके पत्ते हैं, उस संसारवृक्षको जो जानता है, वह सम्पूर्ण वेदोंको जाननेवाला है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.1।। श्री भगवान् ने कहा -- (ज्ञानी पुरुष इस संसार वृक्ष को) ऊर्ध्वमूल और अध:शाखा वाला अश्वत्थ और अव्यय कहते हैं; जिसके पर्ण छन्द अर्थात् वेद हैं, ऐसे (संसार वृक्ष) को जो जानता है, वह वेदवित् है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "They speak of an immutable Asvattha tree with its roots above and branches below; its leaves are the Vedas. He who knows it knows the Vedas."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said, "They say that the peepul tree, which has its roots upward and its branches downward, and of which the Vedas are the leaves, is imperishable. He who realizes it is a knower of the Vedas."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said: They (the wise) speak of the indestructible peepul tree, with its roots above and branches below, whose leaves are the meters or hymns; he who knows it is a knower of the Vedas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said, [The scriptures] speak of a non-perishing holy fig tree, whose roots are high and branches are low, and of which the [Vedic] hymns are the leaves. He who knows this tree is the knower of the Vedas.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna continued: This phenomenal creation, which is both ephemeral and eternal, is like a tree, with its seed above in the Highest and its ramifications below on this earth. The scriptures are its leaves, and he who understands this knows.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.2।।उस संसारवृक्षकी गुणों-(सत्त्व, रज और तम-) के द्वारा बढ़ी हुई तथा विषयरूप कोंपलोंवाली शाखाएँ नीचे, मध्यमें और ऊपर सब जगह फैली हुई हैं। मनुष्यलोकमें कर्मोंके अनुसार बाँधनेवाले मूल भी नीचे और ऊपर (सभी लोकोंमें) व्याप्त हो रहे हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.2।। उस वृक्ष की शाखाएं गुणों से प्रवृद्ध हुईं नीचे और ऊपर फैली हुईं हैं; (पंच) विषय इसके अंकुर हैं; मनुष्य लोक में कर्मों का अनुसरण करने वाली इसकी अन्य जड़ें नीचे फैली हुईं हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text:
(a) Its branches extend both above and below, nourished by the Gunas. Their shoots are sense objects. 

(b) And their secondary roots extend downwards, resulting in actions that bind in the world of humans.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The branches of that Tree, extending downwards and upwards, are strengthened by the qualities and have sense-objects as their shoots. And the roots, which are followed by actions, spread downwards in the human world [According to A.G. and M.S. manusya-loke means a body distinguished by Brahminhood etc.].

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Its branches spread below and above, nourished by the Gunas; its buds are sense-objects, and its roots stretch forth below in the world of men, originating action.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Of which tree, the branches spreading downward and upward, well-developed with strands, have sense objects as sprouts; and its roots below in the human world, stretching successively, have actions as their sub-knots.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Its branches shoot upwards and downwards, deriving their nourishment from the qualities; its buds are the objects of sense; and its roots, which follow the law, causing man's regeneration and degeneration, pierce downwards into the soil.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.3।।इस संसारवृक्षका जैसा रूप देखनेमें आता है, वैसा यहाँ (विचार करनेपर) मिलता नहीं; क्योंकि इसका न तो आदि है, न अन्त है और न स्थिति ही है। इसलिये इस दृढ़ मूलोंवाले संसाररूप अश्वत्थवृक्षको दृढ़ असङ्गतारूप शस्त्रके द्वारा काटकर --

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.3।। इस (संसार वृक्ष) का स्वरूप जैसा कहा गया है वैसा यहाँ उपलब्ध नहीं होता है, क्योंकि इसका न आदि है और न अंत और न प्रतिष्ठा ही है। इस अति दृढ़ मूल वाले अश्वत्थ वृक्ष को दृढ़ असङ्ग शस्त्र से काटकर ...৷৷৷৷।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Its form is not perceived here, nor its end, nor its beginning, nor its support. Having cut off this firmly-rooted Asvattha with the strong axe of detachment.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Its form is not perceived here in that way; nor its end, nor beginning, nor its continuance. After felling this Peepul tree whose roots are well developed, with the strong sword of detachment.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Its form is not perceived here as such, nor its end, origin, foundation, or resting place; having cut asunder this firmly rooted peepul tree with the strong axe of non-attachment.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The nature of this is not perceived in that way, nor its end, nor its beginning, nor its center (middle). Cutting this holy fig tree, with its firmly and variedly grown roots, by means of the sharp (or strong) axe of non-attachment;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: In this world, its true form is not known, neither its origin nor its end, and its strength is not understood—until the tree, with its roots striking deep into the earth, is hewn down by the sharp axe of non-attachment.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.4।।उसके बाद उस परमपद-(परमात्मा-) की खोज करनी चाहिये जिसको प्राप्त होनेपर मनुष्य फिर लौटकर संसारमें नहीं आते और जिससे अनादिकालसे चली आनेवाली यह सृष्टि विस्तारको प्राप्त हुई है, उस आदिपुरुष परमात्माके ही मैं शरण हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.4।। (तदुपरान्त) उस पद का अन्वेषण करना चाहिए जिसको प्राप्त हुए पुरुष पुन: संसार में नहीं लौटते हैं। "मैं उस आदि पुरुष की शरण हूँ, जिससे यह पुरातन प्रवृत्ति प्रसृत हुई है"।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Then, one should seek that goal, attaining which one never returns. One should seek refuge with that Primal Person from whom this ancient activity streamed forth.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Thereafter, that state must be sought, going where they do not return again: I take refuge in that Primeval Person Himself, from whom the eternal Manifestation has ensued.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Then, that goal should be sought for, to which, having gone, none returns again. I seek refuge in that Primeval Purusha, from whence streamed forth the ancient activity or energy.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Then, that Abode must be sought, having reached which one would not return. The Yogin would attain nothing but that Primal Person, from Whom the old activity (world creation) commences.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Beyond lies the path, from which, once found, there is no return. This is the Primal God from whom this ancient creation has sprung.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.5।।जो मान और मोहसे रहित हो गये हैं, जिन्होंने आसक्तिसे होनेवाले दोषोंको जीत लिया है, जो नित्य-निरन्तर परमात्मामें ही लगे हुए हैं, जो (अपनी दृष्टिसे) सम्पूर्ण कामनाओंसे रहित हो गये हैं, जो सुख-दुःखरूप द्वन्द्वोंसे मुक्त हो गये हैं, ऐसे (ऊँची स्थितिवाले) मोहरहित साधक भक्त उस अविनाशी परमपद-(परमात्मा-) को प्राप्त होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.5।। जिनका मान और मोह निवृत्त हो गया है, जिन्होंने संगदोष को जीत लिया है, जो अध्यात्म में स्थित हैं जिनकी कामनाएं निवृत्त हो चुकी हैं और जो सुख-दु:ख नामक द्वन्द्वों से विमुक्त हो गये हैं, ऐसे सम्मोह रहित ज्ञानीजन उस अव्यय पद को प्राप्त होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Without the delusion of perverse notions concerning the self, victorious over the evil of attachment, ever devoted to the Self, turned away from desires and liberated from dualities such as pleasure and pain, the undeluded go to that imperishable state.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The wise ones, free from pride and non-discrimination, who have conquered the evil of association [hatred and love arising from association with foes and friends], who are ever devoted to spirituality, completely free from desires, and free from the dualities of happiness and sorrow, reach that undecaying state.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Free from pride and delusion, victorious over the evil of attachment, dwelling constantly in the Self, their desires having completely turned away, freed from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain, they, the undeluded, reach the eternal goal.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Those who are rid of pride and delusion, have put down the evils of attachment, remain constantly in their own nature of the Self, have their desires completely departed, and are fully liberated from the pairs known as pleasures and pains—these undeluded men go to that changeless Abode.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The wise attain Eternity when, freed from pride and delusion, they have conquered their love for the things of sense; when, renouncing desire and fixing their gaze on the Self, they cease to be tossed to and fro by the opposing sensations, such as pleasure and pain.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.6।।उस-(परमपद-) को न सूर्य, न चन्द्र और न अग्नि ही प्रकाशित कर सकती है; और जिसको प्राप्त होकर जीव लौटकर (संसारमें) नहीं आते, वही मेरा परमधाम है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.6।। उसे न सूर्य प्रकाशित कर सकता है और न चन्द्रमा और न अग्नि। जिसे प्राप्त कर मनुष्य पुन: (संसार को) नहीं लौटते हैं, वह मेरा परम धाम है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That supreme light, upon reaching which they do not return any more, is Mine; neither the sun nor the moon nor the fire illuminates It.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire illuminates That. That is My supreme Abode, to which they do not return.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Neither does the sun illuminate there, nor the moon, nor the fire; having gone there, they do not return; that is My supreme abode.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The sun does not illuminate That; nor the moon nor the fire; That is My Supreme Abode, having gone to Which they (Yogins) never return.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor fire shines there. Those who go there never come back. For, O Arjuna, that is my celestial home!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.7।।इस संसारमें जीव बना हुआ आत्मा मेरा ही सनातन अंश है; परन्तु वह प्रकृतिमें स्थित मन और पाँचों इन्द्रियोंको आकर्षित करता है (अपना मान लेता है)।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.7।। इस जीव लोक में मेरा ही एक सनातन अंश जीव बना है। वह प्रकृति में स्थित हुआ (देहत्याग के समय) पाँचो इन्द्रियों तथा मन को अपनी ओर खींच लेता है अर्थात् उन्हें एकत्रित कर लेता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: An everlasting part of Myself, having become the bound self in the world of life, attracts the senses, of which the mind is the sixth, and which abide in Prakriti.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: It is indeed a part of Mine that, becoming the eternal individual soul in the region of living beings, draws the organs, which have the mind as their sixth, and which abide in Nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: An eternal portion of Myself having become a living soul in the world of life, draws to itself the five senses, with the mind as the sixth, abiding in Nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: A portion of My own Self, having become the eternal individual Soul in the world of the living beings, draws [into service] the sense organs, of which the sixth is the mind, and which rest in Prakriti.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is only a very small part of My Eternal Self, which is the life of the universe, that draws around itself the six senses, with the mind being the last, which have their source in Nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.8।।जैसे वायु गन्धके स्थानसे गन्धको ग्रहण करके ले जाती है, ऐसे ही शरीरादिका स्वामी बना हुआ जीवात्मा भी जिस शरीरको छोड़ता है, वहाँसे मनसहित इन्द्रियोंको ग्रहण करके फिर जिस शरीरको प्राप्त होता है, उसमें चला जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.8।। जब (देहादि का) ईश्वर (जीव) (एक शरीर से) उत्क्रमण करता है, तब इन (इन्द्रियों और मन) को ग्रहण कर अन्य शरीर में इस प्रकार ले जाता है, जैसे गन्ध के आश्रय (फूलादि) से गन्ध को वायु ले जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Whatever body its lord acquires and from whatever body it departs, it goes on its way, taking these senses as the wind carries scents from their places.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: When the master leaves it and even when he assumes a body, he departs taking these with him, just as the wind carries away odours from their receptacles.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: When the Lord, as the individual soul, obtains a body and when He leaves it, He takes these with Him, just as the wind takes the scents from their seats (flowers, etc.).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whatever body He attains to and also from whatever He goes up, the Lord proceeds taking them with Him just as the wind takes odours from their receptacle.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When the Supreme Lord enters a body or leaves it, He gathers these senses together and travels with them, as the wind gathers perfume while passing through the flowers.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.9।।यह जीवात्मा मनका आश्रय लेकर श्रोत्र, नेत्र, त्वचा, रसना और घ्राण -- इन पाँचों इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा विषयोंका सेवन करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.9।। (यह जीव) श्रोत्र, चक्षु, स्पर्शेन्द्रिय, रसना और घ्राण (नाक) इन इन्द्रियों तथा मन को आश्रय करके अर्थात् इनके द्वारा विषयों का सेवन करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Presiding over the ear, the eye, the sense of touch, the tongue, the nose, and the mind, It experiences these objects of the senses.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: This one enjoys the objects by presiding over the ears, eyes, skin, tongue, nose, and mind.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Presiding over the ears, eyes, touch, taste, smell, and mind, it enjoys the objects of the senses.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Presiding over the ear, the eye, the sense of touch, the sense of taste, the sense of smell, and the mind, He enjoys the sense objects.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He is the perception of the ear, the eye, the touch, the taste, the smell, and the mind; and the enjoyment of the things they perceive is also His.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.10।।शरीरको छोड़कर जाते हुए या दूसरे शरीरमें स्थित हुए अथवा विषयोंको भोगते हुए भी गुणोंसे युक्त जीवात्माके स्वरूपको मूढ़ मनुष्य नहीं जानते, ज्ञानरूपी नेत्रोंवाले ज्ञानी मनुष्य ही जानते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.10।। शरीर को त्यागते हुये, उसमें स्थित हुये अथवा (विषयों को) भोगते हुये, गुणों से समन्वित आत्मा को विमूढ़ लोग नहीं देखते हैं; (परन्तु) ज्ञानचक्षु वाले पुरुष उसे देखते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The deluded do not perceive it conjoined with the Gunas when departing, staying, or experiencing. Those who have the eye of knowledge, however, see it.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Persons who are diversely deluded do not see it even when it is leaving, residing, experiencing, or in association with the qualities; those with the eye of knowledge see.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The deluded do not see Him who departs, stays, and enjoys; but those who possess the eye of knowledge behold Him.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The deluded do not perceive Him; [but] the men of knowledge-eye do see Him, as He dwells, rises up, or enjoys what is endowed with strands.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The ignorant do not see that it is He who is present in life and departs at death, or even that it is He who enjoys pleasure through the qualities. Only the eye of wisdom sees this.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.11।।यत्न करनेवाले योगीलोग अपने-आपमें स्थित इस परमात्मतत्त्वका अनुभव करते हैं। परन्तु जिन्होंने अपना अन्तःकरण शुद्ध नहीं किया है, ऐसे अविवेकी मनुष्य यत्न करनेपर भी इस तत्त्वका अनुभव नहीं करते।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.11।। योगीजन प्रयत्न करते हुये ही अपने हृदय में स्थित आत्मा को देखते हैं, जब कि अशुद्ध अन्त:करण वाले (अकृतात्मान:) और अविवेकी (अचेतस:) लोग यत्न करते हुये भी इसे नहीं देखते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The striving yogis perceive it established in themselves. But, though striving, those of unrefined minds, devoid of intelligence, do not perceive it.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And the diligent yogis see this one existing within themselves. The non-discriminating ones who lack self-control, however, do not see this one, even though they are diligent.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The yogis striving for perfection behold Him dwelling in the Self; but, the unrefined and unintelligent, even though striving, do not see Him.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The exerting men of Yoga perceive Him dwelling in the Self; [however], the unintelligent men, with their uncontrolled selves, do not perceive Him, even though they exert.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The saints, with great effort, find Him within themselves; however, the unintelligent, despite their efforts, cannot control their minds.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.12।।सूर्यमें आया हुआ जो तेज सम्पूर्ण जगत् को प्रकाशित करता है और जो तेज चन्द्रमामें है तथा जो तेज अग्निमें है, उस तेजको मेरा ही जान।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.12।। जो तेज सूर्य में स्थित होकर सम्पूर्ण जगत् को प्रकाशित करता है तथा जो तेज चन्द्रमा में है और अग्नि में है, उस तेज को तुम मेरा ही जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Know that the brilliance which illumines the whole universe in the sun, the moon, and fire is Mine.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That light in the sun which illuminates the whole world, that which is in the moon, and that which is in fire—know that light to be Mine.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That light which resides in the sun, illuminating the whole world; that which is in the moon and in the fire—know that light to be Mine.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: That light which is found in the sun, which is in the moon, and which is also in fire—illuminating the entire world—know that light to be of Mine.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Remember that the light which proceeds from the sun, illuminating the whole world, and the light which is in the moon, and that which is in the fire, all are born of me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.13।।मैं ही पृथ्वीमें प्रविष्ट होकर अपनी शक्तिसे समस्त प्राणियोंको धारण करता हूँ; और मैं ही रसमय चन्द्रमाके रूपमें समस्त ओषधियों-(वनस्पतियों-) को पुष्ट करता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.13।। मैं ही पृथ्वी में प्रवेश करके अपने ओज से भूतमात्र को धारण करता हूँ और रसस्वरूप चन्द्रमा बनकर समस्त औषधियों का अर्थात् वनस्पतियों का पोषण करता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: And, entering the earth, I uphold all beings with My strength. I nourish all herbs, becoming the juicy Soma.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And entering the Earth, I sustain the beings through My power; and nourish all the plants by becoming Soma, which is of the nature of sap.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Permeating the earth, I support all beings with My energy; and having become the watery moon, I nourish all herbs.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: And, penetrating the earth, I support all beings with My energy; being the sap-filled moon, I nourish all plants.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I enter this world and animate all My creatures with My vitality; and by My cool moonbeams, I nourish the plants.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.14।।प्राणियोंके शरीरमें रहनेवाला मैं प्राण-अपानसे युक्त वैश्वानर होकर चार प्रकारके अन्नको पचाता हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.14।। मैं ही समस्त प्राणियों के देह में स्थित वैश्वानर अग्निरूप होकर प्राण और अपान से युक्त चार प्रकार के अन्न को पचाता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Becoming the digestive fire, I function within the bodies of all living beings. In union with the inward and outward breath, I digest the four kinds of food.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Taking the form of Vaisvanara, and residing in the bodies of creatures, I, in association with Prana and Apana, digest the four kinds of food.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having become the fire Vaisvanara, I abide in the bodies of living beings and, associated with the Prana and the Apana, digest the fourfold food.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Being the digestive fire dwelling within the bodies of living creatures, and being in association with the upward and downward winds [of the body], I digest the four kinds of food.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Becoming the fire of life, I pass into their bodies and, uniting with the vital streams of Prāṇa and Apāna, I digest the various kinds of food.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.15।।मैं सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हृदयमें स्थित हूँ। मेरेसे ही स्मृति, ज्ञान और अपोहन (संशय आदि दोषोंका नाश) होता है। सम्पूर्ण वेदोंके द्वारा मैं ही जाननेयोग्य हूँ। वेदोंके तत्त्वका निर्णय करनेवाला और वेदोंको जाननेवाला भी मैं ही हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.15।। मैं ही समस्त प्राणियों के हृदय में स्थित हूँ। मुझसे ही स्मृति, ज्ञान और अपोहन (उनका अभाव) होता है। समस्त वेदों के द्वारा मैं ही वेद्य (जानने योग्य) वस्तु हूँ तथा वेदान्त का और वेदों का ज्ञाता भी मैं ही हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: And I am seated in the hearts of all. From Me come memory, knowledge, and their removal too. Indeed, I alone am to be known from all the Vedas. I bring about the fruition of the Vedic rituals; I alone am the knower of the Vedas.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And I am seated in the hearts of all. From Me come memory, knowledge, and their loss. I alone am the object to be known through all the Vedas; I am also the originator of the Vedanta, and I Myself am the knower of the Vedas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And I am seated in the hearts of all; from Me come memory and knowledge, as well as their absence. I am verily That which has to be known by all the Vedas; I am indeed the author of the Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: I am entered in the hearts of all; from Me come the faculties of memory, knowledge, and discernment; none but Me is to be known by means of all the Vedas, and I am alone the author of the final part of the Vedas and also the author of the Vedas themselves.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am enthroned in the hearts of all; memory, wisdom, and discrimination owe their origins to Me. I am He Who is to be realized in the scriptures; I inspire their wisdom, and I know their truth.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.16।।इस संसारमें क्षर (नाशवान्) और अक्षर (अविनाशी) -- ये दो प्रकारके पुरुष हैं। सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके शरीर नाशवान् और कूटस्थ (जीवात्मा) अविनाशी कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.16।। इस लोक में क्षर (नश्वर) और अक्षर (अनश्वर) ये दो पुरुष हैं, समस्त भूत क्षर हैं और 'कूटस्थ' अक्षर कहलाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There are two kinds of persons spoken of in the sastra: the perishable (ksara) and the imperishable (aksara). All beings are perishable, while the imperishable is called the unchanging (kutastha).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: There are two kinds of people in the world: the mutable and the immutable. The mutable consists of all things; the one existing as Maya is referred to as the immutable.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Two Purushas there are in this world: the perishable and the imperishable. All beings are perishable, and the Kutastha—the unchanging—is called the imperishable.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: There are two people in the world: the perishable and the imperishable. The perishable is all elements, and the one that speaks is called the imperishable.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There are two aspects in Nature: the perishable and the imperishable. All life in this world belongs to the former, while the unchanging element belongs to the latter.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.17।।उत्तम पुरुष तो अन्य ही है, जो परमात्मा नामसे कहा गया है। वही अविनाशी ईश्वर तीनों लोकोंमें प्रविष्ट होकर सबका भरण-पोषण करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.17।। परन्तु उत्तम पुरुष अन्य ही है, जो परमात्मा कहलाता है और जो तीनों लोकों में प्रवेश करके सबका धारण करने वाला अव्यय ईश्वर है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There is a Supreme Person other than these. He is referred to as the Supreme Self (Paramatma) in all the Vedas. He, as the Immutable One and the Lord, enters the threefold world and supports it.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But the Supreme Person is different; He is spoken of as the transcendental Self, permeating the three worlds and upholding them, and He is the imperishable God.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But distinct is the Supreme Purusha, called the highest Self, indestructible and Lord, who pervades the three worlds and sustains them.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: But the Highest Person, distinct from both [this], is spoken of as the Supreme Self, which, being the changeless Lord, sustains the triad of the world by entering into it.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But I am higher than all, the Supreme God, the Absolute Self, the Eternal Lord, who pervades and upholds all the worlds.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.18।।मैं क्षरसे अतीत हूँ और अक्षरसे भी उत्तम हूँ, इसलिये लोकमें और वेदमें पुरुषोत्तम नामसे प्रसिद्ध हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.18।। क्योंकि मैं क्षर से अतीत हूँ और अक्षर से भी उत्तम हूँ, इसलिये लोक में और वेद में भी पुरुषोत्तम के नाम से प्रसिद्ध हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Because I transcend the perishable person and am also higher than the imperishable one, therefore I am referred to in the Smrti and the Veda as the Supreme Person (Purusotama).

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Since I am transcendental to the mutable and above even the immutable, therefore I am well known in the world and in the Vedas as the supreme Person.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: As I transcend the perishable and am even higher than the imperishable, I am declared to be the highest Purusha in the world and in the Vedas.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Because I have transcended both the perishable and the imperishable, I am acclaimed in the world and in the Vedas as the highest of persons.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am beyond comparison with the Eternal and the non-eternal; I am known as the Supreme Personality, the Highest God, by scriptures and sages.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.19।।हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन ! इस प्रकार जो मोहरहित मनुष्य मुझे पुरुषोत्तम जानता है, वह सर्वज्ञ सब प्रकारसे मेरा ही भजन करता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.19।। हे भारत ! इस प्रकार, जो, संमोहरहित, पुरुष मुझ पुरुषोत्तम को जानता है, वह सर्वज्ञ होकर सम्पूर्ण भाव से अर्थात् पूर्ण हृदय से मेरी भक्ति करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who, without delusion, thus knows Me as the Supreme Self, knows all, O Arjuna, and worships Me in every way.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, he who, being free from delusion, knows Me, the supreme Person, thus, is all-knowing and adores Me with his whole being.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who, undeluded, knows Me as the highest Purusha, he, knowing all, worships Me with his whole being (heart), O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who, not being deluded, thus knows Me as the Highest of persons—he knows all and serves Me with his entire being, O descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who, with unclouded vision, sees Me as the Lord-God, knows all that there is to be known and will always worship Me with his whole heart.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।15.20।।हे निष्पाप अर्जुन ! इस प्रकार यह अत्यन्त गोपनीय शास्त्र मेरे द्वारा कहा गया है। हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन ! इसको जानकर मनुष्य ज्ञानवान् (तथा प्राप्त-प्राप्तव्य) और कृतकृत्य हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।15.20।। हे निष्पाप भारत ! इस प्रकार यह गुह्यतम शास्त्र मेरे द्वारा कहा गया, इसको जानकर मनुष्य बुद्धिमान और कृतकृत्य हो जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus, O sinless one, this most mysterious doctrine has been imparted by Me. By understanding this, one will become truly wise and fulfill their duty.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O sinless one, I have thus uttered this most secret scripture. Understanding this, one becomes wise and fulfills their duties, O scion of the Bharata dynasty.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Thus, I have imparted to you this most secret science, O sinless one; by knowing this, one becomes wise, and all their duties are accomplished, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, O sinless one, I have imparted to you the most secret scripture; by understanding this, let a man become wise and also become one who has accomplished what needs to be accomplished, O descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thus, O sinless one, I have revealed this most mystic knowledge to thee. He who understands it gains wisdom and attains the consummation of life.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.1।।श्रीभगवान् बोले -- भयका सर्वथा अभाव; अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धि; ज्ञानके लिये योगमें दृढ़ स्थिति; सात्त्विक दान; इन्द्रियोंका दमन; यज्ञ; स्वाध्याय; कर्तव्य-पालनके लिये कष्ट सहना; शरीर-मन-वाणीकी सरलता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.1।। श्री भगवान् ने कहा -- अभय, अन्त:करण की शुद्धि, ज्ञानयोग में दृढ़ स्थिति, दान, दम, यज्ञ, स्वाध्याय, तप और आर्जव।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord said, "Fearlessness, purity of mind, devotion to meditation on the knowledge of the self, alms-giving, self-control, worship, study of the Vedas, austerity, and uprightness—

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, purity of mind, persistence in knowledge and yoga, charity, control of the external organs, sacrifice, scriptural study, austerity, and rectitude.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in knowledge and yoga, almsgiving, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity, and straightforwardness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The Bhagavat said: Fearlessness, complete purity of the Sattva, steadfastness in knowledge-Yoga, charity, self-restraint, Vedic sacrifice, recitation of scriptures, austerity, and uprightness.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Lord Shri Krishna continued: Fearlessness, clean living, unceasing concentration on wisdom, readiness to give, self-control, a spirit of sacrifice, regular study of the scriptures, austerity, candor,

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.2।।अहिंसा, सत्यभाषण; क्रोध न करना; संसारकी कामनाका त्याग; अन्तःकरणमें राग-द्वेषजनित हलचलका न होना; चुगली न करना; प्राणियोंपर दया करना सांसारिक विषयोंमें न ललचाना; अन्तःकरणकी कोमलता; अकर्तव्य करनेमें लज्जा; चपलताका अभाव।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.2।। अहिंसा, सत्य, क्रोध का अभाव, त्याग, शान्ति, अपैशुनम् (किसी की निन्दा न करना), भूतमात्र के प्रति दया, अलोलुपता , मार्दव (कोमलता), लज्जा, अचंचलता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Non-injury, truth, freedom from anger, renunciation, tranquility, non-slandering of others, compassion for all beings, freedom from desire, gentleness, a sense of shame, and freedom from fickleness;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Non-injury, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, control of the inner self, absence of vilification, kindness to creatures, non-covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and freedom from restlessness;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Harmlessness, truth, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness, absence of crookedness, compassion for beings, non-covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and absence of fickleness.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Harmlessness, truth, absence of anger, renunciation, absence of attachment, absence of slander, compassion for living beings, and absence of greed, gentleness, modesty, and absence of thoughtlessness;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Harmlessness, truth, absence of wrath, renunciation, contentment, straightforwardness, compassion towards all, uncovetousness, courtesy, modesty, and constancy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.3।।तेज (प्रभाव), क्षमा, धैर्य, शरीरकी शुद्धि, वैरभावका न रहना और मानको न चाहना, हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन ! ये सभी दैवी सम्पदाको प्राप्त हुए मनुष्यके लक्षण हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.3।। हे भारत ! तेज, क्षमा, धैर्य, शौच (शुद्धि), अद्रोह और अतिमान (गर्व) का अभाव ये सब दैवी संपदा को प्राप्त पुरुष के लक्षण हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Grandeur, patience, fortitude, purity, freedom from hatred and over-pride—these, O Arjuna, belong to one who is born to a divine destiny.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Vigour, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, freedom from malice, absence of haughtiness—these, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, are the qualities of one born destined to have the divine nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of hatred, absence of pride—these belong to one born for a divine state, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Vital power, forgiveness, fortitude, contentment, absence of treachery, and absence of excessive pride—these are in the person who is born for divine wealth, O Descendant of Bharata!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Valor, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, freedom from hate and vanity—these are the qualities of one who possesses the divine, O Arjuna!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.4।।हे पृथानन्दन ! दम्भ करना, घमण्ड करना, अभिमान करना, क्रोध करना, कठोरता रखना और अविवेकका होना भी -- ये सभी आसुरीसम्पदाको प्राप्त हुए मनुष्यके लक्षण हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.4।। हे पार्थ ! दम्भ, दर्प, अभिमान, क्रोध, कठोर वाणी (पारुष्य) और अज्ञान यह सब आसुरी सम्पदा है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Pompousness, arrogance, self-conceit, wrath, rudeness, and ignorance—these, O Arjuna, belong to one who is born with a demonic destiny.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Prtha, the attributes of one destined to have the demoniacal nature are religious ostentation, pride, haughtiness, self-conceit, anger, rudeness, and ignorance.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Hypocrisy, arrogance, and self-conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance—these belong to one who is born for a demoniacal state, O Partha.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Ostentation, arrogance, pride, anger, and harshness, as well as ignorance, are characteristics of a person born of demoniac wealth, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Hypocrisy, pride, insolence, cruelty, and ignorance belong to those born of godless qualities.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.5।।दैवी-सम्पत्ति मुक्तिके लिये और आसुरी-सम्पत्ति बन्धनके लिये है। हे पाण्डव तुम दैवी-सम्पत्तिको प्राप्त हुए हो, इसलिये तुम्हें शोक (चिन्ता) नहीं करना चाहिये।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.5।। हे पाण्डव ! दैवी सम्पदा मोक्ष के लिए और आसुरी सम्पदा बन्धन के लिए मानी गयी है, तुम शोक मत करो, क्योंकि तुम दैवी सम्पदा को प्राप्त हुए हो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The divine destiny is said to lead to liberation, while the demoniac to bondage. Do not grieve, O Arjuna, for you are born with a divine destiny.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The divine nature is Liberation, while the demoniacal is considered to be an inevitable bondage. Do not grieve, O son of Pandu! You are destined to possess the divine nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The divine nature is deemed conducive to liberation, and the demonic to bondage. Grieve not, O Arjuna, for you are born with divine endowments.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The divine wealth is meant for total emancipation, and the demoniac one for complete bondage. Grieve not, O son of Pandu, for the divine wealth you are born.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Godly qualities lead to liberation; godless ones lead to bondage. Do not be anxious, Prince! You have the Godly qualities.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.6।।इस लोकमें दो तरहके प्राणियोंकी सृष्टि है -- दैवी और आसुरी। दैवीका तो मैंने विस्तारसे वर्णन कर दिया, अब हे पार्थ ! तुम मेरेसे आसुरीका विस्तार सुनो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.6।। हे पार्थ ! इस लोक में दो प्रकार की भूतिसृष्टि है, दैवी और आसुरी। उनमें देवों का स्वभाव (दैवी सम्पदा) विस्तारपूर्वक कहा गया है; अब असुरों के स्वभाव को विस्तरश: मुझसे सुनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There are two types of beings in this world: the divine and the demoniac. The divine have been described at length. Hear from Me, O Arjuna, about the demoniac.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: In this world, there are two kinds of creation of beings: the divine and the demoniacal. The divine has been spoken of elaborately. Hear about the demoniacal from Me, O son of Prtha.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There are two types of beings in this world: the divine and the demoniacal. The divine has been described at length; hear from Me, O Arjuna, about the demoniacal.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: There are two types of creations of beings in this world: the divine and the demoniac. The divine one has been described in detail; hear now the demoniac one from Me, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: No changes needed.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.7।।आसुरी प्रकृतिवाले मनुष्य प्रवृत्ति और निवृत्तिको नहीं जानते और उनमें न बाह्यशुद्धि, न श्रेष्ठ आचरण तथा न सत्य-पालन ही होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.7।। आसुरी स्वभाव के लोग न प्रवृत्ति को; जानते हैं और न निवृत्ति को उनमें न शुद्धि होती है, न सदाचार और न सत्य ही होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The demoniacal men neither know action nor renunciation. They have no cleanliness, nor right conduct, nor truth.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Neither do the demoniacal persons understand what should be done and what should not be done; nor do they possess purity, good conduct, or truthfulness.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The demoniacal do not know what to do and what to refrain from; they have neither purity, nor right conduct, nor truth.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The demoniac do not know the origin and the dissolution; neither purity, nor good conduct, nor truth exists in them.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The godless do not know how to act or renounce; they lack both purity and truth. They do not understand the right principles of conduct.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.8।।वे कहा करते हैं कि संसार असत्य, अप्रतिष्ठित और बिना ईश्वरके अपने-आप केवल स्त्री-पुरुषके संयोगसे पैदा हुआ है। इसलिये काम ही इसका कारण है, और कोई कारण नहीं है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.8।। वे कहते हैं कि यह जगत् आश्रयरहित, असत्य और ईश्वर रहित है, यह (स्त्रीपुरुष के) परस्पर कामुक संबंध से ही उत्पन्न हुआ है, और (इसका कारण) क्या हो सकता है?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: They maintain: 'The universe is without truth, with no foundation, and without a Lord (Isvara). What else could exist without mutual causation? It has lust as its cause.'

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: They say that the world is unreal, having no basis, and without a God. It is born of mutual union brought about by passion! What other cause could there be?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: They say, "This universe is without truth, without a moral basis, without a God, brought about by mutual union, with lust as its cause; what else?"

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: They say that this world is without truth; has no basis; and has no Lord; this is not born on the basis of the mutual cause-and-effect-relation [of the things]; it has nothing [beyond] and has no cause.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: They say the universe is an accident with no purpose and no God. Life is created through sexual union, a product of lust and nothing else.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.9।।उपर्युक्त (नास्तिक) दृष्टिका आश्रय लेनेवाले जो मनुष्य अपने नित्य स्वरूपको नहीं मानते, जिनकी बुद्धि तुच्छ है, जो उग्रकर्मा और संसारके शत्रु हैं, उन मनुष्योंकी सामर्थ्यका उपयोग जगत्का नाश करनेके लिये ही होता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.9।। इस दृष्टि का अवलम्बन करके नष्टस्वभाव के अल्प बुद्धि वाले, घोर कर्म करने वाले लोग जगत् के शत्रु (अहित चाहने वाले) के रूप में उसका नाश करने के लिए उत्पन्न होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Holding this view, these men of lost souls and feeble understanding do cruel deeds, leading to the destruction of the world.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Holding onto this view, those of depraved character, poor intellect, and given to fearful and harmful actions, wax strong and bring ruin to the world.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Holding this view, these ruined souls of small intellect and fierce deeds come forth as enemies of the world, intent on its destruction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Clinging to this view, the inauspicious men of ruined souls, poor intellect, and cruel deeds strive to destroy the world.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thinking thus, these degraded souls, these enemies of mankind—whose intelligence is negligible and whose deeds are monstrous—come into the world only to destroy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.10।।कभी पूरी न होनेवाली कामनाओंका आश्रय लेकर दम्भ, अभिमान और मदमें चूर रहनेवाले तथा अपवित्र व्रत धारण करनेवाले मनुष्य मोहके कारण दुराग्रहोंको धारण करके संसारमें विचरते रहते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.10।। दम्भ, मान और मद से युक्त कभी न पूर्ण होने वाली कामनाओं का आश्रय लिये, मोहवश मिथ्या धारणाओं को ग्रहण करके ये अशुद्ध संकल्पों के लोग जगत् में कार्य करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Turning to insatiable desires, seizing through delusion unjustly acquired wealth, and following impious vows, they act, full of ostentation, pride, and arrogance.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Giving themselves up to insatiable passion, filled with vanity, pride, and arrogance, adopting bad adjectives due to delusion, and having impure resolves, they engage in actions.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Filled with insatiable desires, full of hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance, holding evil ideas due to delusion, they work with impure intentions.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Holding to their insatiable desires, being possessed by hypocrisy, avarice, and pride, and holding evil intentions, these cruel men wander with impure resolves.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Giving themselves up to insatiable passions, hypocritically, self-sufficiently, and arrogantly, cherishing false conceptions founded on delusion, they work only to carry out their own unholy purposes.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.11।।वे मृत्युपर्यन्त रहनेवाली अपार चिन्ताओंका आश्रय लेनेवाले, पदार्थोंका संग्रह और उनका भोग करनेमें ही लगे रहनेवाले और 'जो कुछ है, वह इतना ही है' -- ऐसा निश्चय करनेवाले होते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.11।। मरणपर्यन्त रहने वाली अपरिमित चिन्ताओं से ग्रस्त और विषयोपभोग को ही परम लक्ष्य मानने वाले ये आसुरी लोग इस निश्चित मत के होते हैं कि "इतना ही (सत्य, आनन्द) है"।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Obsessed by unlimited cares that end with dissolution, looking upon the enjoyment of desires as their highest aim, and convinced that this is all;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Beset with innumerable cares that end only with death, holding that the enjoyment of desirable objects is the highest goal, they feel sure that this is all.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Giving themselves over to immeasurable cares that end only with death, regarding the gratification of lust as their highest aim, and feeling sure that that is all.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Adhering to their ultimate and unending anxiety; viewing the gratification of their desires as their highest goal; believing that this is all that exists;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Poring anxiously over evil resolutions that only end in death, seeking only the gratification of desire as the highest goal, and seeing nothing beyond—

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.12।।वे आशाकी सैकड़ों फाँसियोंसे बँधे हुए मनुष्य काम-क्रोधके परायण होकर पदार्थोंका भोग करनेके लिये अन्यायपूर्वक धन-संचय करनेकी चेष्टा करते रहते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.12।। सैकड़ों आशापाशों से बन्धे हुये, काम और क्रोध के वश में ये लोग विषयभोगों की पूर्ति के लिये अन्यायपूर्वक धन का संग्रह करने के लिये चेष्टा करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Bound by hundreds of fetters of hopes, given over to desire and anger, they strive unjustly to gather wealth for the gratification of their desires.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Bound by hundreds of shackles in the form of hope, giving themselves wholly to passion and anger, they endeavor to amass wealth through foul means for the enjoyment of desirable objects.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Bound by a hundred ties of hope, given over to lust and anger, they strive to obtain hoards of wealth by unlawful means for sensual enjoyment.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Bound by hundreds of ropes of longing, devoted to their desire and anger, they seek, by unjust means, hoards of wealth for the gratification of their desires.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Caught in the toils of a hundred vain hopes, enslaved by passion and wrath, they accumulate hoards of unjust wealth, only to indulge their sensual desires.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.13।।इतनी वस्तुएँ तो हमने आज प्राप्त कर लीं और अब इस मनोरथको प्राप्त (पूरा) कर लेंगे।,इतना धन तो हमारे पास है ही, इतना धन फिर हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.13।। मैंने आज यह पाया है और इस मनोरथ को भी प्राप्त करूंगा, मेरे पास यह इतना धन है और इससे भी अधिक धन भविष्य में होगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: 'This I have gained today, and this desire I shall attain. This wealth is mine, and this will also be mine hereafter.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: 'I have gained this today; I will acquire this desired object. This is in hand; and this wealth will also come to me.'

author: Swami Sivananda
text: "I have gained this today; I will fulfill this desire of mine; this is mine, and this wealth will be mine in the future."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: 'I have gained this today; I will attain this object of my desire in the future; this is mine now; and this wealth will also be mine soon.'

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Today I have gained this; tomorrow I will fulfill another desire; this wealth is mine now, and the rest will soon be mine.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.14।।वह शत्रु तो हमारे द्वारा मारा गया और उन दूसरे शत्रुओंको भी हम मार डालेंगे। हम सर्वसमर्थ हैं। हमारे पास भोग-सामग्री बहुत है। हम सिद्ध हैं। हम बड़े बलवान् और सुखी हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.14।। "यह शत्रु मेरे द्वारा मारा गया है और दूसरे शत्रुओं को भी मैं मारूंगा", "मैं ईश्वर हूँ और भोगी हूँ", "मैं सिद्ध पुरुष हूँ", "मैं बलवान और सुखी हूँ",।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: 'I have slain this enemy, and I shall slay others too. I am the Lord, the enjoyer, the successful one, the strong one, and the happy one.'

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: 'I have slain that enemy, and I shall slay others too. I am the Lord, the Enjoyer, firmly established, powerful and joyous.'

author: Swami Sivananda
text: "I have slain that enemy, and I shall slay others too. I am the Lord; I enjoy, I am perfect, powerful, and happy."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: 'I have slain that enemy; and I shall slay others too; I am the Lord; I am a man of pleasure; I am victorious, powerful, and content.'

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I have slain one enemy; I will slay the others too. I am worthy of enjoying; I am the Almighty, perfect, powerful, and happy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.15।।हम धनवान् हैं, बहुत-से मनुष्य हमारे पास हैं, हमारे समान और कौन है? हम खूब यज्ञ करेंगे, दान देंगे और मौज करेंगे -- इस तरह वे अज्ञानसे मोहित रहते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.15।। "मैं धनवान् और श्रेष्ठकुल में जन्मा हूँ। मेरे समान दूसरा कौन है?",'मैं यज्ञ करूंगा', 'मैं दान दूँगा', 'मैं मौज करूँगा' - इस प्रकार के अज्ञान से वे मोहित होते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: 'I am wealthy and high-born; who else is equal to me? I shall sacrifice, I shall give alms, I shall rejoice.' Thus they think, deluded by ignorance.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: 'I am wealthy and of noble birth; who else is like me? I shall perform sacrifices; I shall give, I shall rejoice'- thus, they are deluded by a lack of discernment in diverse ways.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: "I am wealthy and born into a noble family. Who is my equal? I shall perform sacrifices, give charity, and rejoice," thus deluded by ignorance.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: 'I am wealthy; I am of noble birth; who else is equal to me? I shall perform sacrifices; I shall give gifts; and I shall rejoice' - deluded by these wrong ideas;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I am wealthy, I am of noble birth; who can compare with me? I will sacrifice, I will give, I will pay—and I will enjoy. Thus blinded by ignorance,

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.16।।कामनाओंके कारण तरह-तरहसे भ्रमित चित्तवाले, मोह-जालमें अच्छी तरहसे फँसे हुए तथा पदार्थों और भोगोंमें अत्यन्त आसक्त रहनेवाले मनुष्य भयङ्कर नरकोंमें गिरते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.16।। अनेक प्रकार से भ्रमित चित्त वाले, मोह जाल में फँसे तथा विषयभोगों में आसक्त ये लोग घोर, अपवित्र नरक में गिरते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Bewildered by numerous thoughts, ensnared in the net of delusion, addicted to sensual pleasures, they fall into a wretched Naraka.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Bewildered by numerous thoughts, ensnared in the net of delusion, and engrossed in the enjoyment of desirable objects, they fall into a foul hell.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Bewildered by many fancies, entangled in the snare of delusion, addicted to the gratification of lust, they fall into a foul hell.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Endowed with many thoughts, highly confused, enslaved by their delusion, and addicted to the gratification of desires, they fall into the hellish and foul.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Perplexed by discordant thoughts, entangled in the snares of desire, infatuated by passion, they sink into the depths of hell.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।16.17।।अपनेको सबसे अधिक पूज्य माननेवाले, अकड़ रखनेवाले तथा धन और मानके मदमें चूर रहनेवाले वे मनुष्य दम्भसे अविधिपूर्वक नाममात्रके यज्ञोंसे यजन करते हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।16.17।। अपने आप को ही श्रेष्ठ मानने वाले, स्तब्ध (गर्वयुक्त), धन और मान के मद से युक्त लोग शास्त्रविधि से रहित केवल नाममात्र के यज्ञों द्वारा दम्भपूर्वक यजन करते हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Self-conceited, self-sufficient, intoxicated by wealth and pride, they perform sacrifices in name only, ostentatiously and not according to the injunctions of the scriptures.

author: Swami Gambirananda
