text: That action is said to be born of tamas which is undertaken out of delusion, without consideration of its consequences, loss, harm, and ability.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That action which is undertaken from delusion, without regard for the consequences, loss, injury, and one's own ability, is declared to be Tamasic (dark).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The object which is gained, due to ignorance, without considering the result, the loss, the injury to others, and one's own strength—that is declared to be of the Tamas (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: An action undertaken through delusion, with no regard to the spiritual implications, the capacity of the doer, or the potential harm that may follow, is assumed to be a product of ignorance.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.26।।जो कर्ता रागरहित, अनहंवादी, धैर्य और उत्साहयुक्त तथा सिद्धि और असिद्धिमें निर्विकार है, वह सात्त्विक कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.26।। जो कर्ता संगरहित, अहंमन्यता से रहित, धैर्य और उत्साह से युक्त एवं कार्य की सिद्धि (सफलता) और असिद्धि (विफलता) में निर्विकार रहता है, वह कर्ता सात्त्विक कहा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That agent is said to be Sattvika, who is free from attachment, who does not boast of himself, who is endowed with steadiness and zeal, and who is unaffected by success and failure.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: [Ast. introduces this verse with 'Idanim kartrbhedah ucyate, Now is being stated the distinctions among the agents.'-Tr.] The agent who is free from attachment [Attachment to results or the idea of agentship.], not egotistical, endowed with fortitude and diligence, and unperturbed by success and failure is said to be possessed of sattva.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: An agent who is free from attachment, non-egoistic, endowed with firmness and enthusiasm, and unaffected by success or failure, is considered to be of a Sattvic (pure) nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The agent who is free from attachment, who does not make any speech of egoism, who is full of contentment and enthusiasm, and who does not change mentally in success or failure—that agent is said to be of the Sattva nature.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But when a person has no sentiment or ego, when they possess courage and confidence, not caring whether they succeed or fail, then their action arises from purity.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.27।।जो कर्ता रागी, कर्मफलकी इच्छावाला, लोभी, हिंसाके स्वभाववाला, अशुद्ध और हर्षशोकसे युक्त है, वह राजस कहा गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.27।। रागी, कर्मफल का इच्छुक, लोभी, हिंसक स्वभाव वाला, अशुद्ध और हर्षशोक से युक्त कर्ता राजस कहलाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That doer is called Rajasika who is passionate, who seeks the fruits of his actions, who is greedy, harmful, and impure, and who is swayed by delight and grief.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The agent who has attachment, who is desirous of the results of actions, covetous, cruel by nature, unclean, and subject to joy and sorrow, is declared to be possessed of rajas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Passionate, desiring to obtain the reward of their actions, greedy, cruel, impure, moved by joy and sorrow, such an agent is said to be Rajasic.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The agent, who is a man of passion, who craves the fruit of his actions, and is avaricious; who is injurious by nature, impure, and is overpowered by joy and grief—that agent is proclaimed to be of the Rajas (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: In one who is impulsive, greedy, looking for reward, violent, impure, and torn between joy and sorrow, it may be assumed that Passion is predominant.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.28।।जो कर्ता असावधान, अशिक्षित, ऐंठ-अकड़वाला, जिद्दी, उपकारीका अपकार करनेवाला, आलसी, विषादी और दीर्घसूत्री है, वह तामस कहा जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.28।। अयुक्त, प्राकृत, स्तब्ध, शठ, नैष्कृतिक, आलसी, विषादी और दीर्घसूत्री कर्ता तामस कहा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That doer is said to be Tamasika who is unqualified, unrefined, obstinate, depraved, dishonest, indolent, despondent, and lethargic.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The agent who is unsteady, naive, inflexible, deceitful, wicked, lazy, sullen, and procrastinating is said to be possessed of tamas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Unsteady, vulgar, inflexible, deceitful, malicious, lazy, despondent, and procrastinating—such an agent is called Tamasic.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The agent who does not exert is vulgar, obstinate, and deceitful; they are a man of wickedness and are lazy, sorrowful, and procrastinating—such an agent is said to be of the Tamas strand.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: While he whose purpose is weak, who is low-minded, stubborn, dishonest, malicious, lazy, despondent, and procrastinating—he may be assumed to be in darkness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.29।।हे धनञ्जय ! अब तू गुणोंके अनुसार बुद्धि और धृतिके भी तीन प्रकारके भेद अलग-अलगरूपसे सुन, जो कि मेरे द्वारा पूर्णरूपसे कहे जा रहे हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.29।। हे धनंजय ! मेरे द्वारा अशेषत: और पृथकत: कहे जाने वाले, गुणों के कारण उत्पन्न हुए बुद्धि और धृति के त्रिविध भेद को सुनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Hear now, O Arjuna, the threefold division of Buddhi (reason) and Dhrti (fortitude) according to the Gunas, to be fully and severally set forth.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Dhananjaya, listen to the classification of the intellect and fortitude, which is threefold according to the gunas, while it is being stated in detail and separately.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Hear thou the threefold division of intellect and firmness, according to the Gunas, as I declare them fully and distinctly, O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: You must listen to Me as I expound the three-fold division of the intellect and content, both completely and individually, based on the Strands, O Dhananjaya!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Reason and conviction are threefold, according to the quality that is dominant. I will explain them fully and separately, O Arjuna!

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.30।।हे पृथानन्दन ! जो बुद्धि प्रवृत्ति और निवृत्तिको, कर्तव्य और अकर्तव्यको, भय और अभयको तथा बन्धन और मोक्षको जानती है, वह बुद्धि सात्त्विकी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.30।। हे पार्थ ! जो बुद्धि प्रवृत्ति और निवृत्ति, कार्य और अकार्य, भय और अभय तथा बन्ध और मोक्ष को तत्त्वत जानती है, वह बुद्धि सात्विकी है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That Buddhi, O Arjuna, which knows activity and renunciation, what should be done and what should not be done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and release—that Buddhi is Sattvika.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, that intellect is born of sattva which understands action and withdrawal, duty and what is not duty, the sources of fear and fearlessness, as well as bondage and freedom.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The intellect which knows the path of work and renunciation, what should be done and what should not be done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation—that intellect is Sattvic (pure), O Arjuna.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The intellect which knows the activity and the cessation from activity, the proper and improper actions, the fear and non-fear, and the bondage and emancipation—that intellect is considered to be of the Sattva (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: That intellect which understands the creation and dissolution of life, what actions should be done and what not, which discriminates between fear and fearlessness, bondage and deliverance, is Pure.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.31।।हे पार्थ ! मनुष्य जिसके द्वारा धर्म और अधर्मको, कर्तव्य और अकर्तव्यको भी ठीक तरहसे नहीं जानता, वह बुद्धि राजसी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.31।। हे पार्थ ! जिस बुद्धि के द्वारा मनुष्य धर्म और अधर्म को तथा कर्तव्य और अकर्तव्य को यथावत् नहीं जानता है, वह बुद्धि राजसी है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Buddhi that gives an erroneous knowledge of Dharma and Adharma (its opposite) and also of what should be done and what should not be done, O Arjuna, is Rajasic.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, that intellect is born of rajas with which one wrongly understands virtue and vice, as well as what ought to be done and ought not to be done.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That by which one wrongly understands dharma and adharma, and also what ought to be done and what ought not to be done—that intellect, O Arjuna, is rajasic (passionate).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The intellect, by means of which one decides incorrectly between the righteous and the unrighteous, and what is a proper action and an improper one—that intellect is of the Rajas, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The intellect that does not understand what is right and wrong, and what should be done and not done, is under the sway of Passion.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.32।।हे पृथानन्दन ! तमोगुणसे घिरी हुई जो बुद्धि अधर्मको धर्म और सम्पूर्ण चीजोंको उलटा मान लेती है, वह तामसी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.32।। हे पार्थ ! तमस् (अज्ञान अन्ध:कार) से आवृत जो बुद्धि अधर्म को ही धर्म मानती है और सभी पदार्थों को विपरीत रूप से जानती है, वह बुद्धि तामसी है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That Buddhi, O Arjuna, which, enveloped in darkness, regards Adharma as Dharma and reverses every value, is Tamasika.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, that intellect is born of tamas which, being covered by darkness, considers vice as virtue, and indeed perceives all things contrary to what they are.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That intellect, O Arjuna, which is enveloped in darkness and sees Adharma as Dharma and all things perverted, is Tamasic (dark).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The intellect which, containing darkness (ignorance), conceives the unrighteous one as righteous and all things topsy-turvy—that intellect is deemed to be of the Tamas (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: And that which, shrouded in Ignorance, thinks wrong to be right, and sees everything perversely, O Arjuna, that intellect is ruled by Darkness.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.33।।हे पार्थ ! समतासे युक्त जिस अव्यभिचारिणी धृतिके द्वारा मनुष्य मन, प्राण और इन्द्रियोंकी क्रियाओंको धारण करता है, वह धृति सात्त्विकी है।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That fortitude, by which one maintains the unwavering activities of the mind and vital force, and these sense-organs, through yoga—that fortitude is of the nature of sattva.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, the firmness that is unfailing through concentration, with which one restrains the functions of the mind, vital forces, and the organs—that firmness is born of sattva.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The unwavering firmness, through which Yoga restrains the functions of the mind, life-force, and senses—that firmness, O Arjuna, is Sattvic (pure).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The unfailing content, by which one restrains, through Yoga, the activities of the mind, the living breath, and the senses, is considered to be of the Sattva (strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The conviction and steady concentration with which the mind, the energy, and the senses are controlled—O Arjuna!—are the result of purity.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.34।।हे पृथानन्दन अर्जुन ! फलकी इच्छावाला मनुष्य जिस धृतिके द्वारा धर्म, काम (भोग) और अर्थको अत्यन्त आसक्तिपूर्वक धारण करता है, वह धृति राजसी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.34।। हे पृथापुत्र अर्जुन ! कर्मफल का इच्छुक पुरुष अति आसक्ति (प्रसंग) से जिस धृति के द्वारा धर्म, अर्थ और काम (इन तीन पुरुषार्थों) को धारण करता है, वह धृति राजसी है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That dhṛti, O Arjuna, by which one, who is desirous of fruits, longs for them with intense attachment and holds fast to duty, desire, and wealth—that dhṛti is rajasika.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: But, O Partha, the steadfastness with which one holds on to righteousness, covetable things, and wealth, desiring their fruits as the occasion for each arises, that steadfastness is born of rajas.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: But that, O Arjuna, by which one holds fast to Dharma (duty), enjoyment of pleasures, and earning of wealth, on account of attachment and desire for reward—that firmness, O Arjuna, is Rajasic (passionate).

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Arjuna! The contentment by which one restrains one's bounden duty, pleasure, and wealth, and consequently desires the fruits of action—that contentment is of the Rajas strand, O son of Prtha!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The conviction that always holds fast to rituals, self-interest, and wealth for the sake of what they may bring forth comes from passion.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.35।।हे पार्थ ! दुष्ट बुद्धिवाला मनुष्य जिस धृतिके द्वारा निद्रा, भय, चिन्ता, दुःख और घमण्डको भी नहीं छोड़ता, वह धृति तामसी है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.35।। हो पार्थ ! दुर्बुद्धि पुरुष जिस धारणा के द्वारा, स्वप्न, भय, शोक, विषाद और मद को नहीं त्यागता है, वह धृति तामसी है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That Dhrti, by which a foolish person does not give up sleep, fear, grief, depression, and passion, O Arjuna, is of the nature of Tamas.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That firmness is considered to be born of tamas, due to which a person with a corrupt intellect does not give up sleep, fear, sorrow, despondency, as well as sensuality.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That firmness, O Arjuna, by which a stupid man does not abandon sleep, fear, grief, despair, and conceit, is Tamasic.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The contentment where a foolish man does not give up his sleep, fear, grief, despondency, and arrogance—that contentment is deemed to be of the Tamas (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: And that which clings perversely to false idealism, fear, grief, despair, and vanity is the product of ignorance.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.36।।हे भरतवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! अब तीन प्रकारके सुखको भी तुम मेरेसे सुनो। जिसमें अभ्याससे रमण होता है और जिससे दुःखोंका अन्त हो जाता है, ऐसा वह परमात्मविषयक बुद्धिकी प्रसन्नतासे पैदा होनेवाला जो सुख (सांसारिक आसक्तिके कारण) आरम्भमें विषकी तरह और परिणाममें अमृतकी तरह होता है, वह सुख सात्त्विक कहा गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.36।। हे भरतश्रेष्ठ ! अब तुम त्रिविध सुख को मुझसे सुनो, जिसमें (साधक पुरुष) अभ्यास से रमता है और दु:खों के अन्त को प्राप्त होता है (जहाँ उसके दु:खों का अन्त हो जाता है।)।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Now hear from Me, O Arjuna, the threefold division of pleasure৷৷ that in which one rejoices through long practice and in which one comes to the end of pain.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Now hear from Me, O scion of the Bharata dynasty, as regards the three kinds of joy: That in which one delights due to habit, and certainly attains the cessation of sorrows; [S. and S.S. take the second line of this verse along with the next verse referring to sattvika happiness.-Tr.]

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And now, O Arjuna, hear from Me of the threefold pleasure, in which one rejoices through practice and surely comes to the end of pain.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O best among the Bharatas! Now, you must also listen to Me about the three-fold happiness, wherein one delights in practice and attains the end of suffering.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Hear further the three kinds of pleasure. That which increases day by day delivers one from misery.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.37।।हे भरतवंशियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन ! अब तीन प्रकारके सुखको भी तुम मेरेसे सुनो। जिसमें अभ्याससे रमण होता है और जिससे दुःखोंका अन्त हो जाता है, ऐसा वह परमात्मविषयक बुद्धिकी प्रसन्नतासे पैदा होनेवाला जो सुख (सांसारिक आसक्तिके कारण) आरम्भमें विषकी तरह और परिणाममें अमृतकी तरह होता है, वह सुख सात्त्विक कहा गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.37।। जो सुख प्रथम (प्रारम्भ में) विष के समान (भासता) है, परन्तु परिणाम में अमृत के समान है, वह आत्मबुद्धि के प्रसाद से उत्पन्न सुख सात्त्विक कहा गया है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That which, at first, is like a poison but becomes like an elixir in the end, born from the serene state of mind focused on the Self—such pleasure is said to be Sattvika.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That which is like poison in the beginning, but is likened to nectar in the end, and which arises from the purity of one's intellect—that joy is spoken of as born of sattva.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That which is like poison at first but in the end like nectar—that happiness is declared to be sattvic, born of the purity of one's own mind due to self-realization.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: [The happiness] which is like poison in the moment but is like nectar in the result—that happiness, born of serenity of the soul and intellect, you must know to be of the sattva (strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Which at first seems like poison but afterwards acts like nectar—that pleasure is pure, for it is born of wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.38।।जो सुख इन्द्रियों और विषयोंके संयोगसे आरम्भमें अमृतकी तरह और परिणाममें विषकी तरह होता है, वह सुख राजस कहा गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.38।। जो सुख विषयों और इन्द्रियों के संयोग से उत्पन्न होता है, वह प्रथम तो अमृत के समान, परन्तु परिणाम में विष तुल्य होता है, वह सुख राजस कहा गया है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That pleasure which arises from the contact of senses with their objects, which is like elixir at first but like poison in the end, is said to be Rajasic.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That joy is referred to as born of rajas, which, arising from the contact of the organs and their objects, is like nectar in the beginning but like poison in the end.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That happiness which arises from the contact of the senses with the objects, which is initially like nectar but eventually like poison, is said to be Rajasic.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: [The happiness] which is like nectar at its due time due to the contact between the senses and sense-objects; but which is like poison at the time of its result—that is considered to be of the Rajas (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: That which, at first, is like nectar, as the senses revel in their objects, but in the end acts like poison—that pleasure arises from Passion.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.39।।निद्रा, आलस्य और प्रमादसे उत्पन्न होनेवाला जो सुख आरम्भमें और परिणाममें अपनेको मोहित करनेवाला है, वह सुख तामस कहा गया है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.39।। जो सुख प्रारम्भ में और परिणाम (अनुबन्ध) में भी आत्मा (मनुष्य) को मोहित करने वाला होता है, वह निद्रा, आलस्य और प्रमाद से उत्पन्न सुख तामस कहा जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: That pleasure which, at the beginning and end, deludes the self, through sleep, sloth, and error, is declared to be tamasic.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: That joy is said to be born of tamas, which, both in the beginning and in the end, is delusive to oneself and arises from sleep, laziness, and inadvertence.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: That happiness which at first, as well as in the end, deludes the self, and which arises from sleep, indolence, and heedlessness—that is declared to be Tamasic.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The happiness that, both initially and subsequently, is of the nature of deluding the Self; and which results from sleep, indolence, and heedlessness—that is stated to be of the Tamas (Strand).

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: While the pleasure that drugs the senses from start to finish, which springs from indolence, lethargy, and folly—that pleasure flows from Ignorance.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.40।।पृथ्वीमें या स्वर्गमें अथवा देवताओंमें तथा इनके सिवाय और कहीं भी वह ऐसी कोई वस्तु नहीं है, जो प्रकृतिसे उत्पन्न इन तीनों गुणोंसे रहित हो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.40।। पृथ्वी पर अथवा स्वर्ग के देवताओं में ऐसा कोई प्राणी (सत्त्वं अर्थात् विद्यमान वस्तु) नहीं है जो प्रकृति से उत्पन्न इन तीन गुणों से मुक्त (रहित) हो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There is no creature, on earth or among the gods in heaven, that is exempt from these three Gunas born of Prakrti.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: There is no entity in the world, nor among the gods in heaven, that can be free from these three gunas born of Nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There is no being on earth or in heaven among the gods that is liberated from the three qualities born of Nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whether on earth or among the gods in heaven, there is not a single being that is free from these three strands born of material nature.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There is nothing anywhere on earth or in the higher worlds that is free from the three Qualities, for they are born of Nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.41।।हे परंतप ! ब्राह्मण, क्षत्रिय, वैश्य और शूद्रोंके कर्म स्वभावसे उत्पन्न हुए तीनों गुणोंके द्वारा विभक्त किये गये हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.41।। हे परन्तप!  ब्राह्मणों, क्षत्रियों, वैश्यों और शूद्रों के कर्म, स्वभाव से उत्पन्न गुणों के अनुसार विभक्त किये गये हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The duties of the Brahmanas, Ksatriyas, Vaisyas, and Sudras are clearly divided, O Arjuna, according to their Gunas, born of their nature.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O scorcher of enemies, the duties of the Brahmanas, Ksatriyas, Vaisyas, and Sudras have been fully classified according to the gunas born from Nature.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Of Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaisyas, and Sudras, O Arjuna, the duties are distributed according to the qualities born of their own nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: The duties of the Brahmanas, the Kshatriyas, the Vaishyas, and the Shudras are properly classified according to the qualities that are the sources of their nature, O scorcher of foes!

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! The duties of spiritual teachers, the soldiers, the traders, and the servants have all been fixed according to the dominant quality in their nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.42।।मनका निग्रह करना इन्द्रियोंको वशमें करना; धर्मपालनके लिये कष्ट सहना; बाहर-भीतरसे शुद्ध रहना; दूसरोंके अपराधको क्षमा करना; शरीर, मन आदिमें सरलता रखना; वेद, शास्त्र आदिका ज्ञान होना; यज्ञविधिको अनुभवमें लाना; और परमात्मा, वेद आदिमें आस्तिक भाव रखना -- ये सब-के-सब ब्राह्मणके स्वाभाविक कर्म हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.42।। शम, दम, तप, शौच, क्षान्ति, आर्जव, ज्ञान, विज्ञान और आस्तिक्य - ये ब्राह्मण के स्वाभाविक कर्म हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The control of the senses and mind, austerity, purity, forbearance, uprightness, knowledge, special knowledge, and faith—all these constitute the duty of a Brahmana, born of his inherent nature.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The natural duties of the Brahmanas are the control of the internal and external organs, austerity, purity, forgiveness, straightforwardness, knowledge, as well as wisdom [Knowledge refers to the understanding of subjects presented by the scriptures; wisdom means making them matters of one's own experience.], and faith.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Serenity, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forgiveness, and uprightness, as well as knowledge, realization, and belief in God, are the duties of Brahmanas, born of their own nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Quietude, self-control, purity, forbearance, straightforwardness, knowledge, wisdom, and faith in another world are the duties of the Brahmanas, born of their nature.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Serenity, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forgiveness, uprightness, knowledge, wisdom, and faith in God—these constitute the duty of a spiritual teacher.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.43।।शूरवीरता, तेज, धैर्य, प्रजाके संचालन आदिकी विशेष चतुरता, युद्धमें कभी पीठ न दिखाना, दान करना और शासन करनेका भाव -- ये सबकेसब क्षत्रियके स्वाभाविक कर्म हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.43।। शौर्य, तेज, धृति, दाक्ष्य (दक्षता), युद्ध से पलायन न करना, दान और ईश्वर भाव (स्वामी भाव) - ये सब क्षत्रिय के स्वाभाविक कर्म हैं।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Valor, invincibility, steadiness, dexterity, and not fleeing in battle, generosity, and regality are the duties of a Kshatriya, born of his inherent nature.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The natural duties of the Ksatriyas are heroism, boldness, fortitude, capability, and not retreating from battle, generosity, and lordliness.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Prowess, splendor, firmness, dexterity, and not fleeing from battle, generosity, and lordliness are the duties of the Kshatriyas, born of their own nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Heroic deeds, fiery energy, firmness, dexterity, and also non-attachment form the duties of the Ksatriyas, born of their nature, in battle, giving gifts, and overlordship.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Valor, glory, firmness, skill, generosity, steadiness in battle, and the ability to rule—these constitute the duty of a soldier. They flow from his own nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.44।।खेती करना, गायोंकी रक्षा करना और शुद्ध व्यापार करना -- ये सब-के-सब वैश्यके स्वाभाविक कर्म हैं, तथा चारों वर्णोंकी सेवा करना शूद्रका भी स्वाभाविक कर्म है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.44।। कृषि, गौपालन तथा वाणिज्य - ये वैश्य के स्वाभाविक कर्म हैं, और शूद्र का स्वाभाविक कर्म है परिचर्या अर्थात् सेवा करना।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Agriculture, cattle-breeding, and trade are the duties of the Vaisya, born of their nature. And the duty of a Sudra is one of service, born of their nature.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: The natural duties of the Vaisyas are agriculture, cattle-rearing, and trading. The natural duty of the Sudras, too, is in the form of service.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Agriculture, cattle-rearing, and trade are the duties of the Vaisya (merchant), born of their own nature; and service is the duty of the Sudra (servant-class), born of their own nature.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Ploughing, tending to cattle, and trading are the actions of the Vaisyas, born of their nature. The action of service is of the Sudras, born of their nature.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Agriculture, the protection of cows, and trade are the duties of a trader, in accordance with his nature. The duty of a servant is to serve, which also agrees with his nature.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.45।।अपने-अपने कर्ममें तत्परतापूर्वक लगा हुआ मनुष्य सम्यक् सिद्धि-(परमात्मा-)को प्राप्त कर लेता है। अपने कर्ममें लगा हुआ मनुष्य जिस प्रकार सिद्धिको प्राप्त होता है? उस प्रकारको तू मेरेसे सुन।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.45।। अपने-अपने स्वाभाविक कर्म में अभिरत मनुष्य संसिद्धि को प्राप्त कर लेता है। स्वकर्म में रत मनुष्य किस प्रकार सिद्धि प्राप्त करता है, उसे तुम सुनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Devoting oneself to one's duty, one attains perfection. Hear now how one devoted to their own duty attains perfection.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Being devoted to his own duty, one attains complete success. Hear how one devoted to their own duty achieves success.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Each person devoted to their own duty attains perfection. How they attain perfection while being engaged in their own duty, hear now.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: A person, devoted to their own respective action, attains success. How one attains success through devotion to their own action, that you must hear from Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Perfection is attained when one diligently attends to their duty. Listen, and I will tell you how it is attained by one who always minds their own duty.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.46।।जिस परमात्मासे सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंकी उत्पत्ति होती है और जिससे यह सम्पूर्ण संसार व्याप्त है, उस परमात्माका अपने कर्मके द्वारा पूजन करके मनुष्य सिद्धिको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.46।। जिस (परमात्मा) से भूतमात्र की प्रवृत्ति अर्थात् उत्पत्ति हुई है और जिससे यह सम्पूर्ण जगत् व्याप्त है, उस (परमात्मा) की स्वकर्म द्वारा पूजा करके मनुष्य सिद्धि को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: From whom all beings arise and by whom all this is pervaded—by worshipping Him with one's own duty, one reaches perfection.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: A human being achieves success by adoring, through his own duties, Him from whom is the origin of creatures and by whom all this is pervaded.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He from whom all the beings have evolved and by whom all this is pervaded, worshipping Him with his own duty, one attains perfection.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: From whence the activities of the beings arise, and by which this universe is pervaded—worshipping That by one's own prescribed action, one attains success.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: One reaches perfection by dedicating their actions to God, Who is the source of all being and fills everything.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.47।।अच्छी तरहसे अनुष्ठान किये हुए परधर्मसे गुणरहित अपना धर्म श्रेष्ठ है। कारण कि स्वभावसे नियत किये हुए स्वधर्मरूप कर्मको करता हुआ मनुष्य पापको प्राप्त नहीं होता।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.47।। सम्यक् अनुष्ठित परधर्म की अपेक्षा गुणरहित स्वधर्म श्रेष्ठ है। (क्योंकि) स्वभाव से नियत किये गये कर्म को करते हुए मनुष्य पाप को नहीं प्राप्त करता।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Better is one's own duty, though ill done, than the duty of another, though well-performed. When one does the duty ordained by their own nature, they incur no stain.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One's own duty, though defective, is superior to another's duty well performed. By performing a duty according to one's own nature, one does not incur sin.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Better is one's own duty, even if it is destitute of merits, than the duty of another well performed. He who does the duty ordained by his own nature incurs no sin.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Better is one's own prescribed duties, born of one's nature, even though it is devoid of excellence, than another's duty well executed; the doer of duty, dependent on one's own nature, does not incur sin.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: It is better to do one's own duty, however defective it may be, than to follow the duty of another, however well one may perform it. He who does his duty as his own nature reveals it, never commits a sin.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.48।।हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! दोषयुक्त होनेपर भी सहज कर्मका त्याग नहीं करना चाहिये; क्योंकि सम्पूर्ण कर्म धुएँसे अग्निकी तरह किसी-न-किसी दोषसे युक्त हैं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.48।। हे कौन्तेय ! दोषयुक्त होने पर भी सहज कर्म को नहीं त्यागना चाहिए; क्योंकि सभी कर्म दोष से आवृत होते है, जैसे धुयें से अग्नि।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: One should not relinquish one's work, O Arjuna, though it may be imperfect; for, all endeavors are enveloped by imperfections as fire is by smoke.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O son of Kunti, one should not give up the duty to which one is born, even though it be imperfect. For all undertakings are surrounded by evil, as fire is with smoke.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: One should not, O Arjuna, abandon the duty to which one is born, though it may be faulty; for, all undertakings are enveloped by evil, just as fire is by smoke.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Kunti! One should not give up their nature-born duty, even if it appears to be defective. For, all beginnings are enveloped by harm, just as fire is by smoke.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: The duty that falls to one's lot of its own accord should not be abandoned, though it may have its defects. All acts are marred by defects, just as fire is obscured by smoke.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.49।।जिसकी बुद्धि सब जगह आसक्तिरहित है, जिसने शरीरको वशमें कर रखा है, जो स्पृहारहित है, वह मनुष्य सांख्ययोगके द्वारा नैष्कर्म्य-सिद्धिको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.49।। सर्वत्र आसक्ति रहित बुद्धि वाला वह पुरुष जो स्पृहारहित तथा जितात्मा है, संन्यास के द्वारा परम नैर्ष्कम्य सिद्धि को प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He whose understanding is unattached on all sides, whose self is controlled, who is free from desires—he attains the supreme perfection transcending all activity through renunciation.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He whose intellect remains unattached to everything, who has conquered his internal organs and is desireless, attains through monasticism the supreme perfection consisting of the state of one free from duties.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He whose intellect is unattached everywhere, who has subdued his self, from whom desire has fled, he attains the supreme state of freedom from action through renunciation.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He, whose mind entertains no attachment to anything, who is self-controlled and is free from craving—he attains, by means of renunciation, the supreme success of actionlessness.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He whose mind is entirely detached, who has conquered himself, and whose desires have vanished, reaches that stage of perfect freedom through his renunciation, where action completes itself and leaves no seed.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.50।।हे कौन्तेय ! सिद्धि-(अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धि-) को प्राप्त हुआ साधक ब्रह्मको, जो कि ज्ञानकी परा निष्ठा है, जिस प्रकारसे प्राप्त होता है, उस प्रकारको तुम मुझसे संक्षेपमें ही समझो।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.50।। सिद्धि को प्राप्त पुरुष किस प्रकार ब्रह्म को प्राप्त होता है, तथा ज्ञान की परा निष्ठा को भी तुम मुझसे संक्षेप में जानो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Learn from me, O Arjuna, in brief how one who has attained perfection attains the Brahman—the supreme consummation of knowledge.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Understand for certain from Me, in brief indeed, O son of Kunti, that process by which one who has achieved success attains Brahman, which is the supreme consummation of knowledge.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Learn from Me, O Arjuna, in brief how one who has attained perfection reaches Brahman—the Eternal, that supreme state of knowledge.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having attained success, how one attains Brahman—an attainment which is confirmed to be the final beatitude of true knowledge—that you must learn from Me briefly.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: I will now state briefly how one who has reached perfection finds the Eternal Spirit, the state of Supreme Wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.51।।जो विशुद्ध (सात्त्विकी) बुद्धिसे युक्त, वैराग्यके आश्रित, एकान्तका सेवन करनेवाला और नियमित भोजन करनेवाला साधक धैर्यपूर्वक इन्द्रियोंका नियमन करके, शरीर-वाणी-मनको वशमें करके, शब्दादि विषयोंका त्याग करके और राग-द्वेषको छोड़कर निरन्तर ध्यानयोगके परायण हो जाता है, वह अहंकार, बल, दर्प, काम, क्रोध और परिग्रहका त्याग करके एवं निर्मम तथा शान्त होकर ब्रह्मप्राप्तिका पात्र हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.51।। विशुद्ध बुद्धि से युक्त, धृति से आत्मसंयम कर, शब्दादि विषयों को त्याग कर और राग-द्वेष का परित्याग कर....৷৷৷৷।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Endowed with a purified understanding, subduing the mind with steadiness, relinquishing sound and other objects of the senses and casting aside love and hate;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Endowed with a pure intellect and controlling oneself with fortitude, one should reject the objects beginning from sound [sound, touch, form, color, taste, and smell - Tr.] and eliminate attachment and hatred.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Endowed with a pure intellect, controlling the self through firmness, relinquishing sound and other objects and abandoning attraction and hatred.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: He who has attained a completely pure intellect by firmly controlling his mind and renouncing sense-objects, sound, and driving out desire and hatred;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Guided always by pure reason, bravely restraining himself, renouncing the objects of sense and giving up attachment and hatred; he attains peace.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.52।।जो विशुद्ध (सात्त्विकी) बुद्धिसे युक्त, वैराग्यके आश्रित, एकान्तका सेवन करनेवाला और नियमित भोजन करनेवाला साधक धैर्यपूर्वक इन्द्रियोंका नियमन करके, शरीर-वाणी-मनको वशमें करके, शब्दादि विषयोंका त्याग करके और राग-द्वेषको छोड़कर निरन्तर ध्यानयोगके परायण हो जाता है, वह अहंकार, बल, दर्प, काम, क्रोध और परिग्रहका त्याग करके एवं निर्मम तथा शान्त होकर ब्रह्मप्राप्तिका पात्र हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.52।। विविक्त सेवी, लघ्वाशी (मिताहारी) जिसने अपने शरीर, वाणी और मन को संयत किया है, ध्यानयोग के अभ्यास में सदैव तत्पर तथा वैराग्य पर समाश्रित।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Resorting to solitude, eating sparingly, restraining speech, body, and mind, ever engaged in the yoga of meditation and taking refuge in dispassion;

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who resorts to solitude, eats sparingly, has control over their speech, body, and mind, for whom meditation and concentration are ever the highest duty, and who is possessed of dispassion;

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Dwelling in solitude, eating sparingly, with speech, body, and mind subdued, always engaged in meditation and concentration, and resorting to dispassion.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Who enjoys solitude, eats sparingly, has controlled their speech, body, and mind; who is devoted to the practice of meditation and yoga; and who has taken refuge in perpetual desirelessness;

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Enjoying solitude, being abstemious, having his body, mind, and speech under perfect control, and being absorbed in meditation, he becomes free—always filled with the spirit of renunciation.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.53।।जो विशुद्ध (सात्त्विकी) बुद्धिसे युक्त, वैराग्यके आश्रित, एकान्तका सेवन करनेवाला और नियमित भोजन करनेवाला साधक धैर्यपूर्वक इन्द्रियोंका नियमन करके, शरीर-वाणी-मनको वशमें करके, शब्दादि विषयोंका त्याग करके और राग-द्वेषको छोड़कर निरन्तर ध्यानयोगके परायण हो जाता है, वह अहंकार, बल, दर्प, काम, क्रोध और परिग्रहका त्याग करके एवं निर्मम तथा शान्त होकर ब्रह्मप्राप्तिका पात्र हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.53।। अहंकार, बल, दर्प, काम, क्रोध और परिग्रह को त्याग कर ममत्वभाव से रहित और शान्त पुरुष ब्रह्म प्राप्ति के योग्य बन जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Forsaking egoism, power, pride, desire, wrath, and possessions, with no feeling of 'mine' and tranquil, he becomes worthy of the state of Brahman.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Having discarded egotism, force, pride, desire, anger, and superfluous possessions, and being free from the idea of possession and serene, that person is fit for becoming Brahman.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having abandoned egoism, strength, arrogance, desire, anger, and covetousness, and being free from the notion of 'mine' and peaceful, he is fit for becoming Brahman.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Relinquishing egotism, violence, pride, desire, wrath, and the sense of possession - he, the unselfish and calm one, is capable of becoming the Brahman.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Having abandoned selfishness, power, arrogance, anger, and desire, possessing nothing of his own, and having attained peace, he is fit to join the Eternal Spirit.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.54।।वह ब्रह्मभूत-अवस्थाको प्राप्त प्रसन्न मनवाला साधक न तो किसीके लिये शोक करता है और न किसीकी इच्छा करता है। ऐसा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें समभाववाला साधक मेरी पराभक्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.54।। ब्रह्मभूत (जो साधक ब्रह्म बन गया है), प्रसन्न मन वाला पुरुष न इच्छा करता है और न शोक, समस्त भूतों के प्रति सम होकर वह मेरी परा भक्ति को प्राप्त करता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Having realized the state of Brahman, tranquil, he neither grieves nor craves. Regarding all beings alike, he attains supreme devotion to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: One who has become Brahman and has attained the blissful Self does not grieve nor desire. Becoming the same towards all beings, he attains supreme devotion to Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Becoming Brahman, serene in the Self, he neither grieves nor desires; he is the same to all beings, and obtains supreme devotion to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having become the Brahman, the serene-minded one neither grieves nor rejoices; remaining equal to all beings, he gains the highest devotion to Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: When he unites with the Eternal and his soul knows the bliss of the Self, he feels no desire or regret, he regards all beings equally, and enjoys the blessing of supreme devotion to Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.55।।उस पराभक्तिसे मेरेको, मैं जितना हूँ और जो हूँ -- इसको तत्त्वसे जान लेता है तथा मेरेको तत्त्वसे जानकर फिर तत्काल मेरेमें प्रविष्ट हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.55।। (उस परा) भक्ति के द्वारा मुझे वह तत्त्वत: जानता है कि मैं कितना (व्यापक) हूँ तथा मैं क्या हूँ। (इस प्रकार) तत्त्वत: जानने के पश्चात् तत्काल ही वह मुझमें प्रवेश कर जाता है, अर्थात् मत्स्वरूप बन जाता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Through devotion, he comes to know Me fully—who and what I am in reality, who I am and how I am. Knowing Me thus in truth, he forthwith enters into Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Through devotion, he knows Me in reality, as to what and who I am. Then, having known Me truly, he enters into Me immediately afterward.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: By devotion, he knows Me in truth, who and what I am; then, having known Me in truth, he immediately enters into the Supreme.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Through devotion he comes to know of Me: Who I am and how, in fact, I am—having correctly known Me, he enters into Me. Then afterwards,

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: By such devotion, he sees Me, who I am and what I am; and thus realizing the truth, he enters into My kingdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.56।।मेरा आश्रय लेनेवाला भक्त सदा सब कर्म करता हुआ भी मेरी कृपासे शाश्वत अविनाशी पदको प्राप्त हो जाता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.56।। जो पुरुष मदाश्रित होकर सदैव समस्त कर्मों को करता है, वह मेरे प्रसाद (अनुग्रह) से शाश्वत, अव्यय पद को प्राप्त कर लेता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Taking refuge in Me and performing all works constantly, one, by My grace, attains the eternal and immutable abode.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Ever engaging in all actions, one to whom I am the refuge, attains the eternal, immutable state through my grace.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Having taken refuge in Me and doing all actions, by My grace he obtains the eternal, indestructible state of being.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Performing all his actions all the time and taking refuge in Me, he attains, through My grace, the eternal, changeless state.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Relying on Me in all his actions and doing them for My sake, he attains, by My grace, eternal and unchangeable life.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.57।।चित्तसे सम्पूर्ण कर्म मुझमें अर्पण करके, मेरे परायण होकर तथा समताका आश्रय लेकर निरन्तर मुझमें चित्तवाला हो जा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.57।। मन से समस्त कर्मों का संन्यास मुझमें करके मत्परायण होकर बुद्धियोग का आश्रय लेकर तुम सतत मच्चित्त बनो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Surrender all your actions to Me with your mind, keep Me as your goal, and practice Buddhi-yoga, focusing your thoughts on Me always.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Mentally surrendering all actions to Me and accepting Me as the supreme, keep your mind ever fixed on Me by resorting to the concentration of your intellect.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Mentally renouncing all actions in Me, having Me as the highest goal, and resorting to the yoga of discrimination, do thou ever fix thy mind on Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Hence, O descendant of Bharata, renounce all actions in Me by your mind and take hold of the knowledge-Yoga. Always keep your thought-organ directed towards Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Surrender your actions to Me, live in Me, focus your intellect on Me, and always think of Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.58।।मेरेमें चित्तवाला होकर तू मेरी कृपासे सम्पूर्ण विघ्नोंको तर जायगा और यदि तू अहंकारके कारण मेरी बात नहीं सुनेगा तो तेरा पतन हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.58।। मच्चित्त होकर तुम मेरी कृपा से समस्त कठिनाइयों (सर्वदुर्गाणि) को पार कर जाओगे; और यदि अहंकारवश (इस उपदेश को) नहीं सुनोगे, तो तुम नष्ट हो जाओगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Focusing your thoughts on Me, you shall, by My grace, cross over all difficulties. If, however, out of self-conceit, you do not heed Me, you will perish.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Having your mind fixed on Me, you will cross over all difficulties by My grace. If, however, you do not listen out of egotism, you will be destroyed.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Fixing your mind on Me, you shall, by My grace, overcome all obstacles; but if you will not hear Me due to egoism, you shall perish.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Having your thought-organ turned towards Me, you shall pass over all obstacles by My Grace. On the other hand, if you don't give up your sense of ego, you will not liberate yourself; instead, you will perish.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Fix your mind on Me, and by My grace you will overcome the obstacles in your path. But if, misled by pride, you do not listen, then you will indeed be lost.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.59।।अहंकारका आश्रय लेकर तू जो ऐसा मान रहा है कि मैं युद्ध नहीं करूँगा, तेरा यह निश्चय मिथ्या (झूठा) है; क्योंकि तेरी क्षात्र-प्रकृति तेरेको युद्धमें लगा देगी।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.59।। और अहंकारवश तुम जो यह सोच रहे हो, "मैं युद्ध नहीं करूंगा", यह तुम्हारा निश्चय मिथ्या है, (क्योंकि) प्रकृति (तुम्हारा स्वभाव) ही तुम्हें (बलात् कर्म में) प्रवृत्त करेगी।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: If, in your self-conceit, you think, 'I will not fight,' your resolve is futile. Nature will compel you.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Though you think 'I shall not fight', relying on egotism, this determination of yours is vain. Your nature will impel you!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: If, filled with egoism, thou thinkest, "I will not fight," then thy resolve is vain; nature will compel thee.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: In case, holding fast to the sense of ego, you think, 'I shall not fight,' that resolve of yours will be useless. Your own natural condition will incite you to fight.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: If you in your vanity think of avoiding this fight, your will shall not be fulfilled, for Nature herself will compel you.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.60।।हे कुन्तीनन्दन ! अपने स्वभावजन्य कर्मसे बँधा हुआ तू मोहके कारण जो नहीं करना चाहता, उसको तू (क्षात्र-प्रकृतिके) परवश होकर करेगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.60।। हे कौन्तेय ! तुम अपने स्वाभाविक कर्मों से बंधे हो, (अत:) मोहवशात् जिस कर्म को तुम करना नहीं चाहते हो, वही तुम विवश होकर करोगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: O Arjuna, bound by your own duty born out of your own inner disposition, and having no control over your own will, you will be compelled to do that very thing which you now do not desire to do through delusion.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Being bound by your own duty, born of nature, O son of Kunti, you will, being helpless, verily do that which you do not wish to do, owing to indiscretion.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: O Arjuna, bound by your own Karma (action) born of your own nature, that which from delusion you wish not to do, even that you shall do helplessly.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Kunti! Being firmly bound by your own duty, born of your own nature, and thus not being independent, you would perform what you do not wish to do, due to your delusion.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna! Your duty binds you. It has arisen from your own nature, and that which you do not desire to do in your delusion, you will do that very thing. You are helpless.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.61।।हे अर्जुन ! ईश्वर सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हृदयमें रहता है और अपनी मायासे शरीररूपी यन्त्रपर आरूढ़ हुए सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंको (उनके स्वभावके अनुसार) भ्रमण कराता रहता है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.61।। हे अर्जुन (मानों किसी) यन्त्र पर आरूढ़ समस्त भूतों को ईश्वर अपनी माया से घुमाता हुआ (भ्रामयन्) भूतमात्र के हृदय में स्थित रहता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: The Lord, O Arjuna, abides in the heart of every being, spinning them round and round, as if mounted on a wheel, by His power.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Arjuna, the Lord resides in the region of the hearts of all creatures, revolving through Maya all the creatures as though mounted on a machine!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: The Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings, by His illusory power, to revolve as if mounted on a machine.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O Arjuna! This Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, causing, by His trick of illusion, all beings to whirl round as if they are mounted on a revolving mechanical contrivance.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: God dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna! He causes them to revolve, as it were, on a wheel, by His mystic power.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.62।।हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन ! तू सर्वभावसे उस ईश्वरकी ही शरणमें चला जा। उसकी कृपासे तू परमशान्ति-(संसारसे सर्वथा उपरति-) को और अविनाशी परमपदको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.62।। हे भारत ! तुम सम्पूर्ण भाव से उसी (ईश्वर) की शरण में जाओ। उसके प्रसाद से तुम परम शान्ति और शाश्वत स्थान को प्राप्त करोगे।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Seek refuge in Him alone, O Arjuna, with the entirety of your being. By His grace, you shall find supreme peace and an eternal abode.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Take refuge in Him alone with your whole being, O scion of the Bharata dynasty. Through His grace, you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Fly to Him for refuge with all your being, O Arjuna; by His grace you will obtain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: To Him alone, O descendant of Bharata, you must go for refuge with all your thoughts. Through My grace, you will attain success and the eternal abode.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: With all your strength, fly to Him and surrender yourself, and by His grace you will attain Supreme Peace and reach the Eternal Home.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.63।।यह गुह्यसे भी गुह्यतर (शरणागतिरूप) ज्ञान मैंने तुझे कह दिया। अब तू इसपर अच्छी तरहसे विचार करके जैसा चाहता है, वैसा कर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.63।। इस प्रकार समस्त गोपनीयों से अधिक गुह्य ज्ञान मैंने तुमसे कहा; इस पर पूर्ण विचार (विमृश्य) करने के पश्चात् तुम्हारी जैसी इच्छा हो, वैसा तुम करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Thus, the knowledge, the mystery of mysteries, has been declared to you by Me. Reflecting on it fully, do as you will.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: To you I have imparted this knowledge, which is more secret than any secret. Ponder over this as a whole and do as you like.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Thus, wisdom more secret than secrecy itself has been declared to you by me. Reflect on it fully, then act as you wish.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Thus, the path of wisdom, a better secret than all other secrets, has been expounded to you by Me; comprehend it fully and then act as you wish.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Thus, I have revealed to you the truth, the mystery of mysteries. Having thought it over, you are free to act as you wish.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.64।।सबसे अत्यन्त गोपनीय वचन तू फिर मेरेसे सुन। तू मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय है, इसलिये मैं तेरे हितकी बात कहूँगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.64।। पुन: एक बार तुम मुझसे समस्त गुह्यों में गुह्यतम परम वचन (उपदेश) को सुनो। तुम मुझे अतिशय प्रिय हो, इसलिए मैं तुम्हें तुम्हारे हित की बात कहूंगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Hear again My supreme word, the most secret of all; as I am exceedingly fond of you, I am telling you what is good for you.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Listen again to My highest utterance, which is the most profound of all. Since you are ever dear to Me, I shall speak what is beneficial to you.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Hear again My supreme word, most secret of all; for you are dearly beloved of Me, I will tell you what is good.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Yet again, you must listen to My ultimate, supreme message—the highest secret of all. You are My dear one and have a firm intellect, so I shall tell you what is good for you.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Listen to My last word once more, the deepest secret of all; you are My beloved, you are My friend, and I speak for your welfare.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.65।।तू मेरा भक्त हो जा, मेरेमें मनवाला हो जा, मेरा पूजन करनेवाला हो जा और मेरेको नमस्कार कर। ऐसा करनेसे तू मेरेको ही प्राप्त हो जायगा -- यह मैं तेरे सामने सत्य प्रतिज्ञा करता हूँ; क्योंकि तू मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.65।। तुम मच्चित, मद्भक्त और मेरे पूजक (मद्याजी) बनो और मुझे नमस्कार करो; (इस प्रकार) तुम मुझे ही प्राप्त होगे; यह मैं तुम्हे सत्य वचन देता हूँ,(क्योंकि) तुम मेरे प्रिय हो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Focus your mind on Me. Be My devotee. Worship Me. Prostrate before Me. You will come to Me alone. I promise you truly; for you are dear to Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, offer sacrifices to Me, and bow down to Me. Thus, you will come to Me alone. I promise you this truth, for you are dear to Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. You will come to Me; I truly promise you this, for you are dear to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; offer oblations to Me and bow down to Me; you will come to Me alone. I promise you this truly, for you are dear to Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Dedicate yourself to Me, worship Me, sacrifice all for Me, prostrate yourself before Me, and you will surely come to Me. I truly pledge to you; you are My beloved.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.66।।सम्पूर्ण धर्मोंका आश्रय छोड़कर तू केवल मेरी शरणमें आ जा। मैं तुझे सम्पूर्ण पापोंसे मुक्त कर दूँगा, चिन्ता मत कर।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.66।। सब धर्मों का परित्याग करके तुम एक मेरी ही शरण में आओ, मैं तुम्हें समस्त पापों से मुक्त कर दूँगा, तुम शोक मत करो।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Relinquishing all Dharmas completely, seek Me alone for refuge. I will release you from all sins; grieve not.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Abandon all forms of rites and duties, and take refuge in Me alone. I will free you from all sins; do not grieve.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Abandon all duties and take refuge in Me alone; I will liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Abandon all attributes and come to Me as your only refuge; I will rescue you from all sins; do not be sorrowful.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Give up your earthly duties, surrender yourself to Me alone. Do not be anxious; I will absolve you from all your sins.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.67।।यह सर्वगुह्यतम वचन अतपस्वीको मत कहना; अभक्तको कभी मत कहना; जो सुनना नहीं चाहता, उसको मत कहना; और जो मेरेमें दोषदृष्टि करता है, उससे भी मत कहना।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.67।। यह ज्ञान ऐसे पुरुष से नहीं कहना चाहिए, जो अतपस्क (तपरहित) है, और न उसे जो अभक्त है; उसे भी नहीं जो अशुश्रुषु (सेवा में अतत्पर) है और उस पुरुष से भी नहीं कहना चाहिए, जो मुझ (ईश्वर) से असूया करता है, अर्थात् मुझ में दोष देखता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Never should this be told by you to one who is not austere, who is not a devotee, nor to one who has no desire to listen, nor certainly to him who speaks ill of Me.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: This (that I have taught) should never be taught to one who is devoid of austerities, one who is not a devotee, one who does not render service, or one who cavils at Me.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Never speak this to one who is devoid of austerities or devotion, who does not render service, who does not desire to listen, or who cavils at Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: This knowledge is for you, and it should never be imparted to one who does not observe austerities; to one who has no devotion; to one who has no desire to listen; and to one who is indignant towards Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Do not speak this to one who has not practiced austerities, or to one who does not love, or who will not listen, or who mocks.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.68।।मेरेमें पराभक्ति करके जो इस परम गोपनीय संवाद-(गीता-ग्रन्थ) को मेरे भक्तोंमें कहेगा, वह मुझे ही प्राप्त होगा -- इसमें कोई सन्देह नहीं है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.68।। जो पुरुष मुझसे परम प्रेम (परा भक्ति) करके इस परम गुह्य ज्ञान का उपदेश मेरे भक्तों को देता है, वह नि:सन्देह मुझे ही प्राप्त होता है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: He who proclaims this supreme mystery among My devotees shall come to Me, acquiring supreme devotion towards Me. There is no doubt about this.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: He who, entertaining supreme devotion to Me, speaks of this highest secret to My devotees, will without doubt reach Me alone.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: He who, with supreme devotion to Me, teaches this supreme secret to My devotees, shall undoubtedly come to Me.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever declares this highest secret to My devotees, cultivating an utmost devotion towards Me and not entertaining any doubt, shall reach Me.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: But he who teaches this great secret to My devotees, his is the highest devotion, and he shall surely come to Me.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.69।।उसके समान मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय कार्य करनेवाला मनुष्योंमें कोई भी नहीं है और इस भूमण्डलपर उसके समान मेरा दूसरा कोई प्रियतर होगा भी नहीं।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.69।। न तो उससे बढ़कर मेरा अतिशय प्रिय कार्य करने वाला मनुष्यों में कोई है और न उससे बढ़कर मेरा प्रिय इस पृथ्वी पर दूसरा कोई होगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: There is no one among men who does dearer service to Me than him. Nor shall there be anyone on earth dearer to Me than him.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And compared to him, no other human is better at accomplishing what is dear to Me. Furthermore, there will be no one else in the world dearer to Me than him.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: There is no one among men who does service dearer to Me, nor shall there be anyone on earth dearer to Me than him.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: And, except for him, there would be no one among men who is the best performer of what is dear to Me; and there shall be no one else dearer to Me on earth.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: There is no one among men who can perform a service dearer to Me than this, nor is there any man on earth more beloved by Me than him.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.70।।जो मनुष्य हम दोनोंके इस धर्ममय संवादका अध्ययन करेगा, उसके द्वारा भी मैं ज्ञानयज्ञसे पूजित होऊँगा -- ऐसा मेरा मत है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.70।। जो पुरुष, हम दोनों के इस धर्ममय संवाद का पठन करेगा, उसके द्वारा मैं ज्ञानयज्ञ से पूजित होऊँगा - ऐसा मेरा मत है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: And he who will study our dialogue, which is consistent with Dharma, by him I shall be worshipped through the sacrifice of knowledge; that is My view.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And he who will study this sacred conversation between us two, which is conducive to virtue, by him I shall be adored through the Sacrifice of Knowledge. This is My judgment.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And he who studies this sacred dialogue of ours, by him I shall have been worshipped through the sacrifice of wisdom; such is my conviction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Whoever would learn this sacred dialogue of both of us, by them I am delighted through the knowledge-sacrifice: This is my opinion.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: He who will study this spiritual discourse of ours, I assure you, they shall thereby worship Me at the altar of Wisdom.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.71।।श्रद्धावान् और दोषदृष्टिसे रहित जो मनुष्य इस गीता-ग्रन्थको सुन भी लेगा, वह भी सम्पूर्ण पापोंसे मुक्त होकर पुण्यकारियोंके शुभ लोकोंको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.71।। तथा जो श्रद्धावान् और अनसुयु (दोषदृष्टि रहित) पुरुष इसका श्रवणमात्र भी करेगा, वह भी (पापों से) मुक्त होकर पुण्यकर्मियों के शुभ (श्रेष्ठ) लोकों को प्राप्त कर लेगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: And the man who listens to it with faith and without cavil, he too shall be released and shall reach the auspicious realms of those who have performed virtuous deeds.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Any man who, being reverent and free from caviling, listens to this (teaching) with reverence, he too, becoming free, shall attain the blessed worlds of those who perform virtuous deeds.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Also, the man who hears this, full of faith and free from malice, shall attain to the happy worlds of those of righteous deeds, and be liberated.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: A man who would at least hear this with faith and without indignation—he too, freed from sins, will attain the auspicious worlds of those who have performed meritorious acts.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Yes, he who listens to it with faith and without doubt, even he, freed from evil, will rise to the worlds which the virtuous attain through righteous deeds.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.72।।हे पृथानन्दन ! क्या तुमने एकाग्र-चित्तसे इसको सुना ?और हे धनञ्जय ! क्या तुम्हारा अज्ञानसे उत्पन्न मोह नष्ट हुआ ?

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.72।। हे पार्थ ! क्या इसे (मेरे उपदेश को) तुमने एकाग्रचित्त होकर श्रवण किया ? और हे धनञ्जय ! क्या तुम्हारा अज्ञान जनित संमोह पूर्णतया नष्ट हुआ ?

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Have you heard this, O Arjuna, with a single-minded focus? Has your delusion, caused by ignorance, been dispelled?

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O Partha, have you listened to this with a one-pointed mind? O Dhananjaya, has your delusion caused by ignorance been dispelled?

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Has this been heard, O Arjuna, with one-pointed focus? Has the delusion of your ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O son of Prtha! Have you heard this with an attentive mind? O Dhananjaya! Has your strong delusion, born of ignorance, been completely destroyed?

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O Arjuna, have you listened attentively to My words? Has your ignorance and delusion gone?

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.73।।अर्जुन बोले -- हे अच्युत ! आपकी कृपासे मेरा मोह नष्ट हो गया है और स्मृति प्राप्त हो गयी है। मैं सन्देहरहित होकर स्थित हूँ। अब मैं आपकी आज्ञाका पालन करूँगा।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.73।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- हे अच्युत ! आपके कृपाप्रसाद से मेरा मोह नष्ट हो गया है, और मुझे स्मृति (ज्ञान) प्राप्त हो गयी है? अब मैं संशयरहित हो गया हूँ और मैं आपके वचन (आज्ञा) का पालन करूँगा।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Arjuna said, "My delusion has been destroyed, and, by Your grace, O Krsna, I have gained knowledge (Smrti). Freed from doubts, I stand steadfast, and I will fulfill Your will."

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Arjuna said, "O Acyuta, my delusion has been destroyed and my memory has been regained through Your grace. I stand with my doubt removed; I shall follow Your instructions."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Arjuna said, "My delusion has been destroyed, for I have gained my knowledge (memory) through Your grace, O Krishna. I am now free from doubts. I will act according to Your word."

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Arjuna said, "My delusion is destroyed; I have regained my recollection through your grace, O Acyuta! I stand firm, free of doubts; I shall execute your command."

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Arjuna replied: "My Lord! O Immutable One! My delusion has fled. By Your Grace, O Changeless One, the light has dawned. My doubts have been dispelled, and I stand before You ready to do Your will."

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.74।।सञ्जय बोले -- इस प्रकार मैंने भगवान् वासुदेव और महात्मा पृथानन्दन अर्जुनका यह रोमाञ्चित करनेवाला अद्भुत संवाद सुना।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.74।। संजय ने कहा -- इस प्रकार मैंने भगवान् वासुदेव और महात्मा अर्जुन के इस अद्भुत और रोमान्चक संवाद का वर्णन किया।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Sanjaya said, Thus I have heard this wondrous dialogue between Vasudeva and the great-minded Arjuna, which makes my hair stand on end.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Sanjaya said, "I thus heard this conversation between Vasudeva and the great-souled Partha, which is unique and makes one's hair stand on end."

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Sanjaya said, Thus, I have heard this wonderful dialogue between Krishna and the high-souled Arjuna, which causes one's hair to stand on end.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Sanjaya said: Thus, I have heard this wonderful and thrilling dialogue between Vasudeva and the mighty-minded son of Prtha.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Sanjaya said: "I heard this rare, wonderful, and soul-stirring discourse between Lord Shri Krishna and the great-souled Arjuna.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.75।।व्यासजीकी कृपासे मैंने स्वयं इस परम गोपनीय योग (गीता-ग्रन्थ) को कहते हुए साक्षात् योगेश्वर भगवान् श्रीकृष्णसे सुना है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.75।। व्यास जी की कृपा से मैंने इस परम् गुह्य योग को साक्षात् कहते हुए स्वयं योगोश्वर श्रीकृष्ण भगवान् से सुना।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: By the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this supreme mystery of Yoga, declared in person by Krsna, the Lord of Yoga.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Through the favor of Vyasa, I heard this secret concerning the supreme Yoga from Krishna, the Lord of Yogas, while He Himself was speaking!

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Through the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this supreme and most secret Yoga, directly from Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, Himself declaring it.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Through the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this highly secret supreme Yoga from Krsna, the Lord of Yogis, while He was imparting it personally.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Through the blessing of the sage Vyasa, I heard this secret and noble science from the lips of its Master, Lord Shri Krishna.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.76।।हे राजन् ! भगवान् श्रीकृष्ण और अर्जुनके इस पवित्र और अद्भुत संवादको याद कर-करके मैं बार-बार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.76।। हे राजन् ! भगवान् केशव और अर्जुन के इस अद्भुत और पुण्य (पवित्र) संवाद को स्मरण करके मैं बारम्बार हर्षित होता हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: O King, remembering again and again this wondrous and auspicious dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna, I rejoice time and again.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: And, O King, while repeatedly remembering this unique, sacred dialogue between Kesava and Arjuna, I rejoice every moment.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: O King, remembering this wonderful and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I continually rejoice.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O King! By repeatedly recollecting this wonderful, pious dialogue between Kesava and Arjuna, I am filled with delight again and again.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: O King! The more I think of that marvelous and holy discourse, the more I lose myself in joy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.77।।हे राजन् ! भगवान् श्रीकृष्णके उस अत्यन्त अद्भुत विराट्रूपको याद कर-करके मेरेको बड़ा भारी आश्चर्य हो रहा है और मैं बार-बार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.77।। हे राजन ! श्री हरि के अति अद्भुत रूप को भी पुन: पुन: स्मरण करके मुझे महान् विस्मय होता है और मैं बारम्बार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: And, remembering over and over again that most marvelous form of Hari, great is my amazement, O King, and I rejoice again and again.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: O King, repeatedly recollecting that greatly extraordinary form of Hari, I am struck with wonder and rejoice again and again.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: And, remembering again and again that most wonderful form of Hari, I am filled with great wonder, O King; and I rejoice again and again.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: O great king! On recalling in my mind that extremely wonderful, supreme form of Hari, I am amazed and experience joy again and again.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: As I recall again and again the exceeding beauty of the Lord, I am filled with amazement and joy.

author: Swami Ramsukhdas
text: ।।18.78।।जहाँ योगेश्वर भगवान् श्रीकृष्ण हैं और जहाँ गाण्डीवधनुषधारी अर्जुन हैं, वहाँ ही श्री, विजय, विभूति और अचल नीति है -- ऐसा मेरा मत है।

author: Swami Tejomayananda
text: ।।18.78।। जहाँ योगेश्वर श्रीकृष्ण हैं और जहाँ धनुर्धारी अर्जुन है वहीं पर श्री, विजय, विभूति और ध्रुव नीति है, ऐसा मेरा मत है।।

author: Swami Adidevananda
text: Wherever there is Sri Krsna, the Lord of Yoga, and Arjuna the archer, there is always fortune, victory, wealth, and sound morality. This is my firm conviction.

author: Swami Gambirananda
text: Where there is Krishna, the Lord of yogas, and where there is Partha, the wielder of the bow, there is fortune, victory, prosperity, and unfailing prudence. Such is my conviction.

author: Swami Sivananda
text: Wherever Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, is; and wherever Arjuna, the wielder of the bow, is; there is prosperity, victory, happiness, and a firm policy; this is my conviction.

author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
text: Where Krishna, the Lord of Yogis, remains, where the son of Prtha holds his bow, there lies fortune, victory, prosperity, and firm justice—so I believe.

author: Shri Purohit Swami
text: Wherever Lord Shri Krishna, the Prince of Wisdom, and Arjuna, the Great Archer, are, I am certain that good fortune, victory, happiness, and righteousness will follow.
